build.zig.short_description: Zig build script that defines build targets, links native deps (V8, curl, brotli, zlib, boringssl, nghttp2), and creates artifacts (executables, snapshot creator, tests).
build.zig.description:
This is the project's central Zig build script. It validates the running Zig toolchain version, resolves the package version, configures build options and sanitizers, and composes the full build graph for the Lightpanda product. The script creates the main library module (lightpanda), wires in imports and build-time options, enforces a code format check as the default step, and adds linked native dependencies (V8, libcurl and its transitive C-libraries, brotli, zlib, nghttp2, boringssl-zig). It defines the primary artifacts: the lightpanda executable, a snapshot-creator executable, a legacy_test executable, and the unit tests; it also exposes run steps and convenience steps (fmt, check, run, version, snapshot_creator).

The file is designed to be both the local developer entry point and CI entry point: it exposes options for prebuilt V8 path and snapshot path, and supports sanitizer toggles (TSAN, ASAN, CSAN). Native C libraries are compiled from bundled sources via Zig's C source integration (e.g., zlib, brotli, nghttp2, curl). The script also calls cargo to build the html5ever Rust crate and adds the produced static archive to the link line. There are platform-specific conditionals (macOS frameworks) and ABI/OS aware configuration used when building libcurl and its dependencies.

Design decisions reflected here: build-time version resolution and percent-encoding of the version for safe use in telemetry/crash URLs (see commit insights); a dedicated lightweight "check" step that compiles libraries only to enable faster CI checks; and a modular set of build helper functions (linkV8, linkCurl, linkHtml5Ever, and multiple build* functions) so each third-party dependency is encapsulated and configured in one place. The script expects several external toolchains (cargo for html5ever, a V8 dependency or prebuilt static archive, and Zig >= configured minimum). Because the remainder of the file is large and contains a long curl configuration and many C source files, edits must respect the large set of build flags and files defined later in the file (the provided snippet is truncated).

key_constructs.name: Build
key_constructs.purpose: A compile-time alias that checks the running Zig version against the minimum and resolves to std.Build if the check passes.
key_constructs.reasoning: Top-level Zig version gate: any change to minimum_zig_version semantics or how the check is done must preserve the compile-time error behavior when the Zig version is too old.

key_constructs.name: build
key_constructs.purpose: Main entry point invoked by 'zig build': declares targets, options, modules, build steps, and artifacts (executables, test suite, snapshot creator).
key_constructs.reasoning: This constructs the entire build graph including default steps (format check), options exposure (prebuilt_v8_path, snapshot_path, tsan/asan), and installation of artifacts. Changes here will affect all build artifacts and CI behavior.

key_constructs.name: linkV8
key_constructs.purpose: Adds the V8 dependency to the main module and wires options (prebuilt_v8_path, inspector, sandbox flags, cache path).
key_constructs.reasoning: V8 is critical and has its own dependency helper. Any change must preserve the expected dependency name 'v8' and the dependency options; CI relies on .lp-cache and prebuilt_v8_path semantics.

key_constructs.name: linkHtml5Ever
key_constructs.purpose: Invokes cargo to build the Rust html5ever crate and adds the produced static archive (liblitefetch_html5ever.a) to the module's object files.
key_constructs.reasoning: This creates a bridge to Rust-built parsing code. It depends on 'cargo' being available and on the layout produced by Cargo; changes must keep the target-dir invocation and object file path consistent.

key_constructs.name: linkCurl
key_constructs.purpose: Constructs and links a libcurl build and wires its transitive dependencies (zlib, brotli, nghttp2, boringssl). Adds macOS frameworks where necessary.
key_constructs.reasoning: curl is configured/compiled from bundled sources with many compile-time flags. Altering how curl is built or linked will affect network behavior and supported protocols; platform conditionals are significant.

key_constructs.name: buildZlib
key_constructs.purpose: Creates a zlib compile step from bundled C sources and exposes headers for other libraries.
key_constructs.reasoning: zlib is compiled with specific defines; modifications must ensure source files and flags remain correct for cross-platform builds.

key_constructs.name: buildBrotli
key_constructs.purpose: Creates compile steps for brotli's three components (common, dec, enc) and installs headers.
key_constructs.reasoning: brotli is used by curl for compression; its include paths and source lists are explicit and relied upon by the curl build config.

key_constructs.name: buildBoringSsl
key_constructs.purpose: Adds the boringssl-zig dependency artifacts (ssl, crypto) and adjusts bundling flags.
key_constructs.reasoning: Boringssl is provided by a zig dependency; returned artifacts have bundling behavior toggled. Changes might affect symbol bundling and UBSAN/RT.

key_constructs.name: buildNghttp2
key_constructs.purpose: Compiles nghttp2 from source, creates a config header, installs headers, and lists the C sources to compile.
key_constructs.reasoning: HTTP/2 support depends on this; the function sets many defines and must remain synchronized with the included C source tree.

key_constructs.name: buildCurl
key_constructs.purpose: Build helper that configures curl compile-time options and wires platform/ABI specifics, include paths and links to other compiled libraries.
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains a large config map controlling curl features (e.g., disabling protocols, enabling brotli, enabling websockets) and target-specific flags; edits here affect the entire network stack behavior and supported features.

key_behaviors: validates Zig toolchain version at compile-time
resolves and prints project version and exposes it to build options
adds/links native dependencies and compiles bundled C sources
creates build artifacts: lightpanda, snapshot creator, legacy_test, and test runner
enforces code formatting check as a default build step

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or bypassing the Zig version check (Build constant) or changing its semantics.
pitfalls.consequence: Builds can succeed with an unsupported Zig version, causing subtle compile-time or runtime breakage across the project.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the builtin.zig_version comparison logic and the compile-time error behavior when minimum_zig_version is not met.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing how version_string / version_encoded are created or removing percent-encoding.
pitfalls.consequence: Telemetry or crash-report URLs may be constructed with unsafe characters, breaking query semantics (histor bug fixed in commit 9661204).
pitfalls.prevention: Keep version_encoded logic (replace '+' with '%2B') or use an equivalent percent-encoding approach; ensure version_string is still exposed as an option.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering linkHtml5Ever command arguments, output path, or object filename without updating how the produced static archive is referenced.
pitfalls.consequence: The build may fail to find liblitefetch_html5ever.a or may link an unexpected artifact, breaking HTML parsing integration.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the --target-dir behavior and the expected output path of html5ever's static archive; keep the obj path in sync.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying libcurl build flags or the long C source list without understanding platform and ABI conditionals used later in the file.
pitfalls.consequence: Network features (protocol enablement, websocket support), SSL backend selection, or platform-specific proxy support may be inadvertently removed or misconfigured.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing curl config flags or C source lists, review platform-specific branches (macOS frameworks, ABI checks) and test network-related functionality across platforms.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing the formatting check (fmt_step) from b.default_step or setting .check=false unintentionally.
pitfalls.consequence: CI will no longer validate formatting by default, causing style drift and CI failures elsewhere that assume fmt is validated on every build.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve fmt step wiring to default_step or update dependent CI workflows accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming cargo is optional and removing the cargo invocation for html5ever.
pitfalls.consequence: The build will fail on any environment without a prebuilt html5ever artifact; html parsing integration will be broken.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep cargo build invocation and document cargo as a build requirement; if you change the mechanism, provide an alternative artifact path.

key_sections: build: top-level build graph and options (defines modules, artifacts, default steps)
linkV8: how the V8 dependency is wired and which options are passed (prebuilt_v8_path, cache)
linkHtml5Ever: cargo invocation and object file inclusion for Rust html5ever
linkCurl and buildCurl: curl's configuration, include paths, and how transitive C libraries are compiled and linked
skip_unless_needed: the large curl config map and long file lists until you are editing network behaviors or compiling issues
low-level C source lists for brotli/nghttp2/zlib if you are not changing these libraries

tests.example_command:
zig build            # builds default step (includes fmt check)
zig build test       # runs unit tests via test_runner.zig
zig build snapshot_creator -- <args>  # run the snapshot creator artifact

related_files.relationship: Provides the version and minimum_zig_version values read during build startup.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the versioning behavior or minimum Zig version requirement is edited, update build.zig.zon accordingly.

related_files.relationship: The main executable's root module referenced by the build script.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to main executable's imports or expected build options (e.g., snapshot_path) may require build target updates.

related_files.relationship: Root source file for the snapshot creator executable built here.
related_files.reason_to_check: If snapshot creator CLI or snapshot handling options change, ensure build target remains compatible and snapshot_path propagation is correct.

related_files.relationship: Cargo manifest used by linkHtml5Ever to build the html5ever Rust crate.
related_files.reason_to_check: Editing cargo profiles, output names, or crate names will affect the produced static archive consumed by build.zig.

related_files.relationship: Cache root used by the v8 dependency helper (linkV8).
related_files.reason_to_check: If v8 dependency caching or path semantics change, verify .lp-cache usage and location.

insights.title: Percent-encode version in crash report query string
insights.problem: Version string could contain plus signs or other characters that break URL/query semantics when sent to crash server.
insights.root_cause: build.fmt version string inserted directly into query parameter without encoding.
insights.solution: Create version_encoded with '+' replaced by '%2B' and expose it to the build opts; use version_encoded when constructing crash report URL (commit 9661204).
insights.lesson_learned: Always percent-encode query parameter values when composing HTTP URLs; treat build metadata as untrusted string data.

insights.title: Add a compilation-only 'check' build step
insights.problem: CI and local workflows needed a lightweight verification that the codebase compiles without producing full executables.
insights.root_cause: Previously build produced full artifacts for verification; adding a 'check' step allows faster shallow verification.
insights.solution: Added a 'check' step that builds libraries named lightpanda_check, lightpanda_exe_check, snapshot_creator_check and legacy_test_check to verify compilation. check step depends on those library steps.
insights.lesson_learned: Providing a fast, compilation-only CI step helps catch build regressions quickly without expensive artifact creation.

insights.title: Simplify nightly versioning behavior
insights.problem: Nightly version flags logic was complex and used separate pre_version and version_string flags.
insights.root_cause: Previous versioning flow used several flags and special-cased 'nightly' vs tags.
insights.solution: Simplified to single -Dversion flag. Updated resolveVersion to accept -Dversion and to enrich pre-release builds with commit count and short hash when appropriate; preserves stable release behavior.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize and simplify version override semantics; when enriching dev/nightly versions include commit count/hash to make builds traceable.

insights.title: Add -Dpre_version build option and use for nightly builds
insights.problem: Nightly builds previously hard-coded pre version handling via -Dversion_string; more control was needed to override pre version.
insights.root_cause: CI workflow needed a way to set pre version via environment lacking a clean zig option mapping.
insights.solution: Added b.option 'pre_version' to build.zig to allow overriding the pre version string; resolveVersion now uses provided pre_version when computing semantic version pre component.
insights.lesson_learned: Expose build-time options for CI-driven version strings; ensure resolveVersion handles optional pre/build metadata consistently.

src/browser/webapi/MutationObserver.zig.short_description: DOM MutationObserver implementation and MutationRecord wrapper
src/browser/webapi/MutationObserver.zig.description: Implements a MutationObserver API for the browser DOM, tracking observed targets and queuing MutationRecord objects for delivery to a JS callback. Contains logic to observe attribute, characterData and childList changes, accumulate records per observer and deliver them into V8 while managing arenas and lifetimes.

key_constructs.name: MutationObserver
key_constructs.purpose: Core observer object providing observe/disconnect/takeRecords and notification entrypoints.

key_constructs.name: MutationRecord
key_constructs.purpose: Represents a single mutation entry with accessors exposed to JS and arena-managed lifetime.

key_constructs.name: notifyAttributeChange
key_constructs.purpose: Creates attribute-change MutationRecord entries when an element attribute changes.

key_constructs.name: notifyCharacterDataChange
key_constructs.purpose: Creates character-data MutationRecord entries on text node changes.

key_constructs.name: notifyChildListChange
key_constructs.purpose: Creates childList MutationRecord entries when nodes are added or removed.

key_constructs.name: deliverRecords
key_constructs.purpose: Delivers queued records to the JS callback using a V8 local scope and try/catch.

key_behaviors: observes DOM mutations on nodes
queues mutation records for delivery to JS callbacks
delivers records into V8 and handles exceptions
manages arena allocation and record lifetime

insights.title: Do not release pending MutationObserver records via releaseRef
insights.problem: Pending MutationObserver records (not handed to V8 yet) were released via releaseRef(page._session) which expects an acquired reference path, potentially causing incorrect reference counting / double-release.
insights.root_cause: Pending records are local and haven't been transferred to JS/V8; releasing them via the ref-count path used for JS-owned objects was inappropriate.
insights.solution: Call record.deinit(page._session) to free pending records directly and clear the pending list.
insights.lesson_learned: Different lifetimes exist for pending/JS-owned objects — use appropriate deinit/free paths and avoid applying JS ref-counting semantics to non-JS-owned resources.

insights.title: Explicitly deinit pending records and acquire/release observer refs
insights.problem: Pending records and observing list management relied on RC +/- manipulation in various places and a deinitIfUnused helper.
insights.root_cause: Inconsistent ownership semantics when records never reached V8 and when registering first observation.
insights.solution: On deinit, deinit pending records immediately; on observe/unobserve, call page.registerMutationObserver/unregisterMutationObserver at the right times and use acquireRef/releaseRef pairing (commit 77b60ce).
insights.lesson_learned: When an API can be referenced by VM and native code, ensure pending native-only items are cleaned up explicitly and that registration/unregistration increments/decrements ownership consistently.

insights.title: MutationObserver updated to RC model and Session-level unregistering
insights.problem: MutationObserver used weak/global finalizers and identity-level tracking; this caused lifetime issues across contexts.
insights.root_cause: Finalizer/weak model didn't work reliably across IsolatedWorlds; observers could be referenced after Identity deinit.
insights.solution: Introduce _rc, acquire/releaseRef, change deinit to session-only, modify observe/disconnect/unobserve to adjust _rc._refs and call releaseRef when observations drop to zero, and move unregistering order to avoid use-after-free.
insights.lesson_learned: Observers that register with pages must increment a visible refcount and unregister before releasing the last reference. Session-level finalizer registry ensures cleanup on page reset.

src/browser/webapi/css/CSSStyleSheet.zig.short_description: CSSStyleSheet implementation with rule parsing and mutation
src/browser/webapi/css/CSSStyleSheet.zig.description: Provides a DOM-facing CSSStyleSheet type, including methods to read rules, insert/delete rules and to replace stylesheet text synchronously or as a Promise. It integrates a CSS Parser to turn textual rules into CSSStyleRule objects and notifies the page style manager when sheets change.

key_constructs.name: CSSStyleSheet
key_constructs.purpose: Represents a stylesheet and exposes DOM methods and properties (cssRules, href, disabled, etc.).

key_constructs.name: CSSError
key_constructs.purpose: Enumerates DOM-exposed error cases for stylesheet operations (syntax, OOM, index errors).

key_constructs.name: insertRule
key_constructs.purpose: Parses a single rule string and inserts a CSSStyleRule at a given index, handling skipped at-rules.

key_constructs.name: replaceSync
key_constructs.purpose: Parses full stylesheet text and replaces the sheet's rules synchronously, notifying the style manager.

key_constructs.name: getCssRules
key_constructs.purpose: Lazily initializes and returns a CSSRuleList for the sheet, loading owner <style> text if present.

key_behaviors: parses stylesheet text into rule objects
inserts and deletes CSS rules from a stylesheet
exposes cssRules and sheet properties to JavaScript
notifies style manager when rules change

insights.title: insertRule must not accept multiple rules in one string
insights.problem: insertRule previously allowed a string containing multiple rules (e.g., 'a{...} b{...}') which should be rejected.
insights.root_cause: Parser iteration over rules didn't ensure only one rule was present before creating CSSStyleRule.
insights.solution: After parsing the rule, check if iterator has more nodes and return SyntaxError if it does (reject multiple rules).

src/Notification.zig.short_description: Scoped notification/event bus for browser contexts
src/Notification.zig.description: Implements an event/notification registry scoped per BrowserContext used to register, unregister and dispatch strongly-typed events to listeners. It keeps per-event intrusive lists and per-receiver registrations so listeners can be removed en masse and delivers typed event payloads to registered functions safely.

key_constructs.name: Notification
key_constructs.purpose: Top-level notification registry instance providing register/unregister/dispatch API.

key_constructs.name: EventListeners
key_constructs.purpose: Struct holding intrusive linked lists for each supported event type.

key_constructs.name: register
key_constructs.purpose: Registers a listener for a specific event type tied to a receiver object.

key_constructs.name: unregisterAll
key_constructs.purpose: Removes all listener registrations associated with a receiver.

key_constructs.name: dispatch
key_constructs.purpose: Invokes all listeners for a given event type, delivering typed payloads.

key_behaviors: registers and unregisters event listeners per scope
dispatches typed notifications to listeners
allows bulk unregister for a receiver

insights.title: Add PageDOMContentLoaded and PageLoaded notifications
insights.problem: Lifecycle events (DOMContentLoaded/load) needed to be emitted at different times to align CDP event order with Chrome.
insights.root_cause: Previously DOMContentLoaded and load were emitted nested inside page_navigated; reordering was required to match expected external client timings.
insights.solution: Add new notification types PageDOMContentLoaded and PageLoaded to Notification, and extend EventListeners/Events union to include them (commit 752184b).
insights.lesson_learned: Define explicit, small-grained notifications for lifecycle milestones to allow fine-grained control over event ordering for external protocols.

src/TestWSServer.zig.short_description: Small WebSocket echo/test server used by tests
src/TestWSServer.zig.description: Lightweight WebSocket echo server that accepts connections, performs the handshake, and echoes or handles control commands (force-close, send-large, close with code). It is intended for integration tests and supports text/binary frames, close handling and generating large payloads for client tests.

key_constructs.name: TestWSServer
key_constructs.purpose: Encapsulates listener socket, shutdown flag and control methods for the test server.

key_constructs.name: run
key_constructs.purpose: Main accept loop that spawns a handler thread per client and performs socket setup.

key_constructs.name: handleClient
key_constructs.purpose: Per-connection handler that upgrades to WebSocket and processes frames/commands.

key_constructs.name: RecvBuffer
key_constructs.purpose: Buffer helper to read and unmask WebSocket frames incrementally from a socket.

key_constructs.name: sendLargeMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Sends a large text frame composed of A..Z pattern to test large payload handling.

key_behaviors: accepts WebSocket clients and performs handshake
echos text and binary frames and supports test commands
sends close frames and large messages for client testing

insights.title: Add a small WebSocket test server used by WebSocket WebAPI tests
insights.problem: Need a deterministic server to exercise WebSocket client behaviors (echo, large messages, close frames, force-close).
insights.root_cause: Lack of a local test server made testing websocket behaviors fragile.
insights.solution: Add TestWSServer with upgrade handshake and frame handling (text/binary/close/large messages) used in multiple websocket tests.
insights.lesson_learned: Testing network protocols benefits from a small, deterministic server implementation to exercise edge behaviors (payload sizes, close semantics).

src/crash_handler.zig.short_description: Central crash/panic handler and optional crash reporter
src/crash_handler.zig.description: Overrides Zig's panic handler to produce a human-readable crash dump, print stack trace and attempt to report the crash to a telemetry endpoint via curl (unless disabled or in debug). It handles re-entrancy of panics, acquires a mutex to avoid interleaved output, and tries to spawn curl in a forked child to submit the report.

key_constructs.name: panic
key_constructs.purpose: Custom global panic handler that forwards to crash with context.

key_constructs.name: crash
key_constructs.purpose: Formats and prints crash info, dumps stack trace and triggers report upload.

key_constructs.name: report
key_constructs.purpose: Attempts to find curl and fork/exec to send crash telemetry to remote endpoint.

key_constructs.name: curlPath
key_constructs.purpose: Searches PATH for curl binary and returns a path into a provided buffer if found.

key_behaviors: captures panics and prints an actionable crash report
optionally uploads crash details to a remote collector
prevents interleaved panic messages across threads

insights.title: Use percent-encoded version in crash report URL
insights.problem: Crash report URL used raw version string which could produce invalid or misinterpreted query values.
insights.root_cause: build-provided version value contained characters needing percent-encoding.
insights.solution: Use lp.build_config.version_encoded when constructing report URL (commit 9661204).
insights.lesson_learned: Sanitize/encode query parameters in any telemetry/crash submissions to avoid server parsing errors and accidental leaks.

src/browser/EventManager.zig.short_description: DOM event registration and dispatch manager with phases
src/browser/EventManager.zig.description: Manages addEventListener/removeEventListener and dispatching of DOM events across capture/target/bubble phases including shadow DOM retargeting, inline handlers, once/once removal semantics and microtask scheduling. It delegates low-level storage to EventManagerBase while implementing DOM-specific propagation rules, activation state for inputs, and optimizations (ignore lists for script-added load handlers).

key_constructs.name: EventManager
key_constructs.purpose: High-level event manager attached to a Page that orchestrates listener registration and dispatch.

key_constructs.name: register
key_constructs.purpose: Registers listeners using the base manager and handles load-listener tracking and ignore lists.

key_constructs.name: dispatch
key_constructs.purpose: Top-level entry that acquires event refs and routes to node or direct dispatch.

key_constructs.name: dispatchNode
key_constructs.purpose: Implements full DOM event propagation (capture/at-target/bubble) with shadow DOM retargeting.

key_constructs.name: dispatchPhase
key_constructs.purpose: Iterates listeners for a phase, applies capture/once/ignore semantics and invokes handlers safely.

key_behaviors: registers and removes DOM event listeners
dispatches events through capture/target/bubble phases
applies shadow DOM retargeting and inline handlers
manages listener lifetime and once/remove semantics

insights.title: Adapt EventManager to use EventManagerBase for non-DOM dispatch
insights.problem: EventManager needed to reuse the new non-DOM dispatch logic.
insights.root_cause: EventManager previously implemented both DOM and non-DOM dispatch paths inline.
insights.solution: Delegate non-DOM listener storage/dispatch to EventManagerBase; keep DOM propagation-specific logic in EventManager.
insights.lesson_learned: After extracting a base module, update callers to reference the base via composition to avoid duplicating logic.

insights.title: Switch event deinit semantics to releaseRef instead of deinit(shutdown)
insights.problem: Event lifecycle previously used deinit(shutdown) and direct deinit calls; finalizer model migrated to acquire/release/deinit and Session-level cleanup.
insights.root_cause: Global finalizer rework required consistent use of releaseRef instead of deinit to manage reference counts and allow sessions to enforce final cleanup.
insights.solution: dispatchOpts/dispatchDirect now call event.acquireRef() and defer event.releaseRef(session) instead of deinit, ensuring balanced refcounting.
insights.lesson_learned: Use acquire/release semantics in event dispatch to avoid early deallocation; prefer releaseRef for normal flow and deinit for forced session shutdown.

src/sys/libcurl.zig.short_description: Zig FFI wrapper and typed bindings for libcurl
src/sys/libcurl.zig.description: Provides Zig-friendly types, constants and error mapping for libcurl C API; includes typed enums for options/info, wrappers for global init and error conversion helpers. It validates layout at compile time and exposes callback type aliases to integrate libcurl into the project's HTTP client layer.

key_constructs.name: Curl
key_constructs.purpose: Alias for the C CURL handle type imported from curl/curl.h.

key_constructs.name: CurlGlobalFlags
key_constructs.purpose: Struct representing curl_global_init flags with helper to convert to C flags.

key_constructs.name: CurlOption
key_constructs.purpose: Enum mapping Zig symbols to CURLoption constants for callsites.

key_constructs.name: errorFromCode
key_constructs.purpose: Converts a C CURLcode into a Zig Error enum with exhaustive mapping.

key_constructs.name: curl_global_init
key_constructs.purpose: Wrapper to call curl_global_init optionally with a provided allocator set of callbacks.

key_behaviors: maps libcurl constants/types into Zig equivalents
converts C error codes into Zig errors
initializes libcurl with optional custom allocators

insights.title: Expose CURLINFO_HTTP_CONNECTCODE as connect_code
insights.problem: libcurl's connect status info was not enumerated in the project's libcurl FFI wrapper.
insights.root_cause: sys.libcurl's CurlInfo enum lacked the connect_code entry, preventing curl_easy_getinfo from being called with that info enum.
insights.solution: Added connect_code = c.CURLINFO_HTTP_CONNECTCODE to CurlInfo enum and included it in the curl_easy_getinfo switch.
insights.lesson_learned: FFI enums must include all used CURLINFO values; forgetting them leads to inability to query important connection-level information (e.g. CONNECT status).

insights.title: Expose curl_easy_reset wrapper
insights.problem: Higher-level code needed to reset an easy handle but a wrapper was missing.
insights.root_cause: New Connection.reset uses curl_easy_reset; sys wrapper didn't expose it.
insights.solution: Added curl_easy_reset() wrapper to sys/libcurl.zig and used it from Connection.reset.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding new libcurl usage patterns in higher layers, expose safe wrappers in sys bindings to centralize FFI changes.

src/browser/js/Value.zig.short_description: JS Value wrapper and helpers for V8 interop
src/browser/js/Value.zig.description: Encapsulates V8 value handles in a Zig-friendly Value API with type checks, conversions (numbers, strings, promises), structured cloning and persistence semantics (temp/global). It also exposes utilities to persist values across V8 local scopes and serialize to JSON or Zig types.

key_constructs.name: Value
key_constructs.purpose: Represents a V8 value in the current Local scope and provides many type predicates and conversions.

key_constructs.name: toString
key_constructs.purpose: Converts a V8 value to a js.String, handling Symbol descriptions and ToString semantics.

key_constructs.name: structuredClone
key_constructs.purpose: Performs structured cloning using V8 serializers and throws on host objects that cannot be cloned.

key_constructs.name: persist
key_constructs.purpose: Promotes a local value to a tracked global handle so it outlives the local scope.

key_constructs.name: Temp
key_constructs.purpose: Type for temporary global handles tracked per session for cleanup.

key_behaviors: inspect JS value types and convert them to Zig primitives
serialize or clone JS values via V8 APIs
persist values across V8 local scopes and free them correctly

insights.title: Provide ValueSerializer host-object callback to avoid V8 assertion
insights.problem: When serializing (structuredClone) host objects (DOM elements, Blob, File, etc.), V8 expects delegate callbacks to be provided to ValueSerializer. The wrapper previously supplied null/missing callbacks, causing V8 to assert or crash.
insights.root_cause: The wrapper created a ValueSerializer without providing a write_host_object callback. V8 asserts that a delegate is present or that appropriate exceptions are thrown when encountering host objects.
insights.solution: Provide a SerializerDelegate with writeHostObject that throws a DataCloneError (and a no-op throw_data_clone_error). This returns false to V8 and ensures an exception is thrown instead of hitting an assertion (commit c3c4653).

test_functions: Host objects (DOM elements) cannot be cloned - should throw, not crash
Objects containing host objects cannot be cloned - should throw, not crash

insights.lesson_learned: When wrapping runtime serializers (V8 ValueSerializer), implement required delegate behavior for host objects and ensure proper exceptions are thrown rather than returning 'nothing' or null, which can trigger runtime assertions. Add explicit tests for host-object cloning failure modes.

src/browser/webapi/Event.zig.short_description: DOM Event type implementation and JS bindings
src/browser/webapi/Event.zig.description: Defines the Event base type used throughout the DOM, supports initialization, option population, reference counting and helper methods like composedPath. It also exposes the Event API to JavaScript via a bridge, handles trusted/untrusted flags and supports specialized sub-event unions.

key_constructs.name: Event
key_constructs.purpose: Core DOM Event struct holding type, flags, timestamps, propagation state and union-typed specialized payloads.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Allocates and initializes a generic Event with options and immutable timestamp rounding.

key_constructs.name: composedPath
key_constructs.purpose: Computes the event propagation path taking shadow DOM and closed boundaries into account.

key_constructs.name: acquireRef
key_constructs.purpose: Reference-counts the event to manage cross-language ownership between Zig and V8.

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: JS bridge exposing Event methods/properties and constants to JavaScript.

key_behaviors: creates and initializes DOM events
exposes event API to JavaScript
computes composed paths and handles propagation state
manages cross-language event lifetime

insights.title: Event lifecycle now uses RC abstraction and releaseRef
insights.problem: Event.deinit previously mixed shutdown and runtime deinit semantics; finalizer refactor requires a consistent approach.
insights.root_cause: Old patterns mixed immediate deinit vs refcount decrement. With the new model, deinit is for forced session cleanup and releaseRef decrements refs and triggers deinit when needed.
insights.solution: Replaced numeric _rc with lp.RC(u8), made deinit(session) for shutdown cleanup, added releaseRef which delegates to RC.release, and changed callers to use releaseRef. dispatch flows now call acquireRef/defer releaseRef.
insights.lesson_learned: Differentiate between forced shutdown deinit (session cleanup) and normal lifetime release (releaseRef). Use RC helper to centralize refcount semantics.

insights.title: Add FormDataEvent to Event union and type checks
insights.problem: Event union didn't include FormDataEvent; is<T> checks couldn't detect it.
insights.root_cause: New FormDataEvent type not integrated into Event union.
insights.solution: Added form_data_event member to the Event union and updated is() pattern to return FormDataEvent when requested.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding event types, integrate them into central Event discriminated union to enable polymorphic behavior.

insights.title: Add submit_event to Event.Type union and exhaustive switch
insights.problem: Event.Type did not include submit_event, and exhaustive type checks/dispatches could miss SubmitEvent.
insights.root_cause: SubmitEvent was introduced later and Event.Type needed to be extended to include it.
insights.solution: Add submit_event variant mapping to event/SubmitEvent.zig and update is() switch to return correct typed pointer for SubmitEvent.

src/browser/webapi/Navigator.zig.short_description: Navigator API implementation exposing browser properties
src/browser/webapi/Navigator.zig.description: Implements the Navigator object with properties (userAgent, platform, languages, plugins) and methods like getBattery and registerProtocolHandler. It provides validation helpers for protocol handler schemes/URLs and exposes permissions/storage manager accessors to JS.

key_constructs.name: Navigator
key_constructs.purpose: Holds navigator state such as plugins, permissions and storage manager and exposes accessors.

key_constructs.name: getUserAgent
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the session/browser configured User-Agent string.

key_constructs.name: registerProtocolHandler
key_constructs.purpose: Validates and registers a protocol handler, delegating to validation helpers.

key_constructs.name: validateProtocolHandlerScheme
key_constructs.purpose: Validates allowed custom protocol schemes and web+ prefixed schemes per spec.

key_behaviors: exposes navigator properties to web pages
validates and registers protocol handlers
provides access to plugins, permissions and storage manager

insights.title: Return both regional and base language in getLanguages
insights.problem: getLanguages returned only one language ("en-US"), breaking compatibility with sites expecting the base language to be present.
insights.root_cause: Hard-coded return type and data omitted base language fallback.
insights.solution: Expanded returned array to include both "en-US" and "en" by changing the returned static array size and contents.
insights.lesson_learned: When implementing static / hard-coded WebAPI values, prefer matching common browser behaviors; small deviations in API semantics (like language negotiation) cause compatibility issues.

src/mcp.zig.description: Simple module that re-exports MCP submodules: protocol, router and Server so callers can import them from mcp. Includes a small compile-time test to reference all declarations in the module.

key_constructs.name: protocol
key_constructs.purpose: Re-export of mcp/protocol.zig for protocol definitions.

key_constructs.name: router
key_constructs.purpose: Re-export of mcp/router.zig to process MCP requests.

key_constructs.name: Server
key_constructs.purpose: Re-export of mcp/Server.zig which implements the MCP server runtime.

src/browser/Mime.zig.short_description: MIME content-type parsing, sniffing and charset utilities
src/browser/Mime.zig.description: Parses Content-Type headers into a Mime struct, extracts charset params, prescans HTML bytes for charset declarations and performs simple content sniffing to detect HTML or text via prefixes and BOMs. It exposes helpers to query content type, charset strings and text/html detection to drive parsing/decoding decisions elsewhere.

key_constructs.name: Mime
key_constructs.purpose: Struct representing parsed content-type, parameters, charset and helper buffers.

key_constructs.name: parse
key_constructs.purpose: Parses a Content-Type header string into a Mime object with charset extraction and validation.

key_constructs.name: prescanCharset
key_constructs.purpose: Scans the first 1024 bytes of HTML for <meta> charset or content-type declarations.

key_constructs.name: sniff
key_constructs.purpose: Heuristically determines if a byte slice looks like HTML or contains BOM-based encodings.

key_behaviors: parses Content-Type headers and params
extracts and normalizes charset values
sniffs document bytes to guess content type

insights.title: Mime parsing: ownership/lifetime of type/subtype slices
insights.problem: parseContentType returned slices that pointed into a temporary normalized buffer; callers stored those slices leading to use-after-free/invalid memory when the temporary buffer went out of scope.
insights.root_cause: The normalized buffer used during parsing was a transient local (stack) buffer. The ContentType.other.type/sub_type fields were slices referencing that transient memory.
insights.solution: Update Mime to own fixed-size buffers (type_buf, sub_type_buf) and copy main_type/sub_type into those buffers before returning; change parseContentType signature to accept caller-owned buffers and parse() to pass the Mime instance buffers.

insights.title: Accept []const u8 input and normalize to lowercase copy
insights.problem: The previous parse signature used mutable []u8 and relied on @constCast for trimming and lowercasing which was fragile.
insights.root_cause: Zig's trim API returns []const u8 and casting led to confusion and unsafe assumptions about input mutability.
insights.solution: Change parse to accept []const u8, allocate a stack buffer, produce a normalized lowercase slice into that buffer, and use that for further parsing. Also store persistent subtype/type into owned buffers on the Mime instance.

src/browser/markdown.zig.short_description: Converts DOM tree to markdown text representation
src/browser/markdown.zig.description: Walks a DOM subtree and emits a token-efficient markdown representation, handling block elements, lists, tables, images, links, code blocks and inline formatting with escaping. It is used to provide a compact textual snapshot of a page (e.g., for MCP resource streaming).

key_constructs.name: dump
key_constructs.purpose: Public entry that renders a Node subtree to markdown via a Context writer.

key_constructs.name: Context
key_constructs.purpose: Holds rendering state, writer and page reference; implements recursive rendering logic.

key_constructs.name: analyzeContent
key_constructs.purpose: Scans a subtree to detect visible text and block presence used to format anchors/blocks.

key_constructs.name: isStandaloneAnchor
key_constructs.purpose: Detects link nodes that should be rendered as standalone block links.

key_behaviors: renders DOM to markdown text
handles block-level and inline conversion rules
escapes markdown special characters and preserves preformatted text

insights.title: Optimize anchor rendering and content analysis
insights.problem: Anchor rendering performed multiple tree walks and had redundant checks (hasBlockDescendant, hasVisibleContent).
insights.root_cause: Separate helper functions duplicated traversal work and returned boolean-only info causing repeated traversals.
insights.solution: Introduce analyzeContent which returns both visibility and block presence in one walk; simplify anchor logic and reduce string/emission overhead (commit 71ac2e8).
insights.lesson_learned: When analyzing DOM subtrees for multiple properties, return a small struct with needed flags to avoid multiple traversals; reduce per-character writes with buffered ranges for performance.

src/mcp/Server.zig.short_description: MCP JSON-RPC server harness for processing requests
src/mcp/Server.zig.description: Constructs and manages an MCP server instance with HTTP client, Notification, Browser session and node registry; provides helpers to format/send JSON-RPC responses, results and errors. Includes integration tests that feed JSON RPC requests into the router and verify expected responses.

key_constructs.name: Server
key_constructs.purpose: Main MCP server structure holding browser, http client, notification and session context.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Initializes the server, HttpClient, Notification and creates a browser session.

key_constructs.name: sendResponse
key_constructs.purpose: Serializes and writes a JSON-RPC response to the configured writer with mutex protection.

key_constructs.name: sendError
key_constructs.purpose: Convenience to send JSON-RPC error objects following the protocol shape.

key_behaviors: initializes MCP server state and browser session
sends JSON-RPC responses, results and errors
integrates with router to process incoming requests

src/mcp/resources.zig.short_description: MCP resource registry and page resource streaming (HTML/markdown)
src/mcp/resources.zig.description: Defines available MCP resources (page HTML and markdown) and implements handlers to list resources and stream the current page's content. For reads it packages a StreamingText object that lazily serializes page DOM into HTML or markdown when the JSON serializer invokes its jsonStringify hook.

key_constructs.name: resource_list
key_constructs.purpose: Array of protocol.Resource entries describing supported resources and mime types.

key_constructs.name: handleList
key_constructs.purpose: Handles MCP list requests and returns available resources.

key_constructs.name: handleRead
key_constructs.purpose: Validates params, resolves the requested resource and returns a streaming result for current page content.

key_constructs.name: ResourceStreamingResult.StreamingText.jsonStringify
key_constructs.purpose: Custom JSON writer that serializes either HTML or markdown of the page into the JSON response body.

key_behaviors: lists available MCP resources
streams current page HTML or markdown on demand
validates resource URIs and page availability

insights.title: Centralize resource format selection and robustly handle serialization failures
insights.problem: Resource streaming handlers duplicated logic for HTML vs markdown and didn't properly report serialization failures to the client.
insights.root_cause: Ad-hoc switch logic previously sent results without catching failures during serialization of HTML/markdown.
insights.solution: Introduce Format enum and unify building of ResourceStreamingResult; ensure server.sendResult is wrapped in a catch that maps serialization failure to server.sendError(req_id, .InternalError, ...). Also ensure dumping functions return error.WriteFailed when inner dumps fail.
insights.lesson_learned: When returning streaming or serialized protocol content, always handle possible serialization errors and map them to protocol-level errors so clients aren't left with a broken/bogus response.

src/browser/URL.zig.short_description: URL resolution, parsing and percent-encoding utilities
src/browser/URL.zig.description: Implements URL resolution (relative -> absolute), component extraction and percent-encoding utilities used across the browser. Provides helpers to inspect and canonicalize URLs, build origins, and extract username/password/host/port/path/query/fragment information.

key_constructs.name: resolve
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve a source path against a base URL (handles schemes, network-path refs, ., .. normalization).

key_constructs.name: ensureEncoded
key_constructs.purpose: Ensure URL path/query/fragment segments are percent-encoded according to their context.

key_constructs.name: percentEncodeSegment
key_constructs.purpose: Percent-encode a URL segment given an encode set (path/query/userinfo/fragment).

key_constructs.name: isCompleteHTTPUrl
key_constructs.purpose: Quick check whether a string is a complete http(s)/ws(s)/blob/data URL.

key_constructs.name: getOrigin
key_constructs.purpose: Compute the origin (scheme + authority) for http/https URLs, avoiding default ports and optionally allocating.

key_constructs.name: getPathname
key_constructs.purpose: Extract the pathname component for various URL forms (scheme:path and scheme://host/... cases).

key_behaviors: resolves relative URLs to absolute forms
percent-encodes path/query/fragment segments when required
extracts URL components (protocol, host, port, pathname, search, hash, origin)

insights.title: Tighten getHost signature to accept []const u8
insights.problem: getHost accepted [:0]const u8 and some callers required stricter types after origin comparisons.
insights.root_cause: Small type inconsistencies caused by various callers and the new isSameOrigin implementation.
insights.solution: Change getHost signature to pub fn getHost(raw: []const u8) []const u8 to match usage.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep string slice types consistent (use []const u8 when possible) to avoid casts and subtle mismatches.

insights.title: Handle file scheme edge cases and change InvalidURL -> TypeError
insights.problem: URL.resolve mishandled special schemes (file) and produced InvalidURL where TypeError was preferred; various base/path combination cases were unhandled.
insights.root_cause: Edge cases around scheme detection, presence/absence of '://', and file host empty cases were not covered; tests exposed mismatches.
insights.solution: Improved scheme parsing to allow specific behaviors for file scheme, added checks for allowed scheme characters, changed some error returns to TypeError, and added more test cases for base+path combinations (commits 2d87f5b, 0a222ff).
insights.lesson_learned: URL parsing must carefully handle special schemes (file/blob) and the variety of relative/absolute inputs; comprehensive test coverage for combinations is critical.

exercised_by: src/browser/tests/url (implicit via updated tests in file)

insights.title: Correct handling of scheme in path during URL resolution
insights.problem: resolve() incorrectly treated some 'scheme:rest' path inputs as relative paths instead of recognizing a scheme (e.g., http:, https:, ws:, file: and custom schemes). This produced incorrect resolved URLs.
insights.root_cause: Original algorithm did not detect schemes embedded in the path portion and mishandled special scheme rules (file allowing empty host, differences when '//' present etc.).
insights.solution: Revamp resolve() to duplicate/normalize the input path appropriately, detect presence of ':' and validate scheme length, treat known special schemes (http/https/ws/wss/file/ftp) specially, handle missing '//' for file scheme by adding extra slashes, and otherwise treat unknown scheme as absolute. Add comprehensive tests covering many edgecases (commit 9a0cefad).

exercised_by: built-in resolve tests in URL.zig (new test case 'URL: resolve path scheme')

insights.lesson_learned: URL resolution is full of subtle edge cases (scheme detection, file scheme peculiarities). Implement exhaustive tests for corner cases (different scheme separators, missing slashes) and prefer explicit checks for known 'special' schemes.

insights.title: Authority parsing tightened to avoid @-in-path userinfo spoofing and IPv6 port handling
insights.problem: Previous parsing treated any '@' in the URL as userinfo, including '@' characters that appear in path/query/fragment. This could cause getHost/getOrigin to strip or mis-interpret hosts and ports, creating potential Same-Origin Policy bypasses. Port parsing also mishandled IPv6 bracketed addresses.
insights.root_cause: The code looked for '@' across the entire remainder of the URL instead of restricting the search to the authority portion (between scheme:// and the first /, ?, or #). Port detection used a simplistic last-colon heuristic that did not account for IPv6 bracket notation.
insights.solution: Introduced parseAuthority(raw) that finds authority_start/authority_end and only searches for '@' inside the authority substring. Added findPortSeparator(host) that is IPv6-aware (handles [addr]:port and distinguishes internal colons inside brackets). All higher-level helpers (getUserInfo, getHost, getHostname, getPort, getOrigin) now use parseAuthority/findPortSeparator. Added tests covering @ in path, IPv6 bracketed addresses, and origin semantics.
insights.lesson_learned: URL parsing must explicitly delimit the authority substring before interpreting characters like '@' and ':'; otherwise path/query characters can be misinterpreted and open security holes. IPv6 bracket notation must be handled when extracting host/port.

src/browser/js/Function.zig.short_description: V8-backed JS Function wrapper and invocation helpers
src/browser/js/Function.zig.description: Wraps V8 Function handles and provides safe invocation APIs, this-binding helpers, argument marshaling, TryCatch integration and persistent handle management. Supports creating JS instances, calling with/without rethrow, and converting functions to temporary/global persistent wrappers tracked by the runtime.

key_constructs.name: newInstance

key_constructs.name: call / callWithThis / tryCall
key_constructs.purpose: Invoke the JS function with argument marshaling, call-depth handling, TryCatch and optional rethrow.

key_constructs.name: persist / temp / _persist
key_constructs.purpose: Create persistent V8 Global handles for the function (either tracked global or temp scoped).

key_constructs.name: Temp / Global (G)
key_constructs.purpose: Types that wrap persistent V8 handles with lifecycle operations (deinit, release, local conversion).

key_behaviors: invoke JavaScript functions from Zig with proper marshaling and exception handling
instantiate JS constructors and manage persistent references to functions
bind 'this' values and convert functions to temp/global tracked handles

src/browser/js/Promise.zig.short_description: V8 Promise wrapper and persistence helpers
src/browser/js/Promise.zig.description: Provides a thin wrapper around V8 Promise handles enabling conversion to Object/Value, chaining (then/catch) and creation of persistent temp/global references tracked by the JS context. Used for bridging promise-based JS results into the Zig runtime safely.

key_constructs.name: persist / temp / _persist
key_constructs.purpose: Create persistent V8 Global wrappers for promises (temp or global) tracked by the context.

key_constructs.name: Temp / Global (G)
key_constructs.purpose: Types that manage persistent promise handles and release semantics.

persist and release promise handles safely across call boundaries

src/cdp/domains/target.zig.short_description: CDP Target domain implementation and browser context management
src/cdp/domains/target.zig.description: Implements the Chrome DevTools Protocol 'Target' domain: enumerating/creating/attaching/detaching targets and managing browser contexts and sessions. It maps CDP commands to internal BrowserContext and Page operations, sends target lifecycle events, and handles auto-attach/discover semantics expected by clients.

src/browser/webapi/net/WebSocket.zig.short_description: Browser WebSocket web‑API implementation and event dispatch
src/browser/webapi/net/WebSocket.zig.description: Implements the DOM WebSocket interface for pages: opening connections, framing send/receive, buffering, event handler registration and dispatch to page event listeners. Integrates with the internal HTTP/connection layer to perform handshake, manage send queue, and deliver Message/Error/Close events into the page event system.

key_constructs.name: dispatchMessageEvent / dispatchOpenEvent / dispatchCloseEvent / dispatchErrorEvent

key_constructs.name: ReadyState / BinaryType

insights.title: Add WebSocket WebAPI with binaryType 'blob' support and improved logging
insights.problem: No WebSocket API existed; initial implementation also reused HTTP connection pool causing potential starvation.
insights.root_cause: Lack of libcurl websocket glue and WebSocket WebAPI implementation.
insights.solution: Implement WebSocket API using libcurl's websockets, allow binaryType 'arraybuffer' and 'blob' (creating Blob objects and acquiring refs), improve debug logging and message lifecycle.
insights.lesson_learned: Implementing higher-level web APIs on top of libcurl websockets requires careful lifetime management (blobs), logging for debugging, and separate connection pools to avoid starvation of HTTP requests.

insights.title: Give WebSockets their own connection pool and limit concurrent websockets
insights.problem: Using the HTTP connection pool for websockets allowed a page to exhaust connections and starve HTTP requests; websocket operations need dedicated resources.
insights.root_cause: Single shared connection pool for both HTTP and WebSocket transports; libcurl's connection lifetime semantics differ for websockets.
insights.solution: Add ws_pool, ws_count, ws_max and newConnection()/releaseConnection() logic in Network.zig to manage websocket-specific pool; introduce --ws-max-concurrent config and default/override in testing.
insights.lesson_learned: Long-lived transports (websockets) should use separate pools to avoid resource starvation of short-lived transports (HTTP). Make limits configurable and test with higher default in test harness.

src/Sighandler.zig.short_description: Asynchronous OS signal handling and graceful shutdown callbacks
src/Sighandler.zig.description: Installs a dedicated thread to wait for SIGINT/SIGTERM (and SIGQUIT) and runs registered listener callbacks for graceful shutdown. Provides API to register typed callbacks with stored context and handles a limited number of termination attempts before forcing exit.

key_constructs.purpose: Thread entry that waits for signals and invokes registered listeners, exiting after repeated attempts.

key_constructs.name: Listener
key_constructs.purpose: Type-erased structure holding a callback pointer and its argument bytes.

key_behaviors: capture OS termination signals
invoke registered cleanup callbacks to gracefully shutdown components

src/TestHTTPServer.zig.short_description: Lightweight local HTTP test server fixture

src/id.zig.description: Generates RFC4122 v4 UUIDs (hex string with dashes) using std.crypto.random.bytes and exposes a uuidv4 function. Includes a unit test that validates format and uniqueness across multiple invocations.

src/slab.zig.short_description: Slab allocator implementation and slab allocator wrapper
src/slab.zig.description: Implements a growable slab allocator that allocates fixed-size slots in exponentially sized chunks and an Allocator wrapper (SlabAllocator) that selects slabs by size/alignment. key_constructs.name: getStats

src/string.zig.description: Provides a compact String type with small-string-optimization (SSO) up to 12 bytes and heap-backed storage for longer values, plus interning helpers for common literals. Includes utilities for creation, concatenation, equality checks, and tests exercising the API.

src/cdp/AXNode.zig.description: Serializes DOM nodes into the Accessibility (AX) representation expected by CDP consumers. The Writer produces JSON structures describing roles, names, properties and optional children, supporting list markers, computed strings and many AX properties for form controls and common elements.

insights.title: AX tree generation uses Element.isDisabled instead of ad-hoc attribute checks
insights.problem: AXNode writer previously checked only hasAttributeSafe("disabled") and missed fieldset-disabled semantics.
insights.root_cause: Accessibility export used a simpler check, which could disagree with form/interactive logic.
insights.solution: Use el.isDisabled() to accurately reflect disabled semantics consistently in accessibility tree output.

src/network/websocket.zig.short_description: Low-level WebSocket protocol handling and server connection logic
src/network/websocket.zig.description: Implements websocket frame parsing (Reader), masking/unmasking, fragmentation handling and a WsConnection wrapper to upgrade, read, send and process websocket frames. Used by the network layer to handle incoming WebSocket connections, process ping/pong/close semantics, and send JSON messages over framed websockets.

key_constructs.name: Reader

key_constructs.name: WsConnection

key_constructs.name: Message

key_constructs.name: processMessages
key_constructs.purpose: Loop that consumes Reader messages, responds to ping/close, and delegates message handling to provided handler.

insights.title: Don't assert socket is non-blocking during tests
insights.problem: In test environments a socket created by socketpair may not have non-blocking flags set, and asserting this causes tests to fail.
insights.root_cause: A runtime assert assumed non-blocking sockets unconditionally; test harness uses socketpairs and may not set NONBLOCK for both ends.
insights.solution: Make the non-blocking assert conditional on builtin.is_test == false so tests are not tripped by the production-only assertion.
insights.lesson_learned: Runtime invariants that are assumed in production should be gated or made configurable in tests to avoid false negatives; tests often require relaxed checks or should explicitly set flags.

src/sys/libcrypto.zig.short_description: C FFI bindings and helpers for libcrypto/OpenSSL primitives
src/sys/libcrypto.zig.description: Declares opaque C types and extern functions used from libcrypto (EVP, RAND_bytes, HMAC, X25519, EVP_PKEY APIs) and provides a convenience findDigest function mapping digest names. Acts as a centralized place for crypto FFI types and small utilities used by network and security code.

key_constructs.name: RAND_bytes

key_constructs.name: EVP_sha256 / EVP_sha384 / EVP_sha512 / EVP_sha1

key_constructs.name: findDigest

key_constructs.name: X25519_keypair / EVP_PKEY_*

insights.title: Move low-level crypto bindings to src/sys/libcrypto.zig and add helpers
insights.problem: Bindings and helpers were in src/crypto.zig; introducing sys/ namespace is clearer for low-level platform bindings.
insights.root_cause: Organizational clarity and the need to expose utility functions like findDigest for higher-level crypto modules.
insights.solution: Rename file to src/sys/libcrypto.zig, add findDigest helper, constants EVP_MAX_MD_SIZE and EVP_MAX_MD_BLOCK_SIZE and expose EVP_Digest and related extern declarations.

src/telemetry/telemetry.zig.short_description: Telemetry initialization and lightweight event recording
src/telemetry/telemetry.zig.description: Manages telemetry enablement, IID (installation id) creation and persistence, and provides a Telemetry wrapper that initializes a pluggable provider to send events. key_constructs.name: Event

src/browser/js/Caller.zig.short_description: Bridge for V8 callbacks: invoke Zig functions from JS and handle results
src/browser/js/Caller.zig.description: Implements the caller-side glue used when V8 calls into Zig: it enters handle scopes, extracts arguments, invokes typed Zig callbacks/constructors, converts return values, and translates errors into JS exceptions. It centralizes property/getter/setter/indexed callback handling, error logging, and call-arena lifetime management to safely interoperate with V8.

key_constructs.purpose: Set up and tear down a Caller context bound to a v8::Isolate/Context for an incoming V8 call.

key_constructs.name: handleError

key_behaviors: dispatches V8 callbacks into typed Zig functions
converts JS arguments to Zig values and Zig returns to JS values
maps Zig errors to JS exceptions and logs call metadata
manages per-call arena lifetimes to avoid memory blowup

insights.title: Accessor/function metadata: introduce deletable flag for accessors and functions
insights.problem: No metadata to control property deletability when defining accessors/functions via the binding layer.
insights.root_cause: Property attributes were only partially modelled in the binding metadata (missing deletable control).
insights.solution: Add deletable: bool to Caller.Function struct and wire it through Bridge/attachClass so certain properties get DontDelete set.
insights.lesson_learned: Make the binding metadata expressive enough to represent JS property attributes to avoid inconsistencies between spec semantics and C++/V8 template definitions.

src/browser/js/Identity.zig.short_description: Session-level identity map between Zig instances and V8 objects
src/browser/js/Identity.zig.description: Maintains the identity mapping that ensures a single Zig instance maps to the same v8::Object wrapper within an identity scope. It stores v8::Global handles keyed by Zig instance pointers and provides cleanup logic to reset globals on deinit.

key_constructs.name: identity_map

key_constructs.name: deinit

key_behaviors: preserves one-to-one mapping between Zig objects and JS wrappers

src/browser/js/Origin.zig.short_description: Represents and manages V8 security token for a web origin
src/browser/js/Origin.zig.description: Creates and holds a shared security token (v8::Global) and an arena for contexts that belong to the same origin, enabling cross-context access in V8. Provides init/deinit lifecycle to allocate the token and release resources when the origin is no longer used.

key_constructs.name: security_token

key_behaviors: creates origin-scoped security token for V8 contexts

src/browser/js/PromiseResolver.zig.description: Provides a Zig-native abstraction over v8::Promise::Resolver to create, resolve, reject, and persist JS promises created from Zig. It offers helpers to reject with different error types (including DOM exceptions) and to persist the resolver in a v8::Global for later resolution.

src/browser/webapi/Node.zig.short_description: Core DOM Node implementation and tree-manipulation utilities
src/browser/webapi/Node.zig.description: Implements the Node abstraction and numerous DOM operations: traversal, insertion, removal, textContent management, namespace lookup, connectivity checks, and validation rules. insights.title: URL-related error type changes
insights.problem: URL resolution and Node.cloneNode used inconsistent error types for URL-related failures.
insights.root_cause: URL resolution patches changed some error codes (TypeError vs InvalidURL) and test expectations required harmonization.
insights.solution: Node.zig had InvalidURL removed/added in different commits to align with URL.zig changes (commits 2d87f5b, 0a222ff).
insights.lesson_learned: When changing error semantics in a core utility (URL resolution), reconcile any dependent APIs that propagate those errors to ensure consistent error types.

src/browser/webapi/Permissions.zig.short_description: Minimal Permissions API implementation returning safe defaults
src/browser/webapi/Permissions.zig.description: Implements a Permissions facade and a PermissionStatus object; queries always return the safe default 'prompt'. key_constructs.name: JsApi (Permissions.JsApi)

src/browser/webapi/Selection.zig.short_description: Selection API implementation for managing document ranges
src/browser/webapi/Selection.zig.description: Provides the Selection type and operations per the Selection API: getting anchor/focus, collapsing/extending, adding/removing ranges, modifying by character/word granularity, and dispatching selectionchange events. It coordinates with Range/AbstractRange implementations and updates page event state and ref-counting.

key_constructs.name: Selection (struct)
key_constructs.purpose: Stores selection state including reference-counted range and direction and exposes public selection operations.

key_constructs.name: addRange / removeRange / removeAllRanges
