insights.title: Avoid double free by replacing deinit with releaseRef
insights.problem: Selection.deinit called deinit on underlying AbstractRange which could lead to double-free when combined with new refcounting semantics.
insights.root_cause: Finalizer/refcount changes replaced deinit semantics; calling deinit directly could double-free because ownership is reference-counted.
insights.solution: Replaced call to r.asAbstractRange().deinit(session) with r.asAbstractRange().releaseRef(session) to follow new release pattern.
insights.lesson_learned: When migrating to reference-counted finalization, update all callers to use acquire/release APIs instead of direct deinit to avoid double free and lifecycle mismatches.

src/browser/js/Local.zig.short_description: Local execution context helpers for Zig->V8 interactions and conversions
src/browser/js/Local.zig.description: Encapsulates a v8::Local<Context> and helpers used for script compilation/execution and for converting values between Zig and JS representations. Responsibilities include creating JS values/templates, mapping Zig instances to persistent JS wrappers (identity), running micro/macrotasks, and serializing Zig data into JS structures.

key_constructs.name: mapZigInstanceToJs
key_constructs.purpose: Create or reuse a JS object wrapper for a Zig struct/pointer, store tagged opaque internal data and manage identity globals.

key_constructs.name: zigValueToJs
key_constructs.purpose: Convert arbitrary Zig values (primitives, structs, arrays, slices, json) into JS v8::Value handles.

key_constructs.name: compileAndRun / compileFunction
key_constructs.purpose: Compile source or function bodies into V8 scripts/functions and run them in the local context handling exceptions.

key_constructs.name: newCallback / newObject / newString
key_constructs.purpose: Convenience helpers to create JS objects, strings and function callbacks bound to Zig callbacks.

key_behaviors: compile and run JS code inside a local V8 context
convert Zig types into JS values and vice versa
create and persist JS wrappers for Zig instances (identity)
manage V8 micro/macrotask execution from Zig

insights.title: Map TAOs into identity_arena so they survive context destruction
insights.problem: Local mapping created TAOs in context arena, risking dangling references after context destruction.
insights.root_cause: V8 objects stored in identity_map can be accessed post-context via microtasks etc.; TAOs must have session-level lifetime.
insights.solution: Create TAOs in ctx.identity_arena instead of context_arena to extend TAO lifetime to session/identity level.
insights.lesson_learned: Match allocation lifetime to how long the object can be reachable from other systems (V8).

insights.title: Finalizer wrap updated to explicit RC & identity counting
insights.problem: Mapping Zig instances to V8 objects used finalizers but allowed mismatched acquire/release patterns, risking UAF or hidden leaks.
insights.root_cause: The previous wrapper relied on finalizer_callback.acquireRef semantics and V8 weak finalizers to manage lifetime implicitly.
insights.solution: Adjusted Resolved/Wrap to expose acquire_ref/release_ref and added releaseRefFromZig to explicitly release from Zig path. The V8 weak callback now decrements identity_count and only releases the underlying instance when last identity finalizes (commit 77b60ce).
insights.lesson_learned: When embedding a GCed VM, clearly separate VM finalization (weak callbacks) from engine-owned reference counting; track per-identity counts to manage objects referenced from multiple isolates/worlds.

insights.title: Session-level finalizer and temp/global tracking
insights.problem: Finalizers and tracked v8 Global objects were stored per Identity which could be freed while other IsolatedWorlds still held references, causing use-after-free.
insights.root_cause: A Zig instance can map to multiple v8 objects across IsolatedWorlds; storing temps/globals/finalizers on Identity (which can be released earlier) led to lifetime mismatches.
insights.solution: Moved globals and temps tracking to Session, track FinalizerCallback per resolved finalizer ptr id in Session.finalizer_callbacks, create Identity entries referencing the shared FinalizerCallback.Identity, set v8 weak finalizers with identity_finalizer pointer. Added createFinalizerCallback helper to allocate Session.FinalizerCallback in session arena.
insights.lesson_learned: When dealing with objects potentially referenced across multiple JS isolated contexts, centralize lifetime tracking at a scope that outlives any single identity (Session). Use reference counting atop finalizer callbacks to allow safe cleanup on page reset.

src/browser/Browser.zig.short_description: Top-level Browser instance managing environment and session lifecycle
src/browser/Browser.zig.description: Represents a browser instance with a single session: initializes the V8 environment, creates/tears down sessions, exposes task-running helpers (micro/macrotasks, idle), and holds shared resources like the HTTP client and arena pool. It coordinates creating sessions and triggers memory pressure notifications on close.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Initialize the js.Env and construct a Browser struct with provided HTTP client and app resources.

key_constructs.name: newSession / closeSession
key_constructs.purpose: Create a fresh Session and cleanly tear down the existing session, freeing resources and signalling memory pressure.

key_constructs.name: runMicrotasks / runMacrotasks / hasBackgroundTasks
key_constructs.purpose: Utilities to execute pending JS micro/macrotasks and query background task presence on the embedded environment.

key_behaviors: manage browser session lifecycle
run V8 micro and macrotasks
provide shared services (http client, arena pool) to sessions

src/browser/EventManagerBase.zig.short_description: Core event listener registration and direct dispatch engine
src/browser/EventManagerBase.zig.description: Provides generalized listener storage, registration/removal semantics (with options like once/capture/passive), and a direct dispatch path for non-DOM targets. It handles deferred removals during dispatch, equality checks for function/object listeners, and invokes listener callbacks or handler strings safely within a Local JS scope.

key_constructs.name: EventManagerBase (struct)
key_constructs.purpose: Holds listener pools, lookup maps keyed by (target,type), dispatch depth and deferred removals.

key_constructs.name: register / remove / removeListener
key_constructs.purpose: Register listeners with options, guard duplicates, and support safe removal including during active dispatches.

key_constructs.name: dispatchDirect
key_constructs.purpose: Dispatch a non-propagating event to a target: call property handler then iterate listeners, respecting once/passive and signals.

key_constructs.name: Listener / Function
key_constructs.purpose: Listener record and union representing stored callback references (global function, string, object) used during dispatch.

key_behaviors: register and remove event listeners with options
dispatch events directly to listeners and handlers
defer and apply removals safely during nested dispatches

insights.title: Extract non-DOM event dispatch into EventManagerBase
insights.problem: EventManager mixed DOM-specific propagation logic with basic listener management, making it hard to reuse for Workers/other non-DOM targets.
insights.root_cause: Tight coupling between DOM traversal/propagation logic and low-level listener registration/dispatch.
insights.solution: Introduce EventManagerBase handling listener registration/removal and direct (non-propagating) dispatch logic; EventManager composes this base. Provided register/remove/dispatchDirect/hasDirectListeners APIs.
insights.lesson_learned: Extract the smallest reusable primitives (listener registration, direct dispatch) to enable reuse across different runtime contexts (workers, non-DOM objects).

src/browser/Factory.zig.short_description: Factory utilities to allocate DOM objects with correct prototype chains
src/browser/Factory.zig.description: Provides a Factory that allocates DOM-related objects (EventTarget, Node, Element, Document, Blob, Range, etc.) while wiring up prototype chains and initializing root fields. It also manages a slab allocator for efficient small allocations and offers destroy logic to free chained prototype memory correctly.

key_constructs.name: PrototypeChain
key_constructs.purpose: A compile-time helper type used to allocate contiguous memory for multiple chained types and access/set their fields to build prototype chains.

key_constructs.name: node / element / document / blob / abstractRange
key_constructs.purpose: Factory methods that allocate and initialize common DOM objects with correct prototype roots and defaults.

key_constructs.name: destroy / destroyChain
key_constructs.purpose: Free allocated memory for prototype chains in the reverse order and support slab allocator destruction.

key_behaviors: allocate DOM objects with proper prototype linkage
initialize event objects and ranges with default values
destroy prototype-chained allocations safely

insights.title: Initialize reference counters (RC) in factory-created objects
insights.problem: Objects created via Factory had numeric _rc fields initialized as plain integers; after RC abstraction introduction factories must initialize RC properly.
insights.root_cause: Global migration to lp.RC type requires creating objects with ._rc = .{} to start reference counting correctly.
insights.solution: Factory create paths now initialize ._rc = .{} for types like Event, Blob, AbstractRange, etc.
insights.lesson_learned: When introducing a new RC abstraction, ensure all allocation sites initialize it; missing init leads to invalid refcount state and subtle lifecycle bugs.

src/browser/links.zig.short_description: Collects anchor hrefs under a DOM root for link extraction
src/browser/links.zig.description: Utility that queries for a[href] under a root node and returns an arena-allocated slice of resolved href strings. It uses the selector API and handles errors by logging and skipping malformed hrefs.

key_constructs.name: collectLinks
key_constructs.purpose: Query anchor elements with href under root and return their resolved href strings allocated from provided arena.

key_behaviors: collect href attributes from anchor tags beneath a root node
allocate and return a list of link strings for crawling or analysis

insights.title: Extract link-collection utility
insights.problem: Link extraction logic was duplicated in MCP tool handlers (and in-stream writers).
insights.root_cause: DOM traversal and href resolution were implemented inline in multiple places (mcp/tools.zig), causing duplication and inconsistent error handling.
insights.solution: Add browser/links.zig with collectLinks(arena, root, page) returning a slice of href strings allocated in the provided arena and centralize error logging for href resolution.
insights.lesson_learned: Move DOM traversal utilities into browser modules so both MCP and CDP paths reuse consistent logic and error handling.

src/cdp/Node.zig.short_description: CDP node registry, search management, and serialization to DevTools JSON
src/cdp/Node.zig.description: Implements the mapping between internal DOM nodes and CDP node IDs, search lifecycle (perform/get/discard), and a JSON writer that serializes nodes (optionally with children) into the format expected by the Chrome DevTools Protocol. It provides registration, lookup, reset semantics and unit tests covering registry, searches, and serialization.

key_constructs.name: Registry
key_constructs.purpose: Maintain bidirectional mapping between DOM nodes and CDP Ids, allocate Node wrappers and manage pools/arenas.

key_constructs.name: Search.List
key_constructs.purpose: Track active searches, allocate search names, convert DOM node lists into node id arrays, and support removal.

key_constructs.name: Writer
key_constructs.purpose: Serialize a CDP Node (and children up to configured depth) into JSON with fields expected by clients.

key_constructs.name: register
key_constructs.purpose: Assign a new incremental id to a DOM node and populate both lookup maps atomically.

key_behaviors: register DOM nodes with persistent CDP ids
create and manage search result sets for DOM queries
serialize nodes and children into CDP JSON for remote clients

src/cdp/id.zig.short_description: Utilities to format/parse prefixed CDP identifiers and generate incrementing IDs
src/cdp/id.zig.description: Provides helpers to convert between page ids and prefixed strings (frame/loader/request/intercept ids), parse those IDs back, and a generic Incrementing generator type that emits prefixed incrementing identifiers. Includes unit tests validating parsing, formatting, and counter wrap behavior.

key_constructs.name: toFrameId / toLoaderId / toRequestId / toInterceptId
key_constructs.purpose: Format numeric page identifiers into fixed-width prefixed ID strings used by CDP (e.g., FID-0000000001).

key_constructs.name: toPageId
key_constructs.purpose: Parse a prefixed ID back into the numeric page id, returning typed errors on invalid inputs.

key_constructs.name: Incrementing
key_constructs.purpose: Generic zero-allocation incrementing ID generator producing prefix-N string slices and parsing them back.

key_behaviors: format numeric ids into fixed prefixed strings for CDP
parse prefixed ids back into numeric ids
generate incrementing id strings with no heap allocations

src/network/WebBotAuth.zig.short_description: Sign HTTP requests using a Web-Bot Auth Ed25519 signing scheme
src/network/WebBotAuth.zig.description: Implements reading an Ed25519 PKCS#8 PEM, extracting the raw private key, signing a canonical HTTP message signature string, base64-encoding it, and adding Signature-Agent, Signature-Input and Signature headers to an Http.Headers instance. It uses the project's crypto wrapper around OpenSSL and includes unit tests for parsing, signing determinism, and header emission.

key_constructs.name: parsePemPrivateKey
key_constructs.purpose: Extract the 32-byte Ed25519 private key from a PKCS#8 PEM blob and create an EVP_PKEY object.

key_constructs.name: signEd25519
key_constructs.purpose: Sign an input message with the given EVP_PKEY producing a 64-byte Ed25519 signature using OpenSSL EVP API.

key_constructs.name: signRequest
key_constructs.purpose: Canonicalize signature inputs, sign them, base64 encode, and add the three required HTTP headers to the request.

key_behaviors: load and parse Ed25519 private keys from PEM
create digital signatures of canonical strings
attach HTTP message-signature headers to outgoing requests

insights.title: Use sys/libcrypto import in WebBotAuth
insights.problem: WebBotAuth imported an older crypto abstraction path.
insights.root_cause: After moving bindings to src/sys/libcrypto.zig, imports needed to be updated.
insights.solution: Import ../sys/libcrypto.zig instead of ../crypto.zig.

src/browser/js/Snapshot.zig.short_description: Create, load and manage V8 startup snapshots and external references
src/browser/js/Snapshot.zig.description: Handles creation and loading of V8 startup snapshots including embedding support, external reference collection and serialization. It builds function templates for JS bindings, records data indices for later extraction, and exposes utilities to write and free snapshot data.

key_constructs.name: load
key_constructs.purpose: Load an embedded snapshot if present or create a new one

key_constructs.name: create
key_constructs.purpose: Create a V8 SnapshotCreator, generate templates, and produce a startup blob

key_constructs.name: collectExternalReferences
key_constructs.purpose: Build array of function pointer addresses required by V8 for callbacks

key_constructs.name: countExternalReferences
key_constructs.purpose: Compute compile-time size of external references array

key_constructs.name: generateConstructor
key_constructs.purpose: Generate a v8::FunctionTemplate for a JsApi type and set class name/internal fields

key_behaviors: loads embedded V8 snapshot data at startup
creates V8 snapshots with templates and external references
writes snapshot binary to an io writer
manages allocation ownership and freeing of startup data

insights.title: Set v8::Signature on FunctionTemplates to validate receivers and avoid crashes
insights.problem: Extracting an accessor getter or method and calling it with an incorrect receiver (wrong `this`) could lead to V8 internal field OOB access and crashes.
insights.root_cause: Function templates and accessors were created without v8::Signature validation, allowing execution with arbitrary receivers which can cause the C++ binding to access internal fields that don't exist on the target object.
insights.solution: When building templates, create a v8::Signature tied to the FunctionTemplate (signature = v8__Signature__New(isolate, template)) and pass that signature to FunctionTemplate creation for non-static accessors/functions. Additionally, mark getters without setters as ReadOnly and support DontDelete attribute for certain accessors. Adjusted FunctionTemplate constness usage.
insights.lesson_learned: When binding native-backed JS objects to V8, use v8::Signature to enforce receiver types and avoid subtle crashes caused by calling methods with the wrong `this`. Also ensure property attributes (ReadOnly/DontDelete) reflect JS semantics to avoid accidental mutation.

src/browser/webapi/CustomElementRegistry.short_description: Manage registration, upgrade and whenDefined lifecycle for custom elements
src/browser/webapi/CustomElementRegistry.description: Implements the CustomElementRegistry Web API: defining custom element constructors, storing definitions, upgrading existing undefined custom elements in the DOM, and returning whenDefined promises. It validates names, reads observedAttributes, and wires created constructors into the page's registry.

key_constructs.name: define
key_constructs.purpose: Register a custom element name with its constructor and upgrade existing elements

key_constructs.name: upgradeCustomElement
key_constructs.purpose: Invoke constructor, attach definition and call lifecycle callbacks for an element

key_constructs.name: whenDefined
key_constructs.purpose: Return a promise that resolves when a custom element name is defined

key_constructs.name: validateName
key_constructs.purpose: Validate custom element local name per spec rules

key_behaviors: registers custom element constructors by name
upgrades matching undefined custom elements in the document
resolves whenDefined promises once a name is registered

src/browser/webapi/EventTarget.zig.short_description: Generic EventTarget implementation for DOM objects and event dispatch
src/browser/webapi/EventTarget.zig.description: Provides the EventTarget core API used by many DOM objects: registering/removing listeners and dispatching events through the page EventManager. It wraps different concrete target types (Node, Window, XHR, etc.) and exposes JS bindings.

key_constructs.name: addEventListener
key_constructs.purpose: Register an event listener (function or object) on the target

key_constructs.name: removeEventListener
key_constructs.purpose: Remove a previously registered event listener

key_constructs.name: dispatchEvent
key_constructs.purpose: Dispatch an Event through the EventManager and return whether it was cancelled

key_constructs.name: Type
key_constructs.purpose: Discriminates concrete EventTarget backing types (node, window, xhr, etc.)

key_behaviors: registers and removes event listeners
dispatches events and returns defaultPrevented result
maps EventTarget to concrete DOM subtypes for formatting and toString

src/browser/webapi/HTMLDocument.zig.short_description: HTMLDocument API: accessors and helpers for head/body/title, cookies and location
src/browser/webapi/HTMLDocument.zig.description: Implements HTMLDocument-specific accessors and behaviors (head, body, title, images, scripts, links, cookie, location, doctype). It provides methods that read/modify the DOM (e.g., setTitle), interact with the page cookie jar, and produce various live collections.

key_constructs.name: getTitle
key_constructs.purpose: Return normalized document title text by walking the tree

key_constructs.name: setTitle
key_constructs.purpose: Create or replace the <title> element in the head

key_constructs.name: getCookie
key_constructs.purpose: Read cookies for the document from the page's cookie jar

key_constructs.name: setCookie
key_constructs.purpose: Parse and add a cookie to the cookie jar, honoring HttpOnly rules

key_constructs.name: getHead/getBody
key_constructs.purpose: Return document head/body elements by scanning document element children

key_behaviors: provides HTMLDocument accessors used by scripts
manages document title and doctype
reads and sets cookies via the page cookie jar
schedules navigation when setting location

src/browser/webapi/Range.zig.short_description: DOM Range implementation supporting selection and content manipulation
src/browser/webapi/Range.zig.description: Implements the Range Web API including boundary setters, comparisons, insertion, deletion, cloning of contents and other range utilities. It enforces node-type validations, live-range updates, and implements spec-aligned behaviors for collapsed and multi-container ranges.

key_constructs.name: setStart/setEnd
key_constructs.purpose: Set the start or end boundary of the range with validation and collapsing behavior

key_constructs.name: compareBoundaryPoints
key_constructs.purpose: Compare two range boundary points and return ordering

key_constructs.name: insertNode
key_constructs.purpose: Insert a node at the range start, handling text-node splitting and live updates

key_constructs.name: deleteContents
key_constructs.purpose: Remove the contents of the range, handling same-container and multi-container cases

key_constructs.name: cloneContents
key_constructs.purpose: Produce a DocumentFragment containing a deep clone of the range contents

key_behaviors: sets and compares range boundary points
inserts, deletes, and clones nodes according to range positions
maintains live-range semantics across DOM mutations

src/browser/webapi/StorageManager.zig.short_description: StorageManager Web API exposing storage quota estimate
src/browser/webapi/StorageManager.zig.description: Provides a minimal StorageManager API with an estimate method that returns a StorageEstimate object describing usage and quota. This file registers the types and implements the JS bridge accessors for usage and quota.

key_constructs.name: estimate
key_constructs.purpose: Return a Promise resolved with a StorageEstimate (usage/quota)

key_constructs.name: StorageEstimate
key_constructs.purpose: Holds _usage and _quota with accessors exposed to JS

key_constructs.name: registerTypes
key_constructs.purpose: Register StorageManager and StorageEstimate types for factory creation

key_behaviors: returns a storage quota/usage estimate promise
exposes quota and usage accessors to JavaScript

src/browser/webapi/SubtleCrypto.zig.short_description: Partial SubtleCrypto Web Crypto API: key gen, sign, verify, derive, digest
src/browser/webapi/SubtleCrypto.zig.description: Implements several SubtleCrypto operations (generateKey, exportKey, deriveBits, sign, verify, digest) with HMAC and X25519 support and bindings to platform crypto (libcrypto). It returns Promises to JS contexts and maps algorithm parameters to internal implementations.

key_constructs.name: generateKey
key_constructs.purpose: Generate symmetric keys or public-key pairs (HMAC, X25519) and return Promise

key_constructs.name: exportKey
key_constructs.purpose: Export a CryptoKey in supported formats (raw) or reject unsupported ones

key_constructs.name: digest
key_constructs.purpose: Compute a message digest using libcrypto EVP_Digest and return an ArrayBuffer promise

key_constructs.name: deriveBits
key_constructs.purpose: Derive bits for ECDH/X25519 style algorithms

key_behaviors: generates cryptographic keys and key pairs
exports raw key material
derives shared bits for key agreement
computes cryptographic digests

insights.title: Refactor SubtleCrypto and split algorithms into separate files
insights.problem: Original SubtleCrypto implementation had grown messy and mixed algorithm implementations causing maintainability issues.
insights.root_cause: Single monolithic implementation handled multiple algorithms and helper logic in a tangled manner.
insights.solution: Split algorithm-specific logic into dedicated files (HMAC.zig, X25519.zig, algorithm.zig), introduce CryptoKey abstraction, prefer libcrypto APIs for digest operations.

src/browser/webapi/URL.zig.short_description: JS URL wrapper: parse/manipulate URL components and blob URL management
src/browser/webapi/URL.zig.description: Provides the URL Web API implementation wrapping internal URL utilities and exposing getters/setters for components (href, protocol, hostname, search, hash, etc.). It also manages URLSearchParams caching and implements createObjectURL/revokeObjectURL for Blob objects.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Construct a URL object resolving relative URLs against a base and page URL

key_constructs.name: getSearchParams
key_constructs.purpose: Lazily parse or create a URLSearchParams object for the URL

key_constructs.name: setHref
key_constructs.purpose: Update the URL string and synchronize searchParams if present

key_constructs.name: createObjectURL/revokeObjectURL
key_constructs.purpose: Create and revoke blob: URLs and track them per page

key_behaviors: parses and resolves URLs against a base
reads and sets individual URL components
creates and revokes blob: object URLs
synchronizes URL string with URLSearchParams object

src/cdp/domains/browser.zig.short_description: CDP Browser domain handlers: version and window info for DevTools clients
src/cdp/domains/browser.zig.description: Implements the Chrome DevTools Protocol 'Browser' domain methods such as getVersion and getWindowForTarget, returning hard-coded version/product/user-agent data to appear Chrome-compatible. Several methods are implemented as no-ops to satisfy clients.

key_constructs.name: processMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Dispatch incoming CDP Browser domain commands to handlers

key_constructs.name: getVersion
key_constructs.purpose: Return protocol/product/revision/userAgent/jsVersion info to the client

key_constructs.name: getWindowForTarget
key_constructs.purpose: Return a window id and bounds for DevTools

key_constructs.name: CDP_USER_AGENT
key_constructs.purpose: User-agent string used to identify the CDP server as a Chrome-like client

key_behaviors: responds to Browser.getVersion with client-compatible metadata
provides window information for DevTools tooling
no-ops various browser control commands to satisfy clients

src/cdp/domains/dom.zig.short_description: CDP DOM domain implementation: expose and query DOM nodes over DevTools protocol
src/cdp/domains/dom.zig.description: Implements many CDP DOM domain methods (getDocument, performSearch, querySelector(s), resolveNode, describeNode, getContentQuads, getBoxModel, requestChildNodes) integrating the engine's node registry and selector machinery. It dispatches DOM.setChildNodes events for clients, resolves JS remote objects for nodes, and maps geometry info for elements.

key_constructs.name: processMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Dispatch CDP DOM domain actions to their handlers

key_constructs.name: getDocument
key_constructs.purpose: Register and return the root document node to the CDP client

key_constructs.name: performSearch
key_constructs.purpose: Run selector queries, register search results and emit necessary child-node events

key_constructs.name: dispatchSetChildNodes
key_constructs.purpose: Emit DOM.setChildNodes events top-down for subtrees that cover results

key_constructs.name: getContentQuads/getBoxModel
key_constructs.purpose: Return geometry/quads/box model data based on bounding client rect

key_behaviors: exposes DOM structure and node ids to CDP clients
performs selector searches and returns search results
sends DOM.setChildNodes events for partial subtrees
provides element geometry/quads and box model information

insights.title: Domain functions now accept *CDP.Command instead of anytype
insights.problem: Domain handlers used anytype for Command, relying on generics; after removing generics handlers now accept concrete *CDP.Command.
insights.root_cause: Large refactor to remove CDP generics required uniform function signatures across domains.
insights.solution: Change processMessage and all domain handler functions to accept *CDP.Command and update getNode helper signatures to accept *CDP.BrowserContext (commit 2665312 changes).
insights.lesson_learned: Central API type changes (like removing generics) ripple through domain code; keep domain handlers small and adapt their signatures consistently to minimize future churn.

src/cdp/domains/emulation.zig.short_description: CDP Emulation domain handlers (mostly no-op) for client compatibility
src/cdp/domains/emulation.zig.description: Accepts DevTools Emulation domain commands such as setEmulatedMedia, setDeviceMetricsOverride and setUserAgentOverride and responds; most are implemented as no-ops or logged to maintain compatibility with CDP clients. setUserAgentOverride logs and ignores the override.

key_constructs.name: processMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Route Emulation domain messages to specific handlers

key_constructs.name: setUserAgentOverride
key_constructs.purpose: Log and ignore user-agent override requests

key_behaviors: accepts and acknowledges emulation-related CDP commands
logs ignored user-agent overrides

src/cdp/domains/fetch.zig.short_description: CDP Fetch domain: enable request interception and control intercepted requests
src/cdp/domains/fetch.zig.description: Implements the Fetch domain for request interception: enabling/disabling interception, pausing network transfers, and allowing clients to continue/fulfill/fail or provide auth credentials for paused requests. It tracks intercepted transfers via InterceptState and translates CDP actions into HttpClient transfer operations.

key_constructs.name: InterceptState
key_constructs.purpose: Track paused HTTP transfers keyed by request id and provide operations on them

key_constructs.name: requestIntercept
key_constructs.purpose: Be notified of an intercept, store transfer and send Fetch.requestPaused event

key_constructs.name: continueRequest
key_constructs.purpose: Apply client-provided modifications and resume a paused transfer

key_constructs.name: fulfillRequest
key_constructs.purpose: Fulfill a paused request with a custom response body and headers

key_constructs.name: continueWithAuth
key_constructs.purpose: Resume a paused request using provided credentials for auth challenges

key_behaviors: pauses HTTP requests and notifies CDP clients
lets clients continue, modify, fulfill, or fail intercepted requests
handles auth challenges by resuming with credentials

insights.title: Use http.Header and http.Method types
insights.problem: fetch domain used net_http types; rename moved these to http.
insights.root_cause: Consistent renaming needed across CDP domains.
insights.solution: Updated type annotations to http.Header and http.Method (commit 0604056).
insights.lesson_learned: Third-party protocol glue must be updated with core networking renames to maintain type coherence.

insights.title: Domain handler signatures updated to concrete types
insights.problem: fetch domain used anytype for command and different imports order.
insights.root_cause: Generic removal in CDP required signature changes and reordering of imports to avoid circularities.
insights.solution: Change processMessage(cmd: *CDP.Command), change requestIntercept and requestAuthRequired to accept *CDP.BrowserContext, and re-introduced network import and Allocator definitions in-file. Also adjust params parsing accordingly (commit 2665312).
insights.lesson_learned: When centralizing types, ensure domain-specific code imports the concrete CDP definitions and uses precise pointer types for browser contexts to prevent ambiguous ownership.

src/cdp/domains/input.zig.short_description: CDP Input domain: simulate keyboard and mouse events via CDP
src/cdp/domains/input.zig.description: Implements Input domain methods dispatchKeyEvent, dispatchMouseEvent and insertText to synthesize user input for the page. It converts CDP parameters into internal KeyboardEvent/mouse actions and invokes corresponding Page methods to trigger behavior in the DOM.

key_constructs.name: dispatchKeyEvent
key_constructs.purpose: Translate CDP key event into a trusted KeyboardEvent and dispatch it to the page

key_constructs.name: dispatchMouseEvent
key_constructs.purpose: Handle mouse event commands; primary implementation triggers mouse clicks

key_constructs.name: insertText
key_constructs.purpose: Insert text into the page as user input

key_behaviors: synthesizes keyboard events and forwards them to the page
triggers mouse click events
inserts text programmatically via CDP

src/cdp/domains/security.zig.short_description: CDP Security domain: toggle certificate verification and basic security calls
src/cdp/domains/security.zig.description: Provides minimal Security domain support for the CDP server: enable/disable no-op methods and setIgnoreCertificateErrors which toggles the HTTP client's TLS verification. The module ensures DevTools clients can instruct the engine to ignore cert errors when requested.

key_constructs.name: processMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Route Security domain requests to appropriate handlers

key_constructs.name: setIgnoreCertificateErrors

enables/disables TLS certificate validation per request

src/browser/webapi/crypto/X25519.zig.short_description: X25519 key generation and shared-secret derivation for WebCrypto
src/browser/webapi/crypto/X25519.zig.description: Provides X25519 support used by SubtleCrypto: generate an X25519 key pair (public/private), wrap them as CryptoKey objects and derive shared bits using OpenSSL/BoringSSL EVP APIs. It enforces usage constraints and returns results as JS promises/ArrayBuffers.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Generate X25519 keypair, construct CryptoKey objects and resolve a Promise with the pair

key_constructs.name: deriveBits
key_constructs.purpose: Perform ECDH-like derive operation using EVP_PKEY APIs and return an ArrayBuffer slice

key_behaviors: generate X25519 key pairs for WebCrypto

wrap native crypto keys into engine CryptoKey objects

insights.title: X25519 keypair/generate and deriveBits using libcrypto EVP APIs
insights.problem: X25519 behavior was embedded and not cleanly separated; key derivation and format handling needed clearer implementation.
insights.root_cause: Monolithic code made it harder to reason about key lifetimes and padding/bit handling for derived bits.
insights.solution: Separate X25519 logic into its own module creating EVP_PKEY raw public/private keys, producing CryptoKey pairs, and implement deriveBits with EVP_PKEY_derive and proper bit-length handling and zeroing of unused bits.

src/browser/webapi/event/FormDataEvent.z.short_description: Implementation of the FormDataEvent web API wrapper
src/browser/webapi/event/FormDataEvent.z.description: Defines the FormDataEvent type used to represent formdata events in the DOM, with constructors for normal and trusted events and JS bindings. It integrates with the page arena/factory and exposes accessors for the event's FormData payload to the JS bridge.

key_constructs.name: FormDataEvent
key_constructs.purpose: Type representing a formdata DOM event and its stored formData pointer

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Create an untrusted FormDataEvent by string type and options

key_constructs.name: initTrusted
key_constructs.purpose: Create a trusted FormDataEvent with an already-built type string

key_constructs.name: getFormData
key_constructs.purpose: Accessor returning the FormData associated with the event

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: V8/JS bridge metadata (constructor and accessor) for the JS API

key_behaviors: constructs formdata DOM events
exposes formData payload to JavaScript
integrates events with page arena and event dispatch

insights.title: Add FormDataEvent type and JS bridge
insights.problem: The 'formdata' event wasn't represented as a first-class event type and couldn't be dispatched to listeners with a FormData payload.
insights.root_cause: FormDataEvent was not implemented in the webapi event hierarchy nor exported through the bridge.
insights.solution: Added FormDataEvent type with trusted and untrusted init functions, accessor for formData, registered in Event union and bridge, and added JsApi metadata and finalizer.
insights.lesson_learned: Event types need explicit integration into the Event union and js bridge; remember to implement both construction and accessor plumbing.

src/browser/webapi/event/MessageEvent.zi.short_description: MessageEvent wrapper and JS bindings for postMessage events
src/browser/webapi/event/MessageEvent.zi.description: Implements the MessageEvent type used for message/postMessage semantics, holding data, origin and source references, lifecycle management and JS bridge accessors. Includes support for multiple data representations and test coverage for basic MessageEvent behavior.

key_constructs.name: MessageEvent
key_constructs.purpose: Type representing a message event with data/origin/source

key_constructs.name: Data
key_constructs.purpose: Union of possible message payload representations (js.Value, string, arraybuffer, blob)

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Create a new MessageEvent (untrusted) with options

key_constructs.name: deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Tear down resources held by the MessageEvent (release js values/blobs)

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: JS bridge metadata exposing constructor and accessors

key_behaviors: represents postMessage events in the DOM
manages payload lifetimes and conversions
exposes event properties to JavaScript

insights.title: MessageEvent.data becomes a discriminated union to support multiple binary types including Blob
insights.problem: MessageEvent previously stored data as a single js.Value.Temp (or string/arraybuffer) but WebSocket binaryType 'blob' support required storing a Blob pointer and proper release semantics.
insights.root_cause: Binary blob handling needed explicit refcounting for Blob; previous darted representation didn't express blob vs arraybuffer vs js.Value.
insights.solution: Introduce Data union { value, string, arraybuffer, blob } and adjust deinit to release blob via blob.releaseRef(session).
insights.lesson_learned: When adding new data kinds (Blob), expand event data types to a discriminated union and ensure deinit handles each variant's lifecycle correctly.

src/browser/webapi/event/SubmitEvent.zig.description: Defines SubmitEvent used for form submission events, including creation (trusted/untrusted), access to the submitter element, and JS bindings. It hooks into the page factory and event population like other DOM events.

key_constructs.name: SubmitEvent

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Create an untrusted SubmitEvent with options

key_constructs.name: getSubmitter

key_constructs.purpose: JS bridge exposing constructor and submitter accessor

provides access to the submitter element in JS

insights.title: Implement SubmitEvent to carry submitter pointer
insights.problem: SubmitEvent semantics (submitter property) were missing; events created for form submit didn't set submitter.
insights.root_cause: No SubmitEvent class existed; submitter information was being discarded.
insights.solution: Add new SubmitEvent type with init/initTrusted/getSubmitter, include SubmitEvent in Event.Type union and JS bridge, and use it in form submit logic.

src/browser/webapi/net/FormData.zig.short_description: FormData implementation and utilities for form serialization
src/browser/webapi/net/FormData.zig.description: Implements the FormData API: collecting form control values, storing key/value pairs, iteration and serialization (url-encoded), and dispatching formdata events. It exposes mutation and accessor methods to JS and supports iterators and forEach callbacks.

key_constructs.name: FormData
key_constructs.purpose: Representation of a form data key/value list with arena allocator

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Create FormData from an optional Form and submitter, dispatching a formdata event

key_constructs.name: collectForm
key_constructs.purpose: Traverse form controls to collect names and values according to HTML form rules

key_constructs.name: get/set/append/delete

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: JS bridge bindings including iterators and forEach

dispatches formdata DOM event

insights.title: Dispatch formdata event when FormData constructed with a form
insights.problem: Constructing new FormData(form) did not dispatch a 'formdata' event on the form element as specified.
insights.root_cause: FormData.init returned a FormData object but omitted the required event dispatch step.
insights.solution: After creating the FormData object, if a form is provided, create a trusted FormDataEvent with .formData set and dispatch it via page._event_manager.dispatch to the form's event target.
insights.lesson_learned: When implementing DOM APIs with side-effecting specifications (like events), ensure side effects (event dispatch with correct properties and trusted flag) are present at construction time.

insights.title: Use Element.isDisabled when collecting form data
insights.problem: FormData collection duplicated disabled checks (attribute + fieldset ancestor rules).
insights.root_cause: Prior duplicated logic risked inconsistencies with other parts of code.
insights.solution: Replace manual checks with element.isDisabled() to centralize behavior.

src/browser/webapi/net/Request.zig.short_description: Fetch Request object and JS-exposed body helpers

insights.title: Standardize http.Method type import
insights.problem: Request code referenced net_http.Method; inconsistent with new naming.
insights.root_cause: Module rename required updates in many call-sites.
insights.solution: Switch to http.Method and method parsing maps (commit 0604056).
insights.lesson_learned: Large renames require systematic search-and-replace across the codebase; keep method enums in a single module.

src/browser/webapi/net/Response.zig.short_description: Fetch Response object implementation and stream helpers
src/browser/webapi/net/Response.zig.description: Implements the Response object for fetch-like APIs: status, headers, body storage and conversion helpers (text/json/arrayBuffer/blob/bytes), cloning, and readable stream creation. It manages reference counting, optional underlying HttpClient.Response, and JS bindings.

insights.title: Switch internal response reference from Transfer to HttpClient.Response
insights.problem: Response webapi object kept pointer to Transfer; after refactor, the webapi Response needed to track HttpClient.Response to abort/terminate underlying network requests correctly.
insights.root_cause: Change in HTTP client abstraction from Transfer to Response.
insights.solution: Replace _transfer with _http_response, update deinit/clone to abort via resp.abort and clear the pointer, and adjust lifecycle usages.
insights.lesson_learned: When moving to an abstraction, ensure lifecycle operations (abort/terminate) delegate to the abstracted object.

It sets up a sink that calls back into Zig via provided callbacks and exposes debug memory reporting in debug builds.

insights.title: Remove dead-code-style returns and avoid error-swallowing unwraps
insights.problem: polyglot-scanner warnings flagged explicit returns that make code look like dead code and use of unwrap() that could panic or swallow errors.
insights.root_cause: Rust code used return statements in places where expression returns are idiomatic, and used unwrap() on operations whose failure should be handled more gracefully.
insights.solution: Removed unnecessary explicit return keywords/trailing semicolons, replaced epoch::advance().unwrap() with drop(epoch::advance()), replaced stats::...::read().unwrap() with unwrap_or(0), and used drop(Box::from_raw(...)) to explicitly drop instead of assigning to _.
insights.lesson_learned: Prefer explicit, safe error handling or use unwrap_or for optional stats; don't rely on unwrap() in FFI boundaries. Use drop() to make intent explicit when freeing raw pointers.

src/html5ever/sink.rs.short_description: html5ever TreeSink implementation that forwards parser events to Zig
src/html5ever/sink.rs.description: Implements the TreeSink trait for html5ever to convert parser events into callbacks invoked on the Zig side: element creation, text appends, comments, processing instructions, doctypes and reparenting. It stores minimal element metadata in an arena and marshals node/text data into C-compatible structs for the callbacks.

key_constructs.name: ElementData
key_constructs.purpose: Per-element stored metadata (QualName and integration-point flag) allocated in a typed arena

key_constructs.name: Sink
key_constructs.purpose: TreeSink implementation storing callback function pointers and arena reference for DOM construction

key_constructs.name: impl TreeSink for Sink
key_constructs.purpose: Methods like create_element, append, create_comment, append_doctype_to_document and reparent_children that forward events to Zig callbacks

key_behaviors: translate html5ever parse events into Zig callbacks

insights.title: Fix dead-code style returns in sink implementation
insights.problem: Explicit returns in constructors and callbacks triggered static analysis warnings.
insights.root_cause: Rust code used explicit 'return' where more idiomatic expression forms were preferred.
insights.solution: Removed extraneous 'return' keywords and trailing semicolons to align with idiomatic Rust and eliminate dead-code warnings.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep idiomatic Rust style in FFI crate to reduce scanner warnings and avoid misleading control-flow constructs.

src/html5ever/types.rs.short_description: C-compatible types and callback signatures for html5ever-Zig bridge
src/html5ever/types.rs.description: Defines FFI-friendly types, callback function signatures, and small C-compatible wrappers (CQualName, CAttribute, CNodeOrText, CNullable) used by the Rust parser code to interact with Zig. These types standardize how parser events and attribute iterators are passed across the language boundary.

insights.title: Simplify constructor return style

insights.root_cause: Stylistic issues in FFI types crate.
insights.solution: Return constructed structs with expression form instead of explicit 'return' statements.
insights.lesson_learned: Consistent, idiomatic code in language bindings reduces static-analysis noise and potential reviewer confusion.

insights.title: Cache Canvas 2D context and lock context type per spec
insights.problem: getContext("2d") returned a fresh CanvasRenderingContext2D on each call and allowed multiple incompatible context types, violating spec. Code relying on object identity (ctx === canvas.getContext("2d")) broke and memory wasted by duplicates.
insights.root_cause: Implementation always created a new context instance and didn't record which type was requested first.
insights.solution: Added fields _context_type and _ctx_2d; getContext now returns cached 2D context when present, records first requested context type and returns null if a different type is requested later. WebGL branch also respects the context_type lock.
insights.lesson_learned: Follow the HTML spec for context identity: cache created contexts and lock the canvas to the first requested context type. This prevents identity-dependent JavaScript bugs and reduces allocations.

insights.title: Pass parent canvas to created 2D context
insights.problem: Canvas.getContext created a CanvasRenderingContext2D without a reference to the Canvas element.
insights.root_cause: Factory call used default struct literal without filling _canvas field.
insights.solution: Change getContext(self, ...) signature to use 'self' and pass .{ ._canvas = self } when creating the context.
insights.lesson_learned: Factory calls that create objects bound to parents must set parent pointers to implement bidirectional APIs correctly.

insights.title: Replace numeric _rc with lp.RC and convert deinit to releaseRef pattern
insights.problem: AbstractRange used integer ref counts and deinit(shutdown) semantics incompatible with new global finalizer design.
insights.root_cause: Finalizer rework required a uniform RC abstraction and separate releaseRef/deinit signatures.
insights.solution: Replaced _rc: u8 with _rc: lp.RC(u8) initialized to .{}, implemented acquireRef(), releaseRef(), and changed deinit to Session-only deinit (no shutdown param).
insights.lesson_learned: Introduce a typed RC abstraction to standardize acquire/release behavior across many web API objects. Update all callers to use releaseRef instead of calling deinit directly.

key_constructs.purpose: Internal routine that concatenates parts and optionally normalizes line endings

provides ArrayBuffer/Uint8Array/text/stream access to blob data
slices and duplicates blob content with MIME handling

insights.title: Add RC to Blob and expose acquire/releaseRef
insights.problem: Blob previously used the old finalizer model; needed to conform to new RC/deinit API.
insights.root_cause: Global finalizer change mandates types participating in finalization provide acquire/releaseRef and deinit at correct scope.
insights.solution: Added _rc: lp.RC(u32) member, initialized in factory, added acquireRef/releaseRef methods and adjusted deinit signature to (self, session) void.
insights.lesson_learned: Ensure data-bearing types (Blob) are refcounted and expose release/deinit so v8 Finalizer and Session reset routines work correctly.

src/browser/webapi/CryptoKey.zig.short_description: Representation of Web Crypto CryptoKey and usages
src/browser/webapi/CryptoKey.zig.description: Defines an internal CryptoKey type used by SubtleCrypto operations, storing key bytes, extractability, usage flags, and algorithm-specific vary data (like EVP_PKEY or digest pointer). Provides convenience predicates for allowed usages and exposes the JS bridge metadata.

key_constructs.name: Type
key_constructs.purpose: Enum of algorithm types (e.g. hmac, rsa, x25519)

key_constructs.name: Usages
key_constructs.purpose: Bit flags representing allowed key usages

key_constructs.name: canSign
key_constructs.purpose: Check whether the key allows signing

key_constructs.name: getDigest
key_constructs.purpose: Return EVP_MD pointer for HMAC keys

key_behaviors: represents cryptographic keys and metadata
reports allowed usages and extractability
exposes algorithm-specific handles (digest/pkey)

insights.title: Introduce CryptoKey abstraction
insights.problem: Crypto keys for different algorithms were handled inconsistently and stored as raw bytes or ad-hoc structures.
insights.root_cause: Lack of a unified type representing keys led to duplicate logic and fragile API surface.
insights.solution: New CryptoKey type encapsulates algorithm type, extractable flag, usages bitmask, raw key bytes and variant union for algorithm-specific state (digest pointer or EVP_PKEY).

src/browser/webapi/DOMException.zig.short_description: DOMException wrapper mapping errors to legacy codes and messages
src/browser/webapi/DOMException.zig.description: Implements DOMException construction, mapping from internal errors to legacy numeric codes and standard names/messages, and supports creating exceptions with custom names/messages. Provides string formatting for display and JS bindings including legacy code constants.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Create a DOMException with optional message and name

key_constructs.name: fromError
key_constructs.purpose: Map internal Zig errors to DOMException codes

key_constructs.name: getName
key_constructs.purpose: Get the canonical name string for the exception

key_constructs.name: getMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Return human-readable message for the code

key_constructs.name: Code
key_constructs.purpose: Enum mapping legacy numeric codes and lookup by name

key_behaviors: maps internal errors to DOMException types and legacy codes
provides message and name properties for exceptions
formats exception strings for display

insights.title: Map InvalidAccessError to DOMException properly
insights.problem: Some WebCrypto tests caused a crash because InvalidAccessError was not mapped to DOMException codes.
insights.root_cause: DOMException.fromError missed mapping for error.InvalidAccessError; throwing code path returned null leading to unexpected behavior downstream.
insights.solution: Added mapping error.InvalidAccessError => invalid_access_error in fromError.
insights.lesson_learned: Ensure error-to-DOMException mappings cover all errors used by Web APIs (especially crypto). Missing mappings lead to crashes in conformance tests.

src/browser/webapi/DocumentFragment.zig.short_description: DocumentFragment implementation and DOM subtree utilities
src/browser/webapi/DocumentFragment.zig.description: Implements DocumentFragment behavior: element lookup by id, querySelector/all, child accessors, innerHTML parsing/serializing, append/prepend/replaceChildren, cloning and equality comparisons. Integrates with Page parsing and selector subsystems and exposes JS bridge methods.

key_constructs.name: cloneFragment

src/browser/webapi/FileReader.zig.short_description: FileReader implementation with event dispatch and read methods
src/browser/webapi/FileReader.zig.description: Implements FileReader semantics: supports readAsArrayBuffer, readAsText, readAsBinaryString and readAsDataURL, progress/load/abort events, and synchronous reads from in-memory Blob slices. Manages event handlers, readyState/result/error properties, and dispatches ProgressEvent sequences according to the File API.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Construct a FileReader associated with a Page

key_constructs.name: encodeDataURL

insights.title: FileReader adopt RC pattern and expose acquire/releaseRef
insights.problem: FileReader used old deinit/shutdown pattern; needed to be updated to new RC semantics.
insights.root_cause: Finalizer rework requires explicit acquire/release and deinit(session) signatures.
insights.solution: Added _rc: lp.RC(u8), converted deinit signature to deinit(self, session) and added releaseRef/acquireRef wrappers. Adjusted JsApi metadata accordingly.
insights.lesson_learned: Update lifecycle-managed types to implement RC and provide consistent signatures so Session-level cleanup can operate reliably.

src/browser/webapi/ImageData.zig.short_description: ImageData constructor and RGBA pixel storage binding
src/browser/webapi/ImageData.zig.description: Implements ImageData construction with width/height validation, colorSpace and pixelFormat checks, and allocates a clamped uint8 typed array for pixel storage. Exposes width/height/data accessors and JS constructor metadata.

key_constructs.name: ConstructorSettings

src/browser/webapi/ResizeObserver.zig.short_description: Lightweight JS binding for ResizeObserver (stubbed implementation)
src/browser/webapi/ResizeObserver.zig.description: Provides a minimal ResizeObserver type and JS binding for the web API. It defines constructor and no-op observe/unobserve/disconnect functions and exposes them through the js.Bridge for the embedded JS runtime.

insights.title: Expose unobserve in JS API
insights.problem: ResizeObserver.unobserve was not available to JS code though it existed on the native type.
insights.root_cause: JsApi bridge table omitted the unobserve function, preventing scripts from calling it.
insights.solution: Added pub const unobserve = bridge.function(ResizeObserver.unobserve, .{}); to JsApi.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep JS bridge tables in sync with native API surface to avoid surprising missing methods in scripts.

src/browser/webapi/ShadowRoot.zig.short_description: ShadowRoot implementation and JS bridge with element lookup and stylesheet handling
src/browser/webapi/ShadowRoot.zig.description: Implements ShadowRoot backing structure, lifecycle creation and lookup utilities used by the DOM. src/browser/webapi/VisualViewport.zig.short_description: VisualViewport bindings exposing static viewport properties and page scroll offsets
src/browser/webapi/VisualViewport.zig.description: Provides a VisualViewport object with properties and accessors suitable for a headless environment. It maps page scroll coordinates to pageLeft/pageTop accessors and exposes fixed width/height/scale values to JS via js.Bridge.

src/browser/webapi/collections/NodeList..short_description: NodeList collection implementation with multiple backing strategies and JS iteration APIs
src/browser/webapi/collections/NodeList..description: Implements a flexible NodeList wrapper that can be backed by child node lists, selector results, radio node lists or named live lists. It provides length/indexed access, item retrieval, iterators (keys/values/entries), forEach callback invocation and a js.Bridge mapping to expose the collection to JS.

key_constructs.name: NodeList

key_constructs.name: getAtIndex
key_constructs.purpose: Indexed accessor that returns the node at a given index for the underlying implementation.

key_constructs.name: forEach
key_constructs.purpose: Invokes a JS callback for every node in the list, propagating JS exceptions and logging.

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: js.Bridge describing properties/methods (length, item, iterators) exposed to JS.

key_behaviors: exposes NodeList API for JS (length, item, indexed access)
allows JS iteration (values/entries/keys) and forEach callbacks

insights.title: Iterator now takes a reference to NodeList for forEach/values
insights.problem: forEach created an iterator but didn't clearly pair acquire/release on the list causing potential lifetime issues.
insights.root_cause: Iterators implicitly relied on their parent list remaining alive without explicit ref ownership.
insights.solution: Acquire a reference when creating iterator and defer releaseRef(page._session) in forEach; ensure deinit destroys child types in correct order (commit 77b60ce).
insights.lesson_learned: Iterators that reference a container should acquire a reference to extend container lifetime; pair acquire/release deterministically.

src/browser/webapi/collections/iterator..short_description: Generic iterator wrapper generator producing JS-friendly iterator objects
src/browser/webapi/collections/iterator..description: Provides a compile-time Entry generator that wraps arbitrary inner iterators into JS-consumable iterator objects with next() semantics. It inspects the inner iterator's return types to expose appropriate value shapes and handles reference counting and lifecycle integration with the page factory.

key_constructs.name: Entry
key_constructs.purpose: Comptime function producing a concrete iterator wrapper type for a given inner iterator.

key_constructs.name: reflect
key_constructs.purpose: Analyzes the inner iterator's next() return type to detect error unions and value payload.

key_constructs.name: ValueType
key_constructs.purpose: Resolves the appropriate value type for iterator entries and optional field selection.

key_behaviors: generates JS-compatible iterator objects with next()
adapts Zig iterator error/optional returns to JS semantics

insights.title: Iterator wrappers updated to explicit RC fields
insights.problem: Iterator entry wrapper previously accessed inner types without consistent RC semantics.
insights.root_cause: Inner types sometimes had acquire/release; wrapper didn't consistently acquire on init or release on deinit.
insights.solution: Introduce _rc field for iterator wrapper and manage acquireRef/releaseRef appropriately; rename inner to _inner and call inner.acquireRef on init when available (commit 77b60ce).
insights.lesson_learned: When wrapping another type that may be reference-counted, the wrapper must mirror ownership operations to avoid premature deinit or leaks.

src/browser/webapi/crypto/HMAC.zig.short_description: WebCrypto-style HMAC key handling, sign and verify using libcrypto
src/browser/webapi/crypto/HMAC.zig.description: Interprets CryptoKey parameters for HMAC, generates key material, and implements sign/verify operations returning JS Promises. It integrates OpenSSL-compatible primitives (digest lookup, RAND_bytes, HMAC, CRYPTO_memcmp) and enforces WebCrypto-like usage and access checks.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Generates a random HMAC key according to algorithm parameters and returns a persisted CryptoKey Promise.

key_constructs.name: sign
key_constructs.purpose: Performs HMAC signing of data with the provided CryptoKey and resolves a Promise with ArrayBuffer.

key_constructs.name: verify
key_constructs.purpose: Verifies an HMAC signature in constant time and resolves a Promise with a boolean result.

key_behaviors: creates HMAC CryptoKey material
signs data producing ArrayBuffer results
verifies HMAC signatures and returns boolean

insights.title: HMAC implementation uses libcrypto EVP HMAC primitives and CryptoKey
insights.problem: Previous HMAC handling was tangled inside SubtleCrypto and used less direct primitives.
insights.root_cause: Algorithm-specific behavior mixed into SubtleCrypto made correctness and future additions harder.
insights.solution: Create HMAC module that finds digest via crypto.findDigest, allocates key material, populates CryptoKey with digest reference, and uses crypto.HMAC and CRYPTO_memcmp for constant-time verification.

src/browser/webapi/storage/Cookie.zig.short_description: Cookie parsing, normalization, storage and retrieval (cookie jar)
src/browser/webapi/storage/Cookie.zig.description: Parses Set-Cookie strings, enforces domain/path/samesite/secure rules, normalizes expiration, and maintains an in-memory cookie Jar with add/remove/lookup semantics for requests. It includes percent-encoding utilities, validation routines, expiration handling and implements cookie matching logic for sending cookies with HTTP requests.

insights.title: Reject Set-Cookie domains that are public suffixes (PSL enforcement)
insights.problem: parseDomain accepted multi-level public suffixes (e.g., .co.uk), allowing cookies to be set for public suffixes and polluting cookie jars across unrelated sites.
insights.root_cause: parseDomain checked only for bare TLDs but did not consult the Public Suffix List for multi-level public suffixes.
insights.solution: Consult public_suffix_list() and reject domains that are public suffixes; add tests to cover PSL cases and ensure subdomains of public suffixes remain accepted.
insights.lesson_learned: Follow RFC 6265bis recommendations and reuse existing PSL data to prevent security/privacy issues when implementing cookie domain acceptance.

insights.title: Better handling of cookies with empty domains and rejecting empty-host URLs
insights.problem: Empty host or domain handling was loose; apply rules for default-domain and empty domain fields to avoid creating cookies with empty domains that match everything.
insights.root_cause: parseDomain returned encoded_host or default-domain without checking for empty host; appliesTo didn't handle empty domain correctly.
insights.solution: Require non-empty host to be returned as domain; appliesTo returns false for cookies with empty domain; add tests covering empty host parse errors and appliesTo behavior.
insights.lesson_learned: Explicitly handle empty strings for important attributes (domain/host) and write tests for introduced edge cases (empty host) to prevent cookies that erroneously match.

test_functions: Cookie: parse rejects URL with empty host

src/browser/webapi/element/html/IFrame.z.short_description: HTMLIFrameElement implementation: src/name and content accessors
src/browser/webapi/element/html/IFrame.z.description: Implements the HTMLIFrameElement API including src/name properties, resolving URLs against the page base, and accessors for contentWindow/contentDocument. It also hooks into page lifecycle to trigger frame creation callbacks when src changes and populates initial attributes on build.

key_constructs.name: getContentWindow

key_constructs.name: getContentDocument

notifies page when new iframe src is set to trigger loading

insights.title: IFrame.getContentWindow now returns Window.Access instead of raw *Window
insights.problem: getContentWindow used to return a *Window which made it difficult to enforce cross-origin restrictions consistently.
insights.root_cause: IFrame API returned internal pointer without enforcing origin checks at the call site.
insights.solution: Change signature to getContentWindow(self, page) ?Window.Access and return Access.init(page.window, frame_window) to ensure caller gets either full window or cross-origin wrapper depending on origin.

src/browser/webapi/element/html/Input.zi.short_description: HTMLInputElement implementation: types, value/checked, selection and form behaviors

insights.title: Implement canvas accessor on CanvasRenderingContext2D
insights.problem: The CanvasRenderingContext2D.canvas accessor was previously unimplemented, so ctx.canvas did not point back to the element.
insights.root_cause: Context instances were created without a reference to their owner Canvas element.
insights.solution: Add _canvas:*Canvas field to CanvasRenderingContext2D, a getCanvas() accessor, and expose the accessor via JsApi. Ensure Canvas.getContext passes 'self' into created context.
insights.lesson_learned: When creating associated objects (context -> owner element), wire ownership references immediately so JS accessors behave per spec. Update factory usage to populate backrefs.

src/browser/webapi/collections/ChildNode.short_description: Live childNodes collection implementation and iterators

key_constructs.name: getAtIndex
key_constructs.purpose: Returns node at numeric index with optimization using last visited index/node.

key_constructs.purpose: Iterator used by Key/Value/Entry iterators to iterate child nodes.

key_behaviors: provides live childNodes access for DOM nodes
caches length and last-index to accelerate sequential access
produces iterator and NodeList wrappers for JavaScript

insights.title: Move NodeList created from ChildNode into ChildNode's arena
insights.problem: NodeList was created using page factory (page._factory.create) which used a different allocator; that could cause allocator/arena mismatches and lifetime errors.
insights.root_cause: ChildNodes had its own arena for temporary objects and NodeList needed to live in the ChildNode's arena for correct lifetime handling.
insights.solution: Create NodeList using self._arena.create() and initialize its _data.child_nodes to self so it shares ChildNode arena.
insights.lesson_learned: When objects live within a DOM node's arena, allocate all child structures in the same arena to avoid cross-arena lifetime bugs.

insights.title: Change net_http import to http and adjust populateHttpHeader signature
insights.problem: Signature used net_http.Headers; naming inconsistent with other modules.
insights.root_cause: Earlier module alias was net_http, now standardized to http.
insights.solution: Use http.Headers and update function accordingly (commit 0604056).
insights.lesson_learned: Keep type names aligned with module aliases to avoid import/identifier mismatch errors.

src/browser/webapi/net/URLSearchParams.z.description: Provides a URLSearchParams implementation supporting construction from strings, arrays, FormData and objects, plus iteration, mutation, encoding and decoding logic. supports various construction forms and JS iteration

insights.title: Support passing arrays to URLSearchParams constructor
insights.problem: Constructor accepted objects/formdata/strings but not arrays of pairs (which JS allows).
insights.root_cause: Array case was not handled; arrays are also objects and previously fell into object handling path.
insights.solution: Added paramsFromArray helper to parse array-of-pairs input and integrated it into init (commit b41c86e).
insights.lesson_learned: JS polymorphic inputs need explicit ordering of type checks (Array before Object) because Array is also an Object; test constructor inputs for edge cases.

src/browser/webapi/selector/Parser.zig.short_description: CSS selector parser (tokenizer and AST builder)

key_constructs.name: preprocessInput

key_constructs.name: parse

key_constructs.name: parsePart / pseudoClass / parseNthPattern
key_constructs.purpose: Parse selector atomic parts including IDs, classes, attributes and pseudo-classes

key_behaviors: parses CSS selectors into ASTs
handles complex pseudo-class and functional selectors
prepares selectors for DOM matching

insights.title: Selector parser no longer requires Page parameter
insights.problem: Parser.parse and parseList carried a Page parameter even when not needed for parsing, coupling parsing to Page.
insights.root_cause: Coupling made reuse of parsed selectors outside of direct Page context awkward.
insights.solution: Remove Page parameter from parse/parseList/parsePart/attribute/pseudoClass and ensure callers provide page where necessary for normalized name lookup via separate helper (commit ab6c63b). Also optimize attribute name normalization to use a new normalizeNameForLookupAlloc helper.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep parsing functions pure from heavy runtime types (Page). Provide separate normalization/allocation helpers that accept allocator to perform runtime-dependent tasks later.

src/browser/webapi/selector/Selector.zig.short_description: Selector structures and query helper functions
src/browser/webapi/selector/Selector.zig.description: Defines the Selector AST types (Part, Compound, Segment, Selector, PseudoClass) and helper functions that parse input strings and execute selector queries against the DOM. It also provides fast-path optimizations like id-only lookups and matching utilities used by querySelector/querySelectorAll.

key_constructs.name: Selector / Compound / Part / PseudoClass
key_constructs.purpose: AST types representing CSS selectors and components

key_constructs.name: parseLeaky
key_constructs.purpose: Parse input into Selector ASTs using Parser.parseList

key_constructs.name: querySelector / querySelectorAll / matches
key_constructs.purpose: Execute selectors against DOM nodes and return matching elements

key_constructs.name: Parsed.query
key_constructs.purpose: Query helper that includes fast paths and uses List.collect to gather matches

key_behaviors: parses selector strings
matches selectors against DOM
provides querySelector and querySelectorAll functionality

insights.title: Separate selector parse and query phases
insights.problem: Selectors were parsed for every query; repeated parsing inside polling loops is wasteful.
insights.root_cause: parseList previously required a Page parameter and parsing was coupled to querying.
insights.solution: Introduce Parsed struct and parseLeaky(arena, input) that returns a parsed representation; provide query(root, page) to run the parsed selectors. Update querySelector/querySelectorAll/matches to call parse once or reuse parsed. This enables runner to parse once and query repeatedly (commit ab6c63b).
insights.lesson_learned: Separate expensive parsing from repeated evaluation when polling or executing repetitive workloads; expose a parsed, reusable representation.
