key_behaviors: resolves link hrefs
detects and enqueues loadable link relations
exposes link attributes and relList to scripts

src/browser/webapi/element/html/Media.zi.short_description: HTMLMediaElement implementation handling media state and attributes
src/browser/webapi/element/html/Media.zi.description: Implements a lightweight HTMLMediaElement abstraction (shared by audio/video) with playback state, network/ready state, attributes and JS bindings. Provides methods to play, pause, load, manage src/autoplay/controls/volume and a canPlayType heuristic for MIME support detection.

key_constructs.name: ReadyState
key_constructs.purpose: Enum of media ready states (HAVE_NOTHING..HAVE_ENOUGH_DATA).

key_constructs.name: NetworkState
key_constructs.purpose: Enum representing the network loading state for media.

key_constructs.name: Type
key_constructs.purpose: Tagged union distinguishing generic/audio/video media instances.

key_constructs.name: canPlayType
key_constructs.purpose: Returns "probably"/"maybe"/"" based on MIME heuristics and page buffers.

key_constructs.name: play / pause / load
key_constructs.purpose: Playback control methods that update state and dispatch corresponding events.

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: JS bridge exposing properties and methods of HTMLMediaElement to V8.

key_behaviors: controls media playback state and dispatches play/pause events
queries MIME playback capability via canPlayType
reads and updates media element attributes like src, autoplay, controls and volume

src/browser/webapi/element/html/Script.z.description: Represents <script> element state (src, type, async/defer, nonce, charset) and integrates with page script lifecycle. It updates attributes, resolves src URLs and triggers page.scriptAddedCallback when dynamically added.

key_constructs.name: getSrc / setSrc
key_constructs.purpose: Resolve and set the script src attribute and trigger loading callbacks when needed.

key_constructs.name: getAsync / setAsync
key_constructs.purpose: Manage async behavior, accounting for forced async on dynamic scripts.

key_constructs.name: setInnerText / _text / _innerText
key_constructs.purpose: Access and mutate script text content exposed to JS.

key_constructs.name: Build.complete
key_constructs.purpose: Node build-time hook to capture initial src attribute from element.

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: Defines the JavaScript accessible properties and methods for HTMLScriptElement.

key_behaviors: sets and resolves script src and triggers dynamic script callbacks
exposes script attributes (async, defer, type, nonce, charset) to JS
provides access to script inline text content

src/browser/webapi/canvas/OffscreenCanva.short_description: OffscreenCanvas API shim with context creation and blob export
src/browser/webapi/canvas/OffscreenCanva.description: Implements the OffscreenCanvas API surface: create instances, get 2D contexts, and convert content to a Blob Promise. Rendering is stubbed (no real GPU rendering); convertToBlob returns an empty Blob and transferToImageBitmap is unimplemented.

key_constructs.name: constructor
key_constructs.purpose: Creates an OffscreenCanvas instance with width and height via page factory.

key_constructs.name: getContext
key_constructs.purpose: Returns a DrawingContext (currently only '2d') and constructs the 2D context object.

key_constructs.name: convertToBlob
key_constructs.purpose: Returns a Promise resolving to a Blob (stubbed empty blob) for canvas contents.

key_constructs.name: transferToImageBitmap
key_constructs.purpose: Stub for transferring rendered content to an ImageBitmap (not implemented).

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: JS bridge exposing OffscreenCanvas constructor and methods to scripts.

key_behaviors: creates offscreen canvas instances and 2D contexts
provides a Promise-based API to convert canvas contents to a Blob

src/browser/webapi/canvas/OffscreenCanva.short_description: 2D offscreen canvas rendering context with ImageData support
src/browser/webapi/canvas/OffscreenCanva.description: Provides a minimal OffscreenCanvasRenderingContext2D implementation supporting fillStyle parsing and ImageData creation/reading. Most drawing operations are stubs; ImageData creation/getting is implemented and integrated with the Page factory.

key_constructs.name: getFillStyle / setFillStyle
key_constructs.purpose: Get and set the current fill style string, parsing color values.

key_constructs.name: createImageData
key_constructs.purpose: Creates a new ImageData either from width/height or cloning an existing ImageData.

key_constructs.name: getImageData
key_constructs.purpose: Returns ImageData for a requested rectangle (validates dimensions).

key_constructs.name: putImageData
key_constructs.purpose: Stub for putting ImageData back into the context (no-op).

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: Exposes properties and methods for OffscreenCanvasRenderingContext2D to JS.

key_behaviors: manages fill style and creates/manipulates ImageData objects
provides stubbed drawing operations compatible with API expectations

src/browser/webapi/canvas/WebGLRendering.description: Implements a minimal WebGLRenderingContext facade focused on extension discovery and enabling. It enumerates a set of extensions, provides getExtension and getSupportedExtensions and exposes a few small extension types (debug renderer info, lose context).

key_constructs.name: Extension
key_constructs.purpose: Tagged union of supported WebGL extensions and reified Kind enum for lookups.

key_constructs.name: find
key_constructs.purpose: Case-insensitive lookup of an extension name to an Extension.Kind value.

key_constructs.name: getExtension
key_constructs.purpose: Enables and returns a supported extension object for a given name.

key_constructs.name: getSupportedExtensions
key_constructs.purpose: Returns a list of supported extension names.

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: JS bridge exposing WebGLRenderingContext functions and extension objects.

key_behaviors: reports supported WebGL extensions
enables individual WebGL extensions and returns their JS wrappers

src/browser/webapi/cdata/CDATASection.zi.description: Defines the CDATASection DOM node type wrapper and registers its JS bridge prototype. The file is a thin specialization of Text nodes to represent CDATA sections in the DOM.

key_constructs.name: CDATASection
key_constructs.purpose: Type wrapper representing a CDATASection node.

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: JavaScript bridge exposing the CDATASection prototype to scripts.

src/browser/webapi/cdata/Comment.zig.description: Implements Comment DOM node creation and JS constructor binding. Provides an init function to create Comment nodes from nullable strings and registers the Comment JS class.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Creates a Comment node via the Page factory from an optional string.

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: JS bridge with constructor and prototype information for Comment.

key_behaviors: creates Comment DOM nodes and exposes a JS constructor

src/browser/webapi/cdata/ProcessingInstr.description: Defines ProcessingInstruction DOM node specialization that stores a target string and exposes a read-only target accessor to JS. It registers the corresponding JS prototype and includes a small test runner invocation.

key_constructs.name: getTarget
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the target string for this ProcessingInstruction node.

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: JS bridge exposing ProcessingInstruction prototype and target accessor.

key_behaviors: provides read access to processing instruction target

src/browser/webapi/collections/DOMTokenL.short_description: Live DOMTokenList implementation for tokenized attributes
src/browser/webapi/collections/DOMTokenL.description: Manages a live list of whitespace-separated tokens (classList-like) backed by an element attribute. Implements add/remove/toggle/replace/contains, iteration, and forEach with JS bindings while ensuring live reflection of the underlying attribute.

key_constructs.name: DOMTokenList
key_constructs.purpose: Structure that holds reference to an Element and attribute name for token ops.

key_constructs.name: add / remove / toggle / replace
key_constructs.purpose: Modify the token set and update the underlying attribute accordingly.

key_constructs.name: contains / item / length
key_constructs.purpose: Query token membership, indexed access and length (computed live).

key_constructs.name: forEach
key_constructs.purpose: Iterate tokens and call a JS callback with proper this handling and error capture.

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: Exposes DOMTokenList methods and properties to the JS environment.

key_behaviors: manages tokenized attribute values (add/remove/toggle/replace)
provides live, order-preserving iteration and JS API parity with classList

src/browser/webapi/collections/HTMLAllCo.short_description: HTMLAllCollection providing live all-elements access and callable behavior
src/browser/webapi/collections/HTMLAllCo.description: Implements the legacy HTMLAllCollection that enumerates every element in a document via a TreeWalker, caches versioning for small optimizations, and supports index or name lookup and callable/htmldda semantics required by JS interoperability.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Initializes the collection with a TreeWalker rooted at a node and caches page version.

key_constructs.name: length / getAtIndex / getByName
key_constructs.purpose: Provide indexed length, access by index and lookup by id/name with a fallback tree scan.

key_constructs.name: callable
key_constructs.purpose: Callable/htmldda support returning element by index or name for JS compatibility.

key_constructs.name: iterator
key_constructs.purpose: Produces an iterator over elements using a cloned TreeWalker.

key_behaviors: enumerates all document elements and supports indexed or named lookup
provides callable behavior used by legacy JS patterns

src/browser/webapi/collections/HTMLColle.short_description: Typed HTMLCollection implementations backed by TreeWalker modes
src/browser/webapi/collections/HTMLColle.description: Generic HTMLCollection wrapper supporting multiple modes (tag, class, child lists, links, forms, etc.) implemented via NodeLive/TreeWalker abstractions. Exposes length, indexed and named accessors, and iterator generation customized per mode.

key_constructs.name: Mode
key_constructs.purpose: Enum listing supported collection modes to select traversal strategy.

key_constructs.name: length / getAtIndex / getByName
key_constructs.purpose: Mode-dispatched entry points that forward to the appropriate NodeLive implementation.

key_constructs.name: iterator
key_constructs.purpose: Creates an iterator with the appropriate TreeWalker clone for the current mode.

key_constructs.name: Iterator
key_constructs.purpose: Iterator wrapper that iterates elements according to the mode-specific walker.

key_behaviors: provides indexed and named access to groups of elements filtered by mode
produces iterators tailored to collection type (children, links, selected options, etc.)

src/browser/webapi/collections/HTMLFormC.short_description: Form controls collection with named-item and radio-group handling
src/browser/webapi/collections/HTMLFormC.description: Wraps an underlying HTMLCollection representing form controls and implements namedItem semantics including special-case RadioNodeList creation when multiple controls share a name. Offers iteration and length forwarding while exposing the named-item API to JS.

key_constructs.name: NamedItemResult
key_constructs.purpose: Union result type returning either a single element or a RadioNodeList for multiple matches.

key_constructs.name: namedItem
key_constructs.purpose: Looks up controls by name/id and constructs RadioNodeList when appropriate.

key_constructs.name: iterator
key_constructs.purpose: Provides an iterator over form controls using a form-specific TreeWalker.

key_constructs.name: Iterator
key_constructs.purpose: Internal iterator type that yields form control elements.

key_behaviors: retrieves form controls by name or id and returns RadioNodeList when multiple radios match
forwards iteration and length queries to the underlying HTMLCollection

src/browser/webapi/collections/HTMLOptio.description: Presents HTMLOptionsCollection API that forwards most behavior to an underlying HTMLCollection and interacts with the owning Select element for selectedIndex. Implements add/remove semantics to insert or delete Option elements.

key_constructs.name: getSelectedIndex / setSelectedIndex
key_constructs.purpose: Forward access to the owning Select element's selectedIndex property.

key_constructs.name: add
key_constructs.purpose: Inserts an Option element into the select before a given index or element.

key_constructs.name: remove
key_constructs.purpose: Removes an option at the specified index if present.

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: JS bridge exposing collection accessors and methods to scripts.

key_behaviors: adds and removes Option elements from a Select
exposes and sets the selectedIndex of the owning Select element

src/browser/webapi/collections/RadioNode.short_description: Representation of a radio group as a NodeList with value semantics
src/browser/webapi/collections/RadioNode.description: Implements RadioNodeList that enumerates radio inputs sharing a name and provides value getter/setter semantics (maps to the checked state). Supports indexed access, length calculation, and setting the selected radio by value.

key_constructs.name: getLength

key_constructs.name: getAtIndex

key_constructs.name: getValue / setValue
key_constructs.purpose: Get the currently checked radio's value or set one radio checked by value.

key_constructs.name: matches
key_constructs.purpose: Internal predicate determining if an element belongs to this radio group.

key_behaviors: represents radio groups and reads/sets the selected radio value
iterates matching radio inputs and exposes length/indexed access

src/browser/webapi/css/CSSRule.zig.short_description: Base CSSRule representation and JS bindings
src/browser/webapi/css/CSSRule.zig.description: Provides a generic CSSRule wrapper with a Type union for different CSS rule kinds and exposes properties like type and cssText. It is a lightweight placeholder used by stylesheet parsing/representation and JS bindings.

key_constructs.name: Type

key_constructs.name: init

key_constructs.name: getType
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the numeric type constant for the rule (matches DOM spec numbering).

key_constructs.name: getCssText
key_constructs.purpose: Returns textual representation of the rule (stubbed empty here).

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: Exposes CSSRule constants and accessors to JS.

key_behaviors: represents CSS rule types and exposes rule properties to JavaScript

src/browser/webapi/css/CSSRuleList.zig.short_description: In-memory list container for CSSRule objects with JS bindings
src/browser/webapi/css/CSSRuleList.zig.description: Implements a container that stores pointers to CSSRule objects and exposes list operations. Provides length/indexed access, insertion, removal and clearing, and registers a JS bridge for property/index access from scripts.

key_constructs.name: CSSRuleList
key_constructs.purpose: Represents a list of CSSRule pointers held by a stylesheet or rule list.

key_constructs.name: length

key_constructs.name: item
key_constructs.purpose: Indexed lookup of a CSSRule, used by the JS indexed accessor.

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: Defines the JavaScript bridge (accessors and indexed property) for the CSSRuleList.

key_behaviors: provides indexed access to CSS rules
allows insertion and removal of CSSRule objects
exposes list length to JavaScript

src/browser/webapi/css/CSSStylePropertie.short_description: Indexed property access wrapper mapping camelCase style names to CSS properties
src/browser/webapi/css/CSSStylePropertie.description: Wraps a CSSStyleDeclaration and implements JS-style property accessors so script code can read/write style properties via camelCase or bracket syntax. It converts camelCase to dash-case, filters vendor prefixes, recognizes known properties, and delegates to the underlying CSSStyleDeclaration.

key_constructs.name: CSSStyleProperties

key_constructs.name: getNamed
key_constructs.purpose: Handles property reads from JS, performs camelCase->dash-case conversion and vendor prefix rules.

key_constructs.name: setNamed

key_constructs.name: camelCaseToDashCase
key_constructs.purpose: Utility that converts camelCase (and vendor-prefixed names) to CSS dash-case.

key_behaviors: maps JS-style property names to CSS properties
returns appropriate undefined/empty values for unknown or vendor-prefixed properties

src/browser/webapi/css/CSSStyleRule.zig.short_description: Represents a CSS style rule (selector + declaration) with JS bindings
src/browser/webapi/css/CSSStyleRule.zig.description: Implements a CSSStyleRule object that stores selector text and lazily constructs a CSSStyleProperties for its declaration. Provides getters/setters for selectorText and cssText formatting, and exposes the object through the JavaScript bridge.

key_constructs.name: CSSStyleRule

key_constructs.name: init

key_constructs.name: setSelectorText

key_constructs.name: getStyle
key_constructs.purpose: Lazily creates and returns the CSSStyleProperties object for the rule.

src/browser/webapi/css/MediaQueryList.zi.short_description: Lightweight MediaQueryList implementation exposing media and matches
src/browser/webapi/css/MediaQueryList.zi.description: Implements a minimal MediaQueryList with stored media text and event-target prototype chaining. It exposes media and matches properties to JS and defines no-op add/remove listener helpers, serving the Web API surface required by tests.

key_constructs.name: MediaQueryList
key_constructs.purpose: Represents a media query list object with media string and event target prototype.

key_constructs.name: getMedia

key_constructs.name: addListener
key_constructs.purpose: No-op compatibility method to match legacy listener API.

key_constructs.name: removeListener
key_constructs.purpose: No-op compatibility method to match legacy listener API.

provides matches property and legacy listener stubs

src/browser/webapi/element/DOMStringMap..short_description: Implements element.dataset mapping between camelCase and data- attributes
src/browser/webapi/element/DOMStringMap..description: Provides get/set/delete accessors for DOMStringMap that translate camelCase JS property names to data- kebab-case attributes on an Element. It optimizes short keys with SSO and falls back to allocation for longer names, and supplies utilities to convert between forms.

key_constructs.name: DOMStringMap
key_constructs.purpose: Represents the dataset view of an Element, mapping JS property accesses to attributes.

key_constructs.name: setProperty

key_constructs.name: camelToKebab

key_constructs.name: kebabToCamel
key_constructs.purpose: Converts data- kebab-case attribute names back to camelCase for enumeration/use.

key_behaviors: maps dataset property access to data- attributes
converts camelCase <-> kebab-case with SSO optimization
supports property get/set/delete for element.dataset

src/browser/webapi/element/Svg.zig.short_description: SVG element wrapper with type-dispatch and preserved tag casing
src/browser/webapi/element/Svg.zig.description: Defines an SVG element representation that preserves case-sensitive tag names and encodes different SVG subtypes via a union Type. It exposes convenience casts to Element and Node, provides runtime type queries and registers the object as the SVGElement JS prototype.

key_constructs.name: Svg
key_constructs.purpose: Represents an SVG element with a union type for specialized SVG element data.

key_constructs.name: Type

key_constructs.name: asElement

src/browser/webapi/encoding/TextDecoderS.short_description: Transform stream decoding Uint8Array chunks into UTF-8 strings
src/browser/webapi/encoding/TextDecoderS.description: Implements TextDecoderStream by creating a TransformStream that decodes incoming Uint8Array chunks into JS strings while handling BOM stripping and empty-chunk behavior. key_constructs.name: TextDecoderStream

key_constructs.name: decodeTransform
key_constructs.purpose: Zig transform function that decodes a TypedArray into a string and strips UTF-8 BOM when configured.

key_constructs.name: getReadable

key_behaviors: decodes Uint8Array stream chunks into strings

provides readable and writable stream endpoints

src/browser/webapi/encoding/TextEncoder..short_description: Simple TextEncoder that encodes strings to UTF-8 Uint8Array
src/browser/webapi/encoding/TextEncoder..description: Provides a minimal TextEncoder implementation that validates UTF-8 input and returns a TypedArray view over the given bytes. It registers the constructor and encode method for JavaScript usage and exposes the encoding property.

key_constructs.name: TextEncoder

key_constructs.name: init

key_constructs.name: encode
key_constructs.purpose: Validates and encodes a byte slice as a JS Uint8Array typed array view.

src/browser/webapi/encoding/TextEncoderS.short_description: Transform stream encoding JS strings into Uint8Array chunks
src/browser/webapi/encoding/TextEncoderS.description: Creates a TransformStream that takes JS strings and outputs Uint8Array chunks representing UTF-8 encoded bytes. It wires the transform callback, exposes readable/writable endpoints, and is registered as TextEncoderStream for script usage.

key_constructs.name: TextEncoderStream

key_constructs.name: init

key_constructs.name: encodeTransform

key_constructs.name: getReadable

src/browser/webapi/media/MediaError.zig.short_description: MediaError object with standard error codes and message
src/browser/webapi/media/MediaError.zig.description: Implements the MediaError object used by media elements, including standard MEDIA_ERR_* constants and accessors for code and message. It provides a constructor initializer and exposes the object via the JS bridge for scripts to inspect media errors.

key_constructs.name: MediaError
key_constructs.purpose: Represents an error related to media playback with code and message.

key_constructs.name: init

key_constructs.name: getCode

key_constructs.name: getMessage

key_constructs.name: JsApi
key_constructs.purpose: Exports the MediaError class, constants, and accessors to JavaScript.

key_behaviors: creates MediaError instances with code and message

src/browser/webapi/media/TextTrackCue.zi.short_description: Base TextTrackCue object with timing and event callback support
src/browser/webapi/media/TextTrackCue.zi.description: Defines a generic TextTrackCue structure (with a union of concrete types like VTTCue) storing id, timing, pause behavior and enter/exit callbacks. key_constructs.name: TextTrackCue

key_constructs.name: JsApi

src/browser/webapi/media/VTTCue.zig.short_description: Concrete VTTCue implementation for WebVTT cues
src/browser/webapi/media/VTTCue.zig.description: Implements a VTTCue with text, region, positioning, size and alignment properties and maps into the TextTrackCue prototype. It provides construction, getters/setters for layout properties and a placeholder getCueAsHTML method (not implemented).

key_constructs.name: VTTCue

src/browser/webapi/navigation/Navigation.short_description: Represents a navigation activation event with history references
src/browser/webapi/navigation/Navigation.description: Encapsulates information about a navigation activation including the history entry activated, optional source entry, and navigation type. key_constructs.name: NavigationActivation

src/browser/webapi/navigation/root.zig.short_description: Shared navigation enums and structs (types, kinds, state, transition)
src/browser/webapi/navigation/root.zig.description: Defines common navigation-related types used across the navigation subsystem: NavigationType, NavigationKind union, NavigationState struct, and NavigationTransition. key_constructs.name: NavigationType

key_constructs.name: NavigationState

key_constructs.name: NavigationTransition

src/browser/webapi/net/XMLHttpRequestEve.short_description: EventTarget mixin handling XMLHttpRequest progress and lifecycle callbacks

key_constructs.name: XMLHttpRequestEventTarget

insights.title: Protect against index out-of-bounds on invalid datetime inputs
insights.problem: Parsing RFC822 dates with missing timezone where input ended at time boundary caused index out-of-bounds access.
insights.root_cause: Code consumed a space without checking remaining input length; when input ended exactly at time boundary consumeIf(' ') led to reading past buffer.
insights.solution: Check parser.unconsumed() == 0 before calling consumeIf(' ') and return InvalidTime if no chars left; add test for missing timezone case.
insights.lesson_learned: Parser code must defensively check remaining length before consuming expected separators; add tests for truncated/malformed inputs that previously caused OOB.

insights.title: Add ARIA attribute reflection getters/setters
insights.problem: Elements didn't expose ariaAtomic and ariaLive attribute-backed accessors.
insights.root_cause: ARIA mixin reflection was incomplete.
insights.solution: Added getAriaAtomic/setAriaAtomic and getAriaLive/setAriaLive accessors (commit e430051).
insights.lesson_learned: ARIA attribute reflection is important for accessibility features and should be implemented consistently across elements.

Includes a custom panic handler to annotate which legacy test failed.

insights.title: Add HTMLElement.title and dir/lang accessors
insights.problem: HTMLElement did not have attribute-backed title getter/setter; dir/lang accessors were added to HTML element and document delegations.
insights.root_cause: Missing attribute reflection surface for title and document-level dir/lang delegation.
insights.solution: Added getTitle/setTitle and getDir/setDir/getLang/setLang on HtmlElement and HTMLDocument to delegate to documentElement (commits e412dfe, 68e2140).
insights.lesson_learned: Attribute reflection should be consistent across element prototypes and document delegations to match web platform expectations.

src/test_runner.zig.short_description: Custom Zig test runner with timing and allocation tracking
src/test_runner.zig.description: Implements a bespoke test runner: discovers builtin tests, supports filtering, measures durations, reports slowest tests, tracks allocation statistics via a TrackingAllocator, and manages setup/teardown hooks and custom panic reporting. It drives test execution and prints summary/metrics suitable for CI and local debugging.

src/browser/tests/testing.js.short_description: Client-side test helper utilities for browser tests
src/browser/tests/testing.js.description: Provides assertion helpers (expectEqual, expectTrue/False), asynchronous helpers, and a testing object used by test pages to record observations and determine pass/fail via testing.assertOk(). It also adapts behavior when run outside the test runner to make tests usable in regular browsers.

key_constructs.name: expectEqual

insights.title: Add promise-based testing.async overload to coordinate async test steps
insights.problem: Synchronous testing.async wrapper couldn't reliably keep tests alive until asynchronous DOM/frame events completed; tests would exit early.
insights.root_cause: Lack of robust async coordination primitives in the browser-side test shim.
insights.solution: Added an overload of async() that returns an object with promise, resolve and done() helpers and tracks async_pending. The done() helper waits on the promise and decrements the pending counter so the Zig test runner can poll for completion via testing.assertOk().
insights.lesson_learned: Give JS test code explicit primitives to signal completion to the host runner; keep a small 'pending counter' to coordinate the host's completion check.

exercised_by: frames.html (tests updated to use new testing.async API)

insights.title: Enhance testing.async to support pending counter and promise-based waits
insights.problem: testing.async previously couldn't block the test runner reliably for long-running microtasks; tests could exit prematurely.
insights.root_cause: testing.async implementation only worked for immediate microtasks and didn't integrate with the runner tick loop.
insights.solution: Add overload that returns a promise resolver and increment async_pending; testing harness checks async_pending to avoid early exit. Expose resolve/capture/done helpers on returned object.

exercised_by: many webapi tests updated to use the new async pattern

insights.title: Rename testing.eventually -> testing.onload
insights.problem: testing.eventually name was ambiguous and didn't clearly reflect intent.
insights.root_cause: Naming was unclear; new runner/tick semantics made a clearer API desirable.
insights.solution: Rename eventually to onload and update many tests to call testing.onload(...). Update testing.js to throw a clearer error message for misuse.

src/browser/tests/legacy/testing.js.short_description: Legacy browser-side test utilities for old test suite

src/Config.zig.short_description: Central CLI/configuration schema and accessors for runtime flags and derived values
src/Config.zig.description:
This file defines the program's configuration model (Mode/Serve/Fetch/Mcp/Common), CLI-help text, and a set of accessor functions used throughout the binary to read configuration values in a type-safe way. It exists to centralize runtime configuration (parsed elsewhere) and to provide derived values and defaults (timeouts, ports, UA header formatting, CDP limits). Many subsystems (network/http client, CDP server, telemetry, and CLI entry points) call these accessors so this file is the authoritative place for default values and how user-supplied options are interpreted.

Design-wise, Config.zig uses a union Mode that discriminates between run modes (help, fetch, serve, version, mcp). It exposes small accessor functions (tlsVerifyHost, httpProxy, cdpTimeout, port, etc.) that switch on the active Mode and return the relevant nested field or apply a default. The file also contains a small helper HttpHeaders struct that pre-formats commonly re-used HTTP header values (User-Agent and optionally Proxy-Authorization) and manages allocation and deallocation of those strings via a provided Allocator. A large printUsageAndExit function contains the CLI usage/help text (keeps detailed flag descriptions and help-line defaults dependent on builtin.mode). The file also defines constants used by other components (CDP_MAX_HTTP_REQUEST_SIZE, CDP_MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE) and enums for dump formats and wait semantics.

key_constructs.name: RunMode
key_constructs.purpose: Enum of runtime commands (help, fetch, serve, version, mcp).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to select which Mode union arm is active and to drive behavior in accessors and CLI dispatch. When editing mode-handling code ensure all accessor switches cover the RunMode arms consistently.

key_constructs.name: CDP_MAX_HTTP_REQUEST_SIZE
key_constructs.purpose: Maximum size to accept for CDP HTTP request lines.
key_constructs.reasoning: Downstream code (CDP parser/server) relies on this bound; changing it requires validating buffer sizes and tests for WebSocket/CDP framing.

key_constructs.name: CDP_MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE
key_constructs.purpose: Maximum CDP message payload size with overhead allowances.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used as a guard against overly large websocket messages; other components assume this upper bound for buffer allocation and parsing.

key_constructs.name: Config
key_constructs.purpose: Type alias to refer to this module's top-level struct.
key_constructs.reasoning: Central type used by init/deinit and accessor methods; edits here affect all callers.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Initialize a Config instance and create HttpHeaders using allocator.
key_constructs.reasoning: Allocates header strings that must outlive HTTP activity. Any change to allocation behavior or lifetime requirements must be coordinated with deinit and consumers that hold Config across threads/tasks.

key_constructs.name: deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Tear down configuration, freeing HttpHeaders allocations.
key_constructs.reasoning: Must be called with the same allocator used for init; deinit frees user_agent_header and conditionally frees user_agent and proxy header. When changing HttpHeaders, maintain deinit invariants.

key_constructs.name: HttpHeaders
key_constructs.purpose: Pre-formatted User-Agent and optional Proxy-Authorization header strings and their lifecycle logic.
key_constructs.reasoning: Created once at startup with an allocator that must outlive any HTTP requests. The ctor builds strings using std.fmt.allocPrintSentinel and errdefer semantics; the dcotor logic contains an optimization to avoid freeing the user_agent when it equals the static user_agent_base pointer.

key_constructs.name: httpTimeout
key_constructs.purpose: Return effective HTTP timeout in milliseconds (default 5000) for current mode.
key_constructs.reasoning: Converts optional values in Common into concrete timeouts; callers expect ms units. Maintain type widths and default behavior when editing.

key_constructs.name: httpConnectTimeout
key_constructs.purpose: Return HTTP connect timeout in ms (0 means no timeout) for current mode.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by the HTTP client to configure connect timeouts; keep the semantics that 0 disables the timeout and type is u31.

key_constructs.name: wsMaxConcurrent
key_constructs.purpose: Return configured max concurrent WebSocket connections (default 8).
key_constructs.reasoning: Expose WebSocket pool sizing for consumers. This option was added in commit history; respect its presence and defaults.

key_constructs.name: userAgentSuffix
key_constructs.purpose: Return optional suffix to append to User-Agent header for current mode.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when constructing HttpHeaders; when present, allocator-allocated string is created and deinit must free it unless it points to the static base.

key_constructs.name: cdpTimeout
key_constructs.purpose: Return CDP command timeout (in ms) depending on mode; serves converts seconds->ms and clamps serve timeout to 1 week.
key_constructs.reasoning: Important conversion: serve arm multiplies seconds to ms and clamps to 604_800; mcp returns 10000. Watch integer types and units when editing.

key_constructs.name: Mode
key_constructs.purpose: Union over the RunMode variants that stores mode-specific option structs.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates nested option structs (Serve/Fetch/Mcp) and drives switch logic in all accessor functions; modifying Mode requires updating all switch usages.

key_constructs.name: Fetch
key_constructs.purpose: Configuration for one-shot fetch command (url, dump_mode, wait options, strip options).
key_constructs.reasoning: Controls behavior for CLI fetch command; defaults (e.g., wait_ms=5000) are meaningful for CLI users and have been source of past bugs (default wait_until change). Keep fields consistent with parsing code and help text.

key_constructs.name: Common
key_constructs.purpose: Common fields used by multiple run modes: proxy, timeouts, UA suffix, logging options, caching, web-bot-auth.
key_constructs.reasoning: Shared option bag; many accessors read this struct. When adding or renaming fields, ensure parser and printUsageAndExit remain synchronized (historical mismatches caused bugs).

key_constructs.name: printUsageAndExit
key_constructs.purpose: Emit the program usage/help text and exit; contains canonical flag descriptions and examples.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function is the authoritative help text displayed to users. The help text is tied to parsing logic; prior commits show breakage when help text and parser diverged. When editing flags or naming, update both parser and this text together.

key_behaviors: provides mode-specific accessors for configuration values
constructs preformatted HTTP headers (User-Agent, Proxy-Authorization)
exposes CDP and HTTP related constants used by network and server subsystems
generates CLI usage/help text reflecting runtime defaults

pitfalls.mistake: Modify HttpHeaders.init to allocate or free strings with a different allocator or lifecycle than callers expect.
pitfalls.consequence: Leads to use-after-free or double-free when HTTP connections outlive the allocator or when deinit frees memory still referenced by network code.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the rule that the allocator passed to init must outlive any HTTP activity and that deinit is called only after no network activity remains.

pitfalls.mistake: Change default semantics or units of cdpTimeout, httpTimeout, or connect timeout without updating callers/tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Timeouts will be misinterpreted (seconds vs ms) and could cause premature disconnects or stalls.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the existing units: cdpTimeout returns ms (serve multiplies seconds by 1000 and clamps). Update all switch arms consistently.

pitfalls.mistake: Edit printUsageAndExit help text to use different hyphenation/dash style without updating parsing logic that expects older variants.
pitfalls.consequence: Help output will contradict accepted CLI flags (dash vs underscore), confusing users and tests. Past commits show mismatches between printed help and parser checks.
pitfalls.prevention: When renaming flags or preferring dash-separated forms, ensure parse* functions accept both old and new forms or update all callers and tests simultaneously.

pitfalls.mistake: Change CDP_MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE or CDP_MAX_HTTP_REQUEST_SIZE values without auditing all buffer allocations and protocol parsing code.
pitfalls.consequence: Framing/parser buffer overflows or unnecessary memory growth; mismatched expectations in MCP/CDP server can break message handling.
pitfalls.prevention: Search for usages of these constants (server, websocket buffer allocations) and update consumers; run protocol tests after changes.

pitfalls.mistake: Remove or rename fields in Common/Mode/Fetch without updating parse*Arg functions and any code that reads those accessors.
pitfalls.consequence: Runtime panics/unreachable branches when accessors switch on an unexpected Mode arm or missing fields, especially since many accessors use switch-else unreachable.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing shape, update all accessor functions and parsing code in tandem.

key_sections: HttpHeaders.init: allocation of user-agent and proxy header strings and their error/defer handling
init/deinit: how Config is created and cleaned up (allocator lifetimes)
Accessor functions (tlsVerifyHost, httpProxy, cdpTimeout, port, etc.): how mode-specific values are extracted and defaults applied
printUsageAndExit: canonical CLI help text and examples
skip_unless_needed: Large literal blocks inside printUsageAndExit (help text) if you're only concerned with accessor behavior
The static user_agent_base string definition unless changing UA format

tests.example_command: zig build test  # run Zig test suite for the repository (if available)

related_files.relationship: CLI entry point that parses argv and constructs Config.Mode, then calls Config.init; parse*Arg functions live near/inside main path and must be consistent with Config accessors and help text.
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing defaults or flag names, update the parsing logic and main dispatch to keep behavior coherent.

related_files.relationship: HTTP client consumes HttpHeaders and many timeout/proxy/cache accessors from Config.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to header formatting, timeout semantics, or http_max_response_size must be reflected here.

related_files.relationship: CDP/MCP server uses CDP_MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE and port/cdpTimeout/maxConnections; behaviour depends on these Config accessors.
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing CDP limits or CDP timeout behavior, update server message handling and tests.

related_files.relationship: Fetch.dump_mode and Fetch.strip are passed into the dump module and impact output formatting and flags parsing.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifying DumpFormat enum or fetch defaults requires coordinating with dump.zig logic.

tests_to_run: make build then run key commands: ./zig-out/bin/lightpanda fetch --help and ./zig-out/bin/lightpanda serve --help to verify usage text and parsing

insights.title: Add ws_max_concurrent option and --http-cache-dir flag
insights.problem: No way to control maximum concurrent websocket connections; cache path flag wasn't specific.
insights.root_cause: New websocket pool required configurable max; existing cache flag name ambiguous.
insights.solution: Add wsMaxConcurrent accessor and CLI option --ws-max-concurrent; rename cache_dir to http_cache_dir and update CLI parsing and usage text.
insights.lesson_learned: Exposed new resource limits need CLI hooks and sensible defaults (tests set higher default); be explicit in CLI naming when feature set grows.

insights.title: Normalize cache-dir CLI argument name
insights.problem: CLI help and parsing used inconsistent flag naming (--cache-dir vs --cache_dir), causing confusing error messages and missing argument handling.
insights.root_cause: Mismatch between the printed usage text and the actual option comparison string.
insights.solution: Change parseCommonArg to check for "--cache-dir" and update error messages accordingly; update usage text to show --cache-dir.
insights.lesson_learned: CLI flags should be consistent across usage text and parsing logic; prefer hyphenated flags (POSIX style) and keep help strings aligned with code.

insights.title: Add cache_dir to configuration options
insights.problem: No CLI/config support to enable a filesystem cache directory.
insights.root_cause: Cache feature needed a way to be configured at startup.
insights.solution: Add cacheDir() accessor and common.cache_dir field; parse --cache_dir CLI argument and store in common config.
insights.lesson_learned: Add configuration options and CLI flags early when introducing a feature that relies on runtime configuration.

insights.title: Add --wait-selector and --wait-script options to fetch CLI
insights.problem: The fetch CLI's existing waiting semantics were limited to --wait-until and didn't support waiting for arbitrary DOM selectors or JS expressions.
insights.root_cause: Limited fetch waiting options reduced automation expressiveness (e.g., wait until custom condition true).
insights.solution: Add optional wait_selector, wait_script and wait_script_file configuration options and wire them into CLI parsing and usage. Clarify interaction rules: wait_until is checked first; if wait_until not specified and wait_selector/script are specified, there is no default wait; otherwise default remains .done (commit ab6c63b).
insights.lesson_learned: When adding new CLI waiting semantics, document ordering and default behavior clearly and ensure parsing handles both inline script and file-based script inputs.

insights.title: Remove LIGHTPANDA_MCP_VERSION env var and add explicit --version option
insights.problem: Protocol version selection via env var made configuration less explicit and was removed in favor of CLI option managed in parseMcpArgs.
insights.root_cause: Desire to simplify configuration parsing and centralize version choice in CLI flags instead of env var leading to removing the env var handling.
insights.solution: Removed env var parsing for LIGHTPANDA_MCP_VERSION, added parsing of --version in parseMcpArgs, and stored mcp.version in Config.Mcp. Exposed mcpVersion() helper to return configured or default version.
insights.lesson_learned: Prefer explicit CLI flags to implicit env var overrides for protocol-level settings. Ensure CLI help/usage text documents the option and update tests.

insights.title: Default --wait-until value should be 'done' not 'load'
insights.problem: The default value for Fetch.wait_until was mistakenly set to 'load' while documentation and intended behavior expected 'done'.
insights.root_cause: Developer mistake set WaitUntil default to .load in both struct default and parse defaults.
insights.solution: Change default Fetch.wait_until to .done and update parseFetchArgs default variable accordingly.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep defaults in code in sync with documented behavior; test CLI defaults to catch such regressions early.

insights.title: Prefer dash-separated CLI flags, keep underscore variants for backwards compatibility
insights.problem: CLI flags used underscore style (--log_level). Desire to prefer dash-separated form (--log-level).
insights.root_cause: Consistency/UX improvement to use standard dash-separated flags.
insights.solution: Update usage text and parsing logic to accept both dash and underscore forms; prefer dash in examples and messages. Many flag names updated (e.g., --http_proxy -> --http-proxy) but parser still accepts old underscored names.

src/network/cache/Cache.zig.short_description: Abstract cache API and metadata/parsing helpers for network response caching
src/network/cache/Cache.zig.description:
Provides the high-level Cache abstraction used by the networking layer and helpers to decide whether an HTTP response should be cached. This file exists to centralize cache-related policy (RFC9111-like conservative decisions) and typed data structures that represent cached responses and their metadata. It intentionally exposes a small API surface (Cache union with deinit/get/put wrappers) so concrete cache implementations (currently FsCache) can be switched via the union(kind).

Key responsibilities include parsing Cache-Control headers (CacheControl.parse), representing stored response metadata (CachedMetadata) and request context (CacheRequest), and offering a single helper (tryCache) that callers can use to decide and allocate a CachedMetadata instance into a provided arena when a response is deemed storable. The design centralizes cacheability logic (no-store/no-cache, Vary: *, Set-Cookie, Authorization, public requirement, max-age rules) so callers do not duplicate these checks. The CachedResponse and CachedData types encode how the cache returns on-disk vs in-memory data to callers.

key_constructs.name: Cache
key_constructs.purpose: Union(enum) wrapper that dispatches to concrete cache implementation(s) (currently FsCache).
key_constructs.reasoning: Union-based dispatch replaces an earlier vtable pattern; callers use Cache.get/put/deinit without knowing concrete implementation details. Editors must preserve the union shape when adding new implementations.

key_constructs.name: Cache.deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Deinitializes the concrete cache implementation via switch on Cache.kind.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensure resources like open directory handles are closed by delegating to concrete deinit functions.

key_constructs.name: Cache.get
key_constructs.purpose: Wrapper dispatch that asks the concrete cache to retrieve a cached entry for a CacheRequest.
key_constructs.reasoning: The get signature requires an allocator and CacheRequest with timestamp/request headers for deterministic expiry and Vary matching; callers must construct and pass that struct.

key_constructs.name: Cache.put
key_constructs.purpose: Wrapper dispatch that asks the concrete cache to persist metadata and body.
key_constructs.reasoning: Put accepts a CachedMetadata and body bytes; CachedMetadata should normally be obtained via tryCache to ensure it conforms to policy and arena lifetimes are managed by caller.

key_constructs.name: CacheControl
key_constructs.purpose: Represents parsed cache-control with at least max_age stored and a parse() helper.
key_constructs.reasoning: Parsing cache-control is centralized so callers rely on consistent semantics; parse() returns null for uncacheable directives (no-store/no-cache/zero/max-age missing or not public).

key_constructs.name: CacheControl.parse
key_constructs.purpose: Parses Cache-Control header value and returns ?CacheControl (null when uncacheable).
key_constructs.reasoning: The parser is conservative: requires either max-age or s-maxage, requires public directive, rejects no-store/no-cache, and treats zero max-age as uncacheable. Tests and callers depend on these exact semantics.

key_constructs.name: CachedMetadata
key_constructs.purpose: In-memory representation of stored response metadata (URL, content type, status, timestamps, headers, cache-control info).
key_constructs.reasoning: This struct is the primary on-disk metadata model serialized by FsCache. Its fields are allocated by callers (often via arena) and referenced by FsCache when serializing/deserializing.

key_constructs.name: CachedMetadata.format
key_constructs.purpose: Helper to format key metadata fields for logging (url, status, content_type, max_age, vary headers).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to produce concise debug output; maintain the format contract when modifying metadata fields used for logging.

key_constructs.name: CachedData
key_constructs.purpose: Union representing cached body data: buffer or file reference (with offset/len).
key_constructs.reasoning: FsCache returns a file-backed CachedData.file so callers must handle a file case (close file when done) and respect offset/len semantics.

key_constructs.name: CachedResponse
key_constructs.purpose: Top-level response returned from Cache.get: metadata and data (CachedData).
key_constructs.reasoning: Callers get metadata and either in-memory or file-backed data; the file-backed case requires the caller to close the file when finished.

key_constructs.name: CacheRequest
key_constructs.purpose: Represents the request context used when retrieving from cache: url, timestamp and request headers.
key_constructs.reasoning: Timestamp is required for deterministic expiry checks in tests and runtime; request_headers are used for Vary matching. Call sites must populate these correctly.

key_constructs.name: tryCache
key_constructs.purpose: Decision helper that determines if a response should be cached, returning allocated CachedMetadata in the provided arena or null if uncacheable.
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralizes many of the conservative checks (status, Set-Cookie, Authorization, Vary: *, Cache-Control parsing) — callers should call tryCache and treat null as 'do not cache'.

key_behaviors: decides if an HTTP response is cacheable and prepares metadata
dispatches get/put/deinit to concrete cache implementations
parses Cache-Control headers conservatively

pitfalls.mistake: Treating tryCache return value as long-lived without considering allocator
pitfalls.consequence: Allocated strings in CachedMetadata may be arena-allocated and will be freed when the arena is deinitialized leading to dangling references if retained longer.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the arena lifetime passed to tryCache; ensure the arena remains valid for as long as CachedMetadata is used or duplicate into another allocator.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming CacheControl.parse accepts all cache-control directives
pitfalls.consequence: Changes to parse semantics (e.g., accepting 'private' or missing 'public') could make previously uncacheable responses storable, violating privacy assumptions.
pitfalls.prevention: When modifying parse(), preserve conservative defaults (no-store/no-cache rejection, require max-age/s-maxage, require public) or coordinate changes with security/privacy review and tests.

pitfalls.mistake: Using Cache.get/put without populating CacheRequest.timestamp
pitfalls.consequence: Expiry behavior becomes non-deterministic in tests or may allow expired entries to be served if callers use different time sources.
pitfalls.prevention: Always construct CacheRequest with a deterministic timestamp (use std.time.timestamp() at call site or test-controlled values) when calling Cache.get.

key_sections: CacheControl.parse: centralizes cache-control parsing logic and explains why many responses are rejected
CachedMetadata: fields that are serialized/deserialized by FsCache and used for Vary/expiry checks
Cache.get/put/deinit: thin dispatch wrappers to concrete implementations
skip_unless_needed: CachedData.format and CachedResponse.format (logging helpers)

tests.example_command: zig build test (project-level) or zig test src/network/cache/FsCache.zig -q

related_files.relationship: Concrete filesystem-backed implementation of the Cache union; serializes/deserializes CachedMetadata and body to disk and implements get/put.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes to CachedMetadata, CacheRequest, CacheControl.parse, or Cache.get/put signatures must be mirrored here.

related_files.relationship: Caller of the Cache API; uses tryCache and Cache.put/get to persist HTTP responses and to check cache on requests.
related_files.reason_to_check: HttpClient constructs CacheRequests (timestamp and request headers) and relies on tryCache semantics before calling put; signature changes or policy changes require coordinated updates.

insights.title: Add format() method to CachedMetadata for logging
insights.problem: Logging cache metadata was ad-hoc and verbose; no helper to format key fields for debugging.
insights.root_cause: No string formatting helper existed on CachedMetadata.
insights.solution: Add CachedMetadata.format(writer) that prints url, status, content_type, max_age and Vary headers.
insights.lesson_learned: Small helpers to format complex structures make logging and debugging much easier; prefer concise formatted output over logging full headers.

insights.title: Cache API simplified and short-circuited for early non-cacheable responses
insights.problem: Complex cache-control parsing and many checks scattered in callers made code harder to reason about.
insights.root_cause: Responsibility for determining cacheability was split and sometimes duplicated.
insights.solution: Consolidate cacheability logic inside CacheControl.parse (now returning ?CacheControl); return null early for uncacheable directives (no-store, no-cache, zero max-age, not public). Simplify Cache.put/get signatures to accept structured CacheRequest and metadata. Add tryCache helper for quick decision whether to cache.
insights.lesson_learned: Perform early exits for uncacheable responses and centralize cacheability parsing to avoid duplicated logic and unnecessary work; prefer returning optional parsed structures to indicate uncacheable responses.

insights.title: Cache.get/put signatures changed to take CacheRequest and use arena
insights.problem: Callers previously passed simple string keys; new behavior needs timestamp and request headers.
insights.root_cause: New features (Vary, deterministic expiry) require richer request data.
insights.solution: Introduce CacheRequest struct (url, later timestamp and request_headers) and change get/put signatures accordingly. Update all callers to pass the new struct and use arena allocation where needed.
insights.lesson_learned: Changing fundamental API shapes (get/put) is breaking; ensure a coordinated update of all call sites and add tests to prevent regressions.

insights.title: Do not cache responses with Set-Cookie or Authorization, and reject Vary:*
insights.problem: Sensitive responses could be cached incorrectly, exposing private data.
insights.root_cause: Cache logic allowed caching in some cases where cookies or authorization were present, and didn't treat Vary: * as uncacheable.
insights.solution: Explicitly check for Set-Cookie and Authorization headers and return uncacheable (null). Treat Vary: * as uncacheable. CacheControl.parse returns null for directives that imply non-cacheability (no-store, no-cache).
insights.lesson_learned: Caching must conservatively refuse to cache responses that could leak user-specific or dynamic content; handle Vary:* as uncacheable and block Set-Cookie/Authorization-containing responses.

insights.title: Switch from vtable pointer-based interface to union(enum) kind
insights.problem: Initial Cache interface used a vtable with opaque pointer; this made ownership and dispatch verbose and error-prone.
insights.root_cause: The vtable/ptr pattern introduced indirection and ergonomics issues in Zig; using a union(enum) simplifies calls and keeps concrete implementations static and type-safe.
insights.solution: Replace ptr/vtable with kind: union(enum) { fs: FsCache, } and dispatch get/put via switch on kind.

insights.title: Add CacheControl & Vary types and parsing helpers
insights.problem: Cache decisions were being done manually and not robustly handling directives like s-maxage, public, immutable or Vary.
insights.root_cause: RFC-like cache behavior needs parsing of cache-control and vary header semantics to determine cacheability correctly.
insights.solution: Introduce CacheControl struct with parse(), Vary union(enum) with parse()/toString(), and store them in CachedMetadata.
insights.lesson_learned: Cache logic should parse and persist cache-control and vary metadata early; it simplifies policy checks later.

insights.title: Make CachedMetadata deinit handle Vary and headers
insights.problem: CachedMetadata deinit didn't free parsed Vary/value or header memory consistently after adding headers & Vary value storage.
insights.root_cause: As more metadata (headers, vary value) were attached to CachedMetadata, deinit needed to free them to avoid leaks.
insights.solution: Update deinit to free header name/value, header array, and call Vary.deinit where applicable.

insights.title: FromHeaders: implement conservative cacheability checks
insights.problem: Cache could store responses that violate common caching rules (no-store, Vary: *, missing max-age, Set-Cookie, Authorization).
insights.root_cause: Initial naive caching stored responses without full consideration of RFC9111 semantics; lacked checks for no-store, Vary: *, presence of Set-Cookie/Authorization without 'public', and s-maxage precedence.
insights.solution: Implement fromHeaders to parse headers, respect no-store, treat Vary: * as uncacheable, require explicit max-age (or s-maxage), avoid caching Set-Cookie unless 'public', and avoid caching Authorization responses without public or s-maxage; only cache status 200 currently.
insights.lesson_learned: Caching rules are subtle; be conservative initially and encode explicit checks for Set-Cookie and Authorization to avoid privacy/security pitfalls.

src/network/cache/FsCache.zig.short_description: Filesystem-backed cache implementation: atomic writes, JSON metadata, SHA-256 keys, and striped locking
src/network/cache/FsCache.zig.description:
Implements a durable filesystem-backed Cache (FsCache) used by the networking layer. Its purpose is to persist HTTP response bodies and structured metadata to disk in a format that supports evolution (metadata JSON with versioning). FsCache manages hashing of keys (SHA-256 hex), atomic writes via temporary files and rename, and concurrent access safety with striped locks. It returns file-backed CachedResponse entries so callers can read bodies without copying into memory.

FsCache.get reads an on-disk cache file structured as: an 8-byte little-endian body length, the body bytes, then a JSON-serialized metadata object (CacheMetadataJson). It validates the metadata version, computes expiry deterministically using a timestamp passed in CacheRequest, checks Vary headers by comparing stored vary header values with the incoming request headers, and guards against hash collision by validating the stored URL. FsCache.put writes to a .cache.tmp file while holding the lock, writes the body length header and body, then JSON metadata, flushes, and renames atomically to .cache. Error paths log and attempt to clean up temp files. The implementation uses a fixed LOCK_STRIPES (power-of-two) array of std.Thread.Mutex and maps keys to stripes using Wyhash masked to stripe count.

key_constructs.name: FsCache
key_constructs.purpose: Type/namespace for the filesystem cache implementation (fields: dir, locks).
key_constructs.reasoning: Represents the concrete cache backend that gets embedded into Cache.kind; editors must maintain init/deinit/get/put as its API.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Creates the cache directory (cwd.makeDir) and opens it as a Dir for subsequent operations.
key_constructs.reasoning: Called to initialize FsCache with a filesystem path; errors should be propagated and handled by callers.

key_constructs.name: deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Closes the Dir handle (self.dir.close()).
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensure the Dir is closed to release OS resources; calling code (Cache.deinit) relies on this.

key_constructs.name: get
key_constructs.purpose: Attempts to retrieve and validate a cached entry for a given CacheRequest; returns ?CachedResponse (file-backed) or null for miss.
key_constructs.reasoning: Implements file format parsing, expiry checks (uses req.timestamp), Vary header matching, and collision detection; it locks the stripe while operating on the file to avoid races with put.

key_constructs.name: put
key_constructs.purpose: Persists a CachedMetadata and body to a temporary file then atomically renames it into place, holding the stripe lock for the key.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures atomic replacement semantics and attempts to clean up on error; maintains file format (8-byte length header + body + JSON metadata).

key_constructs.name: hashKey
key_constructs.purpose: Computes SHA-256 digest of the URL and returns its 64-character hex representation used as file base names.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a collision-resistant file key; callers must use the exact hashing routine to compute filenames and to map to stripes consistently.

key_constructs.name: cachePath
key_constructs.purpose: Formats the final cache filename from the hashed key ("{hex}.cache").
key_constructs.reasoning: Filename layout must remain stable because other code expects this path format; also used for deletes and renames.

key_constructs.name: cacheTmpPath
key_constructs.purpose: Formats the temporary cache filename for safe writes ("{hex}.cache.tmp").
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by put to write atomically and by cleanup logic; keep consistent with cachePath for safe renames.

key_constructs.name: getLockPtr
key_constructs.purpose: Maps a hashed key pointer to one of LOCK_STRIPES mutexes using Wyhash and bitmasking.
key_constructs.reasoning: Implements striped locking to allow concurrency across different keys while serializing operations on the same key; ensure the same hashing input and mask are used whenever computing stripe.

key_constructs.name: CACHE_VERSION
key_constructs.purpose: Integer version used to version on-disk metadata JSON for forwards compatibility.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used during deserialization to reject mismatched metadata formats and trigger cleanup; bumping it requires migration logic or coordinated changes across code that reads/writes cache files.

key_behaviors: writes cache files atomically by writing to .tmp and renaming
reads cache files by reading body length, body, then JSON metadata
enforces expiry deterministically using CacheRequest.timestamp
matches Vary header values against incoming request headers
uses SHA-256 hex keys and checks for hash collisions

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing the 8-byte BODY_LEN header format without updating readers
pitfalls.consequence: get will parse wrong offsets, read invalid metadata JSON, and likely treat files as corrupted leading to misses or panics.
pitfalls.prevention: If changing file layout keep get/put in sync and bump CACHE_VERSION or add migration code.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying LOCK_STRIPES or the hashing/mapping logic without handling concurrent operations
pitfalls.consequence: Different code paths could map the same key to different locks leading to race conditions and possible file corruption.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the mapping algorithm (hashKey output -> getLockPtr input) and ensure LOCK_STRIPES remains a power-of-two; update tests that rely on concurrency if you must change it.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming returned CachedResponse.file will be auto-closed
pitfalls.consequence: Leaked file descriptors and resource exhaustion in long-running processes.
pitfalls.prevention: Callers must close the std.fs.File returned in CachedData.file.file after reading; tests in this file explicitly close files—follow the same pattern.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing CachedMetadata layout/fields without updating JSON serialization or CACHE_VERSION
pitfalls.consequence: Existing cache files will be rejected (version mismatch) or mis-parsed; tests expect strict behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: When altering metadata, increment CACHE_VERSION and handle migration/cleanup paths.

pitfalls.mistake: Not passing deterministic timestamps to get() during tests
pitfalls.consequence: Flaky tests or incorrect expiry behavior; earlier bugs were caused by callers using std.time.timestamp() inside get flows.
pitfalls.prevention: Use CacheRequest.timestamp (test-controlled) when calling get in tests and production to ensure deterministic expiry checks.

key_sections: hashKey/cachePath/cacheTmpPath: how filenames are derived and how rename works
get: reading file format, JSON metadata parsing, expiry and vary matching (core validation logic)
put: creating .tmp file, write body length + body + JSON metadata, flush and rename
getLockPtr and locks: concurrency model with striped mutexes
skip_unless_needed: Constants at top (BODY_LEN_HEADER_LEN, HASHED_KEY_LEN) except when changing file format
embedded tests (read them to understand expected behavior but not necessary for API overview)

exercised_by: embedded Zig test blocks inside this file (run with zig test or project tests)
tests.example_command: zig test src/network/cache/FsCache.zig -q OR run project: zig build test (or make test)

relevant_snippets.description: Initialization, hashing, and path helpers used by tests; init() and hashKey() are exercised across tests.
Core get() implementation: header read, body_len parsing, JSON metadata parsing, expiration and Vary checks; tests assert miss/hit semantics here.

related_files.relationship: FsCache is the concrete backend implementing the Cache union; Cache.tryCache and types (CachedMetadata, CacheRequest) are used by FsCache.put/get.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to CachedMetadata fields, CacheRequest, or CacheControl.parse require updates in FsCache serialization/deserialization and tests.

related_files.relationship: Primary caller that constructs CacheRequest and uses Cache.get/put when fetching resources.
related_files.reason_to_check: HttpClient must honor CacheRequest.timestamp and provide request headers used for Vary; changes to cache semantics affect HTTP-client caching behavior.

tests_to_run: zig build test (project-level) or make test

insights.title: Switch metadata serialization to JSON and add metadata versioning
insights.problem: Custom ad-hoc serialization was brittle and hard to evolve.
insights.root_cause: Flat text serialization was cumbersome to maintain and reject/repair on parse errors.
insights.solution: Use std.json to write/read a CacheMetadataFile struct (version + metadata). Added CACHE_VERSION and reject mismatched versions. Use arena allocation for parsing and handle parse failures by cleaning up files.
insights.lesson_learned: Use a structured, versioned format for on-disk metadata to allow future changes and safer parsing; always handle parse failures by cleaning up stale/invalid cache files.

insights.title: Check entry expiration (age) on cache get
insights.problem: Expired cache entries were being served because get used current time instead of stored timestamp or didn't check age properly.
insights.root_cause: get did not compare stored_at + age_at_store against now using a timestamp provided by caller; earlier logic used std.time.timestamp() in callers, making tests/time-based behavior non-deterministic.
insights.solution: Require timestamp in CacheRequest and use req.timestamp for age calculations; delete expired files and return null.
insights.lesson_learned: Cache expiry must be computed deterministically from a provided timestamp (useful for testing) and expired files should be removed to avoid serving stale content.

insights.title: Add striped locks for filesystem cache operations
insights.problem: Parallel cache operations (get/put) on same FS could cause races/corruption.
insights.root_cause: No locking around per-key file operations meant concurrent puts/gets could conflict (rename/create/delete races).
insights.solution: Add an array of std.Thread.Mutex locks (LOCK_STRIPES) and use a hashed stripe to lock per-key critical sections in get and put. Use Wyhash (and later masking) to index stripes.
insights.lesson_learned: Striped locks provide a balance between concurrency and simplicity for per-key file operations; ensure hash -> stripe computation is consistent and efficient.

insights.title: Support HTTP Vary header matching
insights.problem: Responses using Vary were cached but cache lookups did not account for request-specific headers, causing incorrect cache hits.
insights.root_cause: Cache metadata didn't store the request header values used by Vary; FsCache.get didn't compare incoming request headers to stored vary headers.
insights.solution: Store vary_headers (a list of request headers used by Vary) in CachedMetadata.vary_headers; on get, compare incoming request_headers with stored vary header values (case-insensitive) and return null if mismatch.
insights.lesson_learned: Implementing Vary requires storing the subset of request headers used by the response (and their values) and matching them on lookups; mismatches must be treated as cache misses.

insights.title: Switch cache key hashing from Wyhash to SHA256 hex
insights.problem: Wyhash 64-bit hex key risked collisions and resulted in short key length for file names.
insights.root_cause: Wyhash produced a numeric value formatted into a short 16-character hex; collisions are possible and not ideal for file path uniqueness.
insights.solution: Compute SHA-256 digest of the URL and use its hex representation (64 hex chars) for hashed file names. This reduces collision probability at the cost of CPU.
insights.lesson_learned: For on-disk keys where collisions are problematic, prefer a cryptographic hash like SHA-256 even if slightly slower; include collision handling checks as a safety net.

insights.title: Use buffered writers and flush for file writes
insights.problem: Direct writeAll was used for body writes; metadata writer code duplicated buffers.
insights.root_cause: Less consistent usage of writer/flush patterns led to duplicate local buffers and missing flushes.
insights.solution: Use writer interfaces for both meta and body files, call writeAll through the interface, and flush before closing. Consolidate writer buffer usage.
insights.lesson_learned: Use std.fs.File.writer and its interface consistently and flush before close to ensure data durability; reuse a writer buffer where possible.

insights.title: Ensure atomic rename and cleanup of temp files
insights.problem: Temporary .tmp files could be left behind after failures during put, and renames needed correct ordering and cleanup.
insights.root_cause: Original serialization/rename flow had manual paths and error handling spread out, increasing chance of leftover temp files.
insights.solution: Write meta/body to .tmp files while holding locks, use errdefer to delete tmp files on error, then rename tmp files to final names atomically, and remove old files on parse/version mismatch.
insights.lesson_learned: When writing multi-file cache entries, write to temp files and rename atomically while ensuring proper cleanup on all failure paths (use errdefer/defer).

insights.title: Add deinit and cache accessor on Cache wrapper
insights.problem: Network didn't properly deinit the cache and close underlying resources.
insights.root_cause: Cache kind union lacked a deinit wrapper and FsCache had no exported deinit accessor.
insights.solution: Add Cache.deinit wrapper and FsCache.deinit; Network.deinit now calls cache.deinit().
insights.lesson_learned: Provide deinit functions on abstracted resources (union kinds) so callers can cleanly deinit without depending on concrete implementation details.

insights.title: Filesystem-backed cache with atomic writes and hashed keys
insights.problem: Need durable cache storage for responses; simple in-memory cache isn't sufficient for persistence between runs.
insights.root_cause: No persistent cache implementation existed.
insights.solution: Add FsCache implementing get/put using hashed keys, .meta/.body files, writing to temp files then atomic rename, deserializing metadata and headers. Implements serializeMeta/deserializeMeta and uses allocator for duplications.
insights.lesson_learned: When writing persistent cache files, write to temporary files then rename atomically; include metadata versioning considerations for future format changes.

insights.title: Robust deserialization and cleanup on errors
insights.problem: Partial or corrupted meta/body files could cause panics or inconsistent cache state.
insights.root_cause: Deserialization can fail; previous code did not always clean up on failures.
insights.solution: On deserialize errors remove meta/body orphan files and return null; use errdefer to free memory and clean up temp files during put.
insights.lesson_learned: Ensure deserialization is defensive: failed parses should remove corrupt artifacts to avoid repeated failures.

src/browser/HttpClient.zig.short_description: HTTP client used by the browser runtime to manage network transfers, caching, robots handling and request interception.
src/browser/HttpClient.zig.description:
This file implements the high-level HTTP client (Client) used by the browser runtime (Pages, ScriptManager, XHR, Fetch). It wraps the lower-level libcurl-based network primitives (network/http.zig) and the project's caching and robots logic to provide a single place where requests are queued, performed, possibly intercepted by CDP, and routed back to callers via a Response abstraction. The Client is intentionally tied to the browser lifecycle: it's designed to be reused across pages for connection reuse (keepalive) but can abort per-frame or globally.

Design decisions visible in this source: centralization of configuration (Client stores max_response_size, http_proxy, tls_verify, obey_robots), pooling (transfer_pool), queueing of requests when libcurl easies are exhausted (queue), and handling of request interception/blocking so CDP integrations can pause/resume transfers. The file defers low-level I/O into http.Handles/Connection/Easy (network/http.zig) while taking responsibility for higher-level concerns such as cache lookup/serve (Cache), robots.txt fetching with a per-robots-url queue, constructing headers, and replaying buffered callbacks (header/data/done) in a controlled processing phase rather than directly from libcurl callbacks.

Many non-obvious behaviors and historical fixes are encoded in the implementation: transfer lifecycle must be careful when deinit is triggered from within libcurl callbacks (kill marks aborted and no-ops callbacks if currently performing), caching and header collection are carefully ordered to avoid lifetime issues (pending CacheMetadata is allocated), and connection bookkeeping switched from relying on libcurl private pointers to trackConn/removeConn due to curl_easy_reset semantics. There are also many edge-case accommodations: CONNECT proxy auth detection, special-casing certain libcurl RecvError conditions when headers indicate connection-close, and separating websocket vs HTTP transports inside Connection.transport. When editing this file one must be mindful of these sequencing and lifetime invariants because they were written to avoid races, FD leaks, double free/use-after-free from C callbacks, and cache races with callbacks that can re-enter the browser.

key_constructs.name: Client
key_constructs.purpose: Primary HTTP client struct encapsulating handles, queues, pools and runtime config used for network transfers.
key_constructs.reasoning: All page-level network operations (script fetch, XHR, navigation) route through this; changes to fields and invariants must preserve connection tracking, queueing and abort semantics.

key_constructs.name: CDPClient
key_constructs.purpose: Small struct allowing libcurl to also poll CDP sockets and to call blocking_read callbacks during request interception/blocking flows.
key_constructs.reasoning: This binds the client with the CDP message loop: altering read semantics or blocking callbacks affects request interception and parser-blocking script handling.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Constructs and initializes a Client instance with allocator and Network reference, populating config fields.
key_constructs.reasoning: Initialization sets up transfer_pool, handles and extracts network.config values (proxy, tls verify, max_response_size). Any edit to field defaults/config extraction must preserve how these are read from Network.config.

key_constructs.name: deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Cleans up client state: aborts transfers, deinitializes handles and transfer_pool, clears robots pending queues and frees allocator resources.
key_constructs.reasoning: Deinit must run in a controlled order: abort transfers first, then deinit handles, and free per-robots data. Changing the order can leak FDs or call callbacks with freed resources.

key_constructs.name: tick
key_constructs.purpose: Attempts to move queued requests onto available Connections and then calls perform to run libcurl event loop with a timeout.
key_constructs.reasoning: Entry point used by event loop to drive queued requests; it must maintain queue invariants and ensure new transfers are started only when connections are available.

key_constructs.name: request
key_constructs.purpose: Top-level API to submit a Request: handles robots.txt gating (fetch once semantics) and delegates to processRequest or fetchRobotsThenProcessRequest.
key_constructs.reasoning: High-level callers (Page.navigate, XHR, ScriptManager) call this. It preserves robots caching semantics and queueing; changes affect allow/block decisions.

key_constructs.name: processRequest
key_constructs.purpose: Handles cache lookup (GET only), creates Transfer, dispatches interception notifications, and either proceeds to process the network transfer or waits for interception resolution.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is a critical crossroads: caching fast-path, interception handling (blocking vs non-blocking) and transfer creation happen here. Ordering matters (collect headers only when caching), and the function interacts with many other subsystems via Request callbacks.

key_constructs.name: serveFromCache
key_constructs.purpose: Replays a cached response to the Request callbacks, supporting both in-memory buffers and file-backed cached bodies and ensuring files are closed on all paths.
key_constructs.reasoning: Must guarantee file FDs are closed on every exit path and that callbacks receive a Response wrapper rather than a Transfer pointer; lifetime handling is delicate.

key_constructs.name: fetchRobotsThenProcessRequest
