related_files.relationship: Provides Page type and navigation API used by pageTest and runWebApiTest to create pages and navigate to test URLs.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to Page.navigate or JS local scope semantics requires corresponding harness updates to runner.tick, TryCatch handling and navigation wait logic.

related_files.relationship: Session manages page lifecycle and exposes runner/tick used by the polling loop in runWebApiTest.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to runner.tick return values, timing semantics or session.page lifecycle will impact test waiting and timeouts.

related_files.relationship: JS binding layer used to create Local scopes, TryCatch, execute testing.assertOk() and convert JS exceptions into diagnostics.
related_files.reason_to_check: If JS execution API changes (return types, exception propagation), runWebApiTest's TryCatch usage and error reporting must be adjusted.

related_files.relationship: Local HTTP server used to serve test HTML files referenced by htmlRunner and pageTest.
related_files.reason_to_check: URL construction and server port assumptions (127.0.0.1:9582) are used directly; server startup/shutdown changes affect htmlRunner behavior.

related_files.relationship: Test WebSocket server used by web-api tests; tests raise ws_max_concurrent in config to avoid connection limits.
related_files.reason_to_check: Adjusting ws behavior or concurrency limits requires aligning test config and possibly harness defaults.

related_files.relationship: Test-specific config (test_config) is initialized here in beforeAll; contains fields like ws_max_concurrent used to avoid resource limits during tests.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifying config fields or defaults used by the test harness could alter test capacity and concurrency; tests rely on the harness setting fields like ws_max_concurrent = 50.

data_constants_to_check: hardcoded test server URL and port (http://127.0.0.1:9582)

insights.title: Increase test websocket concurrency default
insights.problem: Tests open many websockets in parallel; default ws max could be too low in test environment.
insights.root_cause: New ws_max_concurrent default is small; tests set it higher to avoid artificial failures.
insights.solution: Set ws_max_concurrent = 50 in testing config so tests don't hit websocket concurrency limit.
insights.lesson_learned: Test harness should override resource limits to reflect test concurrency; document when changing test-only defaults.

insights.title: Make pageTest accept options to control wait behavior
insights.problem: pageTest unconditionally waited for navigation to complete, which made some tests implicitly slow or harder to orchestrate.
insights.root_cause: Hard-coded wait semantics in test helpers reduced flexibility.
insights.solution: Add PageTestOpts with wait_until_done: bool and change pageTest signature to accept opts so callers can skip default wait when desired (commit ab6c63b).
insights.lesson_learned: Expose lightweight test helper options instead of embedding implicit waits to make tests more explicit and faster when appropriate.

insights.title: Improve async test support and runner loop to avoid flaky tests
insights.problem: The previous testing.async(...) helper only worked for very quick microtasks and couldn't reliably keep tests alive while asynchronous tasks completed, causing flaky test failures.
insights.root_cause: testing.async returned control prematurely and the test runner didn't tick/wait until asynchronous work was actually complete; there was no robust coordination between JS async tasks and the Zig test runner tick loop.
insights.solution: Added an overload of testing.async() that returns a state object with a promise/resolve/done API; incremented async_pending and ensure the test runner (runWebApiTest) repeatedly ticks the session runner and checks testing.assertOk() until it reports completion or times out. Modified runWebApiTest to loop, call session.runner.tick, sleep where appropriate, and respect a timeout.
insights.lesson_learned: Test harnesses that drive an embedded JS runtime must explicitly coordinate microtask queues and asynchronous promise resolution with the host test runner; provide an API that can block the test until async work finishes and make runner tick loops explicit.

test_functions: web API tests that use testing.async() (frames tests, etc.)

insights.title: Test runner ticks until testing.assertOk() or timeout, integrating async waits
insights.problem: runWebApiTest could return early or not wait correctly for asynchronous test conditions.
insights.root_cause: Test harness didn't tick the Runner and re-check JavaScript assertion state until async counters or promises were resolved.
insights.solution: Change runWebApiTest to loop ticking runner.tick(), call js exec testing.assertOk() each iteration and wait until it returns true or the runner signals done/timeout. This integrates with the new testing.async promise pattern.

src/cdp/domains/page.zig.short_description: Implements the CDP Page domain: command dispatch, navigation, lifecycle events, and page-scoped CDP events.
src/cdp/domains/page.zig.description:
This file implements the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) "Page" domain for Lightpanda. It translates incoming CDP commands into browser actions (navigate, reload, create isolated execution worlds, add/remove scripts to run on new documents, close target, capture screenshots, layout metrics, and dialog handling) and emits Page-related CDP events back to connected clients. It is the protocol bridge that maps CDP method calls and expected event ordering semantics onto the runtime browser machinery (BrowserContext, Session, Page, isolated worlds and JS execution contexts).

Design choices visible in the file include: a central processMessage() dispatcher that maps a textual action name to a Zig enum and then into handler functions; careful lifetime management around arena allocators when pages are replaced or navigations occur; explicit handling of session attachment/detachment (session_id checks) so events are not emitted when no client is attached; and preservation of event ordering and semantics required by drivers (Puppeteer/Playwright) by sending certain navigate results and page lifecycle events at specific moments. The file also stores and executes "scripts on new document" at the BrowserContext level (persisting identifiers and sources) and evaluates them in newly-created JS contexts during navigation.

This module interacts heavily with other subsystems: it reads/writes BrowserContext state (scripts, lifecycle flags, session IDs), uses Notification.* structures to react to internal page lifecycle notifications (pageNavigated, pageDOMContentLoaded, pageLoaded, javascriptDialogOpening, etc.), calls into Page and isolated-world APIs to create contexts and evaluate scripts, and uses CDP helpers to serialize/send events and results. Because compatibility with external drivers depends on exact timing and error semantics, the code contains many checks (e.g., return specific CDP errors when session_id is missing or when no dialog exists) and defensive behaviors (log-and-continue when injected user scripts throw) learned from prior bugs and commits.

key_constructs.name: processMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Entry point: dispatch a CDP page domain command (by action name) to the correct handler function.
key_constructs.reasoning: All Page domain commands funnel through this mapping; changes here affect which actions are supported and how unknown actions are classified. Maintain the exact action enum strings to preserve protocol compatibility.

key_constructs.name: Frame
key_constructs.purpose: Struct template used to construct Page.frame objects emitted in Page events (frameNavigated, getFrameTree, etc.).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used repeatedly when serializing frame state to CDP clients; fields must match the protocol expectations for types and default values.

key_constructs.name: getFrameTree
key_constructs.purpose: Handle Page.getFrameTree and return the current frame tree for the BrowserContext/target.
key_constructs.reasoning: Clients expect a valid frame tree even for unattached targets; the function returns a startup default if context/target are missing and otherwise builds a Frame using BrowserContext properties.

key_constructs.name: setLifecycleEventsEnabled
key_constructs.purpose: Enable/disable Page lifecycle events for the BrowserContext and dispatch immediate lifecycle events for current pages when enabling.
key_constructs.reasoning: Must flip BC flags and, when enabling, emit DOMContentLoaded/load/networkIdle/networkAlmostIdle events for attached pages per CDP semantics.

key_constructs.name: addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument
key_constructs.purpose: Register a script (source) on the BrowserContext that will be evaluated in every new document's execution context.
key_constructs.reasoning: Creates stable numeric identifiers, duplicates the script source into the BrowserContext's arena and appends to scripts_on_new_document storage; runImmediately is logged as unimplemented and should be respected as a no-op in this implementation.

key_constructs.name: removeScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument
key_constructs.purpose: Remove a previously-registered new-document script by identifier.
key_constructs.reasoning: Parses the identifier and removes the stored entry; returning success even when identifier is not present mirrors upstream CDP behavior.

key_constructs.name: close
key_constructs.purpose: Close the target/attached page for the BrowserContext and emit Inspector/Target detached events if a session was attached.
key_constructs.reasoning: Per CDP semantics, must send a result then perform cleanup of session/page/isolation state; handles cases where session_id may be null.

key_constructs.name: createIsolatedWorld
key_constructs.purpose: Create an isolated execution world (JS context) with optional universal access for a given frame.
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposes isolated-world creation via CDP; grantUniveralAccess is presently warned as not fully implemented when false, and the created context id is returned to the client.

key_constructs.name: navigate
key_constructs.purpose: Initiate navigation for the current page in the session (handles push navigations and creation of a new page if needed).
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates session attachment, ensures page is replaced if not waiting, encodes URL and calls Page.navigate with NavigationKind.push and the input CDP id so a later notification can return the navigate result at the right time.

key_constructs.name: doReload
key_constructs.purpose: Handle Page.reload: duplicate current page URL safely then call Page.navigate with NavigationKind.reload and optional cache-ignore flag.
key_constructs.reasoning: Duplicates the URL into the command arena before calling replacePage() to avoid use-after-free when page arenas are replaced; respects ignoreCache parameter if present.

key_constructs.name: pageNavigate
key_constructs.purpose: Emit Page-level events (frameStarted/Requested/ScheduledNavigation) in response to an internal Notification.PageNavigate.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function maps internal navigation notifications into the sequence of CDP events that clients (drivers) expect when a navigation is scheduled/started; it also resets BrowserContext transient state and guards against detached sessions.

key_constructs.name: pageRemove
key_constructs.purpose: Called when a page is removed: clear remote object mappings and remove contexts from isolated worlds.
key_constructs.reasoning: Prevents stale objectIds by resetting inspector context groups and cleaning up isolated-world execution contexts before main page removal.

key_constructs.name: pageCreated
key_constructs.purpose: Called when a new page is created: reset page-related CDP arena, create contexts for existing isolated worlds, and clear captured responses cache.
key_constructs.reasoning: Prepares the BrowserContext and CDP helper arena for a fresh page and ensures persistent isolated worlds are re-created for the page.

key_constructs.name: pageFrameCreated
key_constructs.purpose: Emit Page.frameAttached and optionally Page.lifecycleEvent('init') when a child frame or main frame is created.
key_constructs.reasoning: Translates internal frame creation notifications into CDP events and respects the page_life_cycle_events flag for lifecycle event emission.

key_constructs.name: pageNavigated
key_constructs.purpose: Emit the Page.frameNavigated event, send the saved result for an earlier Page.navigate call (if present), and handle execution context resets and lifecycle event emission on navigation commit.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the central mapping point for navigation-commit semantics: it must emit the navigate result (idempotently and in the right order), send Runtime.executionContextsCleared for main frame navigations, then emit Page.frameNavigated and subsequent lifecycle events in the order expected by external clients.

key_constructs.name: handleJavaScriptDialog
key_constructs.purpose: Handle CDP method Page.handleJavaScriptDialog to accept/dismiss a pending JS dialog and optionally send a prompt text.
key_constructs.reasoning: CDP clients expect a success or a specific error when no dialog is showing (commit history shows returning a -32000 error with message 'No dialog is showing' in headless cases). This function must respect that behavior and the platform's headless auto-dismiss semantics.

key_behaviors: dispatches CDP Page domain commands to handlers
registers and executes scripts on new document
initiates and coordinates page navigations and reloads
emits ordered Page.* CDP events (frameNavigated, lifecycle events, domContentEventFired, loadEventFired)
manages isolated worlds and execution contexts per page
handles JavaScript dialogs and reports errors when none are present

pitfalls.mistake: Referencing page-owned strings or structures after calling session.replacePage() or other code that frees/replaces the page arena.
pitfalls.consequence: Use-after-free memory corruption or crashes at runtime.
pitfalls.prevention: Duplicate any page-owned strings (e.g., URL) into a safe allocator (cmd.arena.dupeZ or similar) before replacing or freeing the page.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the ordering or timing of CDP events emitted during navigation (e.g., sending lifecycle events before frameNavigated or sending Navigate result at the wrong time).
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks compatibility with clients (Puppeteer/Playwright) which rely on precise event ordering; may cause hanging or incorrect behavior in drivers.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the current ordering: send saved navigate result at commit time, then send executionContextsCleared (only for main frame), then frameNavigated, then lifecycle/domContent/load events as implemented.

pitfalls.mistake: Silently accepting handleJavaScriptDialog calls when no dialog is showing.
pitfalls.consequence: Clients cannot detect that the call had no effect; diverges from expected CDP semantics and previous bug fixes.
pitfalls.prevention: Return a CDP error (-32000) with message "No dialog is showing" when appropriate, matching historical commits and tests.

pitfalls.mistake: Evaluating scripts-on-new-document without proper Try/Catch and logging.
pitfalls.consequence: Uncaught JS exceptions could abort navigation or make debugging hard because exception details are missing.
pitfalls.prevention: Wrap evaluations in try/catch and log exception details (caughtOrError) while suppressing the error so navigation proceeds.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying BrowserContext state without handling detached/no-session cases (e.g., assuming session_id exists).
pitfalls.consequence: Attempting to send events with a null session_id or operating on a detached context may be a no-op or cause inconsistent state.
pitfalls.prevention: Honor existing null checks (session_id orelse return) and ensure events are only sent when a session is attached.

key_sections: processMessage: command dispatch mapping to functions (start here to understand supported CDP methods)
addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument / removeScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument: script storage and identifier semantics
navigate and doReload: how navigations are initiated and how CDP input ids are threaded into notifications
pageNavigate / pageNavigated / pageDOMContentLoaded / pageLoaded: event mapping and ordering that clients depend on
skip_unless_needed: low-level event JSON construction details used only when adjusting exact CDP payload shapes
logging boilerplate and small helper branches that merely wrap error cases

related_files.relationship: CDP message and helper types used to send results/events; processMessage consumes CDP.Command and uses CDP.cdp send helpers.
related_files.reason_to_check: If you change event shapes, error codes, or how session_id is attached, validate CDP helper serialization and command parsing.

related_files.relationship: Runtime Page abstraction that actually performs navigate(), replacePage(), holds _frame_id, _req_id and load state referenced by this module.
related_files.reason_to_check: Edits to navigation lifecycle or arena ownership in Page.zig impact how page.zig must duplicate or reference page data.

related_files.relationship: Defines Notification.* structures used as inputs to pageNavigate/pageLoaded/pageDOMContentLoaded etc.
related_files.reason_to_check: If internal notification shapes or timings change, the mapping into CDP events in this file must be updated accordingly.

related_files.relationship: Used to ensure and encode URLs before navigation.
related_files.reason_to_check: If URL encoding/normalization changes, navigation flow tests and expected resulting URLs in events might need adjustment.

related_files.relationship: JS evaluation helpers used when executing scripts-on-new-document and creating execution contexts.
related_files.reason_to_check: If JS execution semantics change (exceptions, contexts), keep the try/catch and logging behavior aligned.

data_constants_to_check: src/cdp/CDP.zig:URL_BASE (used as default securityOrigin in getFrameTree)
BrowserContext.next_script_id and scripts_on_new_document storage (review places that persist identifiers)

insights.title: Emit Page.javascriptDialogOpening for JS dialogs
insights.problem: Clients (Puppeteer page.on('dialog')) couldn't observe JS dialogs because CDP events weren't emitted.
insights.root_cause: Window.alert/confirm/prompt didn't generate CDP notifications; the CDP layer didn't forward such notifications.
insights.solution: Add Notification javascript_dialog_opening, CDP registration and forwarding function javascriptDialogOpening() to emit Page.javascriptDialogOpening. Also add handleJavaScriptDialog method.
insights.lesson_learned: CDP compatibility requires explicit mapping of browser runtime notifications to CDP events; ensure event lifecycle (open vs handle) is aligned with headless behavior.

insights.title: Return explicit error when handleJavaScriptDialog has no effect in headless mode
insights.problem: handleJavaScriptDialog previously was a silent no-op (acknowledgement) when there's no pending dialog, giving no feedback to CDP clients.
insights.root_cause: Dialogs auto-dismiss in headless mode, so by the time the CDP client calls the method there's nothing to act on; code returned success instead of an error.
insights.solution: Return a CDP error (-32000) with message "No dialog is showing" to inform clients that the action had no effect.
insights.lesson_learned: CDP methods should return meaningful error responses when the requested action cannot be performed so clients can detect no-op outcomes.

insights.title: Reorder page navigation lifecycle and split events
insights.problem: CDP Page events (frameNavigated, domContentEventFired, loadEventFired, lifecycle events) were not emitted in the order clients expect (Chrome's order). page_navigated was emitted too late (on document load) causing client-visible differences.
insights.root_cause: The heavy internal page_navigated notification was emitted on document complete; clients expect frameNavigated to be emitted earlier (on header response/commit).
insights.solution: Send page_navigated at header response time, add explicit page_dom_content_loaded & page_loaded handlers that emit Page.domContentEventFired, Page.loadEventFired and lifecycle events. Emit Page.frameNavigated on navigation commit. Update BC registration to listen to the new notifications (commit 752184b and follow-ups).
insights.lesson_learned: Protocol compatibility often requires careful event timing; refactor internal notifications to map one-to-one to protocol events and ensure they are dispatched at the same logical moments as upstream implementations.

insights.title: Better error reporting for addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument runImmediately and script execution
insights.problem: Scripts scheduled to run on new document errors were logged with limited context and runImmediately param was unimplemented without feedback.
insights.root_cause: Execution of script_on_new_document could fail and only generic logs were emitted; runImmediately parameter was silently ignored.
insights.solution: Log a not_implemented warning when runImmediately is used. Wrap ls.local.eval(script.source, null) in TryCatch and when it errors, use try_catch.caughtOrError(arena, err) to include exception details in logs.
insights.lesson_learned: When running untrusted or user-supplied scripts, capture full exception details for easier debugging. Warn on unimplemented params to surface unsupported API usage.

insights.title: Implement Page.addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument and removal
insights.problem: CDP Page.addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument was previously a hardcoded stub returning identifier "1" and did not persist or evaluate scripts across navigations.
insights.root_cause: No storage for registered scripts and no evaluation step in the navigation lifecycle; the stub returned a constant id and did not integrate with BrowserContext or navigation events.
insights.solution: Added ScriptOnNewDocument storage on BrowserContext, generate unique numeric identifiers, store script source (arena.dupe), implemented Page.addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument and Page.removeScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument, and evaluate registered scripts inside pageNavigated after the execution context is created but before frameNavigated/loadEventFired. Evaluation errors are logged (warn) and suppressed so navigation isn't broken.
insights.lesson_learned: Persisted CDP injections must be stored in the browser context and executed at the correct lifecycle point (after execution context creation, before notifying clients). Return stable unique identifiers per spec, and avoid letting injected-script errors break navigation — log and continue.

insights.title: Copy URL safely during reload
insights.problem: doReload copied page.url into a fixed-size stack buffer, risking truncation or misuse and manual memory handling.
insights.root_cause: URL was copied into a stack buffer prior to replacePage() freeing the old arena which is error-prone and fragile.
insights.solution: Use cmd.arena.dupeZ to duplicate the URL into the command's arena before calling replacePage(), ensuring lifetime safety.
insights.lesson_learned: Use the request/command arena helpers (dupeZ) to duplicate strings that must outlive page arena changes instead of manual stack buffers.

insights.title: Implement Page.reload CDP method and prevent use-after-free on page replacement
insights.problem: Clients calling CDP Page.reload received UnknownMethod because reload was not implemented. Additionally, calling replacePage() could free the old page arena while code still referenced page.url leading to a use-after-free.
insights.root_cause: Page.reload wasn't implemented in the Page domain dispatch. The reload flow reused the navigate path but did not account for arena-freed memory when replacing a page instance.
insights.solution: Added doReload handler wired into Page domain dispatch that reuses page.navigate with NavigationKind.reload and copies the current page URL onto a stack buffer before calling replacePage() to avoid referencing freed arena memory.
insights.lesson_learned: When replacing objects that live in arena allocators, copy any needed data to a safe storage (stack or persistent allocator) before freeing/replacing the arena-owned object. Also, implement CDP methods expected by clients to avoid compatibility regressions.

src/browser/ScriptManager.zig.short_description: Manages downloading, queuing, and execution lifecycle of <script> tags and ES module imports for a Page
src/browser/ScriptManager.zig.description:
ScriptManager is the per-Page component that implements the browser's script loading and execution semantics: inline scripts, external scripts (sync/blocking), async scripts, deferred scripts, and ES module imports (sync and async). It exists to encapsulate all logic required to schedule downloads via the shared HttpClient, handle HTTP callbacks, resolve import specifiers (including import maps), maintain ordering guarantees between different script kinds, and coordinate V8 evaluation reentrancy (via is_evaluating). ScriptManager relies on Page for arena allocation, base URL/cookie origin/headers, and on HttpClient to perform network requests. It is responsible for keeping temporary data structures (linked lists for defer/async/ready scripts and a hash map for imported modules) and for cleaning them up at page teardown or navigation.

Design choices visible in the implementation: scripts are represented as Script objects allocated in the page arena; different execution modes (.normal, .async, .defer, .import, .import_async) determine queuing and ordering behavior; blocking scripts yield an explicit wait loop that calls client.tick while waiting for completion; imported modules are cached in imported_modules and may be shared among waiters; importmap entries are stored in importmap to perform specifier resolution prior to URL resolution. The file uses an is_evaluating boolean guard to avoid recursive evaluations caused when callbacks can synchronously evaluate a script (for example when the HTTP client returns from cache synchronously). The module interacts closely with Page, HttpClient, URL resolution utilities, and the JS subsystem (js/js.zig) for debug instrumentation.

key_constructs.name: ScriptManager
key_constructs.purpose: Top-level struct (alias @This()) that holds script queues, imported module state, flags, allocator, and HttpClient reference
key_constructs.reasoning: ScriptManager is the canonical per-Page script runtime; editors must preserve field semantics (arenas, lists, imported_modules, importmap, is_evaluating, static_scripts_done) because other code depends on these invariants.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Constructs a ScriptManager with default empty lists/maps and attaches the Page and HttpClient references
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when creating a Page runtime; yields an object whose allocator is the page arena and which expects imported_modules and importmap to be manipulated with that allocator.

key_constructs.name: deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Tears down ScriptManager: calls reset(), releases imported_modules, but purposely does not deinit importmap because importmap entries use the page arena
key_constructs.reasoning: Deinit must be consistent with page arena lifecycle: it frees structures that own allocator-managed memory but avoids double-freeing memory owned by the page's arena.

key_constructs.name: reset
key_constructs.purpose: Cleans up imported_modules contents, clears importmap, empties script linked lists, and resets static_scripts_done
key_constructs.reasoning: Called on navigation or when page arenas are reset; must avoid using the page arena after it's been reset (uses clearRetainingCapacity and reinitializes importmap to empty).

key_constructs.name: clearList
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that pops and deinitializes all Script nodes from a std.DoublyLinkedList
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to drain defer/async/ready queues; keeps list node removal and Script.deinit pairing consistent.

key_constructs.name: getHeaders
key_constructs.purpose: Creates http.Headers via the HttpClient and populates them with Page-provided headers for a request
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralized header assembly to ensure cookie headers and other request headers come from the Page. Implementations of requests must call this and set cookie_origin = page.url separately.

key_constructs.name: addFromElement
key_constructs.purpose: Main entry to process a <script> Html element: decides kind (javascript/module/importmap), determines inline vs remote, sets the Script.mode (.normal/.async/.defer), queues the Script, issues network request when needed, and if blocking, waits for completion and evaluates
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the most complex and critical flow: it must respect HTML semantics for async/defer/module/default dynamic insertion, set script_element._executed only when content is present, and ensure is_evaluating is set around client.request to guard against synchronous cache returns causing reentrancy.

key_constructs.name: scriptList
key_constructs.purpose: Maps a Script.mode to the corresponding DoublyLinkedList (defer or async) or unreachable for normal (blocking) scripts
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to centralize which list a non-blocking script should be appended to; important to keep consistent with how scripts are queued and drained.

key_constructs.name: resolveSpecifier
key_constructs.purpose: Resolves an ES module import specifier: checks importmap first, otherwise resolves relative to base via URL.resolve
key_constructs.reasoning: Implements importmap semantics (importmap entries are authoritative for mapped specifiers). Editors must preserve the behavior that importmap lookup returns the resolved URL without reallocation unless always_dupe is requested by URL.resolve.

key_constructs.name: preloadImport
key_constructs.purpose: Starts preloading a module URL: inserts a placeholder in imported_modules, creates a Script in .import mode, appends to async_scripts, and issues a GET to the HttpClient
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to satisfy v8's module graph preloading: the map entry keeps waiters and the buffer; returned content can be synchronously available for waiters. Must maintain the imported_modules state machine and increment/decrement waiters appropriately.

key_constructs.name: waitForImport
key_constructs.purpose: When v8 asks for a module, waits (calling client.tick) until the imported_modules entry transitions to done and returns ModuleSource, handling the waiters counter and shared buffer semantics
key_constructs.reasoning: This function uses is_evaluating guard while waiting, must operate correctly if the imported module is already in done state, and must remove the imported_modules entry when last waiter consumes it.

key_constructs.name: getAsyncImport
key_constructs.purpose: Requests an asynchronous import: allocates a Script with mode import_async providing a callback and cb_data, issues a GET, and queues the script for later completion handling
key_constructs.reasoning: This path is used by dynamic import() that resolves asynchronously; it uses the same HttpClient callbacks as other script fetches and relies on the Script.done/error/data callbacks to invoke the provided ImportAsync.Callback.

key_behaviors: queues scripts by HTML semantics (inline, blocking, defer, async)
issues HTTP GET requests for external scripts and wires HttpClient callbacks
preloads and serves ES module imports with waiters and shared buffers
ensures V8 evaluation reentrancy is prevented via is_evaluating guard
resolves module specifiers using importmap and URL.resolve

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying is_evaluating state or removing the guard around client.request calls
pitfalls.consequence: Synchronous cache or fast-path returns from HttpClient may cause immediate evaluation and reentrancy, leading to inconsistent state (double execution) or corrupted flags
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the pattern that wraps client.request with saving/restoring is_evaluating as shown in addFromElement, preloadImport, waitForImport, and other request-triggering functions.

pitfalls.mistake: Using the page arena after the page has reset or been released
pitfalls.consequence: Use-after-free or memory corruption because Script objects and importmap entries are allocated in the page arena and reset/deinit expects to handle that carefully
pitfalls.prevention: Respect errdefer handlers that release arenas, use imported_modules.clearRetainingCapacity() where appropriate, and don't call clearAndRetainCapacity on structures whose allocator memory has been freed.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the signatures or expectations of HttpClient callbacks without updating how Script.*Callback functions handle the new Response abstraction
pitfalls.consequence: Log and debug code may attempt to access Transfer internals that are no longer directly available, and callback code may fail to extract status/contentType/contentLength, causing incorrect script completion handling
pitfalls.prevention: When editing callback-related code, check HttpClient.zig changes (Response vs Transfer) and the commit history notes about startCallback/headerCallback/dataCallback adaptations; maintain conversion code where necessary and keep debug access to underlying transfer guarded.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering how script_element._executed is set (e.g., setting it before ensuring content exists)
pitfalls.consequence: Scripts might be skipped incorrectly (missing execution) or executed twice if dynamically added vs parser paths are inconsistent
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the exact sequence: determine source (inline or remote), only set _executed when you have content to execute, and for inline empty content return without marking executed.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing linked list handling (append/remove) semantics for non-blocking scripts without updating drains and cleanup
pitfalls.consequence: Scripts may never execute or may leak memory and arenas (Script.deinit not called), or ordering guarantees for defer/async/ready scripts could be broken
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the use of std.DoublyLinkedList for defer/async/ready lists and the clearList helper to deinit each Script node on reset; keep scriptList mapping consistent with modes.

key_sections: addFromElement: central flow handling inline vs external scripts, mode selection, queueing, and blocking wait/evaluation (core behavior)
preloadImport / waitForImport: module preload and synchronous wait semantics for v8 module resolution
getAsyncImport: dynamic import() async path with callback plumbing
reset / deinit: arena and state cleanup semantics (important for navigation and avoiding use-after-free)
skip_unless_needed: debug logging branches guarded by IS_DEBUG (only relevant when running in Debug mode)
detailed implementation of Script.*Callback functions (unless modifying HttpClient callback interface)

tests.example_command: zig build test

related_files.relationship: ScriptManager issues requests through the shared HttpClient; callbacks and Response/Transfer shapes must match between the two files
related_files.reason_to_check: When modifying request parameters, callback handling, or Response/Transfer access patterns in ScriptManager, update HttpClient callback types and Response wrappers accordingly.

related_files.relationship: Defines http.Headers, Method, Handles, and Response/Transfer primitives used by HttpClient and ScriptManager
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to low-level networking types (Headers, Response) affect getHeaders, client.request usage, and callback expectations.

related_files.relationship: ScriptManager depends on Page for arena allocation, base URL, headersForRequest, cookie_jar, and lifecycle events
related_files.reason_to_check: Editing how arenas or headers are requested from Page, or renaming Page fields (e.g., cookie_jar, url, _frame_id) will break ScriptManager.

related_files.relationship: Used for debug instrumentation and for integration with V8 evaluation; ScriptManager must respect js local scope management when logging stack traces
related_files.reason_to_check: If debug logging or evaluation ordering is modified, check js/js.zig for expected APIs such as Local.Scope and local.stackTrace().

related_files.relationship: Used to resolve relative script import URLs and data URIs; resolveSpecifier and addFromElement rely on URL.resolve behavior
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes to URL.resolve signature or semantics (always_dupe, allocator requirements) must be reflected in resolveSpecifier and addFromElement.

data_constants_to_check: src/browser/Page.zig: arena allocation behavior and page.url/cookie_jar fields

insights.title: Protect against synchronous evaluation path when loading cached script
insights.problem: If a script is returned synchronously from cache, evaluate() might be called immediately while addFromElement was still in the middle of setting up state, causing reentrancy or inconsistent is_evaluating flag.
insights.root_cause: addFromElement didn't set is_evaluating true around the request call; synchronous cache hit could lead to immediate evaluation and reentrancy.
insights.solution: Wrap the client.request call with a block that sets is_evaluating = true and defers restoring the previous value so synchronous returns are safe.
insights.lesson_learned: When initiating operations that may complete synchronously (cache fast-path), set reentrancy guards around the call so callbacks running inline see consistent state.

insights.title: Adapt ScriptManager callbacks to Response abstraction
insights.problem: ScriptManager's callbacks previously expected Transfer and referenced Transfer internals; Response wrapper needs new handling.
insights.root_cause: Global change to use Response in HttpClient callbacks forces downstream consumers to adapt.
insights.solution: Change startCallback/headerCallback/dataCallback signatures to accept HttpClient.Response, update logging and extraction of status/contentType/contentLength via response methods, and switch on response.inner.transfer to access Transfer-specific debug fields when needed.
insights.lesson_learned: When changing a low-level callback type, adapt all consumers and keep a way to inspect underlying concrete transfer when necessary (e.g., for debug info).

insights.title: Rename net_http import to http
insights.problem: ScriptManager used nonstandard alias net_http for the http module.
insights.root_cause: Inconsistent module alias usage across the codebase.
insights.solution: Standardized the alias to http to match other modules (commit 0604056).
insights.lesson_learned: Consistent aliasing (e.g., http) across modules improves readability and reduces search friction.

insights.title: Pass cookie_origin and simplify header helper
insights.problem: Script loading paths needed to include cookie origin; getHeaders previously required passing an allocator and URL leading to duplicated code.
insights.root_cause: Header assembly logic was scattered and required caller-provided arena/time-limited storage.
insights.solution: Simplify getHeaders to not take an arena and call page.headersForRequest(&headers); propagate .cookie_origin = page.url for script/fetch/import requests.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep helper functions small and consistent with the page-level headers API; ensure cookie origin is set for all resource fetches originating from the page.

insights.title: Update debug instrumentation to reflect connection field rename
insights.problem: Debug fields referenced transfer._conn.easy which was changed to transfer._conn._easy in the connection refactor.
insights.root_cause: Connection struct changed during refactoring; script debug code still referenced old field.
insights.solution: Update debug instrumentation to reference c._easy where appropriate.
insights.lesson_learned: When renaming underlying fields ensure all debug/logging code is updated to avoid misleading diagnostics.

src/network/http.zig.short_description: Libcurl-backed connection and HTTP header helpers used by the browser network stack
src/network/http.zig.description:
This module is the low-level libcurl wrapper used by the higher-level HTTP client (src/browser/HttpClient.zig) and other network consumers (e.g., telemetry, WebSocket glue). It exists to centralize all curl interactions: creating/initializing curl easy handles, applying runtime configuration (timeouts, proxy, TLS), wiring read/write/header callbacks, and providing helpers to inspect response/connect metadata. The file also exposes small utilities (curl_version and several libcurl type aliases) and header iteration/parsing helpers used when translating libcurl headers into internal representations.

The design intentionally separates connection-level concerns from request-level state: Connection wraps a libcurl easy handle and provides methods to reset and reconfigure it according to a Config and optional CA blob. The Connection.Transport union exists so the same easy handle can be owned by different higher-level transports (HTTP Transfer, WebSocket), avoiding unsafe casting of curl's private pointer. Header handling is abstracted via Headers and HeaderIterator so callers can uniformly iterate headers coming either from a live curl easy handle or from an injected list (used for fulfilled/intercepted responses). ResponseHead contains small, in-place storage for content-type plus a mechanism to handle injected headers. AuthChallenge parsing is exposed to detect challenge scheme/realm for WWW-Authenticate/Proxy-Authenticate flows.

This file is closely coupled to src/browser/HttpClient.zig (consumer) and sys/libcurl.zig (bindings). Several methods and behaviors (e.g., Connection.reset, setTimeout, setMethod, setBody, setCallbacks, TLS/proxy behavior, header iteration) are relied upon by HttpClient's request lifecycle. The commit history shows iterative additions: per-connection timeout, websocket helpers, a Transport union, exposure of CONNECT-phase helpers, and a unified header iterator/collect API. Those historical changes explain some non-obvious APIs (e.g., getConnectHeader, getConnectCode, HeaderIterator.collect) and the expectations other modules have about Connection.reset semantics.

key_constructs.name: curl_version
key_constructs.purpose: Return the underlying libcurl version string pointer.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for diagnostics and logging; callers expect a C-string pointer from libcurl and rely on libcurl's lifetime.

key_constructs.name: Blob
key_constructs.purpose: Alias for libcurl.CurlBlob used for in-memory CA blobs.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ca blobs passed to Connection.init/reset are typed as Blob and forwarded to curl via the bindings.

key_constructs.name: Method
key_constructs.purpose: Enum representing HTTP methods used to set per-request custom_request behavior.
key_constructs.reasoning: Higher layers use this to set the header-level method string separately from curl's internal request-handling behavior.

key_constructs.name: Header
key_constructs.purpose: Simple name/value pair used to represent headers for injected responses or when collecting headers.
key_constructs.reasoning: Header is the lightweight representation used by HeaderIterator.collect and injected responses; it must be interoperable with headers read from curl.

key_constructs.name: Headers
key_constructs.purpose: Wrapper around a libcurl.CurlSList to build request header lists and manage lifetime.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when emitting request headers to curl; provides init/add/deinit to centralize memory management for curl_slist.

key_constructs.name: Headers.parseHeader
key_constructs.purpose: Parse 'Name: value' into a Header (name/value slices).
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposed because header sources can be raw libcurl data or injected strings; callers rely on trim and colon parsing behavior.

key_constructs.name: HeaderIterator
key_constructs.purpose: Abstract iterator over headers coming from curl easy handle, curl_slist, or an injected []const Header list.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a uniform next() API and a collect(allocator) helper so callers don't need to know the concrete header source.

key_constructs.name: HeaderIterator.collect
key_constructs.purpose: Collect headers into an ArrayList(Header) using the provided allocator.
key_constructs.reasoning: Higher-level code uses this to materialize headers when it needs owned memory (e.g., to send events or persist response metadata).

key_constructs.name: AuthChallenge
key_constructs.purpose: Representation of an auth challenge (WWW-Authenticate/Proxy-Authenticate) with parsed scheme/realm.
key_constructs.reasoning: Parsing is explicit about challenge source (server vs proxy) and limited to recognized schemes; callers expect parse() to return UnknownAuthChallengeScheme for unsupported schemes.

key_constructs.name: ResponseHead
key_constructs.purpose: Represents response metadata: status, effective URL pointer, redirect count and an in-place content-type buffer.
key_constructs.reasoning: Holds small, fast-access data for the immediate response; contentType() returns a slice if present; injected headers are stored separately and must be iterated via transfer.responseHeaderIterator().

key_constructs.name: Connection
key_constructs.purpose: Core wrapper around libcurl.Curl easy handle exposing configuration, callbacks, and info accessors.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates how curl options are set and how an easy handle is reset between uses; higher-level code depends on consistent reset behavior and the Transport union to manage diverse owners.

key_constructs.name: Connection.Transport
key_constructs.purpose: Tracks which higher-level owner currently uses the connection: none, http Transfer, or websocket.
key_constructs.reasoning: Avoids storing ambiguous pointers in curl's private and makes ownership explicit so callbacks can route context correctly.

key_constructs.name: Connection.init
key_constructs.purpose: Create and initialize a Connection (curl_easy_init + reset).
key_constructs.reasoning: Central entry that must ensure the curl easy handle is configured per-Config and optional CA blob; callers expect proper errdefer semantics and that reset succeeds.

key_constructs.name: Connection.reset
key_constructs.purpose: Reset the easy handle and re-apply runtime options: timeouts, compression, proxy, TLS, debug, etc.
key_constructs.reasoning: This enforces the invariant that every use of an easy handle begins from a known configuration; edits must preserve the ordering and the option values applied here.

key_constructs.name: Connection.setTimeout
key_constructs.purpose: Set per-connection timeout (CURLOPT_TIMEOUT_MS).
key_constructs.reasoning: Added to allow per-request timeouts (e.g., XHR), so consumers may call this to override the default configured timeout.

key_constructs.name: Connection.setMethod
key_constructs.purpose: Set the request method string (CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST) to control header's method string.
key_constructs.reasoning: The code comments explain the distinction between curl's behavioral method and the header method; callers must always set this per-usage to avoid reusing an unintended verb across reused easy handles.

key_constructs.name: Connection.setBody
key_constructs.purpose: Configure the easy handle for sending a request body (CURLOPT_POST, POSTFIELDS, POSTFIELDSIZE).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when uploading or sending POST/PUT bodies; it sets upload and copies fields per curl semantics.

key_constructs.name: Connection.setWriteCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Set the write (response data) callback and pass the Connection as user data.
key_constructs.reasoning: Higher-level transfers provide a comptime data callback; the connection ensures the correct write_data and write_function are set atomically.

key_constructs.name: Connection.setReadCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Set the read (upload) callback and optionally enable CURLOPT_UPLOAD.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for streaming uploads; if upload is true, upload option is enabled so curl will call the read callback.

key_constructs.name: Connection.setHeaderCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Set the header callback to receive individual header lines from curl.
key_constructs.reasoning: Header parsing is delegated to callbacks; the Connection passes itself as context so header callbacks can find their owning Transfer or Transport.

key_constructs.name: Connection.setTlsVerify
key_constructs.purpose: Toggle certificate verification flags (ssl_verify_host/peer and proxy equivalents).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by the Client to enable/disable TLS verification across in-flight connections where safe; callers must respect notes in HttpClient about when toggling is allowed.

key_constructs.name: Connection.getResponseHeader
key_constructs.purpose: Fetch a response header (value and amount) from curl via curl_easy_header; logs and returns null on libcurl errors.
key_constructs.reasoning: Implementations swallow libcurl errors and return null because callers generally expect absent headers rather than errors; this behavior is intentional and relied upon.

key_constructs.name: Connection.getConnectHeader
key_constructs.purpose: Fetch a CONNECT-phase header (used with proxy tunnels).
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposes libcurl's possibility of separate headers during CONNECT; code that manages proxies/tunnels will call this.

key_constructs.name: Connection.getResponseCode
key_constructs.purpose: Return response code from curl_easy_getinfo, mapped safely to u16 with range checks.
key_constructs.reasoning: If curl returns out-of-range values, this method maps them to 0 to keep callers free from handling negative or huge values.

key_behaviors: create and reset curl easy handles according to runtime config
apply per-connection timeouts, proxy and TLS options
set read/write/header callbacks for Transfers and WebSockets
iterate and collect response headers from multiple sources
expose response and connect metadata (status, redirect count, effective URL)

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming an easy handle retains method/body state between uses without re-setting them
pitfalls.consequence: Reused connections may perform HEAD/POST/etc. unintentionally if method/body options are not explicitly set per use
pitfalls.prevention: Always call Connection.setMethod and explicitly set body/read state for each reuse; respect Connection.reset semantics which aims to clear prior options.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing TLS verification or proxy settings while connections are in-flight without understanding the restrictions
pitfalls.consequence: Undefined curl behavior or inconsistent verification for existing connections; HttpClient contains logic and comments that changing some options while in-flight may be unsafe
pitfalls.prevention: Follow HttpClient semantics: toggling tls_verify is allowed in some contexts but other changes (proxy) are restricted unless no inflight requests exist.

pitfalls.mistake: Treating getResponseHeader/getConnectHeader as functions that can return detailed errors
pitfalls.consequence: Callers may expect errors but the implementation logs internal libcurl errors and returns null, so error handling should treat null as 'absent' rather than an exceptional flow
pitfalls.prevention: When editing these functions preserve the current behavior (log and return null) or update all callers consistently if changing semantics.

pitfalls.mistake: Forgetting to provide allocator when calling HeaderIterator.collect or failing to free collected headers
pitfalls.consequence: Memory leaks or crashes due to incorrect ownership assumptions
pitfalls.prevention: Pass a proper allocator and ensure the returned ArrayList(Header) is deinitialized by the caller when no longer needed.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming content-type is an owned string or longer than MAX_CONTENT_TYPE_LEN
pitfalls.consequence: Truncation or accidental reads of uninitialized memory if code expects variable-length content-type ownership
pitfalls.prevention: Use ResponseHead.contentType() which returns a slice with the actual length and respect that content type storage is fixed-size with MAX_CONTENT_TYPE_LEN.

key_sections: Connection.reset: central application of Config (timeouts, proxy, TLS, compression) and the place where many subtle options are re-applied
Connection.setMethod / setBody / setGetMode: understand distinction between curl behavioral method and header method string
HeaderIterator union: shows how headers are normalized from different sources and how collect() materializes them
ResponseHead.contentType: small, in-place content-type handling used by callers
skip_unless_needed: Low-level libcurl constant aliases near the top (Blob/WaitFd/etc.) if you're not changing bindings
debug-only branches guarded by ENABLE_DEBUG unless debugging curl behavior

tests.example_command: zig build test  # runs the project's Zig unit tests; see Makefile: make test

related_files.relationship: Primary consumer: constructs Transfers that use Connection APIs (setMethod, setBody, callbacks), relies on reset semantics, in_use/dirty connection lists and setTlsVerify behavior.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to Connection.reset, callback signatures, header iteration, or TLS/proxy behavior must be reviewed against HttpClient's lifecycle and transfer code.

related_files.relationship: Low-level FFI bindings used by this module.
related_files.reason_to_check: If bindings change (types or enum names), method calls and constant aliases here must be updated accordingly.

related_files.relationship: Uses Connection.transport.websocket and websocket helpers mentioned in commit history; WebSocket code relies on connection-level websocket options and callbacks.
related_files.reason_to_check: WebSocket lifecycle and frame APIs expect Connection to correctly set connect-only/pause/ws meta/start frame behavior.

data_constants_to_check: Config.tlsVerifyHost() (affects reset when ca_blob is null)
Config.httpProxy() (used when applying proxy options in reset)

insights.title: Add per-connection timeout setter and expose websocket/frame helpers
insights.problem: No per-Connection API to set CURLOPT_TIMEOUT_MS and earlier code couldn't enforce per-request timeouts triggered by XHR.
insights.root_cause: Connection abstraction lacked a setTimeout method; also websocket support required additional libcurl wrappers (frame meta, start frame, pause, etc.) and a transport field.
insights.solution: Added Connection.setTimeout (maps to curl_easy_setopt .timeout_ms), ws helpers (wsStartFrame, wsMeta), extras (setWriteCallback, setReadCallback, setHeaderCallback, setConnectOnly, pause), and introduced Connection.transport union to track http/websocket owner.
insights.lesson_learned: Low-level connection wrappers should expose the libcurl features needed by higher layers (timeouts, websocket meta). Also ensure Connection stores enough context (Transport) so callbacks can map back to high-level objects.

insights.title: Add transport union on Connection to avoid storing Transfer in easy's private
insights.problem: Previously the Transfer pointer was stored in curl easy private which made websocket code and mixed transport code awkward.
insights.root_cause: Single-purpose private pointer didn't express multiple transport types and reset semantics.
insights.solution: Introduce Transport union with .http/.websocket/.none and use that to identify owner; reset sets transport to .none.
insights.lesson_learned: When a low-level resource can be used by different higher-level transports, make the connection explicitly polymorphic to avoid casts and fragile private storage.

insights.title: Expose header parsing and add unified header iterator collectors
insights.problem: Different header sources (curl linked list vs internal list) had separate iterators and no easy way to collect headers into a single allocation.
insights.root_cause: Header iteration logic was fragmented (curl list parsing inline vs list iterator) and parseHeader had limited visibility.
insights.solution: Made parseHeader public, replaced iterator signatures to return a HeaderIterator union with multiple variants (curl, curl_slist, list), added collect(allocator) helper to build an ArrayList of Header entries, and added CurlSListIterator to handle libcurl linked list data.
insights.lesson_learned: Unify iteration interfaces and provide a collect utility to reduce duplicated header-parsing code; make parsing primitives public if used outside the module.

insights.title: Add getConnectHeader and getConnectCode helpers for CONNECT phase
insights.problem: CONNECT-phase HTTP headers and status code from libcurl weren't accessible using existing response helpers.
insights.root_cause: Existing Connection API only exposed response headers and response code; libcurl separates CONNECT info (CURLOPT_CONNECT_ONLY / CONNECTCODE) which was not surfaced.
insights.solution: Implemented Connection.getConnectHeader(name, index) returning HeaderValue and Connection.getConnectCode() to expose libcurl connect code via curl_easy_getinfo(..., .connect_code).
insights.lesson_learned: When using libcurl, be aware of separate info buckets (response vs connect). Expose both through your connection abstraction if you use proxies or tunnel (CONNECT) flows.

insights.title: Connection wrapper standardized and reset behavior improved
insights.problem: Connection initialization and reset behaviors were split; various call sites used different fields (.easy vs ._easy), and reset didn't accept runtime config.
insights.root_cause: Incremental refactors left the Connection abstraction inconsistent.
insights.solution: Standardize on _easy field, add Connection.reset(config, ca_blob) which calls curl_easy_reset and re-applies timeouts, proxy, TLS and compression options; update all call sites (Runtime, Handles) to use _easy.
insights.lesson_learned: When wrapping C libs, provide explicit reset/reinit methods that accept the runtime configuration; update all references consistently during refactors to avoid subtle bugs.

insights.title: AuthChallenge parsing simplified and source explicit
insights.problem: Previously AuthChallenge.parse determined source (WWW/Proxy) by parsing header name from a combined header string; header callbacks were refactored and header name wasn't available in raw callback argument.
insights.root_cause: Header parsing logic relied on header name when the upstream API split header name/value earlier.
insights.solution: Change AuthChallenge.parse signature to accept explicit Source enum and the header value string; adjust callers accordingly.
insights.lesson_learned: When lower-level callbacks change their surface (e.g., header/value split), make parsing functions explicit about source to avoid brittle string parsing.

insights.title: Move cookie handling out of Header iteration and into Request/Transfer send path
insights.problem: Header struct previously carried a separate cookies pointer and iterator logic special-cased cookies among headers.
insights.root_cause: Cookies were added at header construction time in multiple places.
insights.solution: Remove cookies field from Headers struct and make cookie insertion explicit in Transfer/request send path via jar.forRequest; update Headers iterator to only iterate libcurl header list.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep header container semantics simple; don't thread cookie-specific fields through a generic header struct — handle cookies as a separate concern closer to request emission.

src/browser/actions.zig.short_description: Implements high-level DOM user-interaction actions (click, fill, hover, press, select, scroll, waitForSelector).
src/browser/actions.zig.description:
This module centralizes the implementation of browser-style user interaction primitives for a Page/DOM model (click, hover, press, selectOption, setChecked, fill, scroll) and a helper to wait for selectors. It exists so higher-level subsystems (MCP/CDP handlers, tools, test harnesses) can emulate user actions by constructing and dispatching appropriate DOM events through Page._event_manager and by calling Element/Node APIs (focus, setValue, setChecked, scrollTo). The file intentionally uses typed Event/MouseEvent/KeyboardEvent constructors (.initTrusted) that are tied to the Page allocation/context and relies heavily on runtime type checks via node.is(Type) to ensure callers pass nodes of expected types.

Design choices visible here: event construction is done via small, focused functions that create and dispatch events rather than manipulating DOM internals directly; input/change dispatch logic was factored into a helper dispatchInputAndChangeEvents to remove duplication and ensure consistent ordering across fill/select/setChecked. Error handling is mixed: some dispatch failures are logged and suppressed while other failures cause the action to return error.ActionFailed. waitForSelector delegates waiting/polling responsibilities to Session.runner() / runner.waitForSelector, and accounts for elapsed time with a nanos->ms timer so it can pass a remaining timeout to the runner. The semantics implemented (order of events for setChecked, focusing before fill) reflect deliberate attempts to match real browser behavior, as documented in the commit history.

key_constructs.name: dispatchInputAndChangeEvents
key_constructs.purpose: Create and dispatch 'input' and 'change' events on an Element via the Page event manager.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by multiple actions (fill, selectOption, setChecked) so the exact event creation parameters and error handling are centralized to avoid small inconsistencies in event semantics and ordering.

key_constructs.name: click
key_constructs.purpose: Simulate a mouse click by creating a MouseEvent('click') and dispatching it on an Element.
key_constructs.reasoning: Entry point for emulating user clicks from higher-level code; it enforces type checking (Element) and converts dispatch errors into error.ActionFailed so callers can detect a failed action.

key_constructs.name: hover
key_constructs.purpose: Simulate hover by dispatching 'mouseover' and 'mouseenter' MouseEvents on an Element.
key_constructs.reasoning: Emits the two mouse events that are commonly observed when hovering, with specific property flags, to reproduce hover-driven behaviors like showing tooltips or menus.

key_constructs.name: press
key_constructs.purpose: Dispatch a 'keydown' and then 'keyup' KeyboardEvent to either a provided Element target or the document.
key_constructs.reasoning: Implements key press semantics; it accepts an optional node to target and falls back to the document when node is null to match typical keyboard focus behavior.

key_constructs.name: selectOption
key_constructs.purpose: Set the value of an HTML select element and dispatch input/change events.
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses Element.Html.Select.setValue then calls dispatchInputAndChangeEvents to ensure listeners are notified consistently after the select value changes.

key_constructs.name: setChecked
key_constructs.purpose: Set checked state on checkbox/radio inputs, dispatch click first then input and change events.
key_constructs.reasoning: Matches browsers' user-click ordering (click -> input/change). It validates input type to be checkbox or radio and logs errors on failures, returning error.ActionFailed on set operation failures.

key_constructs.name: fill
key_constructs.purpose: Focus the element and set its value (input, textarea, or select), then dispatch input/change events.
key_constructs.reasoning: Focus is invoked before changing the value to ensure focus/focusin observers run; supports multiple input-like element kinds via runtime type checks.

key_constructs.name: scroll
key_constructs.purpose: Scroll either a specific Element (set scrollLeft/scrollTop) or the window (page.window.scrollTo).
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides both element-level and window-level scrolling with event dispatch for 'scroll' when scrolling an element; it preserves return semantics depending on whether the operation failed.

key_constructs.name: waitForSelector
key_constructs.purpose: Wait for a CSS selector to appear within a Session with a timeout measured in milliseconds, delegating to the runner.
key_constructs.reasoning: Delegates waiting/polling to Session.runner's implementation for consistent timing semantics and re-use of the central runner.waitForSelector; computes remaining timeout after an initial runner.wait to avoid exceeding the original timeout.

key_behaviors: dispatches DOM events to emulate user interactions
updates form control values and triggers input/change semantics
emulates mouse hover and click event sequences
sends keyboard events to document or specific targets
waits for a selector within a session with timeout

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming dispatch failures are consistently surfaced as errors
pitfalls.consequence: Some dispatch failures are only logged and suppressed (no error returned), while others return error.ActionFailed. Callers relying on a uniform error contract may miss actionable failures.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the existing per-function error-handling choices: do not change logging-only dispatch catch clauses into returning errors without verifying all call sites and test expectations.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing event order for setChecked (e.g., dispatch input/change before click)
pitfalls.consequence: Breaks compatibility with browser-observed ordering; page scripts may behave differently and tests relying on the previous ordering will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the current sequence: click is dispatched first, then input and change, as the commit history emphasizes.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or bypassing el.focus() in fill
pitfalls.consequence: Removes observable focus side-effects that page scripts may rely on (focus/focusin handlers), causing test mismatches.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the focus call before setting value; if changing behavior, run tests that validate focus-driven scripts.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the timing / remaining-time computation in waitForSelector incorrectly
pitfalls.consequence: May introduce negative timeouts/underflow or change wait semantics; could cause spurious timeouts or overly long waits.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the timer measurement (ns -> ms), compute remaining timeout carefully and keep the guard that returns error.Timeout when remaining == 0.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing runtime type checks (node.is(Element)) to looser checks or different error codes
pitfalls.consequence: Will change error signaling (error.InvalidNodeType) expected by higher-level code and tests, causing protocol tool handlers to misbehave.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the explicit node.is(...) checks and the returned error.InvalidNodeType contract for callers.

key_sections: dispatchInputAndChangeEvents: central helper for emitting 'input' and 'change' events used by multiple actions
setChecked: enforces checkbox/radio type and event ordering (click -> input/change)
fill: focus then setValue and dispatch events
waitForSelector: delegates to Session.runner and handles remaining timeout arithmetic
skip_unless_needed: the detailed .initTrusted(...) argument lists for each event creation (boilerplate event fields)
simple logging-only catch blocks unless investigating error behavior

test_functions: MCP - Actions: click, fill, scroll, hover, press, selectOption, setChecked
tests.example_command: make test    # or `zig build test` to run project's unit/integration tests

relevant_snippets.description: Defines the DOM elements (button, inputs, select, scroll box) and event listeners used by MCP action tests to assert that click/fill/select/scroll/hover/press/setChecked produce the expected side-effects.

related_files.relationship: Tool definitions map MCP/CDP tool actions to the high-level browser actions implemented here; tests invoke tools which in turn call these functions.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to action signatures, error codes or behavior should be reflected in tools.zig (input JSON schemas, error handling) and tests that invoke them.

related_files.relationship: Test HTML page that contains event listeners and elements used to validate the action implementations.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifying event ordering, names, focus behavior, or dispatched properties will require updating assertions dependent on this fixture.

tests_to_run: run MCP action integration tests that use src/browser/tests/mcp_actions.html via the tools test harness (CI end-to-end if applicable)

insights.title: Extract dispatchInputAndChangeEvents helper
insights.problem: Input and change event dispatch logic was duplicated across fill, selectOption and setChecked.
insights.root_cause: Repeated inline code to construct and dispatch 'input' and 'change' events in multiple action functions.
insights.solution: Extracted dispatchInputAndChangeEvents(el, page) and replaced duplicated blocks with calls to it (commit 6c9a5dd).
insights.lesson_learned: When multiple action handlers need the same event dispatch semantics, centralize into a helper to avoid small inconsistent differences and make future fixes in one place.

insights.title: Correct event order for setChecked
insights.problem: setChecked previously dispatched input/change events before click which differed from browsers' ordering.
insights.root_cause: Implementation dispatched events in the wrong sequence (click should fire before input/change during a user click).
insights.solution: Reordered event dispatch so click is emitted first, then input and change (commit 58143ee).
insights.lesson_learned: Event order matters for web compatibility — test sequences against real browser behavior when emulating user interactions.

insights.title: Focus element before fill
insights.problem: Setting value via fill didn't focus the element; JS listeners depending on focus/focusin might not run.
insights.root_cause: fill set the input value but did not dispatch focus/focusin or call element.focus().
insights.solution: Call el.focus(page) before setting the value so focus/focusin handlers run (commit 46a63e0).
insights.lesson_learned: Actions that mutate form controls should replicate user-visible side-effects such as focus state; these side-effects are observable by page scripts.

insights.title: Use Runner.waitForSelector instead of repeated parsing loop
insights.problem: actions.waitForSelector implemented a manual polling loop re-parsing selectors and ticking the runner.
insights.root_cause: Duplicated logic and re-parsing across loops reduced performance and risked divergence from runner behavior.
insights.solution: Delegate to runner.waitForSelector and reuse the runner's waiting semantics; simplify timer handling (commit ab6c63b).
insights.lesson_learned: Consolidate polling/waiting behavior in a single runner API to avoid duplicated logic and ensure consistent timing/retry semantics.

src/browser/js/bridge.zig.short_description: Zig-to-V8 runtime bridge that defines how native Zig types/functions/properties are exposed to JS
src/browser/js/bridge.zig.description:
This file implements the core JavaScript bridge used to expose native Zig types, methods, properties, and indexed/name-indexed accessors into the embedded V8 runtime. It exists to centralize the metadata and wrappers that convert between V8 callback conventions and the project's internal Caller/JS helpers, to create function/constructor/accessor templates, and to provide prototype-chain wiring for JsApi-backed types. The design is heavily compile-time driven: Builder(T) returns a typed builder for a Zig type T that contains factory helpers (constructor, accessor, function, indexed, namedIndexed, iterator, callable, property) which produce small wrapper structs that contain raw function pointers with C calling convention (callconv(.c)).

The file is written to be very explicit about runtime ownership and calling conventions: all V8 callbacks are wrapped in small functions that initialize a Caller context, convert/validate arguments and returns, and then call into strongly-typed Zig functions. Several constructs take Caller.Function.Opts or custom Opts to carry metadata (caching, static, deletable, typed-array handling, null/undefined mapping). Debug-only code (gated by IS_DEBUG) adds diagnostic behaviors like unknownObjectPropertyCallback and logging of unexpected window property accesses. There are many interactions with other core parts of the system: Page and Session (for DOM lookups like getElementById), TaggedOpaque prototype-chain metadata, js.v8 bindings (v8.* externs), and per-type JsApi descriptors found elsewhere in the webapi directory.

Because this file does a lot of comptime introspection (getArity using @typeInfo, property() mapping based on pointer/array types, prototypeChain() building using prototypeChainLength(T) and JsApiLookup.getId), editing it requires respecting the compile-time contracts and the V8 ABI. The commit history shows the file has evolved to simplify finalizer semantics (findFinalizerType / finalizerPtrGetter / releaseRef additions) and to add registration of new webapi types (FormDataEvent, SubmitEvent, CryptoKey). The Accessor construct gained a deletable flag to control DontDelete on templates. Debug-only helpers must only exist in Debug builds (unknownObjectPropertyCallback compiles-only in debug).

key_constructs.name: Builder
key_constructs.purpose: Comptime generic that returns a typed builder struct for exposing a Zig type T to V8 (provides constructor, function, accessor, indexed, etc.)
key_constructs.reasoning: Builder is the main entry point for declaring what parts of a Zig type become visible to JS; it centralizes consistent wrappers so other modules produce uniform v8 callbacks and prototype chain entries.

key_constructs.name: Constructor
key_constructs.purpose: Encapsulates a V8 constructor wrapper; constructor.func is a C-callback that initializes a Caller and delegates to the Zig constructor function.
key_constructs.reasoning: V8 requires a function callback pointer for constructors; this struct standardizes creation of that callback while conveying options (e.g., dom_exception).

key_constructs.name: Function
key_constructs.purpose: Represents exported methods: stores a callback pointer, arity, static/noop flags and optional caching metadata.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides arity detection via compile-time introspection and wraps real Zig functions into V8-compatible callbacks that go through Caller.Function.call.

key_constructs.name: Accessor
key_constructs.purpose: Represents property getters/setters exposed to JS, with options like static, deletable and caching.
key_constructs.reasoning: Accessors need separate get/set callbacks and metadata that affects template creation (e.g., DontDelete mapped from deletable).

key_constructs.name: Indexed
key_constructs.purpose: Defines numeric-indexed getters/enumerators (array-like or typed array views) and options like as_typed_array or null_as_undefined.
key_constructs.reasoning: Indexed accessors are used for containers and typed-array emulation; wrappers must initialize Caller context and return u8 success flags to V8.

key_constructs.name: NamedIndexed
key_constructs.purpose: Defines named property getters/setters/deleters with options and returns C-callback pointers for V8.
key_constructs.reasoning: JS property lookups by name must map to native behaviors (including typed-array mapping) and signal whether the property was handled via returned u8.

key_constructs.name: Iterator
key_constructs.purpose: Creates iterator implementations; supports special-case where a type itself is an iterator (returns this) or a function-backed iterator.
key_constructs.reasoning: Iterators need to produce the correct return shape (this for builtin iterator objects) or call a backing Zig function using Caller.Function.call; async flag tracked in Opts.

key_constructs.name: Callable
key_constructs.purpose: Wraps a Zig function as a plain callable JS value, with null_as_undefined option.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for creating function objects in templates with consistent Caller invocation and return semantics.

key_constructs.name: Property
key_constructs.purpose: Holds compile-time constant property values (null, int, float, bool, string) and template/readonly metadata.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to create static properties on prototypes/templates where the value is known at compile time.

key_constructs.name: unknownWindowPropertyCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Runtime handler invoked when a property lookup on window is not intercepted; tries DOM getElementById and logs unknown properties in debug builds.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides heuristic handling for window.X accesses (e.g., window.foo -> document.getElementById('foo')) and centralizes debug logging for unexpected global accesses.

key_constructs.name: unknownObjectPropertyCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Debug-only handler for unknown properties on objects to help detect framework-driven property access patterns.
key_constructs.reasoning: Only compiled in Debug mode; used to selectively silence/ignore known benign property accesses and log unexpected ones to help debugging.

key_behaviors: wraps Zig functions into V8-compatible C callbacks
builds prototype-chain entries and template metadata for JsApi types
maps property/indexed accessors to native implementations
implements debug logging for unknown property accesses

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying V8 callback signatures or callconv(.c) signatures
pitfalls.consequence: Binary incompatibility with V8 callbacks causing crashes/hard-to-debug runtime failures
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve all callconv(.c) wrappers and the small wrapper functions that initialize Caller and call Caller.Function/constructor helpers.

pitfalls.mistake: Using unknownObjectPropertyCallback in non-debug builds or removing the IS_DEBUG guard
pitfalls.consequence: Compile-time errors (function explicitly errors in non-debug) or leaking debug-only logic into release behavior which changes runtime semantics and possibly performance
pitfalls.prevention: Keep IS_DEBUG gating and avoid calling this debug helper from production paths; confirm @compileError conditions before editing.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing comptime introspection (e.g., getArity, property() type checks) without updating all JsApi usages
pitfalls.consequence: Mis-detected arities, wrongly generated wrappers, compile errors where property() raises compileError for unsupported types
pitfalls.prevention: Understand @typeInfo-based logic and ensure any change preserves behavior for pointer/string detection and optional/Page parameter stops.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering prototypeChain ordering or index/offset calculations without matching TaggedOpaque/JsApi metadata
pitfalls.consequence: Wrong prototype wiring leading to runtime method resolution bugs or memory layout mismatches
pitfalls.prevention: When adjusting prototypeChain(), validate prototypeChainLength(T), JsApiLookup.getId(), and any @offsetOf usage across related TaggedOpaque types.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing Accessor.deletable or other options used by template creation
