pitfalls.consequence: Properties might get wrong attributes (e.g., wrongly deletable or non-deletable), causing JS semantics changes that break pages or tests
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve Accessor.Opts attributes and how they're applied to template creation; check places that read these flags when creating object templates.

key_sections: Builder: main entry and how it creates constructor/function/accessor/indexed/namedIndexed/iterator/callable/property definitions
Constructor/Function/Accessor structs: how wrappers initialize Caller and delegate into Zig functions
unknownWindowPropertyCallback: runtime heuristic used across the bridge for unknown global lookups (important for DOM mapping and debug logging)
skip_unless_needed: long static debug-ignore lists in unknownWindowPropertyCallback (these are heuristics used only for debug logging)
low-level v8.* extern wrapper invocations unless debugging V8 interop

tests.example_command: zig build test (runs the project's unit tests, including any that exercise JS bridge if present)

related_files.relationship: Bridge uses Caller to convert V8 callback handles into native calls; Caller.init/deinit and Caller.Function.call are central to all wrappers here
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to callback wrappers, options (caching, null handling), or Caller API require parallel updates in Caller.zig

related_files.relationship: Provides v8 bindings and JS helpers (HandleScope, String conversions) that bridge.zig relies on
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to v8 interactions, string handling or js helper types affects the wrappers created in this file

related_files.relationship: Defines PrototypeChainEntry and prototypeChainLength logic used by Builder.prototypeChain
related_files.reason_to_check: Prototype chain layout changes require aligning offsets/indices here and in TaggedOpaque.zig

related_files.relationship: Bridge callbacks reference Page for DOM operations (e.g., getElementById) and use page buffers for logging keys
related_files.reason_to_check: If Page API changes (names or ownership), bridge wrappers that call page.document or page.buf need updates

related_files.relationship: Session may provide context needed by some JsApi-backed types and is imported here; bridge constructs might reference session lifetime semantics
related_files.reason_to_check: Session lifetime and context changes can affect how Caller/bridge initializes runtime contexts

tests_to_run: make build (if your environment must build with snapshot/V8)
run any project-specific JS-bridge unit or integration tests if present (no test files were provided in this group)
data_constants_to_check: validate any changed template attribute flags (e.g., Accessor.Opts.deletable) are honored where templates are built

insights.title: Remove dead compile-time releaseRef helper
insights.problem: A compile-time generic releaseRef helper that attempted to walk prototype chains and invoke finalizers was present but unused.
insights.root_cause: This helper was dead/unused and added complexity relying on compile-time introspection.
insights.solution: Delete the dead helper function to simplify the bridge implementation (commit a4a024c).
insights.lesson_learned: Remove compile-time reflection helpers when they are not providing value; simpler explicit finalizer patterns are preferable in a systems language.

insights.title: Finalizer model simplified: only acquire/releaseRef and deinit; remove weak boolean
insights.problem: Previous bridge finalizer model used both weak/strong booleans and separate Zig/V8 wrappers; this didn't compose well across prototype chains and IsolatedWorlds.
insights.root_cause: Weak/strong toggles didn't generalize to objects shared across IsolatedWorlds. Finalizers sometimes had to be defined on many types throughout prototype chains creating boilerplate and brittle behavior.
insights.solution: Bridge finalizer helpers removed in favor of composable acquireRef/releaseRef/deinit pattern. Implemented utilities findFinalizerType and finalizerPtrGetter to find the prototype type that actually defines acquireRef and to dereference prototype chains to that type's pointer.
insights.lesson_learned: Make finalizer semantics explicit and uniform: if a type participates in finalization it must provide acquireRef/releaseRef/deinit (possibly delegating to proto). Automate discovery across prototype chain to avoid duplication.

insights.title: Register FormDataEvent in JS bridge
insights.problem: FormDataEvent type wasn't registered in the JS bridge, so it couldn't be constructed or used from JS.
insights.root_cause: New event type added to codebase but bridge declarations were missing.
insights.solution: Added @import of FormDataEvent.zig to JsApis in bridge.zig.
insights.lesson_learned: Whenever adding a new JS-exposed type, register it in the bridge so it becomes available from JS runtime.

insights.title: Expose accessor deletable attribute and propagate to Template attribute
insights.problem: Some accessors (e.g., window.document, window.location) need DontDelete attribute but bridge accessor options did not expose deletable control.
insights.root_cause: Bridge Accessor struct lacked a deletable flag to allow DontDelete to be set on properties.
insights.solution: Added a .deletable boolean to the Accessor options and used it to set v8.DontDelete when creating the property on the template.
insights.lesson_learned: Expose attributes in the language-bridge metadata so property semantics can be enforced at template creation time.

insights.title: Expose SubmitEvent and CryptoKey bindings to JS bridge
insights.problem: New native types (SubmitEvent, CryptoKey) need to be accessible from JS environment.
insights.root_cause: Native types created without registration in JS bridge would be inaccessible.
insights.solution: Add @import of webapi/event/SubmitEvent.zig and webapi/CryptoKey.zig to the JsApis list so they are available to JS.

src/cdp/testing.zig.short_description: CDP test harness: socketpair-based Client + WebSocket frame reader and message expectation helpers
src/cdp/testing.zig.description:
Provides a realistic test harness for CDP domain/unit tests by creating a socketpair, instantiating the production Server.Client on one end, and reading/parsing WebSocket frames from the other end. The file exists to let unit tests drive the CDP layer (send commands into CDP via processMessage) and assert what the server would send back to a real websocket client. It intentionally uses posix socketpair and real framing semantics so tests see the same framing, partial reads and timing behaviors that occur in production.

The core abstraction is TestContext: an arena-backed context which holds the server Client instance, the peer socket used to read frames, and accumulated parsed JSON messages and raw payload copies. TestContext implements lifecycle (deinit), lazy initialization of a CDP instance (cdp()), helpers to load a BrowserContext and navigate a Page (loadBrowserContext), a processMessage helper that accepts either an anytype (serialized to JSON string) or a raw slice, and a set of expectation helpers (expectSent, expectSentCount, expectSentResult, expectSentEvent, expectSentError, getSentMessage) which poll/read the socket and optionally advance the page runner while waiting. The read() implementation handles incremental reads and reassembly of WebSocket text/binary frames with 2- and 4-byte length handling (skips 8-byte length case), parsing the payloads into json.Value and storing both parsed Value and raw payload.

This harness was refactored to address two historical issues: (1) comparing expected anytype messages nondeterministically when stringified, and (2) tests that needed to advance the page runner to surface events. The code canonicalizes expected anytype message into a json.Value (by stringify->parse) and uses base.isEqualJson for comparisons. While waiting for expected messages, expectSent attempts runner.tick for an active page to give the page event loop a chance to emit messages. Because of the real-socket approach and explicit frame parsing, callers must respect socket options, potential WouldBlock read outcomes, partial-frame handling and the arena lifetime used for parsed json.Values.

key_constructs.name: TestContext
key_constructs.purpose: Holds the test harness state: socket, Server.Client, accumulated received JSON and raw frames, and helpers to interact with CDP.
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralizes per-test state and provides methods for message processing and assertions; editing this struct affects all CDP tests that rely on frame parsing, the arena allocator and lifecycle rules.

key_constructs.name: context
key_constructs.purpose: Factory function that creates a TestContext by making a socketpair, setting socket options, and initializing a Server.Client bound to one end of the pair.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by tests to obtain an initialized TestContext; changes here (socket options, timeouts, buffer sizes) influence read behavior and test timing.

key_constructs.name: cdp
key_constructs.purpose: Method on TestContext that lazily initializes and returns a CDP instance bound to the internal Server.Client.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides access to CDP APIs for tests; lazy init means tests that never need CDP avoid initialization cost but callers must ensure proper deinit via TestContext.deinit.

key_constructs.name: loadBrowserContext
key_constructs.purpose: Convenience to create/replace a BrowserContext in the CDP, optionally set IDs and navigate an embedded test page URL; can wait (via runner.wait) for the page to settle.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by page-related tests to ensure a Page is created and navigated; it interacts with CDP.browser_context, session, page and runner, so co-changes in those APIs must be considered.

key_constructs.name: processMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Send a message into the CDP stack. Accepts anytype or pointer (string) and forwards a JSON string to cdp().processMessage.
key_constructs.reasoning: Tests use this to emulate incoming client commands; its handling of anytype->string and pointer inputs must match how production messages are passed in.

key_constructs.name: expectSent
key_constructs.purpose: Wait for a message matching an expected anytype by serializing it to JSON.Value and comparing against parsed messages that were captured from the socket.
key_constructs.reasoning: Canonicalizes expectation into json.Value to avoid nondeterministic stringify differences; poll loop includes runner.tick calls to advance page event loop while waiting.

key_constructs.name: expectSentCount
key_constructs.purpose: Read any pending frames and assert the total number of received messages equals the expected count.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to validate number of outbound CDP messages without inspecting their contents; depends on read() to populate received list.

key_constructs.name: read
key_constructs.purpose: Low-level loop to read bytes from the peer socket, reassemble WebSocket frames (2- and 4-byte payload lengths), parse text/binary payloads into json.Value and append them to received lists.
key_constructs.reasoning: Critical for proper test harness behavior: handles partial frames, WouldBlock semantics, and moves remaining partial data to the buffer start. Any modification must preserve the partial-read and frame-reassembly semantics.

key_constructs.name: getSentMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Poll and return the parsed json.Value at a specified index if available within retries; otherwise returns null after timeout.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by tests that inspect a specific outbound message; relies on read() and the retry loop used elsewhere, so timing changes impact its behavior.

key_constructs.name: dumpReceived
key_constructs.purpose: Debug helper that prints raw received frames for diagnostic failures.
key_constructs.reasoning: Assists debugging failing expectations by outputting raw frame payloads; not part of production logic but useful in test failure contexts.

key_behaviors: creates a socketpair and Server.Client for tests
parses incoming WebSocket frames and accumulates json.Value messages
serializes expected anytype values to json.Value for deterministic comparison
polls and advances a page runner while waiting for expected messages
exposes convenience helpers to create a browser/page and navigate in tests

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming read() returns whole WebSocket frames in a single read
pitfalls.consequence: Tests will intermittently fail to find messages because partial frames are not yet available
pitfalls.prevention: Respect and preserve the incremental frame reassembly logic and buffer shifting in read() when editing anything related to reading/parsing

pitfalls.mistake: Changing expected-message comparison to rely directly on JSON string equality
pitfalls.consequence: Brittle tests due to non-deterministic JSON string formatting/order
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the existing canonicalization (stringify -> parse into json.Value) and comparison via base.isEqualJson

pitfalls.mistake: Removing runner.tick/runner.wait attempts in the expectSent polling loop
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that depend on page-side asynchronous events may time out or fail intermittently
pitfalls.prevention: Retain the behavior of attempting to tick the page runner while waiting for expected outbound messages

pitfalls.mistake: Altering socket options (timeouts, buffer sizes) without running tests on CI-like environments
pitfalls.consequence: May change timing characteristics leading to flakiness on slower CI runners
pitfalls.prevention: If modifications to timeouts/buffer sizes are necessary, run the full test suite (make test / zig build test) and consider environment differences

pitfalls.mistake: Deinitializing arena or freeing duplicated payloads prematurely
pitfalls.consequence: Use-after-free or invalid json.Value references during comparisons or later debug printing
pitfalls.prevention: Respect arena lifetime (base.arena_allocator) and ensure deinit order keeps parsed values valid until tests finish

key_sections: context: shows how the socketpair and Server.Client are created and configured
read: the frame parsing and buffer shifting logic (critical for correct behavior)
expectSent: the expectation loop and how expected anytype is canonicalized
loadBrowserContext: how a browser/page is created and navigated for page tests
skip_unless_needed: dumpReceived: debug printing
small helper wrappers (expectSentResult/expectSentEvent/expectSentError) when focusing on core parsing logic

tests.example_command: zig build test  # or make test

related_files.relationship: CDP API implementation that TestContext lazily initializes and interacts with (cdp(), processMessage, browser_context)
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to CDP message shapes, processMessage signature, or browser_context layout must be reflected here so tests still construct/inspect messages correctly

related_files.relationship: Server.Client implementation instantiated by the test harness; TestContext relies on the Client constructor and framing behavior
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifications to Server.Client init, socket handling, framing or sendJSON will change how tests must interact with the Client and how messages appear on the peer socket

related_files.relationship: Shared test utilities and base.arena_allocator used for JSON parse/stringify and equality checks
related_files.reason_to_check: Alterations to base helpers (arena lifetime, isEqualJson, allocator) affect message comparison and memory lifetime of parsed json.Values

data_constants_to_check: socket recv/send timeouts and buffer sizes set in context()
frame parsing assumptions: only 2- and 4-byte length cases handled (read())

insights.title: Make expected/received message comparison robust and allow event loop ticks while waiting
insights.problem: Comparing expected anytype values recorded by sendJSON was brittle; serialized JSON comparisons were non-deterministic. Tests could also hang if a page needed ticks to produce expected result.
insights.root_cause: sendJSON records anytype -> serializing for comparison produced nondeterministic output; tests sometimes needed the page runner to advance to surface events.
insights.solution: Serialize expected anytype to JSON.Value and use base.isEqualJson to compare values. While waiting for expected messages, attempt to tick the page runner by 1000ms if a page exists to advance events. Also reworked the expectation loop to parse expected JSON into json.Value once.
insights.lesson_learned: Testing frameworks that capture dynamic anytype messages should canonicalize into a deterministic representation (json.Value) before comparison. Tests that involve JS pages may need to advance the runner to avoid timeouts.

insights.title: Rework CDP test harness to use real Server.Client via socketpair and parse WebSocket frames
insights.problem: Previous test harness used an ad-hoc fake Client that accumulated JSON values; with the CDP refactor and the desire to test more realistic behavior it's necessary to simulate framed WebSocket traffic and a real Server.Client instance.
insights.root_cause: Tight coupling between CDP and a single Client implementation meant tests needed to emulate more of the real I/O stack. The older fake client approach could hide framing and I/O edge cases.
insights.solution: Rewrote testing harness to create a socketpair, launch Server.Client on one end and read raw WebSocket frames from the other end. The TestContext now maintains received messages and raw frames, implements a read() function that handles partial frames and 2/4-byte length headers, and exposes helpers (expectSent, expectSentCount, getSentMessage) that poll/read until messages appear. The TestContext now returns errors (context() is !TestContext).
insights.lesson_learned: When testing protocols layered over sockets (WebSocket here), the test harness must account for framing, partial reads and timing. Using a real socketpair + production Client reduces parity gaps between tests and runtime.

src/browser/Session.zig.short_description: Manages per-browser session state: page lifetimes, navigation queues, V8 globals/finalizers, and resource arenas
src/browser/Session.zig.description:
Session.zig implements the Session abstraction: a container for all state that is shared across pages within a browser session and for the per-root-page lifetime (root page + frames). It exists to separate two lifetimes: (1) the overall Session lifetime (cookie jar, navigation history, notification hooks, arena pool references, finalizer/global tracking) and (2) the root page lifetime (page_arena, factory, origins, identity, v8 globals/temps). The module centralizes memory and identity management required for V8 integration and coordinated page teardown, navigation scheduling, and origin sharing between frames.

Key responsibilities are initialization and teardown of Session state, creating/replacing/removing pages, scheduling and processing navigations (including special-case handling for synchronous about:blank navigations), origin acquisition and release, and forcibly cleaning up V8-related resources and finalizers when the page tree is reset. The file intentionally centralizes finalizer callbacks, v8.Global objects (globals and temps), and origin mappings at Session scope to avoid lifetime issues when objects are referenced across multiple V8 isolated worlds; resetPageResources executes a deterministic cleanup sequence and must be kept correct to avoid double-finalization or dangling references.

This file interacts directly with Page.zig (Page lifecycle and navigation), Browser.zig (global browser/app references), Factory.zig (page_arena allocations), the js/v8 bridge (js/js.zig and v8 bindings), Notification.zig (CDP and event dispatch), and the ArenaPool in App.zig (allocator lifecycle). Many functions rely on non-local invariants (for example, a root page vs. frame pages distinction, page.parent == null for root). The code uses careful errdefer/defer ordering and explicit assertion checks; reordering or removing those cleanups risks leaks, double-frees or invalid memory access during teardown and when finalizers run.

key_constructs.name: Session
key_constructs.purpose: Primary struct defining session-scoped state: browser pointer, arenas, cookie jar, origins map, identity, finalizers, globals, temps, and navigation queues.
key_constructs.reasoning: An agent editing the session must respect this as the authoritative place for resources that outlive a single page tree and must avoid moving fields that change the two-lifetime semantics (Session vs page lifetime). Many operations assume these fields exist and are cleared in resetPageResources.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Initialize a Session instance: acquire arenas from ArenaPool, init factory and cookie jar, set up navigation queues and notification pointer.
key_constructs.reasoning: init ties the Session to the Browser/App allocator and ArenaPool. It uses arena_pool.acquire and errdefer release semantics — changes here must preserve correct allocation and release patterns to avoid leaks or double-release.

key_constructs.name: deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Tear down a Session: remove page if present, deinit cookie jar and storage_shed, and release arenas back to the ArenaPool.
key_constructs.reasoning: deinit must run in a stable state; it calls removePage which itself triggers page-level cleanup and notification dispatch. Any edits must keep the precise ordering of page removal and resource releasing to ensure finalizers and V8 globals are not left pointing to freed memory.

key_constructs.name: createPage
key_constructs.purpose: Create and initialize the root Page for this Session, set up NavigationEventTarget, dispatch page_created notification, and return the page pointer.
key_constructs.reasoning: createPage asserts that no page exists and relies on Page.init; it must preserve the notification dispatch ordering so clients (CDP) can create additional contexts/worlds after main page creation.

key_constructs.name: removePage
key_constructs.purpose: Remove the root Page: dispatch page_remove, deinit the Page, reset navigation and page resources and clear page pointer.
key_constructs.reasoning: removePage calls Page.deinit(false) and then resetPageResources. The order of notification.dispatch, deinit, onRemovePage, and resetPageResources is significant for safe finalization of v8-wrapped objects and for preventing dangling references in other worlds.

key_constructs.name: resetPageResources
key_constructs.purpose: Reset everything tied to the root page lifetime: clear identity, run remaining finalizer callbacks, reset globals and temps, deinit leaked origins, reset frame id generator, reset page_arena and reinitialize factory.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function embodies historical bug-fix fixes: it clears identity before forcing finalizers to avoid double-finalize, and centralizes session-scoped finalizer/globals/temps handling. Editors must not alter the cleanup ordering: identity must be cleared before finalizers run, and finalizer_callbacks iteration must call FinalizerCallback.deinit for each entry.

key_constructs.name: getOrCreateOrigin
key_constructs.purpose: Get an existing Origin by key from the session origins map or create/insert a new js.Origin; supports generating an opaque origin when key is null.
key_constructs.reasoning: Origins are reference-counted (origin.rc). The function uses getOrPut and errdefer remove semantics; changes must preserve incrementing/decrementing origin.rc and make sure newly created origins are stored with key and value in the map, and that memory ownership remains consistent.

key_constructs.name: releaseOrigin
key_constructs.purpose: Release an origin reference: decrement rc or deinitialize and remove from the map when count reaches 1.
key_constructs.reasoning: The refcount semantics are delicate: releaseOrigin deinitializes the origin when rc == 1. Editors must preserve this logic and calls to origin.deinit(app) to avoid leaks or use-after-free.

key_constructs.name: processQueuedNavigation
key_constructs.purpose: Process the queued navigations with a double-buffer strategy, handling non-about:blank first, then synchronous about:blank navigations, switching the active buffer to allow new navigations to be appended safely.
key_constructs.reasoning: This implements a subtle, two-pass navigation processing flow to avoid infinite about:blank loops and re-entrancy problems. The double buffer swap and the separate queued_queued_navigation temporary buffer are integral; changing them or the order of operations can reintroduce navigation loops, inconsistent parent pending_loads counters, or missed navigations.

key_constructs.name: processFrameNavigation
key_constructs.purpose: Handle frame (non-root) navigation: preserve iframe reference, deinit and re-init Page with same frame id, reattach iframe and window, and call navigate with queued options.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function contains errdefer cleanup and parent frame list manipulation (swapRemove) to keep parent's frames list consistent if init fails. It also manipulates parent._pending_loads based on _parent_notified. Its precise error handling and resource release semantics must be preserved when editing.

key_constructs.name: FinalizerCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Struct type used as the session-level finalizer helper for Zig objects exposed to V8; used to ensure host-side fallback finalization when V8 does not run finalizers timely.
key_constructs.reasoning: FinalizerCallback semantics were changed historically to use explicit reference counting / release_ref behavior. Session.finalizer_callbacks stores these keyed by pointer; resetPageResources iterates and calls FinalizerCallback.deinit for forced cleanup. Modifications must respect its intended role as a fallback and the acquire/release pairing semantics.

key_behaviors: creates root pages and initializes JS environments
removes and replaces pages safely with deterministic cleanup
schedules and processes frame and root navigations
manages origin sharing and reference counting
tracks and forces cleanup of V8 globals and finalizers

pitfalls.mistake: Reorder resetPageResources cleanup steps (for example, forcing finalizers before clearing identity)
pitfalls.consequence: V8 finalizers could run and reference stale identity/objects leading to double-finalize, use-after-free, or crashes.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve identity clearing before invoking finalizer deinit and maintain the current iteration order over finalizer_callbacks/globals/temps/origins.

pitfalls.mistake: Modify the double-buffer navigation queue logic or remove the separate about:blank pass
pitfalls.consequence: Risk of infinite navigation loops, missed navigations, or corrupting the queue while iterating due to re-entrancy.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the queued_navigation_1/queued_navigation_2 swap behavior and the two-pass approach (async first, about:blank second) including the safety removal of newly queued about:blank entries.

pitfalls.mistake: Change error handling or errdefer ordering in processFrameNavigation or after Page.init without mirroring parent list housekeeping
pitfalls.consequence: Parent.frames may become inconsistent (leaked frames or double entries), pending_load counters may be wrong, and cleanup on errors may be incomplete leading to memory/resource leaks.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve errdefer blocks which remove the page from parent.frames and decrement parent._pending_loads when init fails or an error occurs.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering origin reference counting semantics (origin.rc increments/decrements) or removing map remove calls
pitfalls.consequence: Origins could leak or be deinitialized while still in use by other frames, causing re-use issues or crashes when accessed later.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain getOrCreateOrigin and releaseOrigin logic and the remove-from-map-on-last-release behavior.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing notifications ordering (notification.dispatch .page_created/.page_remove) relative to Page.init/deinit
pitfalls.consequence: External consumers (CDP clients, other worlds) could attempt to create contexts or access page state too early or after it has been torn down, causing race conditions or inconsistencies.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the current notification dispatch points in createPage/removePage/replacePage so that consumers see events at the same lifecycle points.

key_sections: resetPageResources: critical cleanup ordering for v8 finalizers, globals, temps, and origins
createPage / removePage / replacePage: root page lifecycle and notification dispatch sequence
processQueuedNavigation: double-buffered navigation scheduling and about:blank special handling
processFrameNavigation / processRootQueuedNavigation: how page re-init and navigation are actually performed
skip_unless_needed: findPageBy / findPageById / findPageByFrameId: traversal helpers
nextFrameId / nextPageId: trivial id generators

tests.example_command: zig build test (or make test) to run the repository unit tests; run end-to-end flows with make end2end when demo repo is present

related_files.relationship: Page lifecycle is driven by Session functions (createPage, removePage, replacePage, processFrameNavigation). Changes to Session often require coordinated changes to Page.init/deinit and navigation semantics.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any edits that alter page creation/teardown, deinit parameters (true/false) or when windows/iframes are reattached must be mirrored in Page.zig to keep invariants.

related_files.relationship: Holds the App and environment references used by the Session (browser.app, browser.env) and provides global behavior like memoryPressureNotification.
related_files.reason_to_check: Session.init and resetPageResources call browser.app and browser.env; changes to how Browser provides these or memory notification semantics affect Session.

related_files.relationship: Session.factory is initialized with page_arena and reinitialized on page reset; this controls object allocation for page lifetime.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifying page_arena lifecycle or Factory.init signature requires updating Session.resetPageResources and init to preserve allocation lifetimes.

related_files.relationship: Provides ArenaPool and global allocator used by Session; Session acquires/release arenas from App.ArenaPool.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to ArenaPool.acquire/release or reset semantics will affect Session.init/deinit and resetPageResources.

related_files.relationship: V8 and JS bridging (js.Origin, js.Identity, v8.Global) are used heavily by Session for origin management, identity tracking and global resets.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to Origin, Identity or v8.Global APIs (init/deinit/reset) require corresponding changes in Session to keep cleanup ordering correct.

tests_to_run: make end2end (requires ../demo clone and external deps)

insights.title: Clear identity before forcing V8 finalizers to avoid double-finalize
insights.problem: V8 finalizers could run during teardown and attempt to operate on objects that had already been finalized or whose identity was invalid, causing unsafe finalization ordering.
insights.root_cause: Identity (used by V8 finalizer callbacks) was still present while forcing cleanup of remaining finalized objects; finalizers could reference freed state.
insights.solution: Deinitialize and clear self.identity before forcing finalizers in resetPageResources().
insights.lesson_learned: When forcing runtime finalizers (or tearing down embedded runtimes), clear any pointers/identity that finalizers could use beforehand to prevent re-entry into already-destroyed state.

insights.title: Change FinalizerCallback semantics to explicit reference counting
insights.problem: FinalizerCallback previously contained a _deinit used both by v8 and as fallback; identity tracking and fallback semantics were mixed.
insights.root_cause: FinalizerCallbacks were used to hide missing releaseRef calls in Zig; this masked ownership bugs and created complexity in identity lifecycle handling.
insights.solution: Renamed and repurposed fields: _deinit -> release_ref; added identity_count to track how many JS identities reference the finalizer, and deinit now calls release_ref. FinalizerCallbacks are removed only when all identities have released (commit 77b60ce).
insights.lesson_learned: Keep VM finalizer helpers as true fallbacks; require explicit acquire/release pairing in host code and track per-identity references to avoid premature cleanup.

insights.title: Centralize finalizer callbacks, globals and temps at Session scope
insights.problem: Identity-level finalizer tracking caused lifetime issues when objects were referenced across multiple v8 IsolatedWorlds.
insights.root_cause: An object could outlive its Identity but still be referenced by another IsolatedWorld; Identity-level storage freed globals/temps/finalizers too early.
insights.solution: Added Session.finalizer_callbacks (map keyed by ptr id), Session.globals, and Session.temps to track v8.Global instances at session scope. resetPageResources now iterates and calls finalizer deinit, resets v8 globals and clears temps.
insights.lesson_learned: For objects that may be referenced from multiple contexts, track finalization at the broadest lifetime scope (Session) and make page reset forcibly run any remaining finalizers.

src/browser/webapi/net/XMLHttpRequest.zi.short_description: Implementation of the browser-side XMLHttpRequest API and its network lifecycle
src/browser/webapi/net/XMLHttpRequest.zi.description:
This file implements the XMLHttpRequest Web API for the headless browser. It exists to expose the familiar XHR interface to page JavaScript and to translate XHR calls into network requests via the project's HttpClient. The module owns per-XHR state (method, url, headers, body, response buffers, readyState, responseType, callbacks, and an ownership refcount helper) and coordinates the lifecycle across synchronous JS calls and asynchronous native network callbacks. It also bridges to the DOM event model by dispatching progress/load/abort/error/timeout events on the XHR's event target (XMLHttpRequestEventTarget) and invoking the JS onreadystatechange/onload handlers stored as js.Function.Temp values.

Design choices visible in the code: memory for per-request data is allocated from a Page-provided arena so many short-lived allocations can be grouped and released when the page/session cleans up; the code keeps an explicit boolean _active_request that indicates the object holds an acquired lifetime reference across asynchronous network callbacks; network interactions are delegated to HttpClient, and the file implements the set of callbacks (start/header/data/done/error/shutdown) to handle incremental headers and body delivery, errors, timeouts and finalization. The code also converts response bytes into different Response variants depending on responseType (text/json/document/arraybuffer) and uses Page helpers to parse JSON and HTML into JS/DOM objects.

This file is tightly coupled to HttpClient.zig (request lifecycle and callback signatures), Page.zig (arena allocation, URL resolution, cookies, blob URL lookup, DOM parsing), Headers.zig (request header management), XMLHttpRequestEventTarget.zig (event dispatch helpers) and Session.zig (arena and lifetime management). The commit history shows several subtle lifetime, timeout and error-handling fixes were applied here (explicit _active_request flag, passing timeout_ms to the HttpClient Request, mapping OperationTimedout to a timeout event instead of a generic error, and propagating cookie_origin). When editing this file, respect the ownership/rc semantics, the arena allocation patterns and the contracts expected by HttpClient callbacks.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Create and initialize a new XMLHttpRequest instance tied to a Page arena
key_constructs.reasoning: init sets up the XHR event target prototype, allocates request header storage and binds the XHR to the page arena; changes here affect lifetime and allocation context for every XHR created.

key_constructs.name: send
key_constructs.purpose: Start an XHR network request using HttpClient, set up headers/body, and acquire object lifetime
key_constructs.reasoning: send is the entry point for async network activity. It must acquire the self reference and pass callbacks and metadata (including timeout_ms and cookie_origin) to HttpClient.request; errors returned by request must release the ref and leave state consistent.

key_constructs.name: open
key_constructs.purpose: Set HTTP method and resolved request URL and transition XHR to OPENED readyState
key_constructs.reasoning: open resets transient response state and resolves/encodes URL against the page base using Page.resolve. The readyState transition must match what tests expect and subsequent calls (setRequestHeader, send) assume open was called.

key_constructs.name: getResponse
key_constructs.purpose: Return or build a typed Response variant based on responseType from buffered bytes
key_constructs.reasoning: getResponse lazily constructs the union Response (text/json/document/arraybuffer) and may call into page.js.parseJSON or page.parseHtmlAsChildren. Modifying it affects runtime conversion and ownership of JS/DOM objects returned to scripts.

key_constructs.name: handleBlobUrl
key_constructs.purpose: Serve requests for blob: URLs synchronously from the page's blob registry
key_constructs.reasoning: This path bypasses HttpClient and uses page.lookupBlobUrl and local buffers; it must dispatch the same sequence of events and readyState transitions as network requests to match script expectations.

key_constructs.name: httpDoneCallback
key_constructs.purpose: HttpClient completion callback that marks the request done, logs and dispatches final events and releases the held reference
key_constructs.reasoning: Finalization must avoid using the transferred Response object after HttpClient frees it and must call releaseSelfRef to drop the XHR object's acquired lifetime. It also maps status and dispatches load/load_end or error events depending on outcomes.

key_constructs.name: httpErrorCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Handle network errors reported by HttpClient, map them to DOM events (error or timeout) and release ownership
key_constructs.reasoning: The mapping of HttpClient error kinds to 'error' vs 'timeout' events is important and has been a source of previous bug fixes; error paths must also ensure releaseSelfRef() is called to avoid leaks or dangling refs.

key_constructs.name: releaseSelfRef
key_constructs.purpose: Helper to release the XHR's acquired self-reference if one was taken
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralizes the boolean-check-and-release logic so all async callbacks and error paths use a single canonical release point; changing its semantics affects object lifetime across asynchronous boundaries.

key_behaviors: translates JavaScript XHR calls into HttpClient requests
buffers incremental response data and exposes responseText/response
dispatches progress/load/abort/error/timeout DOM events and readyState changes
honors responseType (text/json/document/arraybuffer) conversions
uses Page arena for short-lived allocations and persists JS callbacks using js.Function.Temp

pitfalls.mistake: Acquire or release the XHR lifetime (refcount) in the wrong order relative to starting the HttpClient request
pitfalls.consequence: Leaking refs or double-releasing the XHR; V8 may drop JS handles before HttpClient start callback, causing premature object destruction or crashes.
pitfalls.prevention: Use releaseSelfRef and ensure acquireRef() is called before scheduling the async request and that all error/failure paths call releaseSelfRef()

pitfalls.mistake: Assume response transfer objects remain valid after httpDoneCallback or other finalization callbacks
pitfalls.consequence: Use-after-free or reading freed buffers because HttpClient can free transfer/connection objects when done.
pitfalls.prevention: Copy or persist needed data into the XHR arena buffers before depending on them beyond the callback lifetime; avoid retaining HttpClient transfer pointers.

pitfalls.mistake: Change mapping of HttpClient error kinds without updating DOM event dispatch
pitfalls.consequence: Behavioral differences observed by page scripts (e.g., timeouts should trigger 'timeout' event rather than generic 'error').
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the mapping of OperationTimedout -> 'timeout' and other errors -> 'error' consistent per commit history and tests.

pitfalls.mistake: Modify header iteration/population incorrectly when HttpClient changed its header API
pitfalls.consequence: Duplicate or missing headers, incorrect getAllResponseHeaders output or mismatch with tests.
pitfalls.prevention: Use response.headerIterator() and the Headers abstraction consistently as current code does.

pitfalls.mistake: Free or release the page arena at the wrong time
pitfalls.consequence: Dangling pointers to strings/arrays allocated from arena used by XHR callbacks.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect Page/Session arena ownership and release only in deinit or when the page/session lifecycle indicates it is safe.

key_sections: init: allocation and linking to the Page arena and XHR event target prototype
send: prepares request headers/body, chooses blob vs network path, acquires ref and calls HttpClient.request
http*Callback functions: how headers/data/done/error/shutdown are handled (state transitions and event dispatch)
getResponse/getResponseXML: conversion rules for responseType
skip_unless_needed: low-level event dispatch helper code inside XMLHttpRequestEventTarget.zig (unless changing event semantics)
string formatting helpers used only for header concatenation

tests.example_command: make test  # or `zig build test` using the project's test harness which serves src/browser/tests/** and runs them in the headless runtime

relevant_snippets.description: Initial assertions about the XMLHttpRequest readyState constants and first basic GET request (id='xhr') verifying headers/status/responseText/responseURL and the onload event.
Test for responseType='arraybuffer' and binary response handling (id='xhr6'), validating byteLength and typed array content.
Abort behavior tests (id='xhr_abort' and related) verifying onabort/onerror/onloadend and resetting readyState to UNSENT.
Timeout property test (id='xhr_timeout') asserting the timeout getter/setter and that the request completes normally when server responds before timeout.

related_files.relationship: HttpClient.request() provides the transfer/Response and callback signatures used by XHR to perform the network operation.
related_files.reason_to_check: When changing send() or any http*Callback signatures, update call sites to match HttpClient's Request/Response API and timeout semantics.

related_files.relationship: Page supplies the arena, base URL resolution, cookie handling, blob URL registry and functions to parse JSON/HTML used by XHR getResponse/handleBlobUrl.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifying URL resolution, cookie origin propagation, or the arena API will affect XHR.open/send and response parsing.

related_files.relationship: Event target implementation used to dispatch 'progress', 'load', 'error', 'timeout', 'abort', 'loadstart', 'loadend' events.
related_files.reason_to_check: If event dispatch or callback lifetime is altered, ensure XHR events still match expectations in the tests.

related_files.relationship: Headers abstraction used to collect request headers and format response header lines for getAllResponseHeaders/getResponseHeader.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to header formatting/iteration will affect tests that assert exact header strings.

data_constants_to_check: src/browser/Page.zig:base URL resolution and cookie origin propagation

insights.title: Add XHR timeout property with curl enforcement and proper timeout event dispatch
insights.problem: XMLHttpRequest lacked the timeout property end-to-end, so per-request timeouts couldn't be enforced and timeout events weren't emitted.
insights.root_cause: No storage of timeout on XHR objects; HTTP client wasn't receiving per-request timeout; error handling conflated timeout with other errors.
insights.solution: Added _timeout field, getTimeout/setTimeout accessors, pass timeout_ms to Request, update HTTP client to set per-connection timeout via Connection.setTimeout, and dispatch `timeout` event (instead of `error`) when OperationTimedout occurs.
insights.lesson_learned: Surface JS-level properties to the network stack and map error kinds to distinct DOM events per spec (timeout -> 'timeout' event). When adding JS API properties, ensure the networking layer honors them.

insights.title: Adapt XHR plumbing to Response abstraction and use headerIterator
insights.problem: XHR code used Transfer and had commented header iterator; needed to be updated to Response and new headerIterator.
insights.root_cause: HttpClient callback signature change.
insights.solution: Change httpStartCallback/httpHeaderCallback/httpHeaderDoneCallback/httpDataCallback to use Response, update response field uses, and replace commented-out header iteration with response.headerIterator() where applicable.

insights.title: XMLHttpRequest active request ref semantics changed
insights.problem: Active HTTP request lifecycle used acquireRef after scheduling request; failure in scheduling would leave unbalanced refs
insights.root_cause: Code acquired the self reference after initiating the HTTP request; if initiation failed, cleanup needed but was missing.
insights.solution: AcquireRef and mark _active_request before calling http_client.request, and on request error release the self ref; in httpShutdownCallback release self ref (commit 77b60ce).
insights.lesson_learned: When starting async operations that require extending object lifetime, acquire ownership before starting and ensure error paths release ownership.

insights.title: XHR self-reference lifetime and ownership tracking
insights.problem: V8 could drop the JS reference to an XMLHttpRequest object before network callbacks (start/error/abort) ran. The code previously inferred ownership from _transfer being non-null and acquired/releases the self-reference at different points, causing double-free or premature release.
insights.root_cause: The code relied on _transfer == null to determine whether a self-reference had been acquired. Because V8 can drop the JS handle before the HTTP start callback, ownership inference based on transfer presence was unreliable.
insights.solution: Acquire the XHR self-reference immediately on send() and track ownership with an explicit boolean (_has_ref renamed later to _active_request). Add releaseSelfRef() helper to centralize release logic. Ensure all error/abort paths call releaseSelfRef() rather than relying on _transfer checks.
insights.lesson_learned: Don't infer resource ownership from side-effectful fields (like an active network transfer). Use an explicit ownership flag when lifetimes cross async boundaries and the underlying runtime (V8) may drop handles unpredictably.

insights.title: Rename ownership flag to clarify semantics
insights.problem: The boolean flag name _has_ref was ambiguous about what it represented with respect to an active network request.
insights.root_cause: Naming ambiguity made it hard to reason about whether the boolean meant 'we own a JS handle' or 'there is an active HTTP transfer'.
insights.solution: Rename _has_ref -> _active_request (9716150). Update release and send logic to use the new name.
insights.lesson_learned: Choose boolean names that reflect the precise semantic meaning (active_request vs has_ref) to avoid confusion when reasoning about async lifecycles.

insights.title: Propagate cookie_origin for XHR
insights.problem: XMLHttpRequest send path did not include cookie origin which prevented correct cookie selection.
insights.root_cause: Request construction omitted cookie_origin.
insights.solution: Set .cookie_origin = page.url for XHR internal Request.
insights.lesson_learned: Consistent propagation of origin metadata is necessary across all network APIs.

insights.title: Callers updated to new headersForRequest signature
insights.problem: XMLHttpRequest previously passed an allocator to headersForRequest.
insights.root_cause: API simplified in Page.zig.
insights.solution: Use page.headersForRequest(&headers) without allocator.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep API signatures minimal and update call sites together to avoid mismatches.

src/browser/tests/net/xhr.html.short_description: Browser-side test suite for XMLHttpRequest behaviors (readyState, response types, errors, abort, timeout, blob url)
src/browser/tests/net/xhr.html.description:
This HTML file is a collection of scripted tests that exercise the XMLHttpRequest implementation inside the headless runtime. Each <script id=...> block sets up an asynchronous expectation (via testing.async and Promises) and validates XHR properties and event sequences for a specific scenario: basic GET, responseType=document, responseType=json, POST body handling, readyState change order, binary arraybuffer handling, redirects, 404/500 handling, abort semantics (immediate abort, abort from callback, abort with empty response), blob: URL handling, and timeout behavior. The tests rely on an HTTP server running at 127.0.0.1:9582 which serves endpoints like /xhr, /xhr/json, /xhr/binary, /xhr/redirect, /xhr/404, /xhr/500 and /xhr_empty.

The test file not only validates numeric/status fields (status, statusText, readyState) but also event emission and types (ProgressEvent, Document, ArrayBuffer), exact header string formatting from getAllResponseHeaders(), correct responseURL after redirects and cookie/credential semantics indirectly through same-origin checks. Test harness functions (testing.expectEqual, testing.async, testing.expectEqual) come from ../testing.js and the suite expects the runtime to expose XMLHttpRequest constants and behavior matching real browsers. This test file is used by the project's in-repo JS/DOM test runner and should be re-run whenever XHR semantics or HttpClient behavior change.

key_constructs.name: xhr
key_constructs.purpose: Script block id exercising basic GET XHR behavior and header/response assertions
key_constructs.reasoning: The first block confirms constants, open/send flow, readyState and responseText/responseURL/header expectations; many other tests depend on the same underlying behavior.

key_constructs.name: xhr6
key_constructs.purpose: Script block id testing arraybuffer responseType and typed array reading
key_constructs.reasoning: Verifies binary handling and alignment between response.byteLength and typed array views.

key_constructs.name: xhr_abort
key_constructs.purpose: Test for abort behavior including onabort/onerror/onloadend and readyState reset
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures abort semantics match spec and that XHR internals properly dispatch the sequence of events and cleanup.

key_constructs.name: xhr_timeout
key_constructs.purpose: Test for timeout property getter/setter and ensuring requests finish normally when under timeout
key_constructs.reasoning: Validates that the timeout property exists, can be modified from JS, and that the HttpClient honors per-request timeouts; linked to recent commits handling timeout_ms.

key_behaviors: verifies readyState constants and transitions
asserts responseType conversion and typed-array semantics
asserts correct header formatting from getAllResponseHeaders
verifies abort, error, and timeout event emission and readyState reset
tests blob: URL handling and responseURL correctness after redirects

pitfalls.mistake: Run the HTML file directly in a browser outside the project's test harness
pitfalls.consequence: The test depends on testing.js and a specialized test runner and on a local HTTP server at 127.0.0.1:9582; tests will fail or hang.
pitfalls.prevention: Run via the project's test runner (make test / zig build test) or the harness that serves testing.js and the HTTP endpoints.

pitfalls.mistake: Change server endpoints or response payloads used by tests without updating assertions
pitfalls.consequence: Many tests assert exact header strings, response lengths, or JSON fields which will break if server fixtures change.
pitfalls.prevention: Synchronize changes between test server fixtures and this test file.

pitfalls.mistake: Assume these tests exercise all edge cases of XHR
pitfalls.consequence: Some behaviors like CORS credential handling, multipart progress, and some header edge cases may be untested here.
pitfalls.prevention: Check other test suites or add targeted tests when modifying those areas.

key_sections: xhr: basic GET and header/assertion checks
xhr2/xhr3/xhr4: responseType=document/json and POST body behavior
xhr5: onreadystatechange ordering checks
xhr6: binary arraybuffer handling
xhr_abort and related: abort semantics and lifecycle cleanup
xhr_blob_url: blob URL support and create/revoke lifecycle
xhr_timeout: timeout property tests
skip_unless_needed: trivial expectEqual lines that simply assert constant numbers
duplicated patterns between similar test blocks

tests.example_command: make test  # runs the project's headless JS/DOM tests (ensure the local test HTTP server on 127.0.0.1:9582 is available)

relevant_snippets.description: Initial block: checks readyState constants and runs the first GET XHR (id='xhr') including header and response assertions.
Abort-related tests demonstrate event ordering expected when abort() is called either immediately or in a callback; useful for verifying releaseSelfRef behavior.
Timeout behavior test: checks timeout getter/setter and that setting timeout doesn't erroneously trigger a timeout event when the server responds promptly.

related_files.relationship: This test file directly exercises the implementing source file's network, state-transition and event dispatch logic.
related_files.reason_to_check: When changing send(), readyState transitions, event names or header formatting in the implementation, update these tests accordingly.

related_files.relationship: HttpClient drives the network callbacks that these tests exercise (timeouts, errors, headers, binary responses).
related_files.reason_to_check: If HttpClient changes error kinds, header iteration, or timeout semantics, these tests may start failing and need cross-checking.

related_files.relationship: Provides the testing harness (testing.async, testing.expectEqual) used by this HTML test file.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to the test harness API will require updating how tests are written in xhr.html.

tests_to_run: ensure the local HTTP test server (127.0.0.1:9582) is running and serving the expected endpoints
data_constants_to_check: test server payloads and headers for /xhr, /xhr/json, /xhr/binary, /xhr/redirect, /xhr/404, /xhr/500, /xhr_empty

src/mcp/router.zig.short_description: MCP message reader and dispatcher: parse JSON-RPC lines, route to handlers, and send results/errors
src/mcp/router.zig.description:
This file implements the MCP (machine control protocol / JSON-RPC-like) line-oriented router used by the server. It reads newline-delimited JSON messages from a Reader, enforces message-size limits, parses each message into a protocol.Request, maps the request method to an enum, and dispatches to the appropriate handler (initialize, ping, tools/resources handlers, or a no-op for certain notifications). It exists to centralize protocol parsing, error handling, and dispatch semantics for incoming MCP traffic so higher-level modules (tools, resources, Server) don't need to duplicate JSON parsing and method lookup logic.

Design choices visible here: the router is line-oriented (reader.takeDelimiter('\n')) and uses an arena allocator per loop iteration to parse JSON with std.json.parseFromSliceLeaky; the parsed request has optional id semantics (handlers must guard req.id). Method lookup is done via a compile-time StaticStringMap mapping string method names to a Method enum, keeping method dispatch compact and explicit. The file contains both production code and a synchronous unit test block (Zig test) that exercises initialize/ping/tools listing, method-not-found and parse-error flows. Several historical commits touched this file: error handling for oversized messages (StreamTooLong) was corrected to send an MCP error response, and protocol version defaulting has been adjusted historically (tests expect a particular default protocol.Version tag).

key_constructs.name: processRequests
key_constructs.purpose: Main loop that reads newline-delimited messages from a Reader, handles oversized messages, trims whitespace, and dispatches non-empty messages to handleMessage.
key_constructs.reasoning: Agents editing behavior around input reading must preserve the loop's arena resetting semantics, StreamTooLong handling (which sends an MCP error), and the trimmed-empty-line skip behavior to keep protocol semantics consistent.

key_constructs.name: handleMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Parses a JSON-RPC request from bytes into protocol.Request using a provided arena allocator, looks up the method in method_map and dispatches to the appropriate handler or sends a MethodNotFound error if needed.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the central dispatch function; changes here affect how requests are validated, how unknown methods are handled, and whether/when responses are sent back. Note the function relies on parseFromSliceLeaky with an arena allocator passed in by caller.

key_constructs.name: handleInitialize
key_constructs.purpose: Handles the 'initialize' MCP method: extracts the id, builds an InitializeResult struct (including protocolVersion via @tagName(protocol.Version.default)), and sends it via server.sendResult.
key_constructs.reasoning: Initialize is a critical handshake; tests assert its exact JSON structure and protocolVersion tag. Historical changes moved protocol version selection logic nearby, and tests depend on the default tag value.

key_constructs.name: handlePing
key_constructs.purpose: Handles the 'ping' MCP method by returning an empty result object for requests that include an id.
key_constructs.reasoning: Simple echo/ping endpoint used by clients to verify liveness; must return a result when id is present and avoid panicking when id is missing.

key_constructs.name: Method
key_constructs.purpose: Enum of supported MCP methods used for switch-based dispatch.
key_constructs.reasoning: Centralized method names help ensure compile-time mapping; edits to supported method names require updating this enum and method_map in tandem.

key_constructs.name: method_map
key_constructs.purpose: Comptime-initialized StaticStringMap mapping method name strings to Method enum values.
key_constructs.reasoning: Method lookups are string->enum via this map: adding or renaming methods must keep this map consistent. Note method names include characters like '/' and are represented with @"string" where necessary.

key_behaviors: reads newline-delimited JSON messages from a Reader
parses JSON into protocol.Request objects using an arena
dispatches requests to method handlers and sends results/errors via Server
returns protocol-level errors on parse failures and oversized messages

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying the arena usage (lifetime/reset cadence) or using a shorter-lived allocator for parseFromSliceLeaky calls.
pitfalls.consequence: Leaked or invalid parsed data leading to memory issues or crashes when handlers access parsed request fields.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the per-loop arena.reset(.retain_capacity) pattern and pass the arena.allocator() into handleMessage; ensure parsed values are not used after the arena is reset.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing the StreamTooLong handling branch in processRequests.
pitfalls.consequence: Clients will no longer receive an MCP error when sending oversized messages; tests (and clients) expect a protocol error response on oversized messages.
pitfalls.prevention: Retain the server.sendError(.null, .InvalidRequest, "Message too long") behavior when reader.takeDelimiter returns StreamTooLong.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming req.id is always present and using req.id.? directly in handlers.
pitfalls.consequence: Potential panics or incorrect behavior when requests are notifications (no id) or malformed; tests and past commits indicate id is optional.
pitfalls.prevention: Guard on req.id with orelse return or otherwise handle missing id safely before calling server.sendResult/sendError.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the protocol.Version default/tag logic without updating tests and related Config/Server behavior.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that assert the initialize result (protocolVersion tag) will fail; clients may see an unexpected protocol version.
pitfalls.prevention: When changing protocol defaults, update tests and check where Config or Server previously influenced MCP version selection.

key_sections: handleMessage: main parser + dispatch logic (central to routing behavior)
handleInitialize: handshake result formation (tests assert its output)
method_map and Method enum: authoritative list of supported methods and their mapping
skip_unless_needed: log constant import (boilerplate), test harness setup until you need to adjust tests

exercised_by: embedded Zig unit test in src/mcp/router.zig
tests.example_command: zig test src/mcp/router.zig

relevant_snippets.description: Zig test block that constructs a Server with a testing writer and calls handleMessage with various inputs: initialize, ping, tools/list, unknown_method and invalid json; then asserts writer output contains expected JSON results and tool entries.

related_files.relationship: Server implements sendResult/sendError used by router to emit responses; router depends on Server lifecycle and output writer passed at Server.init.
related_files.reason_to_check: If sendResult/sendError signatures or JSON formatting change, router tests and behavior must be updated accordingly.

related_files.relationship: Defines protocol.Request, protocol.InitializeResult, protocol.Version constants/enums parsed and referenced by router.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to request schema, error codes, or Version tagging affect parsing and initialize result emitted by router.

related_files.relationship: tools.handleList and tools.handleCall are invoked by router; router delegates tool-related methods to this module.
related_files.reason_to_check: Adding/removing tool methods or changing their expected params/results requires updating router's method_map and tests.

related_files.relationship: resources.handleList and resources.handleRead are delegated from router for resources methods.
related_files.reason_to_check: API changes to resources handlers will affect router dispatch and test expectations.

related_files.relationship: Logging used on parse and message errors; tests rely on specific logs to be suppressed or captured in some cases.
related_files.reason_to_check: If log API changes, the test harness (testing.zig) may need adjustments to capture/expect logs.

related_files.relationship: Embedded unit tests rely on testing helpers (allocator, test_app, expectJson) to build a Server and assert outputs.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifying testing helpers will directly affect the embedded tests in this file.

tests_to_run: make test (to run full test suite if present)
data_constants_to_check: Server.zig:sendResult/sendError signatures and JSON formatting expectations

insights.title: Inline mcpVersion helper and allow configuring protocol version via config
insights.problem: mcpVersion helper lived in Config and relied on env var handling; configs were split across modules.
insights.root_cause: Multiple places constructed protocol version; configuration parsing needed to be centralized and allow CLI override.
insights.solution: Inlined logic for determining protocol version into router: read server.app.config.mode and choose opts.version or default. Later commit removed LIGHTPANDA_MCP_VERSION and moved version parsing responsibilities in Config to set Mcp.version.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep protocol/version selection close to where it's used (router) but also ensure Config parses and stores explicit version option; avoid scattering env var logic across modules.

insights.title: Default MCP protocol version changed to 2024-11-05
insights.problem: Default protocol version used in tests and initialize result changed, which can affect MCP clients expecting previous default.
insights.root_cause: Product decision to revert default to older protocol version; code and tests updated accordingly.
insights.solution: Updated protocol.Version default/tag, router tests and Server tests to expect the new default string.
insights.lesson_learned: Changing the default protocol version is a breaking change for clients—explicitly expose configuration and provide CLI/env overrides; update tests to assert new defaults.

insights.title: Return MCP error when incoming message exceeds allowed size
insights.problem: processRequests dropped oversized messages after logging but didn't return an MCP error to the client.
insights.root_cause: Missing call to sendError on StreamTooLong branch.
insights.solution: Call server.sendError(.null, .InvalidRequest, "Message too long") when StreamTooLong occurs.
insights.lesson_learned: Protocol readers should always send a protocol-level error response for malformed or oversized client messages so clients receive actionable feedback.

insights.title: Handle missing request IDs safely
insights.problem: Handlers assumed req.id was present and used req.id.? which could panic or misbehave when id was missing.
insights.root_cause: Missing guard for optional request id in MCP request handling paths.
insights.solution: Guard on req.id orelse return early for handlers that cannot respond; extract id to a local const before calling server.sendResult/sendError.
insights.lesson_learned: Always handle optional request ids in RPC protocols — if an incoming message lacks an id, either return early or avoid calling response functions that require an id.

src/browser/js/Env.zig.short_description: Manages a V8 Isolate: init/deinit, per-isolate templates, contexts and task pumping
src/browser/js/Env.zig.description:
Env.zig encapsulates the lifecycle and runtime services for a single V8 isolate used by the browser runtime. It is responsible for creating the isolate with snapshot data, wiring V8 callbacks (module import, promise rejection, fatal/oom handlers), creating per-isolate global templates and eternal function templates loaded from the startup snapshot, and optionally initializing the V8 Inspector for CDP. Env also maintains a fixed-size collection of active Context instances for that isolate and provides APIs to create and destroy contexts tied to Page/session state.

The file exists to centralize V8-level orchestration so higher-level browser code (Page, ScriptManager, Session, Inspector, etc.) can assume a configured V8 isolate with global templates, external references and private symbol keys. Important design decisions reflected here include: (1) loading function templates from the prebuilt snapshot and making them eternal so per-context template creation is cheap and consistent, (2) storing the TAO (TaggedOpaque) object for the global Window in a longer-lived identity arena (per commit history), and (3) providing explicit microtask/macrotask pumping utilities and memory pressure notifications to control V8 GC and task execution deterministically from the Zig side.

Env tightly interacts with Snapshot (startup blob and external references), Context (per-V8 context wrapper), Page/Window (the Zig runtime objects mapped into V8), Platform (v8 platform message pumping), and Inspector (CDP integration). Many functions in Env are thin wrappers around V8 API calls but enforce ordering, handle HandleScope lifetimes, and manage Zig allocators and arenas. The file is a critical bridge between Zig-managed application state and the embedded V8 runtime and must preserve V8 invariants (scopes, embedder data keys, persistent handles) when being edited.

key_constructs.name: initClassIds
key_constructs.purpose: Populate JsApi Meta.class_id for all JsApis at process start
key_constructs.reasoning: Class IDs are used by the binding layer; this sets stable numeric IDs before any isolate is created preventing race conditions and ensuring consistent mapping between JsApi tables and v8 templates.

key_constructs.name: Env
key_constructs.purpose: Primary per-isolate state structure containing isolate handle, templates and active contexts
key_constructs.reasoning: This struct centralizes all isolate-scoped resources (templates, eternal handles, private symbols, inspector, context list) so other modules can operate against a single Env instance to manage contexts and V8 interactions.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Create and initialize a V8 Isolate with snapshot, templates and optional inspector
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the main entry point to create Env; it must allocate CreateParams, set array buffer allocator and snapshot pointers, initialize eternal templates from the snapshot, configure global template behavior and private symbols, and return a fully-initialized Env. Ordering, errdefer cleanup and handle scope usage are critical here.

key_constructs.name: deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Tear down the Env: free templates, destroy inspector and deinitialize the isolate
key_constructs.reasoning: Must ensure all per-context resources are released and that v8 allocators and creations are cleaned in the reverse order of init; failing to preserve correct ordering or freeing persistent handles prematurely can lead to use-after-free in V8.

key_constructs.name: createContext
key_constructs.purpose: Create a new v8::Context for a Page, hook up Window TAO and register context in Env
key_constructs.reasoning: This function maps a Zig Page/Window into a V8 context (store TAO in global object's internal field), sets up per-context microtask queue, reuses per-isolate templates, sets embedder data to find Context from v8_context, and registers the context in Env.contexts. TAO allocation uses params.identity_arena (session-level) to ensure lifetime correctness across contexts.

key_constructs.name: destroyContext
key_constructs.purpose: Remove a Context from Env and notify Inspector, then deinit the Context
key_constructs.reasoning: Must find the context in the Env array, remove it safely (swap-with-last), optionally notify the Inspector about context destruction (if enabled), and then call context.deinit; this preserves Env's invariants and inspector state.

key_constructs.name: runMicrotasks
key_constructs.purpose: Perform microtask checkpoints for all registered contexts' microtask queues
key_constructs.reasoning: Microtasks must be drained deterministically at points where the Zig runtime integrates with V8. The function guards reentrancy using microtask_queues_are_running flag to prevent nested runs.

key_constructs.name: runMacrotasks
key_constructs.purpose: Run scheduled macrotasks across contexts using each Context's scheduler
key_constructs.reasoning: Bridges timers and macrotask execution from Zig scheduler to V8 contexts. Note it contains a comptime check for builtin.is_test to force macrotasks to run in tests; this special-case affects deterministic test behavior.

key_constructs.name: msToNextMacrotask
key_constructs.purpose: Return milliseconds until the next scheduled macrotask across contexts, or null
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by an event loop to compute sleep/wakeup intervals; must inspect all contexts and use their schedulers' timing info.

key_constructs.name: pumpMessageLoop
key_constructs.purpose: Pump the V8 platform message loop non-blocking until empty
key_constructs.reasoning: Calls Platform.PumpMessageLoop repeatedly; used to drive background tasks that require platform message processing without blocking the thread.

key_constructs.name: waitForBackgroundTasks
key_constructs.purpose: Blockingly pump the platform message loop until V8 reports no pending background tasks
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to synchronously wait for background work to finish (e.g., during shutdown) while continuing to run microtasks; uses pump with blocking=true and calls runMicrotasks between iterations.

key_behaviors: initializes a V8 isolate and its global templates from a snapshot
creates and destroys per-page V8 contexts and maps Window TAO
drains microtasks and runs macrotasks across contexts
pumps platform message loop and waits for background tasks
signals memory pressure and low-memory events to V8

pitfalls.mistake: Allocating TAO (TaggedOpaque) in a context-local arena
pitfalls.consequence: TAO may be freed when the context arena is released while V8 still holds pointers to it, causing use-after-free or crashes
pitfalls.prevention: Allocate TAO in the longer-lived identity_arena (session-level) as done in createContext; respect commit history reason for this change.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying the order of v8 initialization, callbacks or eternal template creation
pitfalls.consequence: Incorrect or missing function templates, broken bindings, or V8 API misuse leading to undefined behavior
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the ordering in init: set CreateParams fields, set callbacks, enter isolate, create eternal templates and global template, then create private symbols and inspector.

pitfalls.mistake: Freeing eternal templates or isolate params early (before isolate deinit)
pitfalls.consequence: Use-after-free in V8 when contexts still reference those persistent handles
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure allocator.free for templates and DELETE of array buffer allocator happen during deinit and after contexts are destroyed.

pitfalls.mistake: Ignoring the fixed size of Env.contexts array (len == 64)
pitfalls.consequence: createContext will return error.TooManyContexts when capacity exceeded and callers must handle it
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the context_count check and handle/propagate error.TooManyContexts; if modifying this limit, update callers/tests accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing the builtin.is_test gating in runMacrotasks
pitfalls.consequence: Tests that rely on deterministic macrotask execution before shutdown may break
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the comptime check or adapt tests if changing behavior; note it's intentionally special-cased for tests.

key_sections: init: sets up the CreateParams, snapshot blob, external references, eternal templates and per-isolate global template
createContext: maps Page/Window into a V8 context, sets TAO in internal field and registers context in Env
destroyContext: unregister context, notify inspector and deinit
runMicrotasks / runMacrotasks / waitForBackgroundTasks: how tasks are drained and message loop is pumped
skip_unless_needed: dumpMemoryStats (and other debug/print helpers)
low-level v8 wrapper calls that mirror V8 API signatures (unless debugging a V8 API interaction)

tests.example_command: zig build test  # runs the project's Zig tests (Makefile: make test)

related_files.relationship: Context represents the per-v8-context wrapper created by Env.createContext; Env sets embedder data and microtask queue used by Context
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes to embedder data indices, microtask queue handling, or Context scheduler APIs require updating both files.

related_files.relationship: Page provides the Zig Window pointer and ScriptManager used when creating contexts; createContext stores the Page.window TAO
related_files.reason_to_check: If Page.Window structure layout or lifetime changes, TAO mapping and identity lookup in createContext must be revisited.

related_files.relationship: Isolate wrapper methods (lowMemoryNotification, memoryPressureNotification, heap stats) are used by Env to signal V8; Env holds js.Isolate
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to Isolate.MemoryPressureLevel or API semantics affect Env.memoryPressureNotification and dumpMemoryStats behavior.

related_files.path: src/ snapshot or bridge related files (Snapshot.zig, bridge.zig, JsApis)
related_files.relationship: Env loads stable function templates and external references from Snapshot and bridge.JsApis snapshot layout
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing snapshot layout, JsApis ordering or external references requires coordinated updates to how init reads snapshot indices and makes eternal templates.

related_files.relationship: Inspector is optionally initialized per-Env when CDP is enabled; Env notifies inspector of contextDestroyed
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to inspector lifecycle or notify APIs require changes to Env.init/deinit and destroyContext notifications.

data_constants_to_check: bridge.JsApis.len and comptime snapshot layout indices

insights.title: Store TAO in identity_arena instead of context_arena
insights.problem: The TAO (TaggedOpaque) objects were previously allocated in a context-level arena and could be freed when a context was destroyed, but TAOs must survive across contexts within the same origin.
insights.root_cause: TAOs can be referenced by V8 objects accessible from other contexts/microtasks; context-level arena could free them prematurely.
insights.solution: Allocate TAOs in params.identity_arena (session-level identity arena) so they outlive individual contexts.
insights.lesson_learned: When storing handles that may be referenced outside the creating context, allocate them in a longer-lived arena.

src/telemetry/lightpanda.zig.short_description: Telemetry batching and delivery module that batches events, serializes to JSON, and posts to telemetry endpoint.
src/telemetry/lightpanda.zig.description:
This Zig source implements a LightPanda telemetry producer: it accepts telemetry.Event values from producers, enqueues them into a lock-protected ring buffer, and periodically flushes them to a remote telemetry ingestion endpoint via the project's Network abstraction. It exists to provide low-cost, batched telemetry delivery that respects a maximum body size and avoids blocking producers. The module integrates with the runtime's Network (getConnection/releaseConnection/submitRequest) through a tick callback registered in init(), so actual HTTP I/O is performed by shared connection pooling logic implemented elsewhere.

Design choices visible in the code: events are enqueued under a mutex in send() while delivery (postEvent) reads the buffer snapshot using atomic head/tail counters; dropped events are tracked with an atomic counter. Serialization is performed with newline-delimited JSON records using std.json.Stringify (and a custom jsonStringify implementation on LightPandaEvent). A fixed BUFFER_SIZE ring buffer prevents unbounded memory growth and MAX_BODY_SIZE enforces the server limit for a single HTTP body; when the composed body would exceed MAX_BODY_SIZE the module stops adding more events to the current request so the rest can be sent on the next tick. The code delegates networking details to Network and relies on build_config and builtin metadata for environment/version fields in telemetry events.

key_constructs.name: URL
key_constructs.purpose: Destination URL used for POSTing telemetry payloads.
