key_constructs.reasoning: Requests are always targeted at this constant; any change requires checking network expectations and CI/ops endpoints.

key_constructs.name: BUFFER_SIZE
key_constructs.purpose: Size of the in-memory ring buffer that holds pending telemetry.Event values.
key_constructs.reasoning: This defines the capacity of the ring buffer; producers check fullness against this value. Changing it affects memory usage, drop behavior, and wrap-around math.

key_constructs.name: MAX_BODY_SIZE
key_constructs.purpose: Maximum allowed bytes for the serialized request body (500KB server limit).
key_constructs.reasoning: The module ensures no POST body exceeds this size by stopping serialization when size would go past this limit; critical for API compatibility.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Initializes the telemetry producer with allocator, network, iid, and run_mode; registers the flush tick callback.
key_constructs.reasoning: Initial setup must register flushCallback with Network.onTick and store references; editing must preserve the registration & stored references to avoid missing ticks or use-after-free.

key_constructs.name: deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Deinitializes the allocating writer used for building request bodies.
key_constructs.reasoning: Releases writer resources. When deinit runs you must ensure no onTick callbacks will run afterwards (or they will access freed writer).

key_constructs.name: send
key_constructs.purpose: Public function for producers to enqueue telemetry.Event into the ring buffer (with overflow counting).
key_constructs.reasoning: send uses a mutex to protect concurrent producers; it performs capacity checks and increments dropped when full. Any modification must respect the lock and atomic ordering used by postEvent.

key_constructs.name: flushCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Callback registered with Network.onTick; invoked by the network tick to attempt flushing queued events.
key_constructs.reasoning: Signature and pointer casting are specific (ctx: *anyopaque). The callback calls postEvent and logs errors. It must remain compatible with Network.onTick's expected callback type.

key_constructs.name: postEvent
key_constructs.purpose: Takes a snapshot of queued events, serializes as newline-delimited JSON up to MAX_BODY_SIZE, and submits an HTTP POST via Network.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the core flush logic: it uses atomic head/tail/dropped to decide what to send, writes overflow event if needed, and updates head on success. It also acquires/releases a Network connection. Orderings and error handling here are delicate (see pitfalls).

key_constructs.name: writeEvent
key_constructs.purpose: Serializes a single telemetry.Event (wrapped with iid and mode) into the writer, ensures body size limit and reverts to checkpoint on overflow.
key_constructs.reasoning: Returns whether the event fit. Uses std.json.Stringify.value to leverage LightPandaEvent.jsonStringify. Uses writer.shrinkRetainingCapacity(checkpoint) when the body would exceed MAX_BODY_SIZE; writer semantics are important to preserve.

key_constructs.name: LightPandaEvent
key_constructs.purpose: Struct used to wrap telemetry.Event with iid and mode and provide JSON serialization via jsonStringify.
key_constructs.reasoning: The struct's jsonStringify method controls the JSON shape of telemetry records and uses builtin metadata and build_config.version. Changes here alter the telemetry schema observed by the server and any downstream tooling.

key_constructs.name: jsonStringify
key_constructs.purpose: Method on LightPandaEvent that writes the JSON representation field-by-field for the wrapper and the inner telemetry.Event.
key_constructs.reasoning: Defines the JSON shape; it uses runtime type inspection of telemetry.Event union fields to emit inner structs. Any change must ensure the expected fields and names remain consistent with server expectations and other telemetry consumers.

key_behaviors: accepts telemetry events from producers and enqueues them
periodically flushes queued events on network ticks
serializes events as newline-delimited JSON
respects MAX_BODY_SIZE and drops/defers excess events
tracks and reports dropped events due to buffer overflow

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering the mutex used in send or changing atomic load/store orderings without coordinating postEvent.
pitfalls.consequence: Data races or missed events, incorrect fullness checks, or head/tail corruption leading to lost or duplicated events.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the mutex in send and the existing atomic orderings (monotonic/acquire/release) unless you fully understand and replace the concurrency protocol.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing Buffer size math (BUFFER_SIZE) or ring indexing without adjusting fullness checks (t - h >= BUFFER_SIZE).
pitfalls.consequence: Off-by-one errors, premature drops, or buffer overwrite of unconsumed events.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the fullness test and modulo indexing consistent; run stress/integration tests that produce many events and verify dropped counts.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying Network connection lifecycle calls in postEvent (getConnection/releaseConnection/submitRequest) or removing errdefer releases.
pitfalls.consequence: Connection leaks or double-release of connections; either can cause resource exhaustion or undefined behavior depending on Network implementation.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing, review Network.getConnection/releaseConnection semantics. Ensure each getConnection has exactly one matching releaseConnection in all control-flow paths (including early returns and error paths).

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming writer allocation/shrinking semantics are irrelevant and removing shrinkRetainingCapacity or clearRetainingCapacity calls.
pitfalls.consequence: Payload construction may fail unexpectedly or memory usage may change; removing shrink may allow exceeding MAX_BODY_SIZE.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the writer checkpointing/rollback pattern used to enforce MAX_BODY_SIZE.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the JSON shape produced by LightPandaEvent.jsonStringify without updating ingestion expectations.
pitfalls.consequence: Telemetry backend and any analytics tooling will receive unexpected fields or missing data, breaking dashboards or alerts.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat jsonStringify output as part of the telemetry schema; coordinate changes with telemetry consumers and CI that may validate schema.

key_sections: send: public enqueue path and overflow handling
postEvent: snapshot, serialization loop, and request submission (the core flush path)
writeEvent: body size enforcement and how an event is serialized into writer
LightPandaEvent.jsonStringify: defines the telemetry JSON shape
skip_unless_needed: the field declarations and atomic initializers at the top (boilerplate state) and the specific writer allocation plumbing unless changing memory behavior

tests.example_command: zig build test  (run the project's test suite that may exercise telemetry indirectly)

related_files.relationship: Telemetry relies on Network for onTick registration and connection management (getConnection/releaseConnection/submitRequest).
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to Network's getConnection/releaseConnection semantics, onTick callback signature, or submitRequest behavior must be reconciled with telemetry's use patterns.

related_files.relationship: Defines telemetry.Event union and types that this module serializes and batches.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to telemetry.Event's union variants or inner struct fields will require updates to jsonStringify and possibly writeEvent behavior.

related_files.relationship: Used for logging flush errors inside flushCallback/postEvent.
related_files.reason_to_check: If log categories or APIs change, flushCallback's call sites must be updated.

related_files.relationship: Provides build_config.version used in telemetry payloads.
related_files.reason_to_check: Ensure build_config.version remains a string-like constant expected by jsonStringify.

related_files.relationship: Provides allocator and network instances at init time and lifetime context.
related_files.reason_to_check: App lifetime must ensure telemetry.init runs when Network is available and telemetry.deinit runs only after Network will no longer call onTick.

tests_to_run: make test (project Makefile wrapper if available)

insights.title: Telemetry uses Network alias and onTick renamed target
insights.problem: Telemetry referenced Runtime; it needed to call onTick/connection helpers on the renamed Network API.
insights.root_cause: Network module rename required telemetry integration update.
insights.solution: Changed runtime -> network field, updated getConnection/releaseConnection/submitRequest usage (commit 0604056).
insights.lesson_learned: Telemetry must use the same networking surface as the runtime to avoid duplication and to share socket pools; test telemetry path separately.

src/ArenaPool.zig.short_description: A thread-safe pool of std.heap.ArenaAllocator instances with optional debug leak/double-free tracking and unit tests.
src/ArenaPool.zig.description:
This module implements an ArenaPool abstraction: a small pool of std.heap.ArenaAllocator instances that can be acquired and released by callers. The pool is intended to reduce allocator churn by reusing arenas via a free list up to a configurable max; arenas beyond that limit are destroyed. It is thread-safe via an internal mutex and optimized to do minimal work while holding the lock (most reset work is done before locking).

The file exists to provide fast short-lived allocation arenas to other parts of the codebase that need temporary allocations (e.g., parsing, temporary DOM/state). It wraps Zig's std.heap.ArenaAllocator and std.heap.MemoryPool(Entry) to manage arena lifecycle and metadata. In Debug builds (builtin.mode == .Debug) it adds a leak/double-free tracker keyed by the debug string passed on acquire() so that test suites and development runs can detect misuse of acquire/release. Several Zig tests live in this file exercising the main behaviors, reflecting that the implementation is low-level and sensitive to correct lifetime semantics.

key_constructs.name: ArenaPool
key_constructs.purpose: Top-level pooled allocator type (alias to the file-scope record via @This()).
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the public type used by callers; its fields define pool configuration (allocator, free list max, retain policy) and runtime state (free list, memory pool, mutex) that other code expects to remain stable. Changes to fields or their names affect pointer arithmetic used elsewhere in the module (see release/reset).

key_constructs.name: Entry
key_constructs.purpose: Represents one node in the free list; contains the ArenaAllocator, next pointer and debug name in debug builds.
key_constructs.reasoning: Entry layout is relied upon to recover the Entry pointer from an ArenaAllocator pointer using @fieldParentPtr("arena", arena). Renaming or reordering fields (or changing the field name 'arena') will break release/reset pointer recovery and MemoryPool usage.

key_constructs.name: DebugInfo
key_constructs.purpose: Small struct to pass a debug string on acquire() that is used to track leaks and double-frees in Debug mode.
key_constructs.reasoning: The debug string is stored verbatim into Entry.debug and also used as the key into the debug leak map. Callers must ensure that the passed slice remains valid for the duration of ArenaPool usage if they rely on the string's address semantics.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Create and initialize an ArenaPool instance with supplied allocator, free_list_max, and retain_bytes.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the canonical constructor that initializes the memory pool and debug map (Debug mode). Consumers use it to obtain a ready pool; changes to semantics here (default values, initialization order) affect all consumers and tests.

key_constructs.name: deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Tear down the pool; in Debug mode asserts no leaks via leak tracker and in all builds deinitializes remaining free-listed arenas and the entry pool.
key_constructs.reasoning: deinit must be called to release resources held by the pool. In Debug builds it will panic if leaks are detected. It walks the free list and calls arena.deinit() on each Entry before destroying the MemoryPool; order matters because MemoryPool.deinit() must run last.

key_constructs.name: acquire
key_constructs.purpose: Return an Allocator pointing to an ArenaAllocator; prefills debug tracking and reuses a free list entry if available, otherwise allocates a new Entry.
key_constructs.reasoning: acquire holds the pool mutex only while manipulating pool state (free list). It initializes Entry.arena with ArenaAllocator.init(self.allocator) for new entries. Debug leak bookkeeping is done here in Debug builds so callers' debug string is important for correct diagnostics.

key_constructs.name: release
key_constructs.purpose: Return an Allocator back to the pool. Resets the arena (with retain limit) outside the lock to reduce contention, performs Debug checks, and either enqueues the entry or destroys it if free list is full.
key_constructs.reasoning: release performs a pointer cast from Allocator back to *ArenaAllocator and then finds the owning Entry using @fieldParentPtr("arena", arena). This pointer math is fragile to layout changes. The code intentionally resets the arena before taking the mutex; this ordering and the use of retain_bytes is important for performance and memory behavior.

key_constructs.name: reset
key_constructs.purpose: Reset a specific Allocator's arena to free memory but keep it in-flight; supports a custom retain value.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function uses the same pointer-casting technique as release to find the ArenaAllocator. It is intended for callers who want to clear used memory but still keep using the same allocator instance.

key_constructs.name: resetRetain
key_constructs.purpose: Reset a specific Allocator's arena with the .retain_capacity option.
key_constructs.reasoning: Convenience wrapper around arena.reset(.retain_capacity) for callers who want to keep arena capacity.

key_behaviors: provides temporary arena allocators to callers via acquire
returns arenas to an internal free list via release
destroys arenas when free list capacity is exceeded
tracks acquire/release counts in debug builds to detect leaks/double-frees
resets arena memory without releasing allocator via reset/resetRetain

pitfalls.mistake: Change the Entry struct layout or rename the 'arena' field.
pitfalls.consequence: Pointer recovery in release/reset (which use @fieldParentPtr("arena", arena)) will break, leading to undefined behavior or crashes.
pitfalls.prevention: Retain the 'arena' field name and its position semantics; if you must rename, update all @fieldParentPtr calls accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Pass a transient/debug string to acquire() that does not remain valid.
pitfalls.consequence: The Debug mode leak-tracker stores the []const u8 slice and uses it as the key; if the slice points to short-lived memory it will lead to incorrect diagnostics or invalid memory access when the map is used.
pitfalls.prevention: Only pass stable string slices (e.g., string literals or memory guaranteed to outlive pool usage) as DebugInfo.debug.

pitfalls.mistake: Assume leak/double-free detection works in Release builds.
pitfalls.consequence: Debug checks are compiled only in Debug mode (builtin.mode == .Debug); in Release builds those checks are omitted and misuse will not be detected.
pitfalls.prevention: Run tests in Debug mode when relying on leak/double-free diagnostics; do not rely on Debug-only behavior for production correctness.

pitfalls.mistake: Hold the mutex for expensive work such as resetting large arenas.
pitfalls.consequence: Increased contention and reduced concurrency.
pitfalls.prevention: The implementation intentionally resets the arena before taking the mutex. Preserve this ordering if editing release.

pitfalls.mistake: Alter how allocator pointers are passed back to release/reset (e.g., wrapping Allocator differently).
pitfalls.consequence: The @ptrCast/@alignCast and fieldParentPtr pattern will fail and retrieving Entry becomes unsafe.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep Allocator.ptr layout compatible with std.heap.ArenaAllocator or update the pointer recovery logic with attention to alignment and layout.

key_sections: Entry: defines per-arena metadata and must remain layout-compatible with pointer recovery logic
acquire: how new arenas are initialized and how free list entries are reused
release: order of operations (arena.reset before lock), debug checks, and free list vs destroy decision
deinit: final cleanup and Debug-mode leak assertion
skip_unless_needed: _leak_track field and its Debug-only uses when working in Release builds
test blocks if you only need to understand runtime behavior (but run them when editing)

test_functions: arena pool - basic acquire and use
arena pool - reuse entry after release
arena pool - free list respects max limit
arena pool - reset clears memory without releasing
arena pool - deinit with entries in free list
tests.example_command:
zig test src/ArenaPool.zig
(or run full suite: make test or zig build test)

relevant_snippets.description: Test 'arena pool - basic acquire and use' validates acquire allocates and that memory written via alloc is readable before release.
Test 'arena pool - reuse entry after release' asserts free_list_len and that released allocator pointer is returned on next acquire.
Test 'arena pool - multiple concurrent arenas' confirms distinct allocator pointers and pooling semantics.

related_files.relationship: Provides logging helpers used by this module for error reporting and debug messages; changes to logging API or formatting may require updating calls here.
related_files.reason_to_check: If you change error logging signatures or formatting conventions, update usage in deinit/release where log.err is called.

related_files.relationship: Build rules determine how Debug vs Release modes are invoked; debug-only leak checks depend on builtin.mode which is affected by build settings.
related_files.reason_to_check: If adjusting build targets or default optimization/mode, be aware that Debug-only checks may be disabled/enabled affecting tests that rely on them.

related_files.relationship: Convenience targets (make test) and CI run patterns call zig test/build; tests here are expected to run in CI via make test.
related_files.reason_to_check: If you change how tests are invoked in CI/Makefile, ensure these unit tests remain included and run in the intended build mode.

data_constants_to_check: free_list_max (passed to init): affects how many arenas are retained in pool
retain_bytes (passed to init): used in release() when resetting an arena

src/browser/interactive.zig.short_description: Collects and classifies user-interactive DOM elements and exposes them as serializable InteractiveElement objects (with tests).
src/browser/interactive.zig.description:
This module discovers interactive elements under a DOM root, classifies why each element is interactive (native tag, ARIA role, contenteditable, event listeners, explicit tabindex), gathers accessibility metadata (role, accessible name, placeholder, value, input type, id/class/href), and produces InteractiveElement records that can be serialized for CDP/MCP responses. It also provides helpers to pre-register those nodes with a registry to populate backendNodeId fields used by remote protocols. The file includes Zig unit tests embedded at the bottom exercising many classification and metadata extraction behaviors.

The design separates concerns: a precomputed ListenerTargetMap is built once from the Page's event-manager data so that per-element classification can quickly check whether event listeners exist. Classification is intentionally conservative (returns null when not interactive) and respects CSS pointer-events:none via Element.hasPointerEventsNone. The module uses an arena allocator passed from the Page for transient allocations (accessible names and URL resolution), and returns slices into that arena; callers must respect the allocator lifetime.

This module ties tightly to the DOM and event subsystems: it references Page internals (page._event_manager.base.lookup etc.), Element/Element.Html methods (getTag(), getTagNameLower(), getAttributeSafe(), isDisabled(), getTabIndex(), is(Element.Html.Input) and input._input_type), Node/TreeWalker traversal, EventTarget pointer identity, and URL.resolve for href normalization. Changes to any of those types or to the EventManager layout will require corresponding updates here. Commit history shows previous refactors that moved the event-manager lookup under base and added backendNodeId population for interactive elements; both are reflected in this file.

key_constructs.name: InteractivityType
key_constructs.purpose: Enum of reasons an element is considered interactive (native, aria, contenteditable, listener, focusable).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to communicate the classification cause to consumers (UI, CDP/MCP clients) and to drive behavior elsewhere; values are intentionally small and discriminative.

key_constructs.name: InteractiveElement
key_constructs.purpose: Container struct holding metadata about a discovered interactive element (node, role, name, listener types, backendNodeId, etc.).
key_constructs.reasoning: Central record type returned by collectInteractiveElements and serialized to clients. Includes backendNodeId to support remote operations that rely on a registered node id.

key_constructs.name: registerNodes
key_constructs.purpose: Registers each InteractiveElement.node with a provided registry and populates backendNodeId for the element.
key_constructs.reasoning: Decouples node registration from collection so callers can choose when to register (e.g., right before sending results to a client). Relies on the registry API returning an object with an id property; registry must accept Node pointers used here.

key_constructs.name: collectInteractiveElements
key_constructs.purpose: Walks a DOM subtree and returns an arena-allocated array of InteractiveElement for every element classified as interactive.
key_constructs.reasoning: Main entry point: performs traversal with TreeWalker.Full, filters by tag (skips script/style/head/template), classifies elements, and populates InteractiveElement fields using helper functions and page state. It returns slices allocated in the provided arena that reference strings from DOM attributes or resolved URLs.

key_constructs.name: ListenerTargetMap
key_constructs.purpose: Type alias for a map keyed by pointer-sized EventTarget identity to a list of event type name strings.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used so classification can perform O(1) checks for whether a target has listeners and to return the list of listener type names for serialization. Implemented with AutoHashMapUnmanaged to avoid heap ownership complications.

key_constructs.name: buildListenerTargetMap
key_constructs.purpose: Builds the ListenerTargetMap by iterating both addEventListener registrations and inline attribute handlers on the page.
key_constructs.reasoning: Precomputes listener sets to avoid re-iterating page event structures per element. Must iterate the exact event-manager internals (page._event_manager.base.lookup.iterator())—this dependency changed in a prior refactor and is fragile to EventManager layout changes.

key_constructs.name: classifyInteractivity
key_constructs.purpose: Determines, for a given Element, which InteractivityType applies or returns null if the element is not interactive.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates the ordered ruleset used to classify elements: pointer-events check, native tags, ARIA roles, contenteditable, event listeners, explicit tabindex. Order matters because native tags and ARIA roles should take precedence in many cases.

key_constructs.name: isInteractiveRole
key_constructs.purpose: Checks an ARIA role string against a compile-time set of interactive roles (case-insensitive).
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses a StaticStringMap initialized at compile time and an ascii.lowerString buffer sized by MAX_LEN to avoid allocation; length guard prevents buffer overflow.

key_constructs.name: getRole
key_constructs.purpose: Returns explicit role attribute if present otherwise supplies an implicit role derived from the element tag and attributes.
key_constructs.reasoning: Combines explicit ARIA role semantics with tag-based implicit roles for common elements (inputs/select/textarea/button/anchor). The mapping includes special handling of input types.

key_constructs.name: getAccessibleName
key_constructs.purpose: Computes an accessible name for an element from aria-label, alt, title, placeholder, input value, or first non-empty text content.
key_constructs.reasoning: Follows a fallback order appropriate for accessible name computation for the purposes of reporting and serialization; returns slices that may be either attribute pointers or arena-allocated text.

key_constructs.name: getTextContent
key_constructs.purpose: Traverses descendant text (skipping script/style) and returns the first non-empty trimmed text chunk or concatenated chunks.
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses TreeWalker.FullExcludeSelf to avoid including the node itself, concatenates chunks with a single space, and returns either a direct slice when only one chunk exists or an arena-allocated buffer for multiple chunks.

key_constructs.name: testInteractive
key_constructs.purpose: Test helper used by the embedded tests: creates a page, inserts HTML into a div and calls collectInteractiveElements.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides the test harness for unit tests in this file; it depends on testing.test_session and page.parseHtmlAsChildren. Tests rely on the test session and page lifetime management performed here.

key_behaviors: collects interactive elements under a DOM root
classifies elements by interactivity reason
builds a per-page listener-target map for fast queries
registers nodes with an external registry to populate backend ids
serializes InteractiveElement records to JSON for remote protocols

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming strings returned (attribute slices, resolved href) outlive the caller
pitfalls.consequence: Use-after-free or invalid memory if the arena allocator used for those allocations is freed before consumers use the slices (e.g., after collectors return).
pitfalls.prevention: Respect and preserve the allocator lifetime passed into collectInteractiveElements; do not convert to longer-lived references without copying into a stable allocator.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing EventManager internals (lookup location or iterator API) without updating buildListenerTargetMap
pitfalls.consequence: buildListenerTargetMap will fail to compile or silently miss listeners, causing misclassification (missing listener-based interactivity).
pitfalls.prevention: When editing EventManager, update callsites here; tests depend on listener discovery and will catch regressions if run.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering Element.getAttributeSafe or Element.Html internals (tabindex/value/input_type) without checking usages here
pitfalls.consequence: Classification and metadata fields (input_type, placeholder, tabIndex, disabled) could become inaccurate, breaking client expectations and tests.
pitfalls.prevention: Run the file's tests after any DOM API changes and verify collected metadata matches expectations.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying the registry.register API signature or return shape used by registerNodes
pitfalls.consequence: registerNodes will fail at compile time or produce incorrect backendNodeId values, causing CDP/MCP operations that rely on backend ids to fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure registry.register returns a record with an id property or adapt registerNodes in lockstep.

pitfalls.mistake: Extending static role maps (isInteractiveRole/isContentRole) without adjusting MAX_LEN buffer logic
pitfalls.consequence: Role strings longer than MAX_LEN will be rejected even if valid; buffer overflow could occur if MAX_LEN logic is removed/changed incorrectly.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep MAX_LEN in sync with the longest role inserted into the static maps and preserve the length guard.

key_sections: buildListenerTargetMap: explains how listener discovery is precomputed and shows dependency on Page._event_manager.base.lookup
classifyInteractivity: contains ordered rules for deciding interactivity and short-circuits (pointer-events none -> not interactive)
InteractiveElement.jsonStringify: shows fields serialized to clients, including conditional backendNodeId
skip_unless_needed: isContentRole: small helper for role classification that is only used in limited contexts
getInputType/getInputValue: trivial wrappers delegating to Element.Html.Input

tests.example_command: zig test src/browser/interactive.zig -fsanitize=address -target x86_64-linux-gnu

relevant_snippets.description: Test 'browser.interactive: button' validates that a <button> is discovered, role and accessible name are set and interactivity_type is native.
Test 'browser.interactive: anchor with href' validates anchor with href is discovered and role/name set.

related_files.relationship: collectInteractiveElements and buildListenerTargetMap use Page internals (page._event_manager, page.base(), call_arena, parseHtmlAsChildren).
related_files.reason_to_check: If Page changes memory lifetime semantics, event_manager layout, or the API for parsing/creating nodes, this file must be updated.

related_files.relationship: This module depends on Element methods: getTag(), getTagNameLower(), getAttributeSafe(), isDisabled(), is(Element.Html.Input), and Element.PointerEventsCache.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifications to attribute accessors, tabIndex semantics, disabled detection, pointer-events logic or Input internals will affect classification and metadata extraction.

related_files.relationship: Tree traversal, Node types, Node.CData/Text handling and pointer identity are relied upon by traversal and getTextContent.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to node kinds or TreeWalker interaction may alter text extraction behavior.

related_files.relationship: ListenerTargetMap keys are EventTarget pointers and inline handler registration iteration uses attribute listener storage; EventTarget layout impacts how listeners are discovered.
related_files.reason_to_check: If inline attributes or event-target pointer identity semantics change, update buildListenerTargetMap accordingly.

related_files.relationship: TreeWalker.Full and TreeWalker.FullExcludeSelf are used for traversal and text extraction.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to traversal semantics (order, skipChildren behavior) will affect which nodes are considered for accessible name extraction and for element discovery.

related_files.relationship: Embedded unit tests use testing.test_session helpers, page creation and removal.
related_files.reason_to_check: If the test harness API changes, the inline tests here will need updating to create pages and parse HTML correctly.

related_files.relationship: Used to resolve href attributes to absolute URLs when collecting elements.
related_files.reason_to_check: If URL.resolve's signature or encoding behavior changes, href normalization here must be rechecked.

tests_to_run: make test (project-wide) to ensure integration with Page and EventManager

insights.title: Switch interactive listener iteration to use event_manager.base.lookup
insights.problem: Code iterating over event manager's lookup needed to reflect EventManagerBase extraction.
insights.root_cause: EventManager.lookup moved under base as part of refactor.
insights.solution: Update buildListenerTargetMap to iterate page._event_manager.base.lookup.iterator().
insights.lesson_learned: Large refactors that extract internals require updating all callers to the new structure; keep changelist of dependent files to avoid missed references.

insights.title: Populate backendNodeId for interactive elements
insights.problem: Interactive elements returned to clients had no backendNodeId, making operations like click/fill fail because MCP/CDP calls expected a backend node identifier.
insights.root_cause: Node registration was performed in multiple places and not consistently populating backendNodeId on InteractiveElement objects.
insights.solution: Added backendNodeId field to InteractiveElement, updated JSON serialization to include it when present, and implemented registerNodes(elements, registry) to register each element's node and store its backend id.
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize cross-cutting tasks (like node registration) into a single helper to avoid mismatch between data returned to clients and what registries expect. Ensure serialization includes backend identifiers when available.

insights.title: Use Element.isDisabled when collecting interactive elements
insights.problem: Disabled checks were duplicated when building interactive element list.
insights.root_cause: Multiple implementations of disabled logic happened independently.
insights.solution: Switch to el.isDisabled() when populating .disabled property of interactive elements.

src/cdp/CDP.zig.short_description: CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol) message router and BrowserContext lifecycle manager
src/cdp/CDP.zig.description:
This module implements the CDP frontend for Lightpanda: it receives JSON CDP messages from a connected Client, parses and validates them, and dispatches them to domain handlers under src/cdp/domains/*. It also manages a single BrowserContext lifecycle (creation, teardown), session/target identifiers, mapping between CDP sessionIds and the internal browser Session, and facilitates emitting CDP events back to the connected client. The file exists to centralize protocol-level semantics (startup session special-casing, session id validation, domain name parsing) and to hold BrowserContext state that must persist across navigations (scripts-on-new-document, intercept state, captured response bodies, inspector session bindings). 

Design decisions visible in the code: it uses arena allocators (message_arena, notification_arena, page_arena, browser_context_arena) to limit per-message or per-context heap churn and to clearly tie lifetimes of temporary allocations to request/message processing or BrowserContext lifetime. Domain dispatch is implemented using a performance-oriented technique: it splits a method like "Page.navigate" at the '.' then selects the domain by matching the domain length and bitcasting the byte slice into fixed-size integers (u16/u24/u32/... as appropriate) and comparing against compile-time asUint(domain_string). This makes adding a domain require updating this mapping carefully. The BrowserContext type contains many responsibilities: it owns the inspector session, node registries, list of isolated worlds, interception state, captured responses map, and registers to Notification events to reflect browser runtime events into CDP events.

Important operational semantics: there is a special STARTUP sessionId handling used during driver startup (eg Puppeteer expects pre-existing targets). The module enforces a single BrowserContext and single page/target at a time (many comments emphasize this), and many teardown/cleanup orders are important: the inspector context group reset and stopping, aborting pending intercepted transfers, unregistering notifications, closing the browser session (closing page) before altering proxy state, and releasing arenas. The module also integrates with Server.Client for sending/receiving JSON messages and the Browser runtime (Browser.init/newSession/closeSession) to manage the actual page(s) and script execution. Commit history indicates prior refactors that removed generics and standardized on a concrete CDP type and Server.Client, and added fields like scripts_on_new_document and next_script_id on BrowserContext.

When editing this file, respect arena lifetimes, notification registration/unregistration symmetry, the single-page assumptions, the special "STARTUP" handling, and the brittle domain dispatch bitcast logic. Changes here commonly ripple into many domain handlers under src/cdp/domains/*. The module is central to protocol compatibility, so tests and format/build steps must be run after edits.

key_constructs.name: URL_BASE
key_constructs.purpose: Default security origin used for new BrowserContext instances (chrome://newtab/)
key_constructs.reasoning: BrowserContext.security_origin is initialized with this value and code elsewhere may depend on this canonical base when creating node/DOM contexts.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Initialize CDP state: build Browser instance, initialize arena allocators and return CDP struct
key_constructs.reasoning: init constructs the Browser used by CDP and sets up arenas; callers must handle and propagate errors from Browser.init and must respect the errdefer browser.deinit() usage in the function until the returned CDP takes ownership.

key_constructs.name: deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Tear down CDP: deinitialize BrowserContext if present, deinit Browser and all arenas
key_constructs.reasoning: deinit contains the correct teardown order and must be preserved; it calls BrowserContext.deinit() if present and then deinitializes the browser and arenas — edits must maintain cleanup order and avoid double-freeing or use-after-free.

key_constructs.name: handleMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Entry point used by Server.Client to hand a single raw JSON message for synchronous processing
key_constructs.reasoning: This wrapper calls processMessage and returns a boolean where false means processing failed; upstream server expects this behavior so any change to return semantics must be coordinated with callers.

key_constructs.name: processMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Parse a single incoming message using the message_arena then dispatch it
key_constructs.reasoning: processMessage resets the message_arena at the end with a retain limit; modifying retain limits or arena usage changes temporary allocations lifetime and memory retention behavior of CDP message handling.

key_constructs.name: pageWait
key_constructs.purpose: Expose a synchronous wait hook that drives pending session runner timeouts/events for the active session
key_constructs.reasoning: This is used for scheduling/processing timeouts from the main server loop and depends on the browser.session presence; callers rely on the specific error.NoPage when no active session exists.

key_constructs.name: dispatch
key_constructs.purpose: Parse the JSON message into an InputMessage and build a Command structure then route to dispatchStartupCommand or dispatchCommand
key_constructs.reasoning: dispatch is central: it validates sessionId (with special STARTUP token), fills a Command with parsed fields and decides whether to call startup handling. It returns protocol error codes using command.sendError on failures — keep these semantics and numeric error codes stable for caller expectations.

key_constructs.name: dispatchStartupCommand
key_constructs.purpose: Handle messages that arrive tagged with the 'STARTUP' sessionId using minimal semantics required for driver startup
key_constructs.reasoning: This implements the special-case behavior (eg Page.getFrameTree) required by some clients during initial handshake; changing this alters client compatibility on initial connection.

key_constructs.name: dispatchCommand
key_constructs.purpose: Extract domain from method (left of '.') and route to the corresponding domain module's processMessage
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses domain length + bitcast/integer comparisons for lookup; maintain exact domain names and lengths when adding/removing domains. This logic is brittle to changes in domain string formatting and to any modifications in asUint helpers; ensure correct numeric sizes (u24, u40, etc.) and that import paths to domains remain correct.

key_constructs.name: isValidSessionId
key_constructs.purpose: Validate that an incoming sessionId matches the active BrowserContext's session_id
key_constructs.reasoning: This implements session isolation: CDP requests carry sessionId to indicate target attachment; it must be strict to reject requests for other sessions because the implementation only supports one BrowserContext/session at a time.

key_constructs.name: createBrowserContext
key_constructs.purpose: Create a new BrowserContext record and initialize BrowserContext state (including calling Browser.newSession and setting up inspector/session binding)
key_constructs.reasoning: This enforces single BrowserContext at a time and produces an id returned to the client; errors during init should leave no partially-initialized state and cleanup must be performed on failure (the init uses errdefer semantics).

key_constructs.name: disposeBrowserContext
key_constructs.purpose: Tear down the current BrowserContext if the provided id matches the active one
key_constructs.reasoning: This validates the id, calls BrowserContext.deinit(), closes the browser session, and nulls out the stored pointer; callers expect a boolean indicating whether the id matched and disposal occurred.

key_constructs.name: sendEvent
key_constructs.purpose: Helper to emit a CDP event JSON message to the client with optional sessionId
key_constructs.reasoning: Wraps sendJSON and collapses null optional fields if desired; domain handlers and notifications call this to emit events and assume the concrete sendJSON behavior and options exist.

key_constructs.name: sendJSON
key_constructs.purpose: Delegate to client.sendJSON to serialize and send a JSON message downstream; hides client options
key_constructs.reasoning: Any change to sendJSON alters how messages are encoded and whether optional fields are emitted; the client.sendJSON contract (emit_null_optional_fields false here) must be preserved or callers updated.

key_constructs.name: BrowserContext
key_constructs.purpose: Encapsulates the CDP-visible per-context state: session, target, node registries, inspector session, arenas, intercept/capture state, registered on-new-document scripts and other domain-related state
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the authoritative in-process store for what CDP clients expect to live across navigations; many domain handlers will read or mutate BrowserContext fields, so changes to shape or semantics require updating domain implementations and possibly tests.

key_behaviors: parse and validate incoming CDP JSON messages
route method names to domain handlers
create and dispose a single BrowserContext
maintain mapping between CDP sessionId and internal session
emit CDP events to the connected Client

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the order of teardown in BrowserContext.deinit (e.g., closing sessions after changing proxy or unregistering notifications out of sequence)
pitfalls.consequence: Could leave inspector contexts attached, leak pending transfers, produce use-after-free when notifications trigger callbacks into partially-destroyed state, or fail when changing proxy due to active connections.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the exact cleanup order: reset inspector context group, stop inspector session, abort intercepted transfers, deinit isolated worlds, unregister notifications, close browser session, then perform proxy restoration and other cleanup.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying dispatchCommand domain-matching logic without following the exact bitcast + integer-width mapping
pitfalls.consequence: Domain names may be misrouted or fail to match; runtime errors or UnknownDomain responses for valid methods can occur.
pitfalls.prevention: When adding/removing domains keep the same pattern (compute indexOf '.' then switch on domain.len and use the corresponding asUint size). Validate with unit tests for each domain string.

pitfalls.mistake: Ignoring arena lifetimes or changing retain limits when calling arena.reset
pitfalls.consequence: Memory may be retained unexpectedly or temporary allocations may be freed too early leading to use-after-free for objects that assume longer lifetimes (e.g., notifications referencing arena-backed memory).
pitfalls.prevention: Respect per-message (message_arena), per-notification (notification_arena), per-page (page_arena) and per-browser-context arena boundaries and their reset/retain semantics.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming multiple BrowserContexts or pages are supported
pitfalls.consequence: Concurrent sessions/targets could be mishandled; session id validation will reject requests and internal assumptions (single target_id, single session) will break.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep invariants: only one BrowserContext and one page/target active; if adding multi-context support update creation, id generation, and domain handlers comprehensively.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering STARTUP special handling for sessionId == "STARTUP"
pitfalls.consequence: Clients (e.g., Puppeteer-like drivers) that expect startup targets/frames may fail to initialize or parse responses incorrectly.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the STARTUP handling branch or ensure equivalent behavior when refactoring client startup semantics.

key_sections: init: constructs Browser and arena allocators; critical to understand resource ownership
dispatch/processMessage: how incoming JSON is parsed into Command and routed
dispatchCommand: domain extraction and routing via bitcast integer comparisons
BrowserContext.init and BrowserContext.deinit: lifecycle, notification registration, inspector/session setup & teardown
skip_unless_needed: lower-level helper definitions inside BrowserContext not related to core routing (some isolated-world internals)
large domain-specific code buried under BrowserContext that only domain handlers use

tests.example_command: zig build test  # run unit tests including CDP domains (if present)

related_files.relationship: Client type used by CDP; Server supplies Client objects that call CDP.handleMessage/sendJSON. Changes to CDP init signature or client.send behavior must be coordinated with Server.
related_files.reason_to_check: If altering message send/receive semantics, error handling or the client API surface, update Server and tests that create Client socketpairs.

related_files.relationship: Domain handlers (Page, DOM, Network, Target, Fetch, etc.) that dispatchCommand routes to. They read and mutate BrowserContext and Command structures.
related_files.reason_to_check: Adding/removing/modifying domain names, or changing Command/BrowserContext layout, requires updates across domain modules and tests.

related_files.relationship: Provides Browser.init, newSession, closeSession, and http client integration used when creating/disposing BrowserContext. Browser lifecycle changes affect CDP behavior.
related_files.reason_to_check: createBrowserContext/newSession and deinit call into Browser; ensure API contract (e.g., newSession return type, inspector session start/stop) is preserved.

related_files.relationship: BrowserContext registers handlers for many notification types and relies on Notification.unregisterAll during deinit.
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing notification API or registration semantics, update BrowserContext init/deinit and event handling code paths.

related_files.relationship: Tests and harness that were updated after refactors; useful to run when changing CDP API or domain dispatching semantics.
related_files.reason_to_check: To validate compatibility and startup behavior across driver-like clients; run when modifying startup/session handling.

tests_to_run: run any domain-specific unit tests under src/cdp/domains if present

insights.title: Remove CDP generics and unify Command/BrowserContext types
insights.problem: CDP code used comptime generics across BrowserContext and Command types which increased complexity across domains and call sites.
insights.root_cause: Generics for CDP_T and Sender made many domain files and calls generic, causing duplication and harder-to-read signatures.
insights.solution: Remove generics: change BrowserContext/comptime CDP_T -> BrowserContext with concrete *CDP pointers; change Command to non-generic struct with Sender union to support both cdp and capture writing. Update dispatch to pass a concrete Command and adjust all domain processMessage functions to accept *CDP.Command (commit 2665312).
insights.lesson_learned: While generics can provide parametric abstraction, for cross-cutting protocol wiring a single concrete representation often improves readability and reduces mechanical churn across many domain modules. When changing such a central type expect many co-changes across domain files.

insights.title: Change proxy restoration to changeProxy(null)
insights.problem: BrowserContext cleanup used restoreOriginalProxy which may not be the correct or available API after proxy change refactors.
insights.root_cause: Proxy management APIs changed; code continued to call an older helper name.
insights.solution: Switch to calling browser.http_client.changeProxy(null) during BrowserContext cleanup and log warnings on failure.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep high-level resource cleanup paths synchronized with lower-level API changes (proxy management) to avoid silent leaks or incorrect state on context close.

insights.title: Add scripts_on_new_document storage to BrowserContext
insights.problem: There was no centralized storage for scripts that must be evaluated on every new document.
insights.root_cause: The CDP Page domain previously lacked BrowserContext fields to persist registered scripts across navigations.
insights.solution: Added scripts_on_new_document: std.ArrayList(ScriptOnNewDocument) and next_script_id counter to BrowserContext and introduced ScriptOnNewDocument struct with identifier and source fields.
insights.lesson_learned: When extending protocol behavior that must persist across navigations, store state on BrowserContext, not ephemeral page objects; include an id generator to ensure unique identifiers.

insights.title: Remove CDP generic wrapper and bind Server.Client directly
insights.problem: The previous CDPT generic existed to inject Browser/Session/Page/Client types for testing, but over time it became used only to inject Client which added unnecessary generic complexity across the CDP implementation and tests.
insights.root_cause: Over-generalization: a generic type abstraction that once served multiple purposes ended up being used for a single dependency, making the code harder to follow and causing extensive boilerplate in tests.
insights.solution: Removed the CDPT generic, renamed cdp.zig to CDP.zig, and made CDP reference Server.Client directly. Updated init/deinit/dispatch/processMessage/pageWait and many other method signatures to the simpler non-generic form. Adjusted tests and testing harness accordingly.
insights.lesson_learned: Avoid keeping generics/APIs that are no longer needed; simplify the module boundary and update dependent code. When removing an abstraction, update tests to mirror the runtime usage (here, switching tests to use a socketpair-backed Server.Client).

test_functions: many CDP domain tests updated to use try testing.context()

insights.title: CDP API surface and initialization contract changed for tests
insights.problem: Tests and callers previously constructed CDP via a generic init with separate http_client and client arguments; after the refactor the init was changed to accept Server.Client and use client.app/client.http internally.
insights.root_cause: The refactor removed the need to pass an HttpClient separately, so function signatures changed.
insights.solution: Updated callers/tests to construct a Server.Client (socket-backed) and call CDP.init(&client). Updated function signatures to return errors (try) where appropriate; test helpers changed to accommodate the new !TestContext context creation.
insights.lesson_learned: Refactors that simplify module API often ripple to test code; update test harnesses to construct real-like dependencies (e.g., socketpairs) rather than mocks when practical.

src/network/Network.zig.short_description: Network runtime: libcurl-backed HTTP connection pool, websocket pool, poll loop and request submission
src/network/Network.zig.description:
This file implements the Network runtime used by the browser to perform HTTP(S) requests, manage long-lived websocket connections, accept incoming sockets, and drive the event loop that integrates libcurl with the application's poll/wakeup model. It exists to own networking resources (connection pools, curl multi handle, wakeup pipe, pollfd array), mediate concurrent use via mutexes, and provide a single place that translates higher-level request submissions into libcurl transfers and HTTP.Connection objects.

Design choices visible in the file: (1) a fixed-size pool of http.Connection objects allocated according to config.httpMaxConcurrent() and tracked through a 'available' doubly-linked list; (2) a separate memory pool for websockets (ws_pool, ws_count, ws_max) to avoid websocket connections starving the short-lived HTTP pool (this is visible in the commit history and fields); (3) a custom allocator adapter ZigToCurlAllocator that implements libcurl.CurlAllocator callbacks and provides malloc/calloc/realloc/strdup/free backed by the Zig allocator and aligned blocks; (4) an explicit wakeup pipe and a pollfd array where index 0 is the wakeup reader and index 1 is the listener socket (if bound) — the remaining entries are reserved for curl file descriptors; (5) a submission queue protected by a mutex to enqueue http.Connection objects for curl multi submission; (6) a tick/callback mechanism (onTick/fireTicks) to allow periodic tasks to run from the main loop; and (7) careful init/deinit pairing and error handling using errdefer in init (globalInit/globalDeinit) so libcurl is initialized once and cleaned up on error or deinit.

This file interacts tightly with other modules: http.Connection (src/network/http.zig) for per-request state, Config.zig for sizing/feature toggles (httpMaxConcurrent, wsMaxConcurrent, tlsVerifyHost, httpCacheDir), Robots.zig and WebBotAuth.zig for robot/crawling policies and authentication, cache/FsCache.zig for optional HTTP cache backing, sys/libcurl.zig for the low-level curl FFI, and App.zig for an arena pool and lifecycle. Changes here affect the runtime behavior of all HTTP requests, websockets, and the CDP server's ability to accept connections, so callers that rely on the Network API (init/deinit/bind/run/submitRequest) are sensitive to API or behavioral changes.

key_constructs.name: Listener
key_constructs.purpose: Small struct wrapping an accepted TCP listener socket and its accept callback/context
key_constructs.reasoning: Listener is used to store the accept socket and the callback to invoke on new connections; edits must preserve the expected fields (socket, ctx, onAccept) and the semantic that pollfds[1] corresponds to this listener.

key_constructs.name: PSEUDO_POLLFDS
key_constructs.purpose: Number of reserved pollfd entries (wakeup pipe + listener)
key_constructs.reasoning: This constant determines the layout/size of the pollfds array; it must remain 2 unless other reserved fds are added, because index computations in preparePollFds/run assume indices 0 and 1 are reserved.

key_constructs.name: MAX_TICK_CALLBACKS
key_constructs.purpose: Maximum number of onTick callbacks allowed
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to bound the callbacks array and assert when registering ticks; changing this affects potential assertions in onTick and capacity for periodic tasks.

key_constructs.name: TickCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Container that pairs a function pointer with an opaque context for onTick callbacks
key_constructs.reasoning: Represents the shape of tick callbacks stored in the Network instance; callers rely on callback.fun(callback.ctx) semantics in fireTicks.

key_constructs.name: ZigToCurlAllocator
key_constructs.purpose: Adapter implementing libcurl.CurlAllocator to route curl allocations through the Zig allocator with required alignment and bookkeeping
key_constructs.reasoning: This singleton maps curl's malloc/calloc/realloc/free/strdup to Zig allocator APIs while storing a small header; its layout and alignment are critical because libcurl expects malloc-compatible memory layout and the header pointer arithmetic is relied upon in realloc/free/strdup.

key_constructs.name: ZigToCurlAllocator.Block
key_constructs.purpose: Header structure placed before returned pointer to store allocation size and enforce alignment
key_constructs.reasoning: Block.fullsize, Block.fromPtr, data(), slice(), and the comptime assert that @sizeOf(Block) == alignment are used throughout; altering this breaks pointer math used in free/realloc.

key_constructs.name: globalInit
key_constructs.purpose: Initialize the ZigToCurlAllocator singleton and call libcurl.curl_global_init with the custom allocator
key_constructs.reasoning: Must be called exactly once before creating curl handles; init() uses it and errdefer's globalDeinit depends on globalInit having been called successfully.

key_constructs.name: globalDeinit
key_constructs.purpose: Calls libcurl.curl_global_cleanup
key_constructs.reasoning: Cleans up global curl state. Paired with globalInit via errdefer in init(); must not be skipped to avoid leaked global state.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Construct and initialize a Network instance: pipes, pollfds, connection pool, ca_blob (if TLS verification enabled), websocket pool, robot_store, web_bot_auth and cache
key_constructs.reasoning: Entry point for creating a Network object. It performs resource allocation and returns a Network value. Error handling uses errdefer to ensure global deinit and frees; callers expect the returned object to own the allocated resources and to call deinit() eventually.

key_constructs.name: deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Tear down a Network instance: cleanup curl multi, close wakeup pipe, free pollfds, free built ca_blob, deinit connections and pools, deinit robot_store/web_bot_auth/cache and call globalDeinit
key_constructs.reasoning: Responsible for cleaning up everything created in init. Order matters: e.g., connections must be deinit'ed before freeing their array; ca_blob memory is freed via allocator.free of the stored pointer.

key_constructs.name: bind
key_constructs.purpose: Create and bind a listening TCP socket, set it to non-blocking, configure socket options and register it in pollfds as the listener
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposes server/listening behavior to higher layers; sets pollfds[1] and the internal listener field and returns error.TooManyListeners if already bound. Respect platform socket option quirks (NODELAY presence) as handled in the function.

key_constructs.name: onTick
key_constructs.purpose: Register a periodic callback executed by fireTicks; protects registration via callbacks_mutex and wakes the poll loop
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by other modules to run periodic tasks on the Network thread. It asserts the callback capacity and uses wakeupPoll so registration becomes visible quickly to the running event loop.

key_constructs.name: fireTicks
key_constructs.purpose: Invoke all registered tick callbacks in order while holding callbacks_mutex
key_constructs.reasoning: Called from the main loop (run) after processing curl completions to let modules submit new requests or flush telemetry; callback implementations may call submitRequest, so reentrancy & mutex design matters.

key_constructs.name: run
key_constructs.purpose: Main event loop: drains submission queue, drives curl multi (perform/process completions), poll on pollfds with timeout, accept connections, drain wakeup pipe, and exit on shutdown when no running curl handles and no pending submissions
key_constructs.reasoning: Central behavior of the Network runtime. It implements the semantics that shutdown stops accepting new connections but waits for outstanding transfers (and possibly for telemetry to be flushed). It also uses platform-specific handling for posix.shutdown errors on BSD/MacOS.

key_constructs.name: submitRequest
key_constructs.purpose: Queue an http.Connection for submission to the curl multi stack and wake the poll loop
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by higher-level HTTP code to schedule a request; it appends to submission_queue under submission_mutex and calls wakeupPoll so the event loop picks it up quickly.

key_constructs.name: wakeupPoll
key_constructs.purpose: Write a single byte to the wakeup pipe writer to interrupt poll
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to notify the main loop of new work (new submission, tick registration) and intentionally ignores write errors.

key_constructs.name: drainQueue
key_constructs.purpose: Dequeue submission_queue entries and attach them to the curl multi handle, creating the multi if necessary; handles failures in conn.setPrivate and carries out connection release on error
key_constructs.reasoning: Converts queued http.Connection nodes into active curl handles; it must create the curl multi lazily and correctly set per-handle private pointers while taking care to release connections on errors.

key_behaviors: allocates and initializes a pool of http.Connection objects at startup
creates a libcurl multi handle lazily on first submission
drives curl_multi_perform and processes transfer completions
manages a wakeup pipe and pollfd array to integrate curl and accepted sockets
accepts incoming TCP connections when bound and notifies provided accept callback
registers and runs periodic 'onTick' callbacks from the network thread
supports an optional filesystem-backed HTTP cache and robot rules parsing

pitfalls.mistake: Treating ZigToCurlAllocator internals as ordinary allocator memory without preserving header alignment/size
pitfalls.consequence: Pointer arithmetic in Block.fromPtr, free, realloc, and strdup will break and cause memory corruption or crashes because libcurl expects malloc-like memory layout.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep ZigToCurlAllocator.Block size/alignment invariant and the comptime assert; ensure any changes maintain the same pointer math and alignment guarantees.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing pollfds layout or PSEUDO_POLLFDS value without updating code that assumes index 0 is wakeup reader and index 1 is listener
pitfalls.consequence: Wakeup signals and accept logic will break; poll will not be interrupted or listener events missed, causing deadlocks or ignored connections.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the reserved indices and update all consumer code (preparePollFds, run, bind) if layout changes are required.

pitfalls.mistake: Closing wakeup pipe endpoints or listener socket from other threads without following the locking/ownership model
pitfalls.consequence: run() may read/write closed descriptors leading to errors; deinit may double-close fds incorrectly.
pitfalls.prevention: Use the provided deinit and shutdown flows; only deinit from the owner thread and ensure proper synchronization if other threads need to stop the loop (use shutdown atomic and wakeupPoll()).

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming shutdown immediately terminates transfers
pitfalls.consequence: Long-lived transfers or websockets may continue until completion; callers that expect immediate termination may leak resources or hang on process exit.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the documented shutdown semantics in run(): it stops accepting new connections but waits for running_handles == 0 and the submission queue to be drained; external code must cancel requests explicitly if immediate termination is required.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering errdefer/globalDeinit pairing in init
pitfalls.consequence: libcurl global state may remain initialized on errors or not be cleaned up at program exit, causing resource leaks or re-init problems across tests.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the init/errdefer globalDeinit pattern and ensure any added early returns still trigger appropriate cleanup.

key_sections: init: constructs Network, allocates pollfds/connection pool and sets up ca_blob/cache/web_bot_auth
ZigToCurlAllocator: critical for libcurl integration and allocation semantics
run: the main event loop that ties everything together (drainQueue, curl_multi_perform, poll, accept, fireTicks)
drainQueue: shows how requests are attached to curl multi and how the multi is created lazily
skip_unless_needed: ZigToCurlAllocator.Block internals (unless making allocator changes)
low-level per-connection deinit loops when only altering high-level control flow

tests.example_command: zig build test  # runs repository unit tests; use make test if available

related_files.relationship: Defines http.Connection used throughout Network for per-request state and to attach handles to curl multi; many functions in Network call into http.Connection methods (init, deinit, setPrivate, etc.)
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes to how Network submits/releases connections or to connection internals (private pointer handling) must be matched here.

related_files.relationship: Network sizing and feature toggles (httpMaxConcurrent, wsMaxConcurrent, tlsVerifyHost, httpCacheDir, maxPendingConnections) are read from Config in init. Changing or renaming config fields affects Network.init.
related_files.reason_to_check: If a config key is renamed or semantics changed (e.g., httpCacheDir → cacheDir), update usage and test vector sizing and error handling.

related_files.relationship: RobotStore is created by Network.init and used to apply robots.txt rules to requests
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to RobotStore initialization or API require Network to adjust creation/deinit and usage.

related_files.relationship: Optional authentication integration created during init (web_bot_auth) to apply bot-auth rules on requests
related_files.reason_to_check: If WebBotAuth.generation or deinit changes, Network.init/deinit may need adjustments.

related_files.relationship: Optional cache implementation used when config.httpCacheDir() is set; Network owns this cache and calls deinit on shutdown
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing cache init/deinit signatures or ownership semantics requires coordinated updates in Network.init/deinit.

related_files.relationship: Thin bindings to libcurl used heavily here (curl_global_init, curl_multi_init, multi_perform, etc.); ZigToCurlAllocator.interface() must match the shape expected by these bindings
related_files.reason_to_check: Any ABI changes to libcurl bindings or allocator function signatures must be reconciled with ZigToCurlAllocator.

related_files.relationship: Network now stores a pointer to App (for arena_pool access per commit history); App lifecycle and pools may be used by Cache or connections
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes in App ownership or arena pools will affect code paths that expect to allocate temporary memory via App in networking / cache operations.

tests_to_run: make build (to ensure linking with prebuilt V8/libcurl still works)

insights.title: Add websocket connection pool and concurrency limits
insights.problem: WebSocket used the same HTTP connection pool and could starve it.
insights.root_cause: No dedicated websocket pool; Network tracked only HTTP connection pool.
insights.solution: Add ws_pool memory pool, ws_count, ws_max, ws_mutex; newConnection/releaseConnection manage websocket connections and enforce ws_max. Initialize ws_max from config.wsMaxConcurrent().
insights.lesson_learned: Different resource classes (short HTTP requests vs long-lived websockets) need separate pooling strategies and accounting to avoid head-of-line blocking.

insights.title: Rename --cache-dir to --http-cache-dir
insights.problem: Flag name ambiguous for HTTP-only cache; better to be explicit for future cache kinds.
insights.root_cause: Config option previously named cache_dir but codebase moved to specialized caches (http).
insights.solution: Rename config field to http_cache_dir and update parsing/usage.
insights.lesson_learned: When adding multiple cache types, prefer explicit CLI/config names to avoid confusion; keep backward-compatibility note if needed.

insights.title: Network now holds App pointer (for arena_pool) and ensures cache deinit
insights.problem: Cache operations needed access to an arena pool on the App struct; Network.init lacked app pointer and Network.deinit didn't deinit cache.
insights.root_cause: Earlier design separated App and Network initializations; cache operations required App's arena_pool for temporary allocations.
insights.solution: Change Network.init signature to accept app: *App and store it; Network.deinit now calls cache.deinit() if present.
insights.lesson_learned: When components need shared utilities (arena pools), pass the owning App/context into subcomponents; ensure resources are deinitialized in the correct order.

insights.title: Create Cache owned by the Network struct
insights.problem: No place to attach a cache instance to the running network, limiting cross-request caching.
insights.root_cause: Cache was added but not wired into Network initialization and configuration.
insights.solution: Add cache: ?Cache to Network struct, initialize it in Network.init when config.cacheDir() is present and set network.cache to the created Cache.
insights.lesson_learned: Top-level network/engine should own shared cross-request services (like cache) to allow global reuse and lifecycle management.

insights.title: Rename Runtime.zig -> Network.zig and standardize http symbol
insights.problem: Module previously named Runtime was imported in many places as Network causing mental mismatch.
insights.root_cause: Historic naming drift between file name and import alias.
insights.solution: Renamed file and normalized imports/identifiers to Network and http types across callers (commit 0604056).
insights.lesson_learned: Consistent module/file naming reduces confusion; prefer API names that match how they're imported in call sites.

src/browser/webapi/Window.zig.short_description: Implementation of the global Window web API bridge: DOM access, timers, events, navigation and cross-origin handling
src/browser/webapi/Window.zig.description:
This file implements the Window web API façade used by the browser runtime. It exposes the Window type (the Zig representation of the JS global window object) and many of the methods and properties expected by web content: navigation and location handling, storage accessors, timer scheduling (setTimeout/setInterval/setImmediate/requestAnimationFrame/requestIdleCallback), event dispatching helpers (reportError, postMessage, queueMicrotask), and convenience utilities (btoa/atob/structuredClone/getComputedStyle/matchMedia). The Window type acts as a bridge between the JS runtime (V8 via the ScriptManager) and the internal Page, Document and event systems. Much of the file is concerned with safe interactions across those boundaries: creating and scheduling callbacks on the page scheduler, allocating temporary data in per-page arenas, and converting between js.Value representations and internal types.

The file integrates tightly with Page (navigation scheduling, frames list, JS scheduler), EventTarget/Event (dispatch semantics), Document (DOM selection and computed style), storage (local/session buckets), and the network/fetch subsystem. There are also several security- and spec-oriented behaviors embedded: cross-origin accesses are handled through a CrossOriginWindow wrapper and an Access union so that callers from different origins receive a restricted object; window.onerror handling follows WHATWG semantics by calling the onerror callback with five arguments and suppressing default error reporting if it returns true; postMessage currently bypasses targetOrigin checking but constructs an event scheduled on the target page's scheduler using a PostMessageCallback. The file also contains logic that mutates page state (for example, sorting frames in getFrame) and must therefore respect Page lifecycle invariants and arena lifetimes.

Historically, this file has been the locus for API-surface and security fixes: a commit updated Window.JsApi definitions to ensure some properties (document and location) are non-deletable, and another introduced the Access/CrossOriginWindow wrappers to limit cross-origin exposures. These changes mean that edits here must respect both web compatibility semantics and the cross-origin constraints enforced by the runtime. Many functions use page-specific arenas and the page's JS scheduler; modifications must keep the lifetime conventions and arena/finalizer patterns intact so that temporary memory and scheduled callbacks remain valid until executed or properly finalized.

key_constructs.name: registerTypes
key_constructs.purpose: Registers the Window and CrossOriginWindow types with the JS binding/system
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the entry point used by the binding generator/initializer to expose Window types to the rest of the system; changing its return shapes or the set of types will affect JS registration and snapshot generation.

key_constructs.name: Window
key_constructs.purpose: Primary type representing the DOM global window object and internal state
key_constructs.reasoning: Holds pointers to Page/Document/EventTarget and manages timers, custom elements, scroll state, and per-window resources; many operations use Window fields directly, so preserving field semantics and invariants is critical.

key_constructs.name: asEventTarget
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the EventTarget pointer for the window (used for event dispatch)
key_constructs.reasoning: Event dispatching and addEventListener/removeEventListener use this to treat the window as an EventTarget; returned pointer must remain consistent with the event manager's expectations.

key_constructs.name: setLocation
key_constructs.purpose: Schedules a navigation caused by script-modified location
key_constructs.reasoning: Delegates to Page.scheduleNavigation with script reason and push state kind; callers expect the same navigation scheduling path as other location changes.

key_constructs.name: fetch
key_constructs.purpose: Window.fetch bridge to Fetch.Init flow that returns a js.Promise
key_constructs.reasoning: Constructs a fetch request using the global Fetch module while tying its lifetime to the page; interacts with network/http.zig and promise handling.

key_constructs.name: setTimeout
key_constructs.purpose: Schedules a one-shot timer via the page scheduler
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses scheduleCallback with metadata (repeat=false, name) and returns a timer id that is stored in Window._timers. Timer lifecycle and removal interact with the page scheduler and _timers map.

key_constructs.name: setInterval
key_constructs.purpose: Schedules a repeating timer
key_constructs.reasoning: Similar semantics to setTimeout but with repeat=true; removal must mark the scheduled callback removed to prevent further runs.

key_constructs.name: requestAnimationFrame
key_constructs.purpose: Schedules an animation frame callback with a small delay
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses scheduleCallback with mode .animation_frame and a short delay; the browser's animation-tick logic and scheduler must honor low_priority flags and ordering semantics.

key_constructs.name: queueMicrotask
key_constructs.purpose: Queues a microtask on the page's JS runtime
key_constructs.reasoning: Calls page.js.queueMicrotaskFunc to ensure microtasks run in the same microtask queue as other JS work; strong coupling with the JS runtime's microtask semantics.

key_constructs.name: clearTimeout
key_constructs.purpose: Cancels a scheduled timer by id
key_constructs.reasoning: Removes the timer from the _timers map and marks the callback as removed. It expects fetchRemove to return an optional and handles absent ids gracefully.

key_constructs.name: reportError
key_constructs.purpose: Creates and dispatches an ErrorEvent, calls window.onerror, and logs if not prevented
key_constructs.reasoning: Implements WHATWG onerror semantics (five args) and dispatches the error event to listeners; its return path (prevent_default and logging) affects telemetry and developer-visible error output.

key_constructs.name: postMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Schedules delivery of a message event to another window
key_constructs.reasoning: Constructs a PostMessageCallback holding message, source, origin and schedules it on the target page's scheduler. It currently ignores targetOrigin checks; uses per-page arenas and the page scheduler for safe cross-page delivery.

key_constructs.name: btoa
key_constructs.purpose: Base64-encodes input bytes using the standard encoder
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses std.base64.standard.Encoder to compute size and encode; callers rely on it returning a slice allocated from page.call_arena, so memory lifetime must match web API expectations.

key_constructs.name: atob
key_constructs.purpose: Base64-decodes input using forgiving rules per WHATWG
key_constructs.reasoning: Trims whitespace, removes padding, validates length and uses standard_no_pad decoder. It returns InvalidCharacterError for malformed input; error values and memory allocation via page.call_arena must be preserved.

key_constructs.name: getFrame
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the Window for a child frame index; sorts frames if not already sorted
key_constructs.reasoning: Has an in-place sort side effect on page.frames and sets frames_sorted flag; callers expect stable ordering after first call and must account for this mutation of the frames array.

key_constructs.name: getFramesLength
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the number of frames in the page
key_constructs.reasoning: Simple accessor but used by cross-origin Access logic and consumers that enumerate frames; casts to u32 and relies on page.frames.items.len correctness.

key_behaviors: schedules JavaScript callbacks on a Page scheduler (setTimeout/setInterval/etc.)
dispatches events to the Page/EventTarget system (reportError/postMessage)
bridges JS global APIs to runtime subsystems (fetch, storage, navigator, performance)
enforces cross-origin boundaries via Access/CrossOriginWindow wrappers
manages per-window resources (timers, custom elements, scroll state, arenas)

pitfalls.mistake: Altering the lifetime or allocation strategy for returned slices or callbacks (e.g., changing to a different allocator) without preserving page arena lifetimes
pitfalls.consequence: Scheduled callbacks or returned slices may reference freed memory leading to use-after-free or crashes when the page scheduler runs the callback
pitfalls.prevention: Keep allocations that are passed to the scheduler or stored in callbacks allocated from the page arena and respect existing finalizer patterns and errdefer usage

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying the _timers map handling or the semantics of ScheduleCallback.removed without updating all clear* functions
pitfalls.consequence: Timers may continue to fire after clearX was called or timer ids may be leaked; external code (tests or pages) will observe wrong timing behavior
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the use of fetchRemove + marking removed flag and ensure atomicity wrt scheduler

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing the cross-origin Access/CrossOriginWindow behavior or the return types of getTop/getParent/getContentWindow
