pitfalls.consequence: Cross-origin pages could gain access to APIs they shouldn't have or tests that rely on restricted wrappers will fail; security semantics would be violated
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the Access union and CrossOriginWindow restrictions; when changing these functions, keep origin checks and the limited surface intact

pitfalls.mistake: Changing reportError invocation order or its use of onerror result
pitfalls.consequence: Event cancellation semantics will differ from WHATWG expectations (onerror returning true should suppress default reporting), and logging/telemetry behavior will change
pitfalls.prevention: Retain onerror call with five args and the prevent_default propagation to event dispatch and logging

pitfalls.mistake: Altering getFrame without preserving the in-place sort and frames_sorted flag semantics
pitfalls.consequence: Frame ordering across subsequent calls may change unpredictably, breaking code and tests that assume stable frame ordering
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the sort-once behavior and the lessThan comparator that uses compareDocumentPosition

pitfalls.mistake: Changing btoa/atob behavior (padding removal, trimming, error returns) without matching WHATWG forgiving-base64 rules
pitfalls.consequence: Pages relying on forgiving decoding/encoding behavior will break and web-platform tests may fail
pitfalls.prevention: Follow the existing whitespace trim, padding removal, invalid-length check, and return error.InvalidCharacterError on malformed input

reading_guide.start_here: Window (the Window type definition and its fields)
key_sections: registerTypes: shows which types are bound to JS
Timer functions (setTimeout/setInterval/setImmediate/requestAnimationFrame/requestIdleCallback and clear* counterparts): how scheduling is implemented and how ids map to ScheduleCallback
Event and error handling (reportError, postMessage, queueMicrotask): how events are created, how onerror/onmessage callbacks are invoked and how event dispatch uses the Page scheduler
Cross-origin handling bits (getTop/getParent/Access/CrossOriginWindow references): critical for security semantics
skip_unless_needed: Simple getters (getConsole/getNavigator/getStorage accessors) which are thin wrappers
Small utility wrappers (btoa/atob) unless debugging encoding issues

tests.example_command: make test  # runs project unit tests (zig build test) and relevant browser tests; for end-to-end HTML tests run the e2e harness

relevant_snippets.lines: unknown (HTML harnesses exercise onerror, timers, postMessage, getFrame ordering)
relevant_snippets.description: Contains tests for window getters, onerror semantics and timer behavior that exercise Window methods
Exercises Access/CrossOriginWindow behaviors and cross-origin access restrictions

related_files.relationship: Page holds per-document runtime state, the JS scheduler and arena used by Window; many Window operations delegate to Page (navigation, scheduling, arenas, frames list)
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing scheduling, arena allocation, frames handling or page lifecycle interactions in Window, inspect Page for matching contract changes

related_files.relationship: Manages V8/JS runtime and function objects (js.Function.Global/Temp) which Window stores and invokes (onerror/onmessage and scheduled callbacks)
related_files.reason_to_check: If altering how js.Functions are stored/called or microtask/macro task interaction, update ScriptManager expectations

related_files.relationship: EventTarget implementations and dispatch logic are used by reportError and postMessage when dispatching events to window
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to event dispatch semantics or Event structure require aligning both sides

related_files.relationship: Window holds a pointer to Document and delegates selection, computed style and custom elements behaviors
related_files.reason_to_check: When editing Window.getSelection or computed style helpers, ensure Document APIs remain compatible

related_files.relationship: Storage bucket types used by getLocalStorage/getSessionStorage
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing storage accessors or lifetimes, validate storage API compatibility

tests_to_run: make e2e or make end2end if HTML integration tests are needed
Run any window-specific HTML tests under the project's test harness (see src/browser/tests/window/*)

insights.title: Mark certain Window accessors as non-deletable
insights.problem: window.document and window.location should not be deletable but were defined with default deletable semantics.
insights.root_cause: Bridge definitions didn't set DontDelete for some core Window properties.
insights.solution: Set .deletable = false for document and location accessors in Window.JsApi to ensure DontDelete attribute is applied.
insights.lesson_learned: Ensure Web API binding definitions explicitly express required property attributes for correct runtime semantics.

insights.title: Return limited CrossOriginWindow wrapper for cross-origin accesses
insights.problem: Previously window properties like parent/top always returned a full *Window even across origins, exposing too many APIs to cross-origin contexts.
insights.root_cause: Window-returning functions didn't distinguish same-origin vs cross-origin callers.
insights.solution: Introduce Access union (window | cross_origin) and CrossOriginWindow type exposing only safe methods (postMessage, length, top/parent accessors that return Access), and change getTop/getParent/getContentWindow to return Access wrappers based on origin checks.

src/browser/Runner.zig.short_description: Implements the page-runner/event-loop helpers for waiting, ticking and high-level waits
src/browser/Runner.zig.description:
This file encapsulates the Runner abstraction used by higher-level code to drive a Page's lifecycle: polling network activity, running scheduled macrotasks, handling CDP socket wakeups and implementing common wait helpers (waitForSelector, waitForScript). It centralizes the wait-and-tick logic so that callers (CLI commands, CDP handlers, tests) have a consistent way to wait for navigation and page readiness without duplicating timing and HTTP/tick coordination logic.

Design-wise the Runner is a thin view over a Session and its Page: it holds pointers to page, session and the browser's HttpClient. The core control loops are _wait/_tick: _wait is a higher-level loop implementing overall timeout semantics (using a Timer to compute elapsed time) and delegating per-iteration work to _tick. _tick is the scheduling function that inspects the page parse state and either runs network ticks, scheduler macrotasks, triggers load/idle notifications, handles CDP socket semantics or returns an advised sleep time to the caller. Wait helpers (waitForSelector and waitForScript) are built on top of tick/_wait: they poll using a parsed selector (parse once) or by executing a small JS expression repeatedly until truthy, honoring timeouts and using recommended sleep times from tick.

This file contains inline Zig unit tests for several behaviors: init when there's no page, waitForSelector/Script timeout and success cases. The implementation contains multiple comptime branches and invariants that are important to respect when editing: behavior differs when is_cdp is true vs false (CDP mode introduces an extra socket and interception differences), the HTTP client may report .cdp_socket, and browser background tasks and V8 macrotasks influence the loop and sleep windows. Historical commit notes indicate the timeout loop was simplified and wait semantics centralized here, so the file is the authoritative place for wait/tick semantics for the runtime.

key_constructs.name: Runner
key_constructs.purpose: Primary module-level type alias (@This()) representing the runner instance structure
key_constructs.reasoning: Runner is the central object accessed by methods in this file; edits that change its layout or expected fields must remain compatible with callers that obtain runner via session.runner() and expect page/session/http_client pointers.

key_constructs.name: Opts
key_constructs.purpose: Options struct used by Runner.init (currently empty placeholder)
key_constructs.reasoning: Present for future extensibility and to keep the init signature stable; consumers construct this when creating a runner.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Constructs a Runner from a Session, returns error.NoPage if the session has no page
key_constructs.reasoning: init enforces the invariant that Runner always has a page pointer; callers rely on error.NoPage for control flow in tests and higher-level code.

key_constructs.name: wait
key_constructs.purpose: Public helper that waits until the requested condition or timeout, delegates to _wait with is_cdp=false
key_constructs.reasoning: Synchronous waiting primitive used by CLI and other synchronous flows; must preserve semantics of returning error on unexpected errors and .done on timeouts as implemented in _wait.

key_constructs.name: waitCDP
key_constructs.purpose: Same as wait but enables CDP socket semantics (comptime is_cdp=true) and returns CDPWaitResult
key_constructs.reasoning: CDP flow depends on distinct behavior when extra CDP sockets are present; this function toggles that behavior via comptime parameter to _wait, so callers using CDP must call waitCDP to get the correct control flow and possible .cdp_socket result.

key_constructs.name: _wait
key_constructs.purpose: Internal timed loop that repeatedly calls _tick, computes elapsed time with a Timer and returns CDPWaitResult
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the authoritative timeout logic for Runner: it uses a single Timer and compares elapsed time against opts.ms. Historical commits simplified timeout logic here; changes to timeout semantics should be made here and preserve how callers interpret .done vs .cdp_socket.

key_constructs.name: TickOpts
key_constructs.purpose: Options passed to per-iteration tick (ms and until WaitUntil)
key_constructs.reasoning: TickOpts controls per-tick timeout and the 'until' condition (done/domcontentloaded/load/networkidle) — any edit changing these semantics impacts how long tick and wait loops allow for page readiness.

key_constructs.name: tick
key_constructs.purpose: Public per-iteration tick wrapper for non-CDP (returns TickResult union), delegates to _tick with is_cdp=false
key_constructs.reasoning: Exposed to code that wants a single tick iteration and an advised sleep time. Must maintain the mapping of _tick's CDPTickResult to TickResult (cdp_socket is unreachable here).

key_constructs.name: tickCDP
key_constructs.purpose: Public per-iteration tick wrapper for CDP mode, delegates to _tick with is_cdp=true
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by CDP server paths; it forwards the CDPTickResult directly so callers handle .cdp_socket appropriately.

key_constructs.name: _tick
key_constructs.purpose: Core per-iteration scheduler: inspects page.parse_state and runs network or macrotask work, may return recommended sleep duration or CDP socket event
key_constructs.reasoning: Most of the file's complexity resides here: it coordinates HTTP ticking, browser macrotasks, load/idle notifications, background tasks and CDP socket wakeups. Edits must preserve the subtle order: run macrotasks before checking network idle, avoid caching http_client.active before calling scheduler.run, and respect the comptime is_cdp branching that changes interception assumptions.

key_constructs.name: waitForSelector
key_constructs.purpose: High-level polling helper that parses a selector once and queries the document until an element matches or timeout occurs
key_constructs.reasoning: Optimized to parse the selector once via Selector.parseLeaky and reuse it across ticks. The function allocates a temporary arena from the session and must release it; callers rely on it returning error.Timeout on expiry and returning a pointer to a Node.Element on success.

key_constructs.name: waitForScript
key_constructs.purpose: Polls by executing a small JavaScript expression repeatedly until truthy or timeout; uses a js Local.Scope and TryCatch per iteration
key_constructs.reasoning: Executes arbitrary script strings in the page context; error handling converts V8 exceptions to error.ScriptError and logs them. It must manage the local scope lifecycle correctly and defer cleanup; timing and tick integration follow the same pattern as waitForSelector.

key_behaviors: polls http client and returns when page reaches requested 'until' condition
runs browser macrotasks and dispatches load events
notifies network almost idle and network idle conditions
supports CDP socket wakeups through specialized tick/wait paths
waits for selector or script truthiness with timeout

pitfalls.mistake: Treating self.page as immutable across an entire wait/tick loop
pitfalls.consequence: session.processQueuedNavigation() can replace the page pointer; using a stale page may lead to incorrect queries or crashes
pitfalls.prevention: Always re-read self.page after calls that may mutate session (e.g., processQueuedNavigation) and avoid caching page pointers across such calls

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming http_client.intercepted is meaningful when in CDP mode
pitfalls.consequence: The code has branches that assume interception can't happen in CDP mode; using intercepted counts incorrectly can mis-evaluate idleness
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the comptime is_cdp branch: when is_cdp is true, code path expects interception isn't possible; do not change interception-related logic without ensuring CDP semantics are preserved

pitfalls.mistake: Changing timeout semantics or replacing timer.read() logic without updating callers
pitfalls.consequence: Historical commits simplified timeout handling and callers rely on _wait returning .done to indicate timeout vs error; altering that contract breaks higher-level callers and tests
pitfalls.prevention: Keep timeout computed from a single Timer and return CDPWaitResult/.done as current implementation does; update tests if semantics must change

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying order of scheduler.run, dispatchLoad, and network checks
pitfalls.consequence: The current ordering avoids missing macrotasks or incorrectly reporting idle network state; reordering may cause flakes in tests or missed background tasks
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the sequence: run macrotasks, then dispatch load, then evaluate http activity/background tasks and then decide on sleep time

pitfalls.mistake: Not respecting comptime is_cdp return value .cdp_socket from http_client.tick
pitfalls.consequence: CDP socket wakeups must be propagated to callers; swallowing .cdp_socket will break CDP server responsiveness
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure http_client.tick results are checked and .cdp_socket is returned from _tick/_wait when comptime is_cdp is true

key_sections: _tick: core scheduling and network/macrotask coordination (most important)
waitForSelector: shows selector parsing reuse and arena lifecycle
waitForScript: shows V8 local scope and TryCatch usage
skip_unless_needed: test blocks at EOF (used for unit verification)
Opts and TickOpts simple struct declarations

tests.example_command: zig test src/browser/Runner.zig -q

relevant_snippets.description: Test: Runner: no page — ensures init returns error.NoPage when session has no page
Test: waitForSelector timeout — constructs a pageTest and expects a Timeout
Test: waitForSelector — success path asserting found element text content
Tests for waitForScript timeout and success

related_files.relationship: Runner polls and manipulates Page parse/load state and uses page.document; changes to Page load state, notifyNetworkIdle/dispatchLoad or parse_state variants require checking Runner logic
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to parse_state enum variants, load state transitions, or dispatchLoad semantics will impact _tick decision branches

related_files.relationship: Runner.init uses session.page and session.getArena/releaseArena; session.processQueuedNavigation may replace the page pointer
related_files.reason_to_check: Session behavior that moves or replaces Page instances affects Runner.init and the assumption that session.page is present and may change during _tick

related_files.relationship: Runner._tick calls http_client.tick and relies on http_client.active, intercepted and .cdp_socket result
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to HttpClient.tick return enum, meaning of intercepted/active counters, or timing behavior will require corresponding updates in Runner's idle detection

related_files.relationship: waitForSelector relies on Selector.parseLeaky and parsed_selector.query semantics
related_files.reason_to_check: Selector API changes (return types, allocation expectations, lifetimes) will break waitForSelector's usage and arena handling

related_files.relationship: waitForScript uses js.Local.Scope and TryCatch primitives to execute code and map errors; V8 exception handling semantics and scope lifetimes are important
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to js execution API, error conversion or local scope lifecycle will require updates to waitForScript

related_files.relationship: Inline tests rely on testing helpers (testing.pageTest, testing.test_session, testing.arena_allocator) to build pages and sessions for tests
related_files.reason_to_check: If tests or test harness change, inline unit tests here may need adaptation

tests_to_run: zig build test (runs full test suite; needed for integration validation)

insights.title: Simplify Runner wait timeout logic and return done instead of error on timeout
insights.problem: Previous timeout logic tried to be smart about resetting ms_remaining based on active work and used error.Timeout for timeouts; this had complex state handling.
insights.root_cause: Complex timing logic was brittle and mixed notions of timeout vs normal completion.
insights.solution: Simplify to compute elapsed ms from timer.read and compare to opts.ms; return .done instead of error.Timeout to indicate normal completion; delegate higher-level handling to callers.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep wait loops simple and return explicit completion enum values; allow callers to interpret timeouts as normal completion when appropriate.

insights.title: Move timeout and busy logic into Runner._wait/_tick
insights.problem: Server-level wait loop duplicated logic for active work detection and timeout management.
insights.root_cause: Inactivity detection was split across Server and lower-level run/tick logic, causing duplication and brittle behavior.
insights.solution: Runner now checks http_client activity and browser macrotasks/background tasks to decide whether to reset timeout; _tick integrated HTTP ticking for CDP mode (commit 1770dc0).
insights.lesson_learned: Centralize scheduling and wait semantics in the Runner so all consumers share consistent behavior; ensure HTTP ticking is coordinated when waiting on CDP sockets.

insights.title: Add Runner.waitForSelector and Runner.waitForScript with tests
insights.problem: Higher level waiting helpers were needed by CLI and actions to wait for selectors/scripts without re-parsing selectors every tick.
insights.root_cause: Previously selector parsing occurred on every tick which is inefficient and caused duplicated work.
insights.solution: Introduce waitForSelector (which parses selector once with Selector.parseLeaky and queries on each tick) and waitForScript (executes JS expression repeatedly). Add tests for both success and timeout paths. Ensure ticks sleep by recommended sleep_ms returned by tick (commit ab6c63b).
insights.lesson_learned: Parse selectors once and reuse the parsed representation across polling loops to reduce overhead; provide dedicated runner-level wait helpers for common use-cases.

src/browser/js/Context.zig.short_description: Represents a V8 execution context mapped to a browser Page, managing module loading, identity, and lifecycle.
src/browser/js/Context.zig.description:
Context.zig defines the Context struct which models a V8 execution context (loosely a Browser Page context) inside the Lightpanda runtime. It bridges Zig-side page/session state with the embedded V8 types and implements the lifecycle, memory ownership, and module-loading/cache behavior required for executing page scripts. The file exists to encapsulate: (1) the mapping between v8::Context and the Zig Context, (2) per-context allocators and arenas, (3) identity maps for object identity across same-origin frames, (4) module compilation/instantiation/evaluation and module cache bookkeeping, (5) tracking of V8 globals and temporaries into session-level storage, (6) microtask queue and profiler lifecycle, and (7) the mechanisms required when V8 calls back into Zig (Caller/local context handling).

Design decisions visible in the file: Context stores both V8 globals and Zig-side ownership metadata so that long-lived V8 handles can be tracked and released reliably; it keeps an arena for the lifetime of the context and a separate call_arena used for short-lived allocations during calls into or from V8. Modules are cached by normalized specifier and have a ModuleEntry struct to coordinate asynchronous vs synchronous compilation/evaluation (module, module_promise, resolver_promise). Module identity to full path mapping uses an AutoHashMap keyed by module identity hash so resolution callbacks can recover the original specifier for nested imports. Many operations require being executed inside a js.Local scope (localScope / local field) — the code enforces that and panics if called without a Caller-local active. Deinitialization carefully clears embedder data in V8, releases arenas back to the pool, resets v8::Global handles, deletes the microtask queue, and pumps the message loop to allow pending tasks to run while the context is still alive.

Context.zig is central for script execution and module resolution. It interacts closely with: Session (for session-wide allocators, tracking of globals/temps and origin management), ScriptManager (for resolving specifiers and preloading imports), Env/Isolate (for V8 isolate operations), Origin (security token), Scheduler (macrotask queue), and the V8 binding layer (js.zig / v8). Because it touches allocation lifetimes and V8 embedding points, edits here require careful attention to allocator choices, order of resource release, invariants around module cache entries (module vs module_promise/resolver_promise), and ensuring that callbacks registered with V8 persist correct external data pointers.

key_constructs.name: Context
key_constructs.purpose: Represents an embedded V8 execution context and owns per-context state (allocators, module cache, microtask queue, identity, profiler handles).
key_constructs.reasoning: Understanding Context is essential for any change that affects script execution, lifetime, or V8 interaction. It is the anchor point for mapping between Zig runtime structures (Page, Session) and V8 contexts; most APIs here are used by callers and V8 callbacks.

key_constructs.name: ModuleEntry
key_constructs.purpose: Holds cached state for a module specifier: compiled module handle, module evaluation promise, and resolver promise for initial loading.
key_constructs.reasoning: ModuleEntry encodes the invariant that compilation, instantiation and evaluation can be interleaved and possibly concurrent; code that touches module_cache must preserve those invariants.

key_constructs.name: fromC
key_constructs.purpose: Return the Zig Context pointer from a raw v8::Context pointer using embedder data.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used in V8 callbacks and resolution paths; modifications to embedder-data layout or how Context is stored must keep this mapping intact.

key_constructs.name: fromIsolate
key_constructs.purpose: Given an isolate, return the (context, v8::Context) pair for the current context in that isolate, falling back to the incumbent context if the current has been destroyed.
key_constructs.reasoning: Handles iframe navigations and destroyed contexts; callers expect a valid Context even if current context embedder data was freed.

key_constructs.name: deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Cleanly dispose of a Context: run scheduler deinit, reset tracked globals/modules, release origin, clear embedder data and free arenas, delete microtask queue and notify isolate.
key_constructs.reasoning: Order of operations matters (enter context, run scheduler, clear embedder data, reset globals, release arena). Edits must preserve this sequence and ensure no dangling pointers remain when V8 keeps contexts alive.

key_constructs.name: setOrigin
key_constructs.purpose: Set the Context's Origin (security token) and update V8 Context SecurityToken so same-origin access works correctly.
key_constructs.reasoning: This manipulates origin reference counts and uses session.getOrCreateOrigin/releaseOrigin; maintain the refcount assertions and token setting to preserve security semantics.

key_constructs.name: trackGlobal
key_constructs.purpose: Append a v8.Global to session.globals so it survives appropriate lifetime and is freed by session cleanup.
key_constructs.reasoning: Recent commits moved global/temp tracking to Session level; editors must preserve session-level tracking rather than reintroducing Context-local tracking which could break lifetimes.

key_constructs.name: trackTemp
key_constructs.purpose: Store a temporary global handle into session.temps (session-level temp set).
key_constructs.reasoning: Same reasoning as trackGlobal; ensures temporaries are tracked against session and cleared appropriately.

key_constructs.name: addIdentity
key_constructs.purpose: Add a pointer to the identity map and get an IdentityResult indicating whether it already existed.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for object identity tracking; relies on identity_arena allocator and identity.identity_map structure — allocator choice and returned found_existing flag are important for callers.

key_constructs.name: localScope
key_constructs.purpose: Initialize a js.Local.Scope: create a HandleScope, enter the V8 context, and set up a js.Local structure for calls into V8.
key_constructs.reasoning: Most Context operations require being performed inside a js.Local scope. This function sets up the environment; callers rely on the localScope/local pair and its deinit behavior.

key_constructs.name: module
key_constructs.purpose: Compile/instantiate/evaluate a JavaScript module, optionally returning a ModuleEntry and interacting with the module cache.
key_constructs.reasoning: Complex flow that handles cached synchronous vs asynchronous load, resolves and preloads imports, uses compileModule/postCompileModule/evaluateModule; any edits must preserve cache invariants and promise wiring.

key_constructs.name: compileModule
key_constructs.purpose: Wrap V8 ScriptCompiler compile to produce a js.Module from source and a provided name (ScriptOrigin).
key_constructs.reasoning: Directly calls V8 APIs and constructs ScriptOrigin/ScriptCompiler::Source; careful with V8 calling conventions and lifetime of handles passed to V8.

key_constructs.name: postCompileModule
key_constructs.purpose: Record module identity mapping and preload/trigger downloads for dependent module specifiers via the ScriptManager.
key_constructs.reasoning: Prepares the module cache and ensures nested imports are preloaded; it relies on script_manager.resolveSpecifier and script_manager.preloadImport and uses the Context and module caches for bookkeeping.

key_constructs.name: evaluateModule
key_constructs.purpose: Evaluate a compiled module; convert evaluation result to a promise, set module_promise entry, and return ModuleEntry when requested.
key_constructs.reasoning: Must correctly handle evaluation errors (including reading module.getException()) and convert non-promise evaluation results to an asserted promise per V8 API; must set module_promise only when cacheable, and return appropriate results.

key_constructs.name: newFunctionWithData
key_constructs.purpose: Create a new v8 Function with an external data pointer for use as callbacks from V8 to Zig.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to register Zig callbacks callable by JS; ensures the external pointer lifetime and function creation uses V8 conventions expected by callback code.

key_constructs.name: resolveModuleCallback
key_constructs.purpose: V8 callback invoked to resolve an import specifier; maps specifier+referrer to a dependent v8::Module (or null on error).
key_constructs.reasoning: This function is part of the V8 embedding contract; it must translate v8 types to Zig, call into Context._resolveModuleCallback and return a module pointer expected by V8. Error paths must return null and log appropriately.

key_constructs.name: stringToPersistedFunction
key_constructs.purpose: Compile a JS function from source string + parameter names and persist it as a global function handle.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to create persistent function handles from dynamically constructed source strings; interact with localScope and ls.local.compileFunction and requires correct persistence lifetime management.

key_behaviors: map v8::Context to Zig Context and vice-versa
manage per-context allocators and call arenas
compile, instantiate, evaluate, and cache ES modules
track long-lived V8 handles into session-level storage
maintain identity map for object identity across same-origin frames
setup and teardown of microtask queue and profilers

pitfalls.mistake: Calling Context APIs that expect an active js.Local without initializing a Caller/local scope.
pitfalls.consequence: Panics (toLocal) or undefined behavior when V8 handles are accessed without an active local scope.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure localScope / js.Local.Scope is initialized (e.g., via Caller) before calling functions like compileModule, stringToPersistedFunction, or toLocal.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the order of resource release in deinit (for example releasing arena before clearing embedder data).
pitfalls.consequence: Dangling pointers in V8 embedder data, memory use-after-free, or leaks if V8 still references embedder data after Context is freed.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve deinit sequence: enter context, scheduler.deinit, reset v8::Global handles, clear embedder data, notify isolate, pump message loop, then release arenas.

pitfalls.mistake: Reverting global/temp tracking back to Context-local storage instead of Session-level storage.
pitfalls.consequence: Finalizer lifetime and object survival across Identity lifetimes will break; objects may be freed too early or leak because Session semantics changed in recent refactor.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect commit insight: use session.globals/session.temps append/put paths (trackGlobal/trackTemp) rather than reintroducing Context-local global/temps lists.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying module_cache/module_identifier invariants (e.g., assuming module will be non-null after compilation without checking).
pitfalls.consequence: Race conditions between asynchronous preload/resolve flows and synchronous compilation paths leading to missing module_promise or double-evaluation errors.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve ModuleEntry lifecycle logic: module may be null during async load, resolver_promise coordinates initial load, and evaluateModule must set module_promise correctly for cacheable modules.

pitfalls.mistake: Allocating per-call temporary memory on the wrong arena (e.g., using context.arena instead of call_arena).
pitfalls.consequence: Memory that should be freed after a call persists for entire context lifetime or conversely freed too early causing use-after-free.
pitfalls.prevention: Follow semantics noted in comments: use call_arena for short-lived allocations inside calls/callbacks, and only reset call_arena when call_depth == 0.

reading_guide.start_here: Context (the struct) — fields show ownership, allocators, and module caches; then read fromC/fromIsolate to understand mapping between v8 and Zig.
key_sections: deinit: teardown ordering and embedder-data clearing
localScope / toLocal / stringToPersistedFunction: how js.Local scopes are created and used
module / compileModule / postCompileModule / evaluateModule: module compilation, caching, and evaluation flow
trackGlobal / trackTemp: how V8 handles are tracked into the Session
skip_unless_needed: low-level v8 API wrapping details that only matter when adding new V8 interop code (e.g., exact ScriptOrigin construction)
large callback blocks unrelated to your change (but read resolveModuleCallback if modifying module resolution)

tests.example_command: make test (or zig build test) from repository root to run the project's unit tests

related_files.relationship: Session supplies session-level arenas, globals/temps storage, origin management, and is the owner for tracked V8 handles referenced by Context.trackGlobal/trackTemp.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any edits to handle tracking, finalizer behavior, or lifetime semantics must be cross-checked with Session's append/put and release semantics.

related_files.relationship: ScriptManager resolves module specifiers, preloads imports and provides helper APIs used by postCompileModule; it coordinates network retrieval and normalization of specifiers.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to module resolution, normalized specifier format, or preload API shapes require corresponding updates in Context.postCompileModule and resolveModuleCallback.

related_files.relationship: Context maps one-to-one with Page in many cases; Page owns per-page arena and call_arena for main world contexts and cooperates with Context for navigation and script execution.
related_files.reason_to_check: If allocator or call depth semantics change, Page must reflect those changes for main-world contexts.

related_files.relationship: Low-level v8 bindings and helper types (js.Local, js.Identity, js.Module) used throughout Context.zig; types and ABI here are assumed by Context implementation.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes in js.zig (types, handle semantics, persist behavior) directly affect Context functions like compileModule, evaluateModule, and persist operations.

related_files.relationship: Contains the isolate and global env state; Context uses env.isolate and env.app.arena_pool; deinit notifies env.isolate.notifyContextDisposed and pumps message loop via env.pumpMessageLoop.
related_files.reason_to_check: If e.g., isolate lifecycle or arena pool semantics change, validate deinit behavior and pumpMessageLoop expectations.

tests_to_run: make test (repo root) or zig build test
run any available JS embedding integration tests in CI that exercise module loading and context teardown
data_constants_to_check: module cache keys and normalization used by ScriptManager.resolveSpecifier

insights.title: Use session-level temps/globals and finalizer creation
insights.problem: Context previously stored globals/temps/finalizer callbacks on Identity; inconsistent with new Session-level tracking.
insights.root_cause: Following finalizer refactor, tracking should live at session to ensure objects survive Identity lifetime.
insights.solution: trackGlobal/trackTemp now append to session.globals/session.temps; createFinalizerCallback implementation moved/rewritten to use Session allocation and new FinalizerCallback layout.
insights.lesson_learned: Keep allocation and lifecycle tied to the broadest relevant scope; Context should delegate to Session for storage of objects that may outlive an Identity.

src/browser/webapi/net/Fetch.zig.short_description: Implements the Web Fetch() WebAPI: builds Request, drives HttpClient, and resolves JS Promise.
src/browser/webapi/net/Fetch.zig.description:
This file implements the Fetch web API surface used by pages running in the Lightpanda browser. It translates a Request/Input into a Response-backed Promise that is resolved or rejected according to networking results. The module bridges higher-level browser concepts (Page, Blob, Request/Response, AbortSignal, DOMException) with the lower-level HttpClient callbacks and the JS promise machinery (page.js). It also contains logic to treat blob: URLs locally without invoking the network stack.

Design decisions visible in the implementation: memory for transient Fetch state is allocated from a Response arena (fetch is created from response._arena), so lifetime and ownership are tied to the Response arena. The implementation relies on HttpClient to drive the network and exposes a set of callbacks (start/header/data/done/error/shutdown) that accept an HttpClient.Response object. Abortion is handled cooperatively by checking AbortSignal._aborted in the header and data callbacks and by returning false / error.Abort depending on the callback signature. The file also contains a Zig test that runs an integration-style HTML fixture via testing.htmlRunner("net/fetch.html").

Because the module interacts with arenas, JS local scopes, and an external HTTP client, edits must respect the ownership, lifetimes, and exact semantics used when resolving/rejecting promises and deallocating Response resources. The commit history notes several refactors: HttpClient.Response signatures changed (necessitating header iterator usage), cookie_origin propagation was added to ensure SameSite cookie behavior, and headersForRequest signature was simplified — these historical points explain some code structure and why certain fields (like page.url as cookie_origin) are set at call sites.

key_constructs.name: Fetch
key_constructs.purpose: Type alias to this file's anonymous struct (holds per-fetch state).
key_constructs.reasoning: Fetch instances are allocated from a Response arena; understanding that Fetch is @This() and thus allocated into another object's arena is critical to avoid use-after-free when that arena is released.

key_constructs.name: Input
key_constructs.purpose: Re-export of Request.Input for callers to pass fetch() input.
key_constructs.reasoning: Other modules use this exported type to create Request inputs compatible with this Fetch implementation.

key_constructs.name: InitOpts
key_constructs.purpose: Re-export of Request.InitOpts used to pass initialization options for Request creation.
key_constructs.reasoning: Maintains API parity with Request and ensures caller-side types match the expected Request initializer.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Main entry point: constructs Request, creates Response & Fetch state, launches HttpClient.request, and returns a js.Promise to the caller.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the primary function invoked when a script calls fetch(). It must prepare headers, pass cookie_origin, handle blob: special-cases, persist the promise resolver, and register HttpClient callbacks.

key_constructs.name: handleBlobUrl
key_constructs.purpose: Handles fetch() when the URL uses the blob: scheme by looking up a local Blob and resolving a Response synchronously.
key_constructs.reasoning: Avoids network I/O for blob: URLs and ensures correct status, headers, and type are set on the Response before resolving the promise.

key_constructs.name: httpStartCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Callback invoked by HttpClient when a request starts; records the HttpClient.Response in the Response object and logs in debug builds.
key_constructs.reasoning: Associates the low-level http response with the higher-level Response wrapper and provides a debug hook for request lifecycle tracing.

key_constructs.name: httpHeaderDoneCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Callback invoked when headers are fully received; populates Response fields (status, url, headers, redirected flag, type) and prepares buffer capacity.
key_constructs.reasoning: Performs origin comparison to set the Response type (basic or cors), copies URL and status text into the response arena, appends headers via headerIterator, and checks abort signal to allow early cancellation.

key_constructs.name: httpDataCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Appends incoming body chunks to an arena-backed buffer and checks for AbortSignal during streaming.
key_constructs.reasoning: Streaming body accumulation must respect arena allocation and abort semantics. It returns an error when aborted so the HttpClient can stop streaming.

key_constructs.name: httpDoneCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Called when the request completes successfully; finalizes Response.body, converts Response to a JS value and resolves the stored Promise resolver.
key_constructs.reasoning: This is the point where ownership transitions: the Response remains valid until JS has a handle. It switches to a JS Local scope to convert the response and resolves the stored resolver. It also sets _owns_response=false since the response is now managed externally.

key_constructs.name: httpErrorCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Invoked on network errors; ensures the Response is deinitialized if still owned and rejects the Promise with a TypeError-like error.
key_constructs.reasoning: Follows the spec-ish behavior where fetch() network errors should reject with a TypeError; also coordinates arena deallocation and avoids leaking Response objects.

key_constructs.name: httpShutdownCallback
key_constructs.purpose: Called on shutdown of the HTTP context; deinitializes the Response (and therefore the arena) if still owned and warns in debug builds.
key_constructs.reasoning: Because Fetch is allocated from the Response arena, this callback must be careful to clear response._http_response, deinit response, and explicitly avoid accessing 'self' after deallocation.

key_constructs.name: IS_DEBUG
key_constructs.purpose: Compile-time flag used to gate debug logging and assertions.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used throughout to provide extra checks and debug logs without affecting release behavior.

key_behaviors: creates a Response and returns a Promise for fetch() calls
special-cases blob: URLs and returns a synthetic Response
registers HttpClient callbacks to accumulate headers and body
checks AbortSignal during headers/body reception to cancel requests
resolves or rejects the JS Promise with a Response or TypeError-like error

pitfalls.mistake: Allocating the Fetch struct from a different allocator than Response._arena or using it after Response.deinit
pitfalls.consequence: use-after-free and memory corruption because Fetch is allocated from response._arena; httpShutdownCallback explicitly deallocates response._arena and warns not to use self afterwards
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the invariant that Fetch is allocated from Response._arena and do not access Fetch fields after calling response.deinit

pitfalls.mistake: Not persisting/retrieving the Promise resolver correctly or using it outside of the appropriate JS Local scope
pitfalls.consequence: Invalid JS handles, crashes, or un-resolved promises leading to memory leaks or broken behavior
pitfalls.prevention: Use resolver.persist() in init then ls.toLocal(self._resolver) inside a js.Local.Scope before resolving/rejecting

pitfalls.mistake: Failing to pass cookie_origin or updating headersForRequest signature assumptions
pitfalls.consequence: Incorrect cookie behavior (SameSite cookies) or mismatches with HttpClient expectations
pitfalls.prevention: Respect that init supplies .cookie_origin = page.url and calls page.headersForRequest(&headers) without extra arena parameters (history/commits changed this API)

pitfalls.mistake: Returning the wrong kind/type from HttpClient callbacks (e.g., returning void where bool is expected or vice versa)
pitfalls.consequence: HttpClient will not behave as intended; streaming may not stop on aborts or header processing may be bypassed
pitfalls.prevention: Match the HttpClient.Response callback signatures exactly: httpHeaderDoneCallback returns bool, httpDataCallback may return error, and error/shutdown callbacks are void

key_sections: init: constructs Request/Response, handles blob:, persists resolver and calls HttpClient.request
httpHeaderDoneCallback: populates Response fields, sets response type via origin comparison, appends headers via headerIterator
httpDataCallback: appends streamed data and checks AbortSignal
httpDoneCallback and httpErrorCallback: finalize and resolve/reject the JS Promise (these change ownership semantics)
skip_unless_needed: IS_DEBUG logging blocks
the test snippet at the bottom (useful for integration test reference but not for behavior reading)

exercised_by: test in same file: WebApi: fetch (uses testing.htmlRunner("net/fetch.html"))
tests.example_command: zig test src/browser/webapi/net/Fetch.zig  -Dtarget=x86_64-linux -I src -isystem src

relevant_snippets.description: Zig test that runs testing.htmlRunner("net/fetch.html") to exercise end-to-end fetch behaviors.

related_files.relationship: Creates Request.Input/InitOpts and is used by Fetch.init to construct the Request; changes to Request.InitOpts or Request.init change how Fetch should invoke it.
related_files.reason_to_check: If Request signatures change (e.g., cookie_origin, header representation), Fetch.init must be updated accordingly.

related_files.relationship: Fetch allocates and populates a Response, uses Response._arena and other fields; Response lifetime management is critical.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to Response.deinit, arena ownership, or how Response is converted to JS will affect fetch behavior and ownership transitions.

related_files.relationship: HttpClient implementation invoked via http_client.request; Fetch registers the callbacks that HttpClient calls.
related_files.reason_to_check: HttpClient.Response callback signatures, headerIterator API, or error-returning semantics are directly relied on by Fetch callbacks.

related_files.relationship: Fetch uses Page for headersForRequest, blob URL lookup, page.url as cookie origin and to access the JS runtime to create/resolve promises.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to Page API (headersForRequest, lookupBlobUrl, js handling) will require corresponding changes in Fetch.init and handleBlobUrl.

related_files.relationship: handleBlobUrl reads the blob slice and mime to populate a Response for blob: URLs.
related_files.reason_to_check: If Blob internal representation (fields) changes, handleBlobUrl mapping must be updated.

tests_to_run: make test (runs full test suite / integration tests used in CI)

insights.title: Adapt Fetch plumbing to Response abstraction and add header iterator usage
insights.problem: Fetch callback signatures changed with HttpClient.Response; header iteration was missing and had TODOs.
insights.root_cause: HttpClient refactor to Response required updates in higher-level webapi handlers.
insights.solution: Change httpStartCallback/httpHeaderDoneCallback/httpDataCallback to accept Response, use response.* accessors, and use headerIterator (or keep TODO but update places where headerIterator now exists).
insights.lesson_learned: When changing low-level network abstractions, propagate changes up the stack and replace TODOs for header handling with the new headerIterator API.

insights.title: Propagate cookie_origin for fetch requests
insights.problem: Fetch requests didn't pass page.url as cookie origin, breaking SameSite cookie behavior.
insights.root_cause: Request construction omitted cookie_origin.
insights.solution: Set .cookie_origin = page.url when building fetch Request.
insights.lesson_learned: Every network path (fetch, XHR, script loads) must provide the originating URL so cookie-scoped logic operates correctly.

insights.title: headersForRequest signature simplified
insights.problem: Callers passed arenas to headersForRequest; after cookie handling centralization the extra argument was unnecessary.
insights.root_cause: Previous cookie/header composition required per-call allocation.
insights.solution: Use page.headersForRequest(&headers) call site; update Fetch to match new signature.
insights.lesson_learned: Remove allocator parameters from higher-level APIs once allocation concerns are moved to lower level modules.

src/App.zig.short_description: Top-level application context: initializes and owns global subsystems and resources
src/App.zig.description:
This file defines the top-level App context used throughout the process. It exists to centralize construction, ownership, and teardown of global subsystems required by the runtime: network, platform integrations, V8 snapshot, telemetry, and an arena pool for short-lived allocations. App acts as the single owner of these resources and provides init and deinit lifecycles so other modules can rely on a consistent global context rather than scattered globals.

Init is responsible for allocating an App structure on the provided allocator and initializing each subsystem in a deterministic order with errdefer rollback semantics. The order matters because some subsystems depend on others (for example Network.init takes the App pointer to access the arena pool). getAndMakeAppDir creates or fetches an OS-appropriate application data directory and returns an allocator-allocated path (or null) — it behaves differently in test builds by returning "/tmp". deinit and shutdown expose two behaviors: explicit deinitialization which destroys owned subsystems in reverse initialization order, and shutdown which reads a shutdown atomic flag from the network subsystem.

Design decisions visible here: use of a single allocator passed into init (App is heap-allocated via that allocator), use of errdefer to unwind partially-initialized resources safely, explicit propagation of the App pointer to subsystems that require back-reference (notably Network), and special-casing behavior for tests with builtin.is_test. These choices centralize ownership and lifecycle while avoiding globals, but require careful ordering and consistent contracts between App and subsystems.

key_constructs.name: App
key_constructs.purpose: Alias to the current struct type (module-level application context type).
key_constructs.reasoning: App is used as the central type name for the top-level context; other modules expect an App pointer to access owned subsystems. Treat it as the canonical application context whose fields represent authoritative ownership.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Allocate and initialize the App context along with its subsystems.
key_constructs.reasoning: init contains the creation protocol: allocate App, set initial undefined fields, initialize each subsystem in a specific order, and use errdefer to unwind on any failure. Editors must preserve the initialization order, errdefer semantics, and allocator usage.

key_constructs.name: shutdown
key_constructs.purpose: Return the shutdown flag read from the network subsystem.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function provides a read-only accessor to the network's shutdown atomic. It relies on the network subsystem exposing and keeping an atomic flag where load(.acquire) semantics are required for correctness.

key_constructs.name: deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Perform orderly teardown of App-owned subsystems and free app resources.
key_constructs.reasoning: deinit must call deinit on owned subsystems and free any allocated app_dir_path, then destroy the App itself on the allocator. The order mirrors reverse init, so changes to init order require mirrored changes here.

key_constructs.name: getAndMakeAppDir
key_constructs.purpose: Obtain OS app data directory and ensure it exists (allocator-allocated path).
key_constructs.reasoning: This helper centralizes platform-specific app directory handling and logs failures. It returns a buffer allocated with the same allocator used for App, or null on failure. In tests, it returns a duplicated "/tmp" string to avoid filesystem mutations. Editors must respect the allocation contract and error-handling behavior.

key_behaviors: allocates and initializes global subsystems
propagates the App pointer into subsystems (Network)
creates or retrieves the app data directory
supports orderly teardown of resources
exposes shutdown status from network via an accessor

pitfalls.mistake: Changing initialization order (init) without updating deinit accordingly.
pitfalls.consequence: Resources may be deinitialized while still needed by other subsystems, causing use-after-free or crashes during shutdown.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain mirrored order between init and deinit; when adding/removing subsystems, update both places and preserve errdefer unwinding.

pitfalls.mistake: Passing a different allocator into subsystems than the one used to create App or to allocate app_dir_path.
pitfalls.consequence: Allocator mismatches lead to freeing memory with the wrong allocator, causing memory corruption or crashes.
pitfalls.prevention: Always use the allocator argument provided to init for App allocation and for any allocator.dupe() calls tied to App lifetime.

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing the signature that passes the App pointer to Network.init.
pitfalls.consequence: Network (or the cache it uses) may no longer be able to access the arena_pool, which caused a previous regression; this can break caching or produce null-pointer behavior.
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the requirement to pass 'app' into Network.init(allocator, app, config) as shown in commit history; treat Network.init as requiring the parent App pointer unless Network's API contract is intentionally changed across the codebase.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering getAndMakeAppDir behavior without preserving the builtin.is_test special-case.
pitfalls.consequence: Unit/integration tests may attempt to create OS-specific application directories or mutate the host filesystem, breaking test isolation and CI expectations.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the test-path behavior (duplicating "/tmp") or ensure equivalent test isolation is provided when running in test mode.

key_sections: init: allocation, errdefer unwinding, and subsystem initialization order
getAndMakeAppDir: platform-specific app data dir handling and test special-case
deinit: resource teardown order and allocator destruction
skip_unless_needed: module-level constants and imports (straightforward), small accessor shutdown

tests.example_command: zig build test || make test

related_files.relationship: Network is initialized here and relies on receiving the App pointer; closely coupled (lifecycle and shutdown flag).
related_files.reason_to_check: If Network.init signature or Network.deinit/shutdown semantics change, App.init/deinit must be updated too.

related_files.relationship: Snapshot.load is called during App.init; snapshot lifetime is managed by App.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to Snapshot.load/deinit semantics or error behavior affect App.init's errdefer and overall startup sequence.

related_files.relationship: Platform.init/deinit are managed by App; platform integration must exist before some other subsystems operate.
related_files.reason_to_check: API or initialization order changes in platform code require mirroring in App.

related_files.relationship: Telemetry is initialized and deinitialized here; App forwards config.mode to it.
related_files.reason_to_check: If telemetry initialization requires different parameters or different deinit semantics, update App accordingly.

related_files.relationship: Arena pool is owned by App and used by other subsystems (e.g., Network cache).
related_files.reason_to_check: ArenaPool parameters or deinit contract changes must be reflected in how App creates and destroys it.

related_files.relationship: App.init receives Config pointer and forwards config.mode to Telemetry.init; Config values influence initialization.
related_files.reason_to_check: If Config shape or fields change, App.init callsites that reference config.mode must be updated.

data_constants_to_check: ArenaPool.init parameters (512, 1024 * 16) if ArenaPool API changes

insights.title: Initialize Network with App pointer
insights.problem: App did not pass itself into Network init which was required for arena_pool usage in cache get.
insights.root_cause: Network.init signature changed to require App pointer (for arena_pool) but App.init still called old signature.
insights.solution: Update App.init to call Network.init(allocator, app, config) and propagate the app pointer into Network.
insights.lesson_learned: When a subsystem needs access to top-level resources (like arena pools), pass the parent context explicitly to avoid global state and make ownership clear.

src/log.zig.short_description: Central logging facility: formatted log emission, value serialization and tests.
src/log.zig.description:
This file implements the project's logging subsystem. It provides compile-time scoped and leveled logging primitives (debug/info/warn/err/fatal), log formatting modes (logfmt and pretty), a serializer for structured data attached to log messages, and small runtime utilities (elapsed timing, timestamp). The logger writes to stderr, serializes structured data fields by introspecting the data via Zig compile-time reflection (@typeInfo/@field), and enforces several compile-time constraints on log messages (max length and allowed characters). The file also contains unit tests that assert the serialized output of logfmt formatting and string escaping behavior.

Why it exists: logging is a cross-cutting concern used by almost every subsystem (network, browser, CDP, telemetry). Rather than rely on third-party crates, this file provides a custom, deterministic lightweight logger tailored to two formats: a machine-friendly logfmt and a human-friendly pretty form. The design favors compile-time checks (message length/characters, use of comptime scope and message text) and runtime determinism (fixed timestamp in test mode). The logger exposes global configuration (opts) which affects format, minimum level and a debug-only scope filter.

How it fits: other modules call the top-level helpers (debug/info/warn/err/fatal) with a comptime Scope and a compile-time msg literal plus a struct of fields to be serialized. The logger uses writer-based APIs and a preallocated buffer in the top-level log() wrapper to avoid allocations during normal stderr emission. The file contains the canonical serialization logic for primitive types, optional values, enums, error sets, slices/strings (with base64 fallback for binary content), and structured values via either a custom .format method or default printing. Tests at the bottom of the file verify formatting specifics and are intended to be run with Zig's test runner.

key_constructs.name: Scope
key_constructs.purpose: Comptime enum of logical logging scopes used as a tag on each message.
key_constructs.reasoning: Scopes are comptime so call sites pass an inlined tag; the compile-time nature ensures message prefixes are stable and allows @tagName() to be used reliably when generating output.

key_constructs.name: Opts
key_constructs.purpose: Configuration struct for the logger: format, level, and debug-only scope filters.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides a single place to control global logging behavior. Note that default values depend on builtin.mode (Debug vs non-Debug).

key_constructs.name: opts
key_constructs.purpose: Global mutable instance of Opts that other modules may adjust at runtime.
key_constructs.reasoning: Changes to this variable affect all logging behavior (e.g., switching to .logfmt or raising the minimum level).

key_constructs.name: enabled
key_constructs.purpose: Determines whether a message for a given scope/level should be emitted.
key_constructs.reasoning: This gate is called at the top of each logging call to short-circuit expensive formatting when not needed; it also implements debug-only scope filtering when builtin.mode==.Debug.

key_constructs.name: log
key_constructs.purpose: Public entry that takes comptime scope/msg and data and writes to stderr if enabled.
key_constructs.reasoning: Manages std.debug.lockStdErr/unlock, sets up a local buffer, constructs a writer, and delegates serialization to logTo; top-level point used by debug/info/warn/err/fatal helpers.

key_constructs.name: logTo
key_constructs.purpose: Per-format dispatcher and validation for comptime message constraints; routes to logLogfmt or logPretty.
key_constructs.reasoning: Performs compile-time validation of the message literal (length <= 30, allowed characters) and selects formatting behavior based on opts.format.

key_constructs.name: logLogfmt
key_constructs.purpose: Produces logfmt output: timestamp, scope, level, msg, and serialized fields in key=value pairs.
key_constructs.reasoning: Iterates the fields of the provided data struct at compile time and serializes each, supporting custom logFmt methods via std.meta.hasMethod.

key_constructs.name: logPretty
key_constructs.purpose: Produces human-friendly pretty output with colored level prefixes and elapsed time.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used for interactive runs (opts.format==.pretty) and includes special-case behavior for console->fatal named "lightpanda" messages.

key_constructs.name: writeValue
key_constructs.purpose: Serializes arbitrary Zig values to the writer in a format-appropriate way.
key_constructs.reasoning: Central serializer using @typeInfo to support primitives, optionals, enums, error sets, slices and custom formatting; failing types trigger @compileError to catch unsupported logging attempts at compile time.

key_constructs.name: writeString
key_constructs.purpose: Serializes string/slice data for logfmt with escaping rules and base64 for binary.
key_constructs.reasoning: Ensures logfmt is stable: prints plain strings when safe, quotes and escapes newlines/quotes, and base64-encodes binary content to keep logfmt parseable.

key_constructs.name: elapsed
key_constructs.purpose: Computes elapsed time from the first log emission using a monotonic timestamp and mutex.
key_constructs.reasoning: Returns elapsed in ms for short durations (< 10s) or s for longer durations; protects first_log with a mutex (last_log_lock) to be safe across threads.

key_constructs.name: timestamp
key_constructs.purpose: Wrapper around datetime.milliTimestamp with a fixed value in test mode.
key_constructs.reasoning: Provides deterministic timestamps during tests (builtin.is_test) so test assertions are stable.

key_behaviors: gates message emission by scope and level
serializes structured data (fields) attached to logs
supports both machine-friendly (logfmt) and human-friendly (pretty) formats
escapes and base64-encodes string/slice contents when needed
provides deterministic timestamp in test mode

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or altering the compile-time checks in logTo for msg length or allowed characters.
pitfalls.consequence: Would break the intent that msg is a short, stable compile-time literal and could introduce runtime parseability issues or compile regressions in callers that rely on those constraints.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the comptime constraints: the message length <= 30 and permitted character set checks are deliberate and enforced via @compileError.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing opts or its default values without considering builtin.mode dependency.
pitfalls.consequence: May change logging behavior in Debug vs Release builds unexpectedly (e.g., too verbose or too silent during development or CI).
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the Debug-dependent defaults and run tests in both modes if defaults are modified.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering last_log_lock or first_log without preserving mutex protection or atomic semantics.
pitfalls.consequence: Race conditions could produce incorrect elapsed times or data races under multi-threaded logging.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain locking around first_log and use the same mutex (last_log_lock) to guard accesses.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying writeValue's supported type handling (e.g., removing base64 fallback or changing optional handling).
pitfalls.consequence: Could make structured logging incorrectly formatted or break log parsing tools that expect logfmt or pretty outputs; tests will fail.
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure any changes to value serialization are backward compatible and update tests accordingly.

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming timestamp() returns real time in tests.
pitfalls.consequence: Tests rely on a fixed timestamp when builtin.is_test is true; changing that will break deterministic assertions.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the test-mode branch intact or update tests if behavior changes.

key_sections: logTo: compile-time validation and format dispatch (ensures message constraints)
logLogfmt: machinery for logfmt prefix + per-field serialization
writeValue & writeString: core serialization logic and escaping/base64 behavior
elapsed & timestamp: time utilities used by pretty formatting and tests
skip_unless_needed: logPrettyPrefix: detailed pretty color/spacing logic (only relevant if altering pretty format)
LogFormatWriter: small helper struct used when custom logFmt methods exist

tests.example_command: zig test src/log.zig -v

relevant_snippets.lines: near end of file (two Zig tests named "log: data" and "log: string escape")
relevant_snippets.description: First test verifies full logfmt serialization of various typed fields and deterministic timestamp; second test verifies string quoting and escaping/encoding behavior.

related_files.relationship: Calls and configures logging options early on (global opts may be mutated at startup); changes to opts default/behavior can affect CLI output
related_files.reason_to_check: If altering default opts or format/level semantics, verify CLI startup messages still match expected format.

related_files.relationship: Telemetry and diagnostics code may rely on logger semantics for emitting telemetry-related events or opt-in messages
related_files.reason_to_check: If changing the pretty output layout or logfmt key names (e.g., $time/$scope/$level/$msg), telemetry ingestion or parsing may be impacted.

related_files.relationship: Many places in runtime will call logger helpers (debug/info/warn/err/fatal); behavior changes affect runtime logs
related_files.reason_to_check: When changing how structured fields are serialized, inspect high-frequency logging call sites for compatibility.

tests_to_run: zig build test  (project-wide Zig test runner)

src/browser/webapi/Document.zig.short_description: DOM Document implementation exposing web API helpers for creating/querying/managing nodes
src/browser/webapi/Document.zig.description:
This file implements the Document abstraction for the in-memory DOM used by the browser runtime. It provides the web-facing Document API (createElement, createElementNS, getElementById, querySelector, createEvent, createRange, append, importNode, adoptNode, etc.) and internal bookkeeping fields for owner document relationships, ready state, element id map, stylesheets, font sets, selection and other per-document state. The Document type is a union supporting generic, HTMLDocument, and XMLDocument specializations; many operations branch on the document type (for example lowercasing tag names for HTML documents, or rejecting CDATA for HTML). Many functions delegate to Page APIs (page.createElementNS, page.setNodeOwnerDocument, page.dupeString, page._factory) and to other DOM modules (Element, Node, Selector, Parser, DOMTreeWalker).

Why it exists: it centralizes web-platform semantics about document creation and queries and acts as the authoritative place for document-scoped state (elements-by-id map, ready state, selection, stylesheets, current script). It mediates operations that involve coordination between the DOM tree (Node/Element), the page allocator/arena, parsing streams, and scripting hooks. The design follows the web platform: document-level factories, node import/adopt behavior, and query APIs that return live collections or single nodes.

How it fits: Document.zig is called by Page.zig when constructing or operating on the main document and is used by script-exposed APIs implemented in the js bridge. It synchronizes with Page for memory ownership, arena allocation, and lifecycle events (page.domChanged, page.removeNode). It interacts closely with Element.zig/Node.zig for node creation and attribute manipulation, Selector for querySelector/querySelectorAll, and parser components for streaming parsing and dynamic markup insertion counters.

The file is large and handles many web edge cases: element name validation, namespace handling, persistence of function listeners via js.Function.persistWithThis, and a mechanism to lazily repopulate the elements-by-id map when removals indicate duplicates may exist. Many APIs return Zig-specific error codes (error.NotSupported) for operations that the spec disallows in certain document types.

key_constructs.name: Type
key_constructs.purpose: Discriminated union enumerating document specializations: generic, html, xml
key_constructs.reasoning: Documents behave differently by type (e.g., tag name casing, content-type, CDATA support); changes to any logic must respect branching based on this union.

key_constructs.name: is
key_constructs.purpose: Type-check helper that returns the underlying specialized document pointer if it matches the requested compile-time type
key_constructs.reasoning: Used throughout to access HTMLDocument/XMLDocument specific APIs; editing must preserve the exact pattern of matching to avoid invalid casts.

key_constructs.name: as
key_constructs.purpose: Unsafe cast wrapper around is that returns *T or panics if wrong type
key_constructs.reasoning: Callers rely on as() when they know the document specialization; altering its behavior affects many call sites.

key_constructs.name: asNode
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the underlying Node prototype that represents the document in the Node tree
key_constructs.reasoning: Document implements Node behaviors via an embedded Node proto; changes affect tree traversal and Node-related operations.

key_constructs.name: getURL
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the document's URL or the page's URL for main documents
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by consumers that need the document location; behavior relies on _url being optionally set for about:blank-style documents.

key_constructs.name: createElement
key_constructs.purpose: Create an element in the correct namespace and with HTML-specific lowercasing behavior; supports 'is' option for custom elements
key_constructs.reasoning: Handles normalization differences between HTML and XML; must maintain lowercasing for HTML docs and correct owner document assignment.

key_constructs.name: createElementNS
key_constructs.purpose: Create an element in the provided namespace without HTML lowercasing; stores unknown namespace URIs for lookup
key_constructs.reasoning: Namespace handling differs from createElement (no lowercase); it also stores namespace URIs for later resolution in page._element_namespace_uris.

key_constructs.name: createAttribute
key_constructs.purpose: Validate attribute name and allocate an Attribute node via the page factory
key_constructs.reasoning: Calls Element.Attribute.validateAttributeName and uses the factory to create the attribute—in-memory allocation and name validation must remain intact.

key_constructs.name: getElementById
key_constructs.purpose: Lookup cached element by id, and lazily repopulate the cache if an id removal was recorded
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains non-obvious logic: empty id returns null, uses _elements_by_id map and _removed_ids set; re-scan of tree may allocate via page.dupeString and re-insert into the map—editing must preserve the failure-tolerant behavior.

key_constructs.name: createEvent
key_constructs.purpose: Factory for a handful of event classes based on a normalized event_type string; enforces max length and maps common names to concrete Event types
key_constructs.reasoning: Serves many event creation code paths (KeyboardEvent, MouseEvent, CustomEvent, etc.); normalization and mapping must remain consistent with callers expecting certain event classes.

key_constructs.name: whatToShow
key_constructs.purpose: Utility that converts optional js.Value for whatToShow into a u32 bitmask, treating undefined as 'show all' (all bits set) and null as 0
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses 4294967295 sentinel for all-bits; this value is implicitly used by TreeWalker/NodeIterator initializers—changing it affects traversal behavior.

key_constructs.name: getActiveElement
key_constructs.purpose: Return current active element, defaulting to body for HTML documents or the document element as fallback
key_constructs.reasoning: Contains logic that tries stored active element, then body (if HTMLDocument), then document element. Many features rely on this fallback chain.

key_constructs.name: append
key_constructs.purpose: Append nodes/text to the document node with validation, special-case DocumentFragment, and owner-document handling
key_constructs.reasoning: Interacts with page.domChanged(), page.removeNode(), page.appendAllChildren(), and validateDocumentNodes—modifying this changes mutation semantics and owner-document recalculation.

key_constructs.name: adoptNode
key_constructs.purpose: Adopt node into this document; disallows adopting Document nodes and removes node from parent
key_constructs.reasoning: Has to preserve parent removal semantics via page.removeNode and reject Document nodes per spec.

key_constructs.name: importNode
key_constructs.purpose: Return a clone of node in this document context, delegating to node.cloneNode
key_constructs.reasoning: Must maintain deep cloning semantics and error handling for invalid node types (Document).

key_behaviors: creates Element/Attribute/Comment/Text/CDATA nodes with correct namespace and owner document
maintains elements-by-id cache and lazy repopulation after removals
maps event type strings to concrete Event objects
implements append/import/adopt semantics with owner-document and connection handling
provides tree traversal factories (TreeWalker/NodeIterator) and whatToShow translation

pitfalls.mistake: Lowercasing tag names for createElement in non-HTML documents
pitfalls.consequence: Incorrect tag name normalization breaks namespace-sensitive behavior and lookupNamespaceURI expectations
pitfalls.prevention: Respect the document Type union branch that chooses lowercasing only for .html

pitfalls.mistake: Removing or changing getElementById's empty-id early-return
pitfalls.consequence: Spec behavior differs; callers expect null for empty id and the elements_by_id cache semantics would be violated
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve the explicit id.len == 0 check and the lazy repopulation logic using _removed_ids

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the 4294967295 constant in whatToShow or changing its meaning
pitfalls.consequence: TreeWalker/NodeIterator will interpret whatToShow incorrectly (should mean 'show all' when undefined)
pitfalls.prevention: Keep undefined => 4294967295 (all-bits set) and null => 0 semantic

pitfalls.mistake: Altering owner-document assignment rules (page.setNodeOwnerDocument) when creating nodes
pitfalls.consequence: Nodes may have incorrect ownerDocument, causing incorrect behavior for node adoption/import and cross-document operations
pitfalls.prevention: When nodes are created for a non-main document, continue to call setNodeOwnerDocument and preserve page.arena allocation patterns

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming page.dupeString always succeeds
pitfalls.consequence: Code currently often catches dupeString failure and returns null; forcing success without handling OOM leads to panics or inconsistent maps
pitfalls.prevention: Respect try/catch patterns and the reality of allocation failures; keep failure-tolerant code paths that return errors or null where present

key_sections: createElement: normalization and custom element 'is' handling
createElementNS: namespace handling and unknown namespace URI storage
getElementById: cache, removed_ids, and lazy repopulation logic
append: document mutation flow, DocumentFragment handling, and page.domChanged interactions
createEvent: event-type normalization and mapping to concrete event classes
whatToShow: how js.Value is translated for TreeWalker/NodeIterator
skip_unless_needed: Trivial getters (getURL, getContentType) and small wrappers that delegate to Page or Node

related_files.relationship: Document delegates allocation, owner-document assignment, node creation, and arena memory management to Page
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to ownerDocument semantics, node creation, or arena allocation requires cross-checking Page APIs and their invariants

related_files.relationship: Element validation and attribute APIs are used for createElement, createAttribute and id tracking
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to attribute validation, Custom element attach logic, or id management must be reconciled with Element implementations

related_files.relationship: Document uses Node for tree operations (firstChild, appendAllChildren, cloneNode) and for implementing the document node
related_files.reason_to_check: Any changes to Node traversal, connection status, or clone semantics directly affect Document behaviors like append/import

related_files.relationship: querySelector/querySelectorAll delegate to Selector module
related_files.reason_to_check: Selector API changes would impact querySelector behavior and must be reconciled

related_files.relationship: Document holds a streaming parser reference and parsing behavior affects dynamic markup insertion counters and script parsing
related_files.reason_to_check: Parser changes can alter parsing lifecycle and interactions with _script_created_parser and throw_on_dynamic_markup_insertion_counter

tests_to_run: run any DOM unit tests that exercise createElement/getElementById/append

src/mcp/protocol.zig.short_description: MCP protocol type definitions, JSON (de)serialization helpers, and unit tests for message formats
src/mcp/protocol.zig.description:
This Zig source file declares the canonical MCP (agent-protocol) types used across the project and provides helpers for serializing/deserializing protocol messages to/from JSON. It exists as the authoritative shape of messages exchanged via the MCP layer (requests, responses, notifications, initialization handshake, capability shapes, and tool descriptions). The file is intentionally self-contained: it defines enums and structs that map directly to JSON structures and includes small helper utilities (minify and JsonEscapingWriter) that are used when constructing JSON payloads that must conform to specific formatting and escaping rules.

The file also bundles unit tests (Zig test blocks) verifying parsing and serialization behavior: parsing Request payloads, nested parsing of InitializeParams, formatting Response structs with and without errors, the behavior of JsonEscapingWriter when writing escaped characters, and the Tool JSON serialization (including the comptime minify helper). Because many components of the system (the CDP server, MCP router, and any code paths that emit or consume MCP messages) depend on these definitions, this file acts as the source of truth for the shape and stable JSON representation of MCP messages.

Design decisions visible in the file: fields are typed to map directly to std.json.Value and concrete types used by std.json.parseFromSlice / std.json.Stringify; optional fields are represented with Zig nullable types in a way that aligns with std.json options (e.g., emit_null_optional_fields). The minify function is a comptime helper intended to produce a compact JSON string literal (used for Tool.inputSchema), and it uses @setEvalBranchQuota to support heavy comptime operations.
