JsonEscapingWriter provides a small custom writer that delegates to std.json.Stringify.encodeJsonStringChars to produce escaped JSON string characters but intentionally writes the escaped chars without surrounding quotes — this behavior matches how some fields (like inputSchema) are embedded raw in the JSON output via beginWriteRaw / endWriteRaw in the Tool.jsonStringify method.

key_constructs.name: Version
key_constructs.purpose: An enum of protocol version string constants (multiple released dates) with a 'default' alias
key_constructs.reasoning: Other parts of the codebase will select a protocol version; this enum centralizes allowed protocol versions and exposes a default value for backward compatibility. When editing, keep the string labels and the 'default' mapping semantics intact because other modules rely on these exact values.

key_constructs.name: Request
key_constructs.purpose: Struct representing a JSON-RPC style request (jsonrpc, id, method, params)
key_constructs.reasoning: Used as the primary input type for parsing incoming MCP messages via std.json.parseFromSlice. Field names and types must match the JSON expected shape (jsonrpc string, optional id as std.json.Value, method string, optional params).

key_constructs.name: Response
key_constructs.purpose: Struct representing a JSON-RPC style response (jsonrpc, id, result, error)
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to emit server replies. The presence/absence of result vs error and the naming (the field is literally named "error" using Zig string field name escape) is important for std.json.Stringify to output exactly the expected JSON used in tests and by clients.

key_constructs.name: Error
key_constructs.purpose: Structured error payload with code, message and optional data
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates standard JSON-RPC error structure; the integer codes are mapped from ErrorCode enum to ensure consistent error values across the system.

key_constructs.name: ErrorCode
key_constructs.purpose: Enum mapping error names to JSON-RPC numeric error codes (includes custom application codes)
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when creating Error structs to ensure canonical error codes are used. Code values must remain stable because clients may rely on the numeric codes.

key_constructs.name: Notification
key_constructs.purpose: Struct for JSON-RPC notifications (no id field)
key_constructs.reasoning: Represents fire-and-forget messages; shape must match clients that send/expect notifications.

key_constructs.name: InitializeRequest
key_constructs.purpose: Convenience struct for the initialize request message shape
key_constructs.reasoning: Typed to help parse/validate initialization handshake payloads; it embeds InitializeParams to make nested parsing explicit in tests and callers.

key_constructs.name: InitializeParams
key_constructs.purpose: Parameters included in the initialize request (protocolVersion, capabilities, clientInfo)
key_constructs.reasoning: Describes client capabilities and version; other code depends on the exact field names and expected nested shapes when negotiating features.

key_constructs.name: Capabilities
key_constructs.purpose: Client capabilities shape that can include experimental fields, roots and sampling
key_constructs.reasoning: A central place to add optional capability flags; structure and nullable fields must be preserved for compatibility.

key_constructs.name: Tool
key_constructs.purpose: Represents a tool's metadata and input schema, and provides jsonStringify for custom serialization
key_constructs.reasoning: Tool.jsonStringify uses beginWriteRaw to embed inputSchema without extra escaping. The ordering and raw write behavior here is relied upon by Tool serialization tests; modifications must preserve these semantics and field names.

key_constructs.name: minify
key_constructs.purpose: A comptime helper that removes whitespace from a JSON string literal while preserving string contents
key_constructs.reasoning: Used to produce compact inputSchema strings at compile time for inclusion in Tool.inputSchema. The function is comptime and uses @setEvalBranchQuota, so changes could impact build-time evaluation or require raising branch quotas.

key_constructs.name: TextContent
key_constructs.purpose: Comptime generic that builds a 'text' content struct with provided text type
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by CallToolResult to define returned content type(s) and kept generic for compile-time specialization.

key_constructs.name: CallToolResult
key_constructs.purpose: Comptime generic producing a struct for tool call results containing TextContent(T) and error flag
key_constructs.reasoning: Simplified representation for tool call results (commit history shows image content was removed); keep this shape consistent with call sites that expect content as an array of TextContent entries.

key_constructs.name: JsonEscapingWriter
key_constructs.purpose: A custom writer that encodes slices as JSON-escaped string characters into an inner writer (without surrounding quotes)
key_constructs.reasoning: Used where JSON string characters must be written escaped but embedded raw into surrounding JSON. The drain vtable implementation and the writer semantics (using last slice as pattern repeated 'splat' times) are subtle and must be preserved for compatibility with std.json.Stringify usage in Tool.jsonStringify and tests.

key_behaviors: parses incoming JSON-RPC requests into typed Request structs
serializes Response structs to compact JSON output for sending to clients
provides comptime JSON minification for embedding schemas
escapes JSON string characters for raw embedding via JsonEscapingWriter

pitfalls.mistake: Treating minify as a runtime function
pitfalls.consequence: minify is intended for comptime evaluation (uses @setEvalBranchQuota); using it at runtime may fail or negatively affect performance and evaluation quotas
pitfalls.prevention: Respect that minify is designed to run at comptime (call sites in the repo use it in comptime contexts); if runtime behavior is required, add an explicit runtime serializer elsewhere instead of changing minify.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying field names or JSON ordering
pitfalls.consequence: std.json.Stringify output and tests expect precise field names and ordering. Changing names or ordering can break tests and external clients that rely on exact JSON shapes.
pitfalls.prevention: When editing structs, preserve JSON field identifiers and the semantics for optional fields (emit_null_optional_fields toggle). Run the file's tests and any integration tests that consume MCP payloads.

pitfalls.mistake: Altering JsonEscapingWriter.drain semantics or vtable setup
pitfalls.consequence: Incorrect escaping or writer behavior will corrupt embedded JSON fields and break consumers that expect raw escaped content (Tool serialization test will fail). The writer uses a specific convention: it treats the last element of data[] as a 'pattern' to be repeated splat times.
pitfalls.prevention: If modifying drain, ensure encodeJsonStringChars is still used and that the last slice handling and splat repeat logic remain correct. Validate with the JsonEscapingWriter unit test.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing ErrorCode numeric values
pitfalls.consequence: Clients and tests rely on these numeric error codes; changing them is a breaking change.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep ErrorCode numeric mappings stable; if new codes are needed, add new enum members rather than renumbering existing ones.

key_sections: Request: core input shape parsed from incoming JSON
Response: server reply shape and the escaped 'error' field naming
Tool and Tool.jsonStringify: custom serialization logic for embedding inputSchema
JsonEscapingWriter: custom writer used by Tool.jsonStringify and the tests
minify: comptime helper used to compress schema literals
skip_unless_needed: lesser capability structs (LoggingCapability, PromptsCapability, etc.) if you only need message parsing logic
the small empty capability marker structs (they exist for typing and future extension)

tests.example_command: zig test src/mcp/protocol.zig -I src

relevant_snippets.description: Test that an initialize request JSON parses into Request and nested InitializeParams with expected fields
Tests for response and error formatting ensuring deterministic JSON string output
JsonEscapingWriter and Tool serialization tests which validate escaping semantics and minify/comptime integration

related_files.relationship: Server-side component that will parse incoming MCP Request values and emit Response/Notification messages defined here
related_files.reason_to_check: If you change any request/response shapes, IDs or version handling in protocol.zig, Server.zig must be updated to parse/emit the new shapes consistently.

related_files.relationship: Modules that implement specific MCP methods (initialize, ping, callTool, etc.) and depend on types from protocol.zig
related_files.reason_to_check: Handler signatures and JSON (de)serialization logic depend on InitializeParams, Tool, and CallToolResult shapes defined here.

tests_to_run: zig build test  # run full test suite if protocol types are widely changed
data_constants_to_check: Version enum values and the 'default' alias (consumers may read Version.default)
ErrorCode numeric mappings used by clients

insights.title: Simplify CallToolResult content types
insights.problem: Protocol had both TextContent and ImageContent variants; screenshot/tool changes made ImageContent unused.
insights.root_cause: Protocol evolved but old types remained, increasing complexity.
insights.solution: Removed ImageContent and simplified CallToolResult to use TextContent directly (commit 1854627).
insights.lesson_learned: Keep protocol minimal and update all call sites during refactor; removing unused variants reduces maintenance surface.

insights.title: Expose/rename protocol default/latest version constants
insights.problem: Code referred to mcp.Version.latest in places; new default constant introduced to change default version without renaming latest.
insights.root_cause: Need to distinguish 'latest' vs 'default' for backward compatibility when selecting a default protocol version.
insights.solution: Added Version enum entries and a 'default' alias mapping to the desired default (2024-11-05) while 'latest' still maps to the newest (2025-11-25).
insights.lesson_learned: When shipping multiple protocol version values, separate the 'default' and 'latest' semantics so code can opt-in to older compatibility defaults while still exposing current latest value.

src/browser/webapi/IntersectionObserver..short_description: Implementation of the IntersectionObserver Web API and its Entry objects for the headless DOM.
src/browser/webapi/IntersectionObserver..description:
This file implements a headless-browser version of the DOM IntersectionObserver API and the IntersectionObserverEntry objects. It exists to provide a JS-exposed API that scripts can use to observe element visibility/intersection in a headless environment where there is no real layout engine. The code is designed around the project's arena allocation model (per-page arenas), explicit reference-counted ownership (lp.RC), and the project's js bridge to expose native types to V8. The implementation purposely simplifies geometry: it treats elements as fully visible when they are attached to the document tree and assumes a fixed viewport (1920x1080) when no Element root is supplied.

Key design decisions visible in the code include: using page-managed arenas for short-lived objects (init allocates the observer on a page arena), managing observer lifetime via lp.RC with acquire/release helpers, tracking observed elements in an ArrayList, tracking prior intersection states in an AutoHashMapUnmanaged, and collecting pending IntersectionObserverEntry objects for delivery to JS. The file carefully separates entries that were never exposed to V8 (which are explicitly deinit'd by native code) from objects that are handed to V8 (which are expected to be finalized by the VM). The file also exposes a JsApi Bridge describing constructor, methods and the IntersectionObserverEntry accessors to the JS environment.

key_constructs.name: IntersectionObserver
key_constructs.purpose: Primary native type representing an observer instance and its internal state.
key_constructs.reasoning: This type holds all observer internal state (observed elements, pending entries, thresholds, root, root margin), ties to a page arena, and exposes behavior that must be coordinated with Page and Session lifecycles. Changes to its fields affect JS-visible behavior and memory lifetime semantics.

key_constructs.name: IntersectionObserverEntry
key_constructs.purpose: Representation of a single observation event (entry) capturing time, target, rects and ratio.
key_constructs.reasoning: Entries are created and either pushed to a pending list (native-owned) or handed to V8. They contain their own arena and an lp.RC for safe lifetime management across native and VM worlds. The deinit method is used for entries that never reached V8.

key_constructs.name: ObserverInit
key_constructs.purpose: Type for constructor options: root, rootMargin and threshold variants.
key_constructs.reasoning: Options are parsed in init; root can be a Node (document or element) and threshold supports scalar or array forms. The mapping of document to null is an explicit pragmatic choice noted in comments.

key_constructs.name: init
key_constructs.purpose: Constructor initializer creating an IntersectionObserver on a page arena with provided callback and options.
key_constructs.reasoning: Allocates memory from the page's arena, duplicates threshold and rootMargin into the arena, resolves Node roots into Element or null, and sets up internal ArrayLists and maps. An agent must respect arena lifetime and the need to call page.releaseArena on errdefer.

key_constructs.name: deinit
key_constructs.purpose: Tear-down logic run when the native object is being fully destroyed.
key_constructs.reasoning: Releases the JS callback, deinitializes native-only pending entries and returns the page arena. This function assumes it owns pending entries that were never given to V8 and must not be called while the page or session resources are already freed.

key_constructs.name: acquireRef
key_constructs.purpose: Increment the native reference counter (lp.RC) for the observer.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when handing references to other subsystems to prevent premature deallocation. Consistency with releaseRef is required for correct lifetime behaviour across Session/V8 interactions.

key_constructs.name: releaseRef
key_constructs.purpose: Decrement the observer's lp.RC and let Session handle finalization when refs hit zero.
key_constructs.reasoning: Part of the refcount model introduced in the repository history; the ordering of unregister/release operations around this call matters to avoid use-after-free bugs.

key_constructs.name: observe
key_constructs.purpose: Start observing a target Element: add to observing list, register observer on Page and check initial intersection.
key_constructs.reasoning: Will append to _observing and call page.registerIntersectionObserver when first observed; it calls checkIntersection immediately so the first observation can produce an entry. Must preserve arena ownership for newly created pending entries and schedule delivery if any are produced.

key_constructs.name: unobserve
key_constructs.purpose: Stop observing a specific Element: remove from observing list, clear previous state and pending entries for that target, and possibly unregister observer on Page.
key_constructs.reasoning: Removes any native-only pending entries for the target (deinit'd immediately). When unobserving leads to zero observed elements, it calls page.unregisterIntersectionObserver; the order of removal/unregister matters relative to lifetime.

key_constructs.name: disconnect
key_constructs.purpose: Stop observing all targets: clear pending entries and previous states and unregister from the Page if needed.
key_constructs.reasoning: Explicitly deinit's pending entries (native-only), clears internal collections while retaining capacity, and unregisters from the Page before clearing observing items. Lifetime ordering is important here to avoid Page accessing freed state.

key_constructs.name: takeRecords
key_constructs.purpose: Return and clear the pending native entries (duplicate into page.call_arena).
key_constructs.reasoning: Used when delivering records to JS; uses the page arena duplication facility to return a slice and then clears the pending_entries list. Consumers must schedule delivery as required.

key_constructs.name: calculateIntersection
key_constructs.purpose: Compute simplified intersection data between target and root/viewport.
key_constructs.reasoning: Because the engine has no layout, this function uses getBoundingClientRect and then returns fully intersecting results if the element has a parent, or zero intersection if detached. It also uses a shared zero_rect to avoid allocations for non-intersecting elements.

key_constructs.name: checkIntersection
key_constructs.purpose: Decide whether an entry should be created for a single target and enqueue it if needed.
key_constructs.reasoning: Creates IntersectionObserverEntry in a fresh arena only when the threshold/previous-state logic indicates a report should be generated. Always updates _previous_states to track transitions. The entry creation uses page._factory.create to produce DOMRect wrappers for JS exposure.

key_constructs.name: checkIntersections
key_constructs.purpose: Run checkIntersection for all observed targets and schedule delivery if entries were produced.
key_constructs.reasoning: Called by Page when some global event might change intersections; leads to scheduleIntersectionDelivery when pending entries are present.

key_constructs.name: deliverEntries
key_constructs.purpose: Convert native pending entries to JS call arguments and invoke the observer callback in a local JS scope.
key_constructs.reasoning: Uses page.js.localScope to get a V8 local scope, duplicates entries via takeRecords, and calls the callback with (entries, observer). It catches JS exceptions via js.TryCatch.Caught and logs errors. After handing entries to V8, ownership transitions; note that entries handed to V8 are not deinit'd here (they are expected to be finalized from the JS side).

key_constructs.name: zero_rect
key_constructs.purpose: Shared zero-valued DOMRect used for non-intersecting cases to avoid allocations.
key_constructs.reasoning: A single mutable var used as a canonical empty rect. Editors must ensure semantics remain read-only from callers and that it is never released as an arena object.

key_behaviors: creates IntersectionObserverEntry when element intersection first meets threshold or changes
maintains per-target previous state to detect transitions
registers/unregisters observer with Page when observing list goes from 0<->1
delivers pending entries to JS callback in a V8 local scope

pitfalls.mistake: Deallocate observer resources or call releaseRef() before unregistering it from Page or removing registrations.
pitfalls.consequence: Page may access freed memory leading to use-after-free or crashes (historical bug referenced in commit history).
pitfalls.prevention: Ensure unregistering from the Page happens before any operation that could free the observer; preserve ordering around unregister and releaseRef calls.

pitfalls.mistake: Calling deinit or releasing arenas for entries that were already handed to V8 or are still referenced by VM-side finalizers.
pitfalls.consequence: Double-free or invalid memory access when the VM finalizer runs, or leaking objects if not transferred properly.
pitfalls.prevention: Only deinit entries that are known to be native-only (pending_entries that were never given to V8). When entries are passed to V8 via deliverEntries, ownership transfers and native code must not deinit them.

pitfalls.mistake: Modifying the shared zero_rect (or treating it as an arena-allocated DOMRect).
pitfalls.consequence: If zero_rect is changed or freed mistakenly, code relying on a stable empty rect will break or cause memory issues.
pitfalls.prevention: Treat zero_rect as a static shared value; do not release it via session.releaseArena.

pitfalls.mistake: Freeing or reusing the arena used to create an IntersectionObserver while the observer or its pending entries are still live.
pitfalls.consequence: Subsequent operations will access invalid memory (arena was released).
pitfalls.prevention: Respect page.getArena / page.releaseArena conventions; observe the errdefer patterns in init and other allocation sites.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing the public JsApi names, prototype chain, or accessor signatures without updating V8 bridging code.
pitfalls.consequence: Breaking JS consumer code or causing the bridge to fail to register class IDs.
pitfalls.prevention: Any change to JsApi (Meta.name, prototype_chain or accessor functions) must be coordinated with V8 bridge expectations and tests.

key_sections: IntersectionObserver: struct fields and invariants (why arenas, RC, and collections are present)
init: how options are parsed and memory is allocated
checkIntersection / calculateIntersection: core decision logic for when entries are created
deliverEntries: how entries are handed to JS and the expected ownership transfer rules
skip_unless_needed: JsApi bridge boilerplate and accessor definitions (unless changing the JS API)
test harness invocation at file bottom (htmlRunner)

tests.example_command: zig test src/browser/webapi/IntersectionObserver.zig || zig build test && make test

relevant_snippets.description: The Zig test case registration calling testing.htmlRunner("intersection_observer", .{}) — this is the integration test that exercises the API behaviour.

related_files.relationship: Page owns arenas and is responsible for registering/unregistering intersection observers, scheduling delivery and providing JS local scopes and factories used here.
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to registerIntersectionObserver/unregisterIntersectionObserver, getArena/releaseArena, scheduleIntersectionDelivery, or the JS bridging/local scope APIs in Page will affect this file.

related_files.relationship: Session provides lifetime management and may be involved when releaseRef triggers finalization.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to session-level finalizers or lp.RC semantics require corresponding adjustments here (releaseRef/deinit behaviour).

related_files.relationship: Element provides getBoundingClientRect and parentNode; Intersection calculation relies on these Element semantics.
related_files.reason_to_check: If Element.getBoundingClientRect or parentNode semantics change, calculateIntersection and visibility heuristics may need to be revisited.

related_files.relationship: DOMRect wrapper type used for entry rects; entries call page._factory.create to wrap DOMRect values.
related_files.reason_to_check: Changes to DOMRect construction or lifetime semantics affect how entries are created and handed to JS.

related_files.relationship: JS bridge utilities used for exposing types and calling into V8.
related_files.reason_to_check: Modifications to js.Bridge, js.Function.Temp, localScope, or TryCatch behaviour will impact JsApi and deliverEntries interactions.

insights.title: Switch to pure RC for entries / observers
insights.problem: Entries that were never handed to V8 had ambiguous finalization semantics (deinitIfUnused), causing duplicated code paths.
insights.root_cause: Previously entries relied on RC counters combined with finalizer logic to determine whether to deinit.
insights.solution: Remove deinitIfUnused logic; entries that are never given to V8 are deinit'd immediately and observer register/unregister logic acquires/releases explicit refs (commit 77b60ce).
insights.lesson_learned: Prefer clear ownership semantics: if an object is owned only by native code, deinit it there. Only use VM finalizers for VM-exposed objects.

insights.title: Unregister before releaseRef to avoid potential use-after-free
insights.problem: Calling releaseRef before unregistering observer could cause code paths to access already freed memory (use-after-free).
insights.root_cause: Observer was unregistered after calling releaseRef, and releaseRef could free resources leading unregister to access freed state.
insights.solution: Swapped order: call page.unregisterIntersectionObserver(self) before releaseRef to ensure the page won't use the object after it's freed.
insights.lesson_learned: When releasing references that may trigger deallocation, remove registrations/callbacks that may reference the object first to avoid use-after-free.

insights.title: Finalizer/refcount migration touches IntersectionObserver
insights.problem: Finalizer model moved to reference-counted acquire/release semantics and session-level tracking; IntersectionObserver needed to be updated to use new RC and release semantics and to avoid relying on context-level weak refs.
insights.root_cause: Previous weak/strong pattern didn't work across v8 IsolatedWorlds; finalizers refactor centralized finalizer logic in Session.
insights.solution: Introduced lp.RC fields, acquire/release functions, tracked pending entries with RC, adjusted observe/unobserve/disconnect to manage _rc._refs and call releaseRef appropriately, and moved unregister after release checks.
insights.lesson_learned: When changing object lifetime semantics globally, update web API types to use acquire/release patterns consistently and ensure registration/unregistration logic decrements refs safely.

insights.title: Allow Document as IntersectionObserver root (treat as viewport)
insights.problem: Only Element roots were supported; rooting an observer at the Document wasn't supported.
insights.root_cause: Implementation only accepted Element pointers; Document nodes are a different Node variant.
insights.solution: Change ObserverInit.root to be ?*Node and accept .document by mapping it to null (treating document like viewport since there is no rendering).
insights.lesson: Mapping Document to viewport (null) is an acceptable pragmatic trade-off while the engine doesn't render, but note it's not strictly spec-correct.

src/cdp/domains/network.zig.short_description: CDP 'Network' domain implementation: maps CDP network methods/events to internal browser/network state and emits CDP events.
src/cdp/domains/network.zig.description:
Provides the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) Network domain implementation for Lightpanda. This file exists to translate incoming CDP commands (Network.*) into operations on the internal BrowserContext, cookie jar, and Transfer/network structures, and to serialize/emit Network.* events back to connected CDP clients. It centralizes request/response lifecycle event emission (requestWillBeSent, responseReceived, loadingFinished, loadingFailed) and implements CDP methods that touch networking state (enable/disable, setExtraHTTPHeaders, cookies operations, and getResponseBody). The file is written to use concrete CDP types (CDP.Command and CDP.BrowserContext) and relies on arena allocators for memory management when copying header/cookie strings into BrowserContext arenas.

Design decisions reflected here: CDP method handling uses a string-to-enum mapping and a single processMessage entrypoint that dispatches to typed helper functions; cookie handling delegates to the storage domain helpers (CdpStorage) and the internal Cookie/Jar structures; serialization logic for requests/responses is implemented via writer structs (TransferAsRequestWriter and TransferAsResponseWriter) that expose jsonStringify to the CDP JSON writer. The file intentionally ignores partition-key (CHIPS) support but logs a not-implemented warning to allow compatibility with clients (e.g., Puppeteer) that may send partition keys. Several helpers carefully join duplicate response header values to match Chrome behavior.

Interaction points: uses CDP.Command and CDP.BrowserContext (session, arena, extra_headers, captured_responses), Transfer and Transfer.req for network metadata, Notification messages (RequestStart/ResponseHeaderDone/RequestDone/RequestFail) to translate runtime notifications into CDP events, and Cookie utilities for domain/path parsing. The unit tests embedded in the file exercise header and cookie behaviors.

key_constructs.name: processMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Entrypoint for CDP Network domain messages; resolves action string to enum and dispatches to specific handler functions.
key_constructs.reasoning: Any edits to how Network messages are routed must preserve this dispatch shape and the mapping between action names and handler functions; it is the single public entrypoint invoked by CDP router code.

key_constructs.name: setExtraHTTPHeaders
key_constructs.purpose: Parses headers from the CDP params and stores them onto the BrowserContext extra_headers arena-backed list.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function copies header strings into the BrowserContext arena and manages capacity; modifications must preserve allocator usage and ownership so stored header pointers remain valid for later requests.

key_constructs.name: cookieMatches
key_constructs.purpose: Helper that checks whether an internal Cookie matches name/domain/path criteria from a delete request.
key_constructs.reasoning: Used by deleteCookies to selectively remove cookies; subtle domain leading-dot normalization and optional path handling are important to maintain.

key_constructs.name: deleteCookies
key_constructs.purpose: Implements CDP Network.deleteCookies by deleting cookies from the BrowserContext cookie_jar matching name/domain/path/url.
key_constructs.reasoning: Relies on Cookie.parseDomain/parsePath and must not use Cookie.appliesTo because deleteCookies semantics differ; it iterates backwards and swap-removes cookies so array mutation semantics must be preserved.

key_constructs.name: getCookies
key_constructs.purpose: Responds to Network.getCookies: prepares URL list (fallback to current page) and writes cookies filtered by those URLs.
key_constructs.reasoning: Constructs PreparedUri list, calls cookie_jar.removeExpired, and returns CookieWriter — any change to URL handling or PreparedUri semantics affects what cookies are returned to clients.

key_constructs.name: getResponseBody
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the captured response body for a previously seen request id, encoding to base64 when necessary.
key_constructs.reasoning: Parses request id using idFromRequestId and reads captured_responses; must preserve base64 semantics and memory allocation into cmd.arena for the encoded buffer.

key_constructs.name: httpRequestStart
key_constructs.purpose: Notification handler that emits Network.requestWillBeSent for a new Transfer/request and injects extra CDP headers into the request.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function mutates the Transfer request headers (adds extra headers from bc.extra_headers) and emits a JSON object using TransferAsRequestWriter — ensure header mutation and subsequent serialization remain consistent.

key_constructs.name: TransferAsRequestWriter
key_constructs.purpose: Writer struct that serializes a Transfer into a JSON object for requestWillBeSent.
key_constructs.reasoning: Encapsulates JSON serialization for requests; historically extended to include Cookie header. Any changes must maintain the exact JSON shape expected by CDP consumers.

key_constructs.name: TransferAsResponseWriter
key_constructs.purpose: Writer struct that serializes Transfer response metadata for responseReceived, including status, mimeType, charset, and joined headers.
key_constructs.reasoning: Joins duplicate headers with '\n' to match Chrome; uses a temporary unmanaged map and the provided allocator — changes must respect allocator lifetime and header joining behavior.

key_constructs.name: idFromRequestId
key_constructs.purpose: Parses CDP requestId strings of the form "REQ-{d}" into an integer transfer id.
key_constructs.reasoning: CDP request identifiers are serialized as strings; failure to preserve the "REQ-" prefix check will mis-handle getResponseBody lookups and return InvalidParams errors.

key_behaviors: emit Network.requestWillBeSent when a request starts
emit Network.responseReceived when headers are available
emit Network.loadingFinished or Network.loadingFailed on request completion
manage extra HTTP headers stored on BrowserContext
create/remove cookies via CDP Network methods
return captured response bodies (base64 if needed)

pitfalls.mistake: Treating header/cookie strings as ephemeral stack-allocated memory
pitfalls.consequence: Stored pointers in BrowserContext (extra_headers) or cookie arenas will become invalid and cause use-after-free or corrupt output when serializing events later.
pitfalls.prevention: Always allocate copied header/cookie strings into the BrowserContext arena or the same arena lifetime expected by consumers (use cmd.arena or bc.arena consistently as in setExtraHTTPHeaders).

pitfalls.mistake: Using Cookie.appliesTo in deleteCookies
pitfalls.consequence: Would incorrectly delete cookies with shorter paths or broader matches; original code intentionally avoids appliesTo.
pitfalls.prevention: Preserve cookieMatches logic that does exact name comparison and optional normalized domain/path equality checks.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing header-joining logic in TransferAsResponseWriter
pitfalls.consequence: CDP clients may observe duplicate headers as separate entries rather than a single joined value, deviating from Chrome behavior and breaking client expectations (e.g., chromedp).
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the join-on-duplicate behavior (join with "\n") and allocator usage for gathered header strings.

pitfalls.mistake: Ignoring 'REQ-' prefix parsing rules in idFromRequestId
pitfalls.consequence: getResponseBody will not find the intended captured response and will return RequestNotFound/InvalidParams incorrectly.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain exact prefix validation and integer parsing semantics for requestId strings.

key_sections: setExtraHTTPHeaders: shows how headers are copied into bc.arena and how extra_headers is managed
cookieMatches/deleteCookies/getCookies: cookie parsing, matching and how cookies are filtered/returned
httpRequestStart/httpResponseHeaderDone/httpRequestDone/httpRequestFail: Notification handlers that emit CDP events for network lifecycle
TransferAsRequestWriter/TransferAsResponseWriter: JSON serialization logic for requests and responses
skip_unless_needed: idFromRequestId: small utility (only relevant when working with request id parsing)
tests at the bottom if not debugging cookie/header interoperability

tests.example_command: zig test src/cdp/domains/network.zig

relevant_snippets.description: Test 'cdp.network setExtraHTTPHeaders' — creates a browser context, calls Network.setExtraHTTPHeaders twice and asserts extra_headers length is 1 (de-dup/behavior).
Test 'cdp.Network: cookies' — exercises setCookie/setCookies/getCookies/deleteCookies and validates cookie jar behavior.

related_files.relationship: cookie storage helpers and CDP Storage domain; network cookie ops delegate to storage helpers and Cookie/Jar implementation
related_files.reason_to_check: Changing cookie serialization, parsing or the Cookie API requires corresponding changes here (deleteCookies/getCookies/setCookie) to keep semantics aligned.

related_files.relationship: Defines CDP.Command and BrowserContext types used by this domain (routing and sendEvent/sendResult APIs)
related_files.reason_to_check: If CDP.Command or BrowserContext shapes change (session_id, arena, extra_headers), processMessage and notification handlers must be updated.

related_files.relationship: Defines Cookie and Jar types and parsing utilities used extensively here (parseDomain/parsePath/appliesTo).
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change in Cookie semantics (domain normalization, appliesTo) will affect matching and enumeration logic in this file.

tests_to_run: zig build test (project-wide) or make test
data_constants_to_check: CDP JSON shapes expected by clients (Network.requestWillBeSent/Network.responseReceived fields)
header join separator '\n' expectation for client compatibility

insights.title: Adapt domain API to concrete CDP types and fix function parameter types
insights.problem: Domain code used anytype for BrowserContext and used inconsistent import/allocator ordering.
insights.root_cause: Removal of CDP generics forced explicit typing across domains.
insights.solution: Change processMessage signatures to accept *CDP.Command, change helper functions to accept *CDP.BrowserContext and Allocator, reorganize imports. Update event handlers (httpRequestFail, httpRequestStart, httpResponseHeaderDone, httpRequestDone) to accept typed BC pointer (commit 2665312).
insights.lesson_learned: Type migrations in core protocol code are broad and require updating many small helpers; run a tidy pass to ensure all domain functions accept concrete types and that allocator types are consistent.

insights.title: Include Cookie header when serializing Transfer for CDP network.requestWillBeSent
insights.problem: CDP domain serialization omitted cookies for requests constructed from a Transfer.
insights.root_cause: Cookie generation was previously in page headers helpers; after moving cookie generation into Transfer, the CDP serializer needed to call jar.forRequest itself.
insights.solution: When serializing TransferAsRequestWriter, call jar.forRequest to include Cookie header in the serialized object if cookies exist.
insights.lesson_learned: When moving cookie/header responsibilities, update protocol serializers to reflect where headers are now composed.

src/cdp/domains/storage.zig.short_description: CDP 'Storage' domain: cookie management and CDP serialization for cookie lists.
src/cdp/domains/storage.zig.description:
Implements the CDP Storage domain surface required for retrieving, setting, and clearing cookies at the browser-context scope. This file exists to provide Storage.clearCookies, Storage.getCookies, and Storage.setCookies methods used by CDP clients. It contains types mirroring CDP cookie structures (CdpCookie, CookiePartitionKey) and a setCdpCookie helper that converts a CdpCookie into the internal Cookie representation and inserts it into the Cookie.Jar, using arena allocation for transient copies. The CookieWriter and writeCookie JSON serialization utilities produce the CDP-compatible cookie array returned to clients.

Design choices: The storage domain reuses the internal Cookie and Cookie.Jar types for authoritative cookie management, delegating parsing and path/domain defaulting to Cookie.parseDomain/parsePath. setCdpCookie intentionally ignores partitionKey (with a logged warning) and rejects features not implemented (priority != Medium, sameParty != null, sourceScheme != null) by returning NotImplemented to callers. CookieWriter may accept an optional list of PreparedUri to filter which cookies to return — used by Network.getCookies to restrict results to provided URLs.

Interaction points: Storage domain functions are invoked by CDP router when Storage.* methods arrive, and other domains (Network) call into CdpStorage.setCdpCookie and CdpStorage.PreparedUri to coordinate cookie semantics. Tests embedded at the bottom exercise storage set/get/clear cookie flows.

key_constructs.name: processMessage
key_constructs.purpose: Dispatches incoming Storage domain action strings to respective methods (clearCookies, getCookies, setCookies).
key_constructs.reasoning: As the domain entrypoint, maintain the mapping between action names and functions; changes to action names or signatures require updates here.

key_constructs.name: clearCookies
key_constructs.purpose: Clears the cookie_jar for the browser context, optionally validating browserContextId param.
key_constructs.reasoning: Basic destructive operation on cookie state; ensure browserContextId validation uses byte-equality against bc.id and that capacity retention semantics are unchanged (clearRetainingCapacity).

key_constructs.name: getCookies
key_constructs.purpose: Returns the CookieWriter representing current cookies, optionally filtered by browserContextId; removes expired cookies first.
key_constructs.reasoning: Removes expired cookies before serialization and hands a CookieWriter back to the CDP serializer — the writer controls filtering based on optional PreparedUri list and must be preserved.

key_constructs.name: setCookies
key_constructs.purpose: Sets multiple cookies provided as CDP CdpCookie array by delegating to setCdpCookie for each element.
key_constructs.reasoning: Loops over param.cookies and calls setCdpCookie; ensure param parsing and browserContextId checks are preserved to prevent accidental cookie insertion into wrong context.

key_constructs.name: CdpCookie
key_constructs.purpose: CDP-facing struct describing cookie fields accepted by Storage.setCookies and Network.setCookie (name, value, url, domain, path, etc.).
key_constructs.reasoning: This struct is the authoritative deserialization target for incoming CDP cookie params — changing default values or field types will affect callers and may require CDP client compatibility considerations.

key_constructs.name: setCdpCookie
key_constructs.purpose: Converts a CdpCookie to an internal Cookie and inserts it into the CookieJar, allocating temporary memory via an ArenaAllocator.
key_constructs.reasoning: This function enforces unsupported-field checks (priority, sameParty, sourceScheme) and performs domain/path/defaulting using Cookie.parseDomain/parsePath; it uses an ArenaAllocator tied to cookie_jar.allocator so memory lifetime is bounded during add(); edits must preserve arena usage to prevent leaks or invalid pointers.

key_constructs.name: CookieWriter
key_constructs.purpose: Provides jsonStringify to serialize a list of Cookie objects into the CDP response format (Storage.getCookies).
key_constructs.reasoning: Can optionally filter cookies by PreparedUri list; its writeCookies implementation iterates cookies and calls writeCookie — any change to serialization (field names or types) is a public API change that affects CDP clients.

key_constructs.name: writeCookie
key_constructs.purpose: Serializes a single internal Cookie to a CDP-compatible JSON object (name, value, domain, path, expires, size, httpOnly, secure, session, sameSite).
key_constructs.reasoning: Produces the exact field set expected by clients that inspect Storage.getCookies; preserves specific defaults (e.g., expires -> -1 when null, sameSite mapping) that client tests and tooling may rely on.

key_behaviors: set cookies from CDP inputs into internal cookie jar
get cookies and serialize them into CDP Storage.getCookies response
clear cookies for a browser context
validate browserContextId when provided

pitfalls.mistake: Allocating cookie name/value/path/domain on the wrong allocator or with incorrect lifetime
pitfalls.consequence: Inserted Cookie objects hold pointers to memory that may be freed or outlive their intended arena, causing corruption or crashes when cookies are read/serialized later.
pitfalls.prevention: Use an ArenaAllocator derived from cookie_jar.allocator as done in setCdpCookie and ensure correct errdefer arena.deinit() semantics (cookie.add copies what it must).

pitfalls.mistake: Assuming partitionKey semantics are supported
pitfalls.consequence: CDP clients that pass partitionKey should not fail; this file currently logs and ignores partitionKey in setCdpCookie and elsewhere — removing this handling would break compatibility.
pitfalls.prevention: Continue logging not-implemented and silently ignoring partitionKey where present to maintain compatibility with Puppeteer workflows.

pitfalls.mistake: Changing writeCookie output fields or formats (e.g., expires default -1, domain leading-dot handling)
pitfalls.consequence: Consumers (tests, clients) expect the specific CDP storage cookie shape; changing formats can break client code and tests.
pitfalls.prevention: Keep the exact field names and default semantics; if changes are required, coordinate with tests and consumers.

pitfalls.mistake: Relaxing the NotImplemented checks for priority, sameParty, sourceScheme
pitfalls.consequence: If these fields are accepted but not fully implemented in Cookie or Jar semantics, it will lead to inconsistent behavior; previously-change required returning NotImplemented.
pitfalls.prevention: Maintain the current rejection behavior unless the underlying cookie implementation actually supports those fields.

key_sections: setCdpCookie: conversion rules from CdpCookie to internal Cookie and arena usage
CookieWriter/writeCookie: JSON output format expected by CDP clients
tests at the bottom: examples of set/get/clear flow for actual behavior
skip_unless_needed: enum declarations (SameSite, CookiePriority, CookieSourceScheme) unless extending support

tests.example_command: zig test src/cdp/domains/storage.zig

relevant_snippets.description: Test 'cdp.Storage: cookies' sets two cookies with Storage.setCookies and asserts Storage.getCookies returns the expected two cookies, then clears and re-checks.

related_files.relationship: Defines the internal Cookie and Jar types and parseDomain/parsePath used heavily here
related_files.reason_to_check: Any change to Cookie fields, parsing semantics, or Jar.add/remove behavior affects CdpStorage conversions and serialization.

related_files.relationship: Network domain delegates setCookie/setCookies calls to CdpStorage.setCdpCookie and shares cookie serialization expectations
related_files.reason_to_check: If Storage-write semantics (e.g., domain normalization) change, Network.deleteCookies/getCookies must stay consistent.

tests_to_run: zig build test (project-wide) or make test
data_constants_to_check: writeCookie field defaults (expires -> -1, sameSite mapping to 'None'/'Lax'/'Strict')

insights.title: Navigator.languages must include base language
insights.problem: Test expected navigator.languages to contain only the regional tag (e.g. ["en-US"]) but browsers include the base language too (e.g. "en").
insights.root_cause: Implementation returned only the primary (regional) language tag and did not include the base language variant required by spec and observed browser behavior.
insights.solution: Test updated to expect two entries; the implementation was changed to return both "en-US" and "en".
insights.lesson_learned: Implement Web API behaviors according to spec and widely used browser semantics — some consumers rely on both regional and base language tags.

insights.title: Add FormDataEvent behavior tests
insights.problem: No tests ensured FormData constructor dispatches formdata event with correct properties and bubbling behavior.
insights.root_cause: FormDataEvent support was newly implemented and needed coverage.
insights.solution: Added multiple tests covering event firing when FormData constructed with a form, non-firing when no form provided, bubbling semantics, cancelable flag, and ability for listeners to modify the FormData.
insights.lesson_learned: Add tests that express event semantics (bubbling, cancelable, event payload mutation) when implementing DOM events.

insights.title: Pass pre_version as build flag from CI
insights.problem: Workflow previously passed -Dversion_string for tags and hardcoded pre version for nightly builds; this was inflexible.
insights.root_cause: CI needed to pass different zig build flags depending on tag vs nightly.
insights.solution: Compute VERSION_FLAG env var to either pass -Dversion_string or -Dpre_version and pass it into zig build invocation.
insights.lesson_learned: Make CI-driven build flag selection explicit and propagate through build invocation to avoid duplicate logic in build scripts.
